   13|       | /* Argon2 Team - Begin Code */
   14|       | /*
   15|       |	Not an exhaustive list, but should cover the majority of modern platforms
   16|       |	Additionally, the code will always be correct---this is only a performance
   17|       |	tweak.
   18|       | */
   19|       |#if (defined(__BYTE_ORDER__) &&                                                \
   20|       |     (__BYTE_ORDER__ == __ORDER_LITTLE_ENDIAN__)) ||                           \
   21|       |    defined(__LITTLE_ENDIAN__) || defined(__ARMEL__) || defined(__MIPSEL__) || \
   22|       |    defined(__AARCH64EL__) || defined(__amd64__) || defined(__i386__) ||       \
   23|       |    defined(_M_IX86) || defined(_M_X64) || defined(_M_AMD64) ||                \
   24|       |    defined(_M_ARM)
   25|       |#define NATIVE_LITTLE_ENDIAN
   26|       |#endif
   27|       | /* Argon2 Team - End Code */

   29|      0|static FORCE_INLINE uint32_t load32(const void *src) {
   30|      0|#if defined(NATIVE_LITTLE_ENDIAN)
   31|      0|	uint32_t w;
   32|      0|	memcpy(&w, src, sizeof w);
   33|      0|	return w;
   34|      0|#else
   35|      0|	const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)src;
   36|      0|	uint32_t w = *p++;
   37|      0|	w |= (uint32_t)(*p++) << 8;
   38|      0|	w |= (uint32_t)(*p++) << 16;
   39|      0|	w |= (uint32_t)(*p++) << 24;
   40|      0|	return w;
   41|      0|#endif

  146|       |	using addr_t = uint32_t;
  147|       |
  148|       |	using int_reg_t = uint64_t;
  149|       |
  150|       |	struct fpu_reg_t {
  151|       |		double lo;
  152|       |		double hi;
  153|       |	};
  154|       |
  155|       |	constexpr uint32_t ScratchpadL1 = RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L1 / sizeof(int_reg_t);
  156|       |	constexpr uint32_t ScratchpadL2 = RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L2 / sizeof(int_reg_t);
  157|       |	constexpr uint32_t ScratchpadL3 = RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L3 / sizeof(int_reg_t);
  158|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL1Mask = (ScratchpadL1 - 1) * 8;
  159|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL2Mask = (ScratchpadL2 - 1) * 8;
  160|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL1Mask16 = (ScratchpadL1 / 2 - 1) * 16;
  161|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL2Mask16 = (ScratchpadL2 / 2 - 1) * 16;
  162|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL3Mask = (ScratchpadL3 - 1) * 8;
  163|       |	constexpr int ScratchpadL3Mask64 = (ScratchpadL3 / 8 - 1) * 64;
  164|       |	constexpr int RegistersCount = 8;
  165|       |	constexpr int RegisterCountFlt = RegistersCount / 2;
  166|       |	constexpr int RegisterNeedsDisplacement = 5; //x86 r13 register
  167|       |	constexpr int RegisterNeedsSib = 4; //x86 r12 register
  168|       |
  169|      0|	inline bool isZeroOrPowerOf2(uint64_t x) {
  170|      0|		return (x & (x - 1)) == 0;
  171|      0|	}
  172|       |
  173|       |	constexpr int mantissaSize = 52;
  174|       |	constexpr int exponentSize = 11;
  175|       |	constexpr uint64_t mantissaMask = (1ULL << mantissaSize) - 1;
  176|       |	constexpr uint64_t exponentMask = (1ULL << exponentSize) - 1;
  177|       |	constexpr int exponentBias = 1023;
  178|       |	constexpr int dynamicExponentBits = 4;
  179|       |	constexpr int staticExponentBits = 4;
  180|       |	constexpr uint64_t constExponentBits = 0x300;
  181|       |	constexpr uint64_t dynamicMantissaMask = (1ULL << (mantissaSize + dynamicExponentBits)) - 1;
  182|       |
  183|       |	struct MemoryRegisters {
  184|       |		addr_t mx, ma;
  185|       |		uint8_t* memory = nullptr;
  186|       |	};
  187|       |
  188|       |	//register file in little-endian byte order
  189|       |	struct RegisterFile {
  190|       |		int_reg_t r[RegistersCount];
  191|       |		fpu_reg_t f[RegisterCountFlt];
  192|       |		fpu_reg_t e[RegisterCountFlt];
  193|       |		fpu_reg_t a[RegisterCountFlt];
  194|       |	};
  195|       |
  196|       |	typedef void(ProgramFunc)(RegisterFile&, MemoryRegisters&, uint8_t* /* scratchpad */, uint64_t);
  197|       |	typedef void(DatasetInitFunc)(randomx_cache* cache, uint8_t* dataset, uint32_t startBlock, uint32_t endBlock);
  198|       |
  199|       |	typedef void(DatasetDeallocFunc)(randomx_dataset*);
  200|       |	typedef void(CacheDeallocFunc)(randomx_cache*);
  201|       |	typedef void(CacheInitializeFunc)(randomx_cache*, const void*, size_t);

   32|      4|#define RANDOMX_ARGON_MEMORY       262144
   33|       |
   34|       |//Number of Argon2d iterations for Cache initialization.
   35|       |#define RANDOMX_ARGON_ITERATIONS   3
   36|       |
   37|       |//Number of parallel lanes for Cache initialization.
   38|       |#define RANDOMX_ARGON_LANES        1
   39|       |
   40|       |//Argon2d salt
   41|       |#define RANDOMX_ARGON_SALT         "RandomX\x03"
   42|       |
   43|       |//Number of random Cache accesses per Dataset item. Minimum is 2.
   44|       |#define RANDOMX_CACHE_ACCESSES     8
   45|       |
   46|       |//Target latency for SuperscalarHash (in cycles of the reference CPU).
   47|       |#define RANDOMX_SUPERSCALAR_LATENCY   170
   48|       |
   49|       |//Dataset base size in bytes. Must be a power of 2.
   50|      0|#define RANDOMX_DATASET_BASE_SIZE  2147483648
   51|       |
   52|       |//Dataset extra size. Must be divisible by 64.
   53|      0|#define RANDOMX_DATASET_EXTRA_SIZE 33554368
   54|       |
   55|       |//Number of instructions in a RandomX program. Must be divisible by 8.
   56|       |#define RANDOMX_PROGRAM_SIZE_V1    256
   57|       |#define RANDOMX_PROGRAM_SIZE_V2    384
   58|       |
   59|       |#define RANDOMX_PROGRAM_MAX_SIZE   384
   60|       |
   61|       |//Number of iterations during VM execution.
   62|       |#define RANDOMX_PROGRAM_ITERATIONS 2048
   63|       |
   64|       |//Number of chained VM executions per hash.
   65|       |#define RANDOMX_PROGRAM_COUNT      8
   66|       |
   67|       |//Scratchpad L3 size in bytes. Must be a power of 2.
   68|       |#define RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L3      2097152
   69|       |
   70|       |//Scratchpad L2 size in bytes. Must be a power of two and less than or equal to RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L3.
   71|       |#define RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L2      262144
   72|       |
   73|       |//Scratchpad L1 size in bytes. Must be a power of two (minimum 64) and less than or equal to RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L2.
   74|       |#define RANDOMX_SCRATCHPAD_L1      16384
   75|       |
   76|       |//Jump condition mask size in bits.
   77|       |#define RANDOMX_JUMP_BITS          8
   78|       |
   79|       |//Jump condition mask offset in bits. The sum of RANDOMX_JUMP_BITS and RANDOMX_JUMP_OFFSET must not exceed 16.
   80|       |#define RANDOMX_JUMP_OFFSET        8
   81|       |
   82|       |/*
   83|       |Instruction frequencies (per 256 opcodes)
   84|       |Total sum of frequencies must be 256
   85|       |*/
   86|       |
   87|       |//Integer instructions
   88|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IADD_RS       16
   89|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IADD_M         7
   90|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISUB_R        16
   91|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISUB_M         7
   92|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IMUL_R        16
   93|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IMUL_M         4
   94|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IMULH_R        4
   95|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IMULH_M        1
   96|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISMULH_R       4
   97|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISMULH_M       1
   98|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IMUL_RCP       8
   99|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_INEG_R         2
  100|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IXOR_R        15
  101|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IXOR_M         5
  102|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IROR_R         8
  103|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_IROL_R         2
  104|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISWAP_R        4
  105|       |
  106|       |//Floating point instructions
  107|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FSWAP_R        4
  108|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FADD_R        16
  109|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FADD_M         5
  110|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FSUB_R        16
  111|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FSUB_M         5
  112|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FSCAL_R        6
  113|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FMUL_R        32
  114|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FDIV_M         4
  115|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_FSQRT_R        6
  116|       |
  117|       |//Control instructions
  118|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_CBRANCH       25
  119|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_CFROUND        1
  120|       |
  121|       |//Store instruction
  122|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_ISTORE        16
  123|       |
  124|       |//No-op instruction
  125|       |#define RANDOMX_FREQ_NOP            0
  126|       |/*                               ------
  127|       |                                  256
  128|       |*/

   40|       |		IADD_RS = 0,
   41|       |		IADD_M = 1,
   42|       |		ISUB_R = 2,
   43|       |		ISUB_M = 3,
   44|       |		IMUL_R = 4,
   45|       |		IMUL_M = 5,
   46|       |		IMULH_R = 6,
   47|       |		IMULH_M = 7,
   48|       |		ISMULH_R = 8,
   49|       |		ISMULH_M = 9,
   50|       |		IMUL_RCP = 10,
   51|       |		INEG_R = 11,
   52|       |		IXOR_R = 12,
   53|       |		IXOR_M = 13,
   54|       |		IROR_R = 14,
   55|       |		IROL_R = 15,
   56|       |		ISWAP_R = 16,
   57|       |		FSWAP_R = 17,
   58|       |		FADD_R = 18,
   59|       |		FADD_M = 19,
   60|       |		FSUB_R = 20,
   61|       |		FSUB_M = 21,
   62|       |		FSCAL_R = 22,
   63|       |		FMUL_R = 23,
   64|       |		FDIV_M = 24,
   65|       |		FSQRT_R = 25,
   66|       |		CBRANCH = 26,
   67|       |		CFROUND = 27,
   68|       |		ISTORE = 28,
   69|       |		NOP = 29,
   70|       |	};
   71|       |
   72|       |	class Instruction {
   73|       |	public:
   74|      0|		uint32_t getImm32() const {
   75|      0|			return load32(&imm32);
   76|      0|		}
   77|      0|		void setImm32(uint32_t val) {
   78|      0|			return store32(&imm32, val);
   79|      0|		}
   80|      0|		int getModMem() const {
   81|      0|			return mod % 4; //bits 0-1
   82|      0|		}
   83|      0|		int getModShift() const {
   84|      0|			return (mod >> 2) % 4; //bits 2-3
   85|      0|		}
   86|      0|		int getModCond() const {
   87|      0|			return mod >> 4; //bits 4-7
   88|      0|		}
   89|      0|		void setMod(uint8_t val) {
   90|      0|			mod = val;
   91|      0|		}
   92|       |
   93|       |		uint8_t opcode;
   94|       |		uint8_t dst;
   95|       |		uint8_t src;
   96|       |		uint8_t mod;
   97|       |		uint32_t imm32;
   98|       |	};

  100|       |	static_assert(sizeof(Instruction) == 8, "Invalid size of struct randomx::Instruction");

   36|       |#include <cstdint>

   38|       |
   39|      0|constexpr int32_t unsigned32ToSigned2sCompl(uint32_t x) {
   40|      0|	return (-1 == ~0) ? (int32_t)x : (x > INT32_MAX ? (-(int32_t)(UINT32_MAX - x) - 1) : (int32_t)x);
   41|      0|}
   42|       |
   43|      0|constexpr int64_t unsigned64ToSigned2sCompl(uint64_t x) {
   44|      0|	return (-1 == ~0) ? (int64_t)x : (x > INT64_MAX ? (-(int64_t)(UINT64_MAX - x) - 1) : (int64_t)x);
   45|      0|}
   46|       |
   47|      0|constexpr uint64_t signExtend2sCompl(uint32_t x) {
   48|      0|	return (-1 == ~0) ? (int64_t)(int32_t)(x) : (x > INT32_MAX ? (x | 0xffffffff00000000ULL) : (uint64_t)x);
   49|      0|}
   50|       |
   51|       |constexpr int RoundToNearest = 0;
   52|       |constexpr int RoundDown = 1;
   53|       |constexpr int RoundUp = 2;
   54|       |constexpr int RoundToZero = 3;
   55|       |
   56|       |//MSVC doesn't define __SSE2__, so we have to define it manually if SSE2 is available
   57|       |#if !defined(__SSE2__) && (defined(_M_X64) || (defined(_M_IX86_FP) && _M_IX86_FP == 2))
   58|       |#define __SSE2__ 1
   59|       |#endif
   60|       |
   61|       |//MSVC doesn't define __AES__
   62|       |#if defined(_MSC_VER) && defined(__SSE2__)
   63|       |#define __AES__
   64|       |#endif
   65|       |
   66|       |//the library "sqrt" function provided by MSVC for x86 targets doesn't give
   67|       |//the correct results, so we have to use inline assembly to call x87 fsqrt directly
   68|       |#if !defined(__SSE2__)
   69|       |#if defined(_MSC_VER) && defined(_M_IX86)
   70|       |inline double __cdecl rx_sqrt(double x) {
   71|       |	__asm {
   72|       |		fld x
   73|       |		fsqrt
   74|       |	}
   75|       |}
   76|       |#define rx_sqrt rx_sqrt
   77|       |
   78|       |void rx_set_double_precision();
   79|       |#define RANDOMX_USE_X87
   80|       |
   81|       |#elif defined(__i386)
   82|       |
   83|       |void rx_set_double_precision();
   84|       |#define RANDOMX_USE_X87
   85|       |
   86|       |#endif
   87|       |#endif //__SSE2__
   88|       |
   89|       |#if !defined(rx_sqrt)
   90|       |#define rx_sqrt sqrt
   91|       |#endif
   92|       |
   93|       |#if !defined(RANDOMX_USE_X87)
   94|       |#define rx_set_double_precision(x)
   95|       |#endif
   96|       |
   97|       |#ifdef __SSE2__
   98|       |#ifdef __GNUC__
   99|       |#include <x86intrin.h>
  100|       |#else
  101|       |#include <intrin.h>
  102|       |#endif
  103|       |
  104|       |typedef __m128i rx_vec_i128;
  105|       |typedef __m128d rx_vec_f128;
  106|       |
  107|       |#define rx_aligned_alloc(a, b) _mm_malloc(a,b)
  108|       |#define rx_aligned_free(a) _mm_free(a)
  109|       |#define rx_prefetch_nta(x) _mm_prefetch((const char *)(x), _MM_HINT_NTA)
  110|       |#define rx_prefetch_t0(x) _mm_prefetch((const char *)(x), _MM_HINT_T0)
  111|       |
  112|       |#define rx_load_vec_f128 _mm_load_pd

  531|       |
  532|       |FORCE_INLINE rx_vec_f128 rx_cvt_packed_int_vec_f128(const void* addr) {
  533|       |	double lo = unsigned32ToSigned2sCompl(load32((const uint8_t*)addr + 0));
  534|       |	double hi = unsigned32ToSigned2sCompl(load32((const uint8_t*)addr + 4));
  535|       |	rx_vec_f128 x;
  536|       |	x = vsetq_lane_f64(lo, x, 0);
  537|       |	x = vsetq_lane_f64(hi, x, 1);
  538|       |	return x;
  539|       |}

  601|       |	a.lo = temp;
  602|       |	return a;

  729|       |	c.u32[1] = load32(ptr + 1);
  730|       |	c.u32[2] = load32(ptr + 2);
  731|       |	c.u32[3] = load32(ptr + 3);
  732|       |	return c;
  733|       |#endif
  734|       |}

  739|       |#else

  743|       |	store32(ptr + 2, b.u32[2]);
  744|       |	store32(ptr + 3, b.u32[3]);

   44|       |  RANDOMX_FLAG_LARGE_PAGES = 1,
   45|       |  RANDOMX_FLAG_HARD_AES = 2,
   46|       |  RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM = 4,
   47|       |  RANDOMX_FLAG_JIT = 8,
   48|       |  RANDOMX_FLAG_SECURE = 16,

   87|       |/**
   88|       | * @return The recommended flags to be used on the current machine.
   89|       | *         Does not include:
   90|       | *            RANDOMX_FLAG_LARGE_PAGES
   91|       | *            RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM
   92|       | *            RANDOMX_FLAG_SECURE
   93|       | *         These flags must be added manually if desired.
   94|       | *         On OpenBSD RANDOMX_FLAG_SECURE is enabled by default in JIT mode as W^X is enforced by the OS.
   95|       | */
   96|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT randomx_flags randomx_get_flags(void);
   97|       |
   98|       |/**
   99|       | * Creates a randomx_cache structure and allocates memory for RandomX Cache.
  100|       | *
  101|       | * @param flags is any combination of these 2 flags (each flag can be set or not set):
  102|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_LARGE_PAGES - allocate memory in large pages
  103|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_JIT - create cache structure with JIT compilation support; this makes
  104|       | *                           subsequent Dataset initialization faster
  105|       | *        Optionally, one of these two flags may be selected:
  106|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_ARGON2_SSSE3 - optimized Argon2 for CPUs with the SSSE3 instruction set
  107|       | *                                   makes subsequent cache initialization faster
  108|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_ARGON2_AVX2 - optimized Argon2 for CPUs with the AVX2 instruction set
  109|       | *                                   makes subsequent cache initialization faster
  110|       | *
  111|       | * @return Pointer to an allocated randomx_cache structure.
  112|       | *         Returns NULL if:
  113|       | *         (1) memory allocation fails
  114|       | *         (2) the RANDOMX_FLAG_JIT is set and JIT compilation is not supported on the current platform
  115|       | *         (3) an invalid or unsupported RANDOMX_FLAG_ARGON2 value is set
  116|       | */
  117|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT randomx_cache *randomx_alloc_cache(randomx_flags flags);
  118|       |
  119|       |/**
  120|       | * Initializes the cache memory and SuperscalarHash using the provided key value.
  121|       | * Does nothing if called again with the same key value.
  122|       | *
  123|       | * @param cache is a pointer to a previously allocated randomx_cache structure. Must not be NULL.
  124|       | * @param key is a pointer to memory which contains the key value. Must not be NULL.
  125|       | * @param keySize is the number of bytes of the key.
  126|       |*/
  127|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_init_cache(randomx_cache *cache, const void *key, size_t keySize);
  128|       |
  129|       |/**
  130|       | * Returns a pointer to the internal memory buffer of the cache structure. The size
  131|       | * of the internal memory buffer is RANDOMX_ARGON_MEMORY KiB.
  132|       | *
  133|       | * @param cache is a pointer to a previously allocated randomx_cache structure. Must not be NULL.
  134|       | *
  135|       | * @return Pointer to the internal memory buffer of the cache structure.
  136|       |*/
  137|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void *randomx_get_cache_memory(randomx_cache *cache);

  139|       |/**

  144|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_release_cache(randomx_cache* cache);
  145|       |
  146|       |/**
  147|       | * Creates a randomx_dataset structure and allocates memory for RandomX Dataset.
  148|       | *
  149|       | * @param flags is the initialization flags. Only one flag is supported (can be set or not set):
  150|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_LARGE_PAGES - allocate memory in large pages
  151|       | *
  152|       | * @return Pointer to an allocated randomx_dataset structure.
  153|       | *         NULL is returned if memory allocation fails.
  154|       | */
  155|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT randomx_dataset *randomx_alloc_dataset(randomx_flags flags);
  156|       |
  157|       |/**
  158|       | * Gets the number of items contained in the dataset.
  159|       | *
  160|       | * @return the number of items contained in the dataset.
  161|       |*/
  162|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT unsigned long randomx_dataset_item_count(void);
  163|       |
  164|       |/**
  165|       | * Initializes dataset items.
  166|       | *
  167|       | * Note: In order to use the Dataset, all items from 0 to (randomx_dataset_item_count() - 1) must be initialized.
  168|       | * This may be done by several calls to this function using non-overlapping item sequences.
  169|       | *
  170|       | * @param dataset is a pointer to a previously allocated randomx_dataset structure. Must not be NULL.
  171|       | * @param cache is a pointer to a previously allocated and initialized randomx_cache structure. Must not be NULL.
  172|       | * @param startItem is the item number where initialization should start.
  173|       | * @param itemCount is the number of items that should be initialized.
  174|       |*/
  175|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_init_dataset(randomx_dataset *dataset, randomx_cache *cache, unsigned long startItem, unsigned long itemCount);
  176|       |
  177|       |/**
  178|       | * Returns a pointer to the internal memory buffer of the dataset structure. The size
  179|       | * of the internal memory buffer is randomx_dataset_item_count() * RANDOMX_DATASET_ITEM_SIZE.
  180|       | *
  181|       | * @param dataset is a pointer to a previously allocated randomx_dataset structure. Must not be NULL.
  182|       | *
  183|       | * @return Pointer to the internal memory buffer of the dataset structure.
  184|       |*/
  185|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void *randomx_get_dataset_memory(randomx_dataset *dataset);
  186|       |
  187|       |/**
  188|       | * Releases all memory occupied by the randomx_dataset structure.
  189|       | *
  190|       | * @param dataset is a pointer to a previously allocated randomx_dataset structure.
  191|       |*/
  192|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_release_dataset(randomx_dataset *dataset);
  193|       |
  194|       |/**
  195|       | * Creates and initializes a RandomX virtual machine.
  196|       | *
  197|       | * @param flags is any combination of these 5 flags (each flag can be set or not set):
  198|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_LARGE_PAGES - allocate scratchpad memory in large pages
  199|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_HARD_AES - virtual machine will use hardware accelerated AES
  200|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM - virtual machine will use the full dataset
  201|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_JIT - virtual machine will use a JIT compiler
  202|       | *        RANDOMX_FLAG_SECURE - when combined with RANDOMX_FLAG_JIT, the JIT pages are never
  203|       | *                              writable and executable at the same time (W^X policy)
  204|       | *        The numeric values of the first 4 flags are ordered so that a higher value will provide
  205|       | *        faster hash calculation and a lower numeric value will provide higher portability.
  206|       | *        Using RANDOMX_FLAG_DEFAULT (all flags not set) works on all platforms, but is the slowest.
  207|       | * @param cache is a pointer to an initialized randomx_cache structure. Can be
  208|       | *        NULL if RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM is set.
  209|       | * @param dataset is a pointer to a randomx_dataset structure. Can be NULL
  210|       | *        if RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM is not set.
  211|       | *
  212|       | * @return Pointer to an initialized randomx_vm structure.
  213|       | *         Returns NULL if:
  214|       | *         (1) Scratchpad memory allocation fails.
  215|       | *         (2) The requested initialization flags are not supported on the current platform.
  216|       | *         (3) cache parameter is NULL and RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM is not set
  217|       | *         (4) dataset parameter is NULL and RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM is set
  218|       |*/
  219|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT randomx_vm *randomx_create_vm(randomx_flags flags, randomx_cache *cache, randomx_dataset *dataset);
  220|       |
  221|       |/**
  222|       | * Reinitializes a virtual machine with a new Cache. This function should be called anytime
  223|       | * the Cache is reinitialized with a new key. Does nothing if called with a Cache containing
  224|       | * the same key value as already set.
  225|       | *
  226|       | * @param machine is a pointer to a randomx_vm structure that was initialized
  227|       | *        without RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM. Must not be NULL.
  228|       | * @param cache is a pointer to an initialized randomx_cache structure. Must not be NULL.
  229|       |*/
  230|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_vm_set_cache(randomx_vm *machine, randomx_cache* cache);
  231|       |
  232|       |/**
  233|       | * Reinitializes a virtual machine with a new Dataset.
  234|       | *
  235|       | * @param machine is a pointer to a randomx_vm structure that was initialized
  236|       | *        with RANDOMX_FLAG_FULL_MEM. Must not be NULL.
  237|       | * @param dataset is a pointer to an initialized randomx_dataset structure. Must not be NULL.
  238|       |*/
  239|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_vm_set_dataset(randomx_vm *machine, randomx_dataset *dataset);
  240|       |
  241|       |/**
  242|       | * Releases all memory occupied by the randomx_vm structure.
  243|       | *
  244|       | * @param machine is a pointer to a previously created randomx_vm structure.
  245|       |*/
  246|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_destroy_vm(randomx_vm *machine);
  247|       |
  248|       |/**
  249|       | * Calculates a RandomX hash value.
  250|       | *
  251|       | * @param machine is a pointer to a randomx_vm structure. Must not be NULL.
  252|       | * @param input is a pointer to memory to be hashed. Must not be NULL.
  253|       | * @param inputSize is the number of bytes to be hashed.
  254|       | * @param output is a pointer to memory where the hash will be stored. Must not
  255|       | *        be NULL and at least RANDOMX_HASH_SIZE bytes must be available for writing.
  256|       |*/
  257|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_calculate_hash(randomx_vm *machine, const void *input, size_t inputSize, void *output);
  258|       |
  259|       |/**
  260|       | * Set of functions used to calculate multiple RandomX hashes more efficiently.
  261|       | * randomx_calculate_hash_first will begin a hash calculation.
  262|       | * randomx_calculate_hash_next  will output the hash value of the previous input
  263|       | *                              and begin the calculation of the next hash.
  264|       | * randomx_calculate_hash_last  will output the hash value of the previous input.
  265|       | *
  266|       | * WARNING: These functions may alter the floating point rounding mode of the calling thread.
  267|       | *
  268|       | * @param machine is a pointer to a randomx_vm structure. Must not be NULL.
  269|       | * @param input is a pointer to memory to be hashed. Must not be NULL.
  270|       | * @param inputSize is the number of bytes to be hashed.
  271|       | * @param nextInput is a pointer to memory to be hashed for the next hash. Must not be NULL.
  272|       | * @param nextInputSize is the number of bytes to be hashed for the next hash.
  273|       | * @param output is a pointer to memory where the hash will be stored. Must not
  274|       | *        be NULL and at least RANDOMX_HASH_SIZE bytes must be available for writing.
  275|       |*/
  276|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_calculate_hash_first(randomx_vm* machine, const void* input, size_t inputSize);
  277|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_calculate_hash_next(randomx_vm* machine, const void* nextInput, size_t nextInputSize, void* output);
  278|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_calculate_hash_last(randomx_vm* machine, void* output);
  279|       |
  280|       |/**
  281|       | * Calculate a RandomX commitment from a RandomX hash and its input.
  282|       | *
  283|       | * @param input is a pointer to memory that was hashed. Must not be NULL.
  284|       | * @param inputSize is the number of bytes in the input.
  285|       | * @param hash_in is the output from randomx_calculate_hash* (RANDOMX_HASH_SIZE bytes).
  286|       | * @param com_out is a pointer to memory where the commitment will be stored. Must not
  287|       | *        be NULL and at least RANDOMX_HASH_SIZE bytes must be available for writing.
  288|       |*/
  289|       |RANDOMX_EXPORT void randomx_calculate_commitment(const void* input, size_t inputSize, const void* hash_in, void* com_out);
  290|       |
  291|       |#if defined(__cplusplus)
  292|       |}
  293|       |#endif
  294|       |
  295|       |#endif

   67|      0|			vmFlags = static_cast<randomx_flags>(static_cast<int>(vmFlags) - RANDOMX_FLAG_V2);

  442|       |    template<class ForwardIter> message_t(ForwardIter first, ForwardIter last)
  443|       |    {
  444|       |        typedef typename std::iterator_traits<ForwardIter>::value_type value_t;
  445|       |
  446|       |        assert(std::distance(first, last) >= 0);

  448|       |          static_cast<size_t>(std::distance(first, last)) * sizeof(value_t);
  449|       |        int const rc = zmq_msg_init_size(&msg, size_);
  450|       |        if (rc != 0)
  451|       |            throw error_t();

  705|       |    /** Dump content to string for debugging.
  706|       |    *   Ascii chars are readable, the rest is printed as hex.
  707|       |    *   Probably ridiculously slow.
  708|       |    *   Use to_string() or to_string_view() for
  709|       |    *   interpreting the message as a string.
  710|       |    */
  711|       |    std::string str(size_t max_size = 1000) const
  712|      0|    {
  713|      0|        // Partly mutuated from the same method in zmq::multipart_t
  714|      0|        std::stringstream os;
  715|      0|
  716|      0|        const unsigned char *msg_data = this->data<unsigned char>();
  717|      0|        size_t size_to_print = std::min(this->size(), max_size);
  718|      0|        int is_ascii[2] = {0, 0};
  719|      0|        // Set is_ascii for the first character
  720|      0|        if (size_to_print > 0)
  721|      0|            is_ascii[0] = (*msg_data >= 32 && *msg_data < 127);
  722|      0|
  723|      0|        os << "zmq::message_t [size " << std::dec << std::setw(3)
  724|      0|           << std::setfill('0') << this->size() << "] (";
  725|      0|        while (size_to_print--) {
  726|      0|            const unsigned char byte = *msg_data++;
  727|      0|
  728|      0|            is_ascii[1] = (byte >= 32 && byte < 127);
  729|      0|            if (is_ascii[1] != is_ascii[0])
  730|      0|                os << " "; // Separate text/non text
  731|      0|
  732|      0|            if (is_ascii[1]) {
  733|      0|                os << byte;
  734|      0|            } else {
  735|      0|                os << std::hex << std::uppercase << std::setw(2) << std::setfill('0')
  736|      0|                   << static_cast<short>(byte);
  737|      0|            }
  738|      0|            is_ascii[0] = is_ascii[1];
  739|      0|        }
  740|      0|        // Elide the rest if the message is too large
  741|      0|        if (max_size < this->size())
  742|      0|            os << "... too big to print)";
  743|      0|        else
  744|      0|            os << ")";
  745|      0|        return os.str();
  746|      0|    }

  895|      0|        ZMQ_ASSERT(rc == 0);

  899|       |    // Shutdown context in preparation for termination (close()).

  903|      0|    {

  905|      0|            return;

  957|       |namespace detail
  958|       |{
  959|       |// A C++11 type emulating the most basic
  960|       |// operations of std::optional for trivial types
  961|       |template<class T> class trivial_optional
  962|       |{
  963|       |  public:
  964|       |    static_assert(std::is_trivial<T>::value, "T must be trivial");
  965|       |    using value_type = T;
  966|       |
  967|       |    trivial_optional() = default;
  968|       |    trivial_optional(T value) noexcept : _value(value), _has_value(true) {}
  969|       |
  970|       |    const T *operator->() const noexcept
  971|       |    {
  972|       |        assert(_has_value);
  973|       |        return &_value;
  974|       |    }
  975|       |    T *operator->() noexcept
  976|       |    {
  977|       |        assert(_has_value);
  978|       |        return &_value;
  979|       |    }
  980|       |
  981|       |    const T &operator*() const noexcept
  982|       |    {
  983|       |        assert(_has_value);
  984|       |        return _value;
  985|       |    }
  986|       |    T &operator*() noexcept
  987|       |    {
  988|       |        assert(_has_value);
  989|       |        return _value;
  990|       |    }
  991|       |
  992|       |    T &value()
  993|       |    {
  994|       |        if (!_has_value)
  995|       |            throw std::exception();
  996|       |        return _value;
  997|       |    }
  998|       |    const T &value() const
  999|       |    {
 1000|       |        if (!_has_value)
 1001|       |            throw std::exception();
 1002|       |        return _value;
 1003|       |    }
 1004|       |
 1005|       |    explicit operator bool() const noexcept { return _has_value; }
 1006|       |    bool has_value() const noexcept { return _has_value; }
 1007|       |
 1008|       |  private:
 1009|       |    T _value{};
 1010|       |    bool _has_value{false};
 1011|       |};
 1012|       |} // namespace detail
 1013|       |
 1014|       |using send_result_t = detail::trivial_optional<size_t>;
 1015|       |using recv_result_t = detail::trivial_optional<size_t>;
 1016|       |using recv_buffer_result_t = detail::trivial_optional<recv_buffer_size>;
 1017|       |
 1018|       |#endif

 1098|       |// mutable_buffer, const_buffer and buffer are based on
 1099|       |// the Networking TS specification, draft:
 1100|       |// http://www.open-std.org/jtc1/sc22/wg21/docs/papers/2018/n4771.pdf
 1101|       |
 1102|       |class mutable_buffer
 1103|       |{
 1104|       |  public:
 1105|      0|    constexpr mutable_buffer() noexcept : _data(nullptr), _size(0) {}
 1106|       |    constexpr mutable_buffer(void *p, size_t n) noexcept : _data(p), _size(n)
 1107|      0|    {
 1108|      0|#ifdef ZMQ_EXTENDED_CONSTEXPR
 1109|      0|        assert(p != nullptr || n == 0);
 1110|      0|#endif
 1111|      0|    }
 1112|       |
 1113|      0|    constexpr void *data() const noexcept { return _data; }
 1114|      0|    constexpr size_t size() const noexcept { return _size; }
 1115|       |    mutable_buffer &operator+=(size_t n) noexcept
 1116|      0|    {
 1117|      0|        const auto shift = std::min(n, _size);
 1118|      0|        _data = static_cast<char *>(_data) + shift;
 1119|      0|        _size -= shift;
 1120|      0|        return *this;
 1121|      0|    }
 1122|       |
 1123|       |  private:
 1124|       |    void *_data;
 1125|       |    size_t _size;
 1126|       |};
 1127|       |
 1128|       |inline mutable_buffer operator+(const mutable_buffer &mb, size_t n) noexcept
 1129|      0|{
 1130|      0|    return mutable_buffer(static_cast<char *>(mb.data()) + std::min(n, mb.size()),
 1131|      0|                          mb.size() - std::min(n, mb.size()));
 1132|      0|}
 1133|       |inline mutable_buffer operator+(size_t n, const mutable_buffer &mb) noexcept
 1134|      0|{
 1135|      0|    return mb + n;
 1136|      0|}
 1137|       |
 1138|       |class const_buffer
 1139|       |{
 1140|       |  public:
 1141|      0|    constexpr const_buffer() noexcept : _data(nullptr), _size(0) {}
 1142|       |    constexpr const_buffer(const void *p, size_t n) noexcept : _data(p), _size(n)
 1143|      0|    {
 1144|      0|#ifdef ZMQ_EXTENDED_CONSTEXPR
 1145|      0|        assert(p != nullptr || n == 0);
 1146|      0|#endif
 1147|      0|    }
 1148|       |    constexpr const_buffer(const mutable_buffer &mb) noexcept :
 1149|       |        _data(mb.data()), _size(mb.size())
 1150|      0|    {
 1151|      0|    }
 1152|       |
 1153|      0|    constexpr const void *data() const noexcept { return _data; }
 1154|      0|    constexpr size_t size() const noexcept { return _size; }
 1155|       |    const_buffer &operator+=(size_t n) noexcept
 1156|      0|    {
 1157|      0|        const auto shift = std::min(n, _size);
 1158|      0|        _data = static_cast<const char *>(_data) + shift;
 1159|      0|        _size -= shift;
 1160|      0|        return *this;
 1161|      0|    }

 1164|       |    const void *_data;
 1165|       |    size_t _size;

 1171|      0|                          + std::min(n, cb.size()),

 1206|       |namespace detail
 1207|       |{
 1208|       |template<class T> struct is_buffer
 1209|       |{
 1210|       |    static constexpr bool value =
 1211|       |      std::is_same<T, const_buffer>::value || std::is_same<T, mutable_buffer>::value;
 1212|       |};
 1213|       |
 1214|       |template<class T> struct is_pod_like
 1215|       |{
 1216|       |    // NOTE: The networking draft N4771 section 16.11 requires
 1217|       |    // T in the buffer functions below to be
 1218|       |    // trivially copyable OR standard layout.
 1219|       |    // Here we decide to be conservative and require both.
 1220|       |    static constexpr bool value =
 1221|       |      ZMQ_IS_TRIVIALLY_COPYABLE(T) && std::is_standard_layout<T>::value;
 1222|       |};
 1223|       |
 1224|       |template<class C> constexpr auto seq_size(const C &c) noexcept -> decltype(c.size())
 1225|      0|{
 1226|      0|    return c.size();
 1227|      0|}
 1228|       |template<class T, size_t N>
 1229|       |constexpr size_t seq_size(const T (& /*array*/)[N]) noexcept
 1230|       |{
 1231|       |    return N;
 1232|       |}
 1233|       |
 1234|       |template<class Seq>
 1235|       |auto buffer_contiguous_sequence(Seq &&seq) noexcept
 1236|       |  -> decltype(buffer(std::addressof(*std::begin(seq)), size_t{}))
 1237|      0|{
 1238|      0|    using T = typename std::remove_cv<
 1239|      0|      typename std::remove_reference<decltype(*std::begin(seq))>::type>::type;
 1240|      0|    static_assert(detail::is_pod_like<T>::value, "T must be POD");
 1241|      0|
 1242|      0|    const auto size = seq_size(seq);
 1243|      0|    return buffer(size != 0u ? std::addressof(*std::begin(seq)) : nullptr,
 1244|      0|                  size * sizeof(T));
 1245|      0|}
 1246|       |template<class Seq>
 1247|       |auto buffer_contiguous_sequence(Seq &&seq, size_t n_bytes) noexcept
 1248|       |  -> decltype(buffer_contiguous_sequence(seq))
 1249|       |{
 1250|       |    using T = typename std::remove_cv<
 1251|       |      typename std::remove_reference<decltype(*std::begin(seq))>::type>::type;
 1252|       |    static_assert(detail::is_pod_like<T>::value, "T must be POD");
 1253|       |
 1254|       |    const auto size = seq_size(seq);
 1255|       |    return buffer(size != 0u ? std::addressof(*std::begin(seq)) : nullptr,
 1256|       |                  std::min(size * sizeof(T), n_bytes));
 1257|       |}
 1258|       |
 1259|       |} // namespace detail

 1369|       |// Buffer for a string literal (null terminated)
 1370|       |// where the buffer size excludes the terminating character.
 1371|       |// Equivalent to zmq::buffer(std::string_view("...")).
 1372|       |template<class Char, size_t N>
 1373|       |constexpr const_buffer str_buffer(const Char (&data)[N]) noexcept
 1374|       |{
 1375|       |    static_assert(detail::is_pod_like<Char>::value, "Char must be POD");
 1376|       |#ifdef ZMQ_EXTENDED_CONSTEXPR
 1377|       |    assert(data[N - 1] == Char{0});
 1378|       |#endif
 1379|       |    return const_buffer(static_cast<const Char *>(data), (N - 1) * sizeof(Char));
 1380|       |}

 1435|       |namespace sockopt
 1436|       |{
 1437|       |// There are two types of options,
 1438|       |// integral type with known compiler time size (int, bool, int64_t, uint64_t)
 1439|       |// and arrays with dynamic size (strings, binary data).
 1440|       |
 1441|       |// BoolUnit: if true accepts values of type bool (but passed as T into libzmq)
 1442|       |template<int Opt, class T, bool BoolUnit = false> struct integral_option
 1443|       |{
 1444|       |};
 1445|       |
 1446|       |// NullTerm:
 1447|       |// 0: binary data
 1448|       |// 1: null-terminated string (`getsockopt` size includes null)
 1449|       |// 2: binary (size 32) or Z85 encoder string of size 41 (null included)
 1450|       |template<int Opt, int NullTerm = 1> struct array_option
 1451|       |{
 1452|       |};
 1453|       |
 1454|       |#define ZMQ_DEFINE_INTEGRAL_OPT(OPT, NAME, TYPE)                                    \
 1455|       |    using NAME##_t = integral_option<OPT, TYPE, false>;                             \
 1456|       |    ZMQ_INLINE_VAR ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_VAR NAME##_t NAME                                  \
 1457|       |    {                                                                               \
 1458|       |    }
 1459|       |#define ZMQ_DEFINE_INTEGRAL_BOOL_UNIT_OPT(OPT, NAME, TYPE)                          \
 1460|       |    using NAME##_t = integral_option<OPT, TYPE, true>;                              \
 1461|       |    ZMQ_INLINE_VAR ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_VAR NAME##_t NAME                                  \
 1462|       |    {                                                                               \
 1463|       |    }
 1464|       |#define ZMQ_DEFINE_ARRAY_OPT(OPT, NAME)                                             \
 1465|       |    using NAME##_t = array_option<OPT>;                                             \
 1466|       |    ZMQ_INLINE_VAR ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_VAR NAME##_t NAME                                  \
 1467|       |    {                                                                               \
 1468|       |    }
 1469|       |#define ZMQ_DEFINE_ARRAY_OPT_BINARY(OPT, NAME)                                      \
 1470|       |    using NAME##_t = array_option<OPT, 0>;                                          \
 1471|       |    ZMQ_INLINE_VAR ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_VAR NAME##_t NAME                                  \
 1472|       |    {                                                                               \
 1473|       |    }
 1474|       |#define ZMQ_DEFINE_ARRAY_OPT_BIN_OR_Z85(OPT, NAME)                                  \
 1475|       |    using NAME##_t = array_option<OPT, 2>;                                          \
 1476|       |    ZMQ_INLINE_VAR ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_VAR NAME##_t NAME                                  \
 1477|       |    {                                                                               \
 1478|       |    }

 1763|       |namespace detail
 1764|       |{
 1765|       |class socket_base
 1766|       |{
 1767|       |  public:
 1768|      0|    socket_base() ZMQ_NOTHROW : _handle(ZMQ_NULLPTR) {}
 1769|      0|    ZMQ_EXPLICIT socket_base(void *handle) ZMQ_NOTHROW : _handle(handle) {}
 1770|       |
 1771|       |    template<typename T>
 1772|       |    ZMQ_CPP11_DEPRECATED("from 4.7.0, use `set` taking option from zmq::sockopt")
 1773|       |    void setsockopt(int option_, T const &optval)
 1774|       |    {
 1775|       |        setsockopt(option_, &optval, sizeof(T));
 1776|       |    }
 1777|       |
 1778|       |    ZMQ_CPP11_DEPRECATED("from 4.7.0, use `set` taking option from zmq::sockopt")
 1779|       |    void setsockopt(int option_, const void *optval_, size_t optvallen_)
 1780|      0|    {
 1781|      0|        int rc = zmq_setsockopt(_handle, option_, optval_, optvallen_);
 1782|      0|        if (rc != 0)
 1783|      0|            throw error_t();
 1784|      0|    }
 1785|       |
 1786|       |    ZMQ_CPP11_DEPRECATED("from 4.7.0, use `get` taking option from zmq::sockopt")
 1787|       |    void getsockopt(int option_, void *optval_, size_t *optvallen_) const
 1788|      0|    {
 1789|      0|        int rc = zmq_getsockopt(_handle, option_, optval_, optvallen_);
 1790|      0|        if (rc != 0)
 1791|      0|            throw error_t();
 1792|      0|    }
 1793|       |
 1794|       |    template<typename T>
 1795|       |    ZMQ_CPP11_DEPRECATED("from 4.7.0, use `get` taking option from zmq::sockopt")
 1796|       |    T getsockopt(int option_) const
 1797|       |    {
 1798|       |        T optval;
 1799|       |        size_t optlen = sizeof(T);
 1800|       |        getsockopt(option_, &optval, &optlen);
 1801|       |        return optval;
 1802|       |    }
 1803|       |
 1804|       |#ifdef ZMQ_CPP11
 1805|       |    // Set integral socket option, e.g.
 1806|       |    // `socket.set(zmq::sockopt::linger, 0)`
 1807|       |    template<int Opt, class T, bool BoolUnit>
 1808|       |    void set(sockopt::integral_option<Opt, T, BoolUnit>, const T &val)
 1809|       |    {
 1810|       |        static_assert(std::is_integral<T>::value, "T must be integral");
 1811|       |        set_option(Opt, &val, sizeof val);
 1812|       |    }
 1813|       |
 1814|       |    // Set integral socket option from boolean, e.g.
 1815|       |    // `socket.set(zmq::sockopt::immediate, false)`
 1816|       |    template<int Opt, class T>
 1817|       |    void set(sockopt::integral_option<Opt, T, true>, bool val)
 1818|       |    {
 1819|       |        static_assert(std::is_integral<T>::value, "T must be integral");
 1820|       |        T rep_val = val;
 1821|       |        set_option(Opt, &rep_val, sizeof rep_val);
 1822|       |    }
 1823|       |
 1824|       |    // Set array socket option, e.g.
 1825|       |    // `socket.set(zmq::sockopt::plain_username, "foo123")`
 1826|       |    template<int Opt, int NullTerm>
 1827|       |    void set(sockopt::array_option<Opt, NullTerm>, const char *buf)
 1828|       |    {
 1829|       |        set_option(Opt, buf, std::strlen(buf));
 1830|       |    }
 1831|       |
 1832|       |    // Set array socket option, e.g.
 1833|       |    // `socket.set(zmq::sockopt::routing_id, zmq::buffer(id))`
 1834|       |    template<int Opt, int NullTerm>
 1835|       |    void set(sockopt::array_option<Opt, NullTerm>, const_buffer buf)
 1836|       |    {
 1837|       |        set_option(Opt, buf.data(), buf.size());
 1838|       |    }
 1839|       |
 1840|       |    // Set array socket option, e.g.

 1844|       |    {
 1845|       |        set_option(Opt, buf.data(), buf.size());
 1846|       |    }

 1853|       |    {

 1857|       |
 1858|       |    // Get scalar socket option, e.g.
 1859|       |    // `auto opt = socket.get(zmq::sockopt::linger)`
 1860|       |    template<int Opt, class T, bool BoolUnit>
 1861|       |    ZMQ_NODISCARD T get(sockopt::integral_option<Opt, T, BoolUnit>) const
 1862|       |    {
 1863|       |        static_assert(std::is_scalar<T>::value, "T must be scalar");
 1864|       |        T val;
 1865|       |        size_t size = sizeof val;
 1866|       |        get_option(Opt, &val, &size);
 1867|       |        assert(size == sizeof val);
 1868|       |        return val;
 1869|       |    }
 1870|       |
 1871|       |    // Get array socket option, writes to buf, returns option size in bytes, e.g.
 1872|       |    // `size_t optsize = socket.get(zmq::sockopt::routing_id, zmq::buffer(id))`
 1873|       |    template<int Opt, int NullTerm>
 1874|       |    ZMQ_NODISCARD size_t get(sockopt::array_option<Opt, NullTerm>,
 1875|       |                             mutable_buffer buf) const
 1876|       |    {
 1877|       |        size_t size = buf.size();
 1878|       |        get_option(Opt, buf.data(), &size);
 1879|       |        return size;
 1880|       |    }
 1881|       |
 1882|       |    // Get array socket option as string (initializes the string buffer size to init_size) e.g.
 1883|       |    // `auto s = socket.get(zmq::sockopt::routing_id)`
 1884|       |    // Note: removes the null character from null-terminated string options,
 1885|       |    // i.e. the string size excludes the null character.
 1886|       |    template<int Opt, int NullTerm>
 1887|       |    ZMQ_NODISCARD std::string get(sockopt::array_option<Opt, NullTerm>,
 1888|       |                                  size_t init_size = 1024) const
 1889|       |    {
 1890|       |        if ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_IF (NullTerm == 2) {
 1891|       |            if (init_size == 1024) {
 1892|       |                init_size = 41; // get as Z85 string
 1893|       |            }
 1894|       |        }
 1895|       |        std::string str(init_size, '\0');
 1896|       |        size_t size = get(sockopt::array_option<Opt>{}, buffer(str));
 1897|       |        if ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_IF (NullTerm == 1) {
 1898|       |            if (size > 0) {
 1899|       |                assert(str[size - 1] == '\0');
 1900|       |                --size;
 1901|       |            }
 1902|       |        } else if ZMQ_CONSTEXPR_IF (NullTerm == 2) {
 1903|       |            assert(size == 32 || size == 41);
 1904|       |            if (size == 41) {
 1905|       |                assert(str[size - 1] == '\0');
 1906|       |                --size;
 1907|       |            }
 1908|       |        }
 1909|       |        str.resize(size);
 1910|       |        return str;
 1911|       |    }
 1912|       |#endif

 1978|       |      "from 4.4.1, use send taking message_t or buffer (for contiguous "

 1981|       |    {

 1985|       |            return true;
 1986|       |        if (zmq_errno() == EAGAIN)
 1987|       |            return false;

 2558|      0|                return false;

 2560|      0|
 2561|      0|        } else {
 2562|      0|            return false;
 2563|      0|        }
 2564|      0|
 2565|      0|#if ZMQ_VERSION_MAJOR >= 4

 2571|      0|        zmq_event_t *event = &msgEvent;
 2572|      0|#else

  260|  1.06M|void fe_invert(fe out, const fe z) {
  261|  1.06M|  fe t0;
  262|  1.06M|  fe t1;
  263|  1.06M|  fe t2;
  264|  1.06M|  fe t3;
  265|  1.06M|  int i;
  266|       |
  267|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t0, z);
  268|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t1, t0);
  269|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t1, t1);
  270|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t1, z, t1);
  271|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t0, t0, t1);
  272|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t2, t0);
  273|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t1, t1, t2);
  274|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t2, t1);
  275|  5.33M|  for (i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
  276|  4.27M|    fe_sq(t2, t2);
  277|  4.27M|  }
  278|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t1, t2, t1);
  279|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t2, t1);
  280|  10.6M|  for (i = 0; i < 9; ++i) {
  281|  9.60M|    fe_sq(t2, t2);
  282|  9.60M|  }
  283|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t2, t2, t1);
  284|  1.06M|  fe_sq(t3, t2);
  285|  21.3M|  for (i = 0; i < 19; ++i) {
  286|  20.2M|    fe_sq(t3, t3);
  287|  20.2M|  }
  288|  1.06M|  fe_mul(t2, t3, t2);

  290|  10.6M|  for (i = 0; i < 9; ++i) {

  314|       |

  317|       |
  318|       |// Montgomery's trick
  319|       |// https://iacr.org/archive/pkc2004/29470042/29470042.pdf 2.2
  320|      0|int fe_batch_invert(fe *out, const fe *in, const int n) {
  321|      0|  if (n == 0) {
  322|      0|    return 0;
  323|      0|  }
  324|       |
  325|       |  // Step 1: collect initial muls
  326|      0|  fe *init_muls = (fe *) malloc(n * sizeof(fe));
  327|      0|  if (!init_muls) {
  328|      0|    return 1;
  329|      0|  }
  330|      0|  memcpy(&init_muls[0], &in[0], sizeof(fe));
  331|      0|  for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
  332|      0|    fe_mul(init_muls[i], init_muls[i-1], in[i]);
  333|      0|  }
  334|       |
  335|       |  // Step 2: get the inverse of all elems multiplied together
  336|      0|  fe a;
  337|      0|  fe_invert(a, init_muls[n-1]);
  338|       |
  339|       |  // Step 3: get each inverse
  340|      0|  for (int i = n; i > 1; --i) {
  341|      0|    fe_mul(out[i-1], a, init_muls[i-2]);
  342|      0|    fe_mul(a, a, in[i-1]);
  343|      0|  }
  344|      0|  memcpy(&out[0], &a, sizeof(fe));
  345|       |
  346|      0|  free(init_muls);
  347|       |
  348|      0|  return 0;
  349|      0|}
  350|       |
  351|       |/* From fe_isnegative.c */
  352|       |
  353|       |/*
  354|       |return 1 if f is in {1,3,5,...,q-2}
  355|       |return 0 if f is in {0,2,4,...,q-1}
  356|       |
  357|       |Preconditions:
  358|       |   |f| bounded by 1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,etc.
  359|       |*/
  360|       |
  361|  2.02M|int fe_isnegative(const fe f) {
  362|  2.02M|  unsigned char s[32];
  363|  2.02M|  fe_tobytes(s, f);
  364|  2.02M|  return s[0] & 1;
  365|  2.02M|}
  366|       |
  367|       |/* From fe_isnonzero.c, modified */
  368|       |
  369|   351k|static int fe_isnonzero(const fe f) {
  370|   351k|  unsigned char s[32];
  371|   351k|  fe_tobytes(s, f);
  372|   351k|  return (((int) (s[0] | s[1] | s[2] | s[3] | s[4] | s[5] | s[6] | s[7] | s[8] |
  373|   351k|    s[9] | s[10] | s[11] | s[12] | s[13] | s[14] | s[15] | s[16] | s[17] |
  374|   351k|    s[18] | s[19] | s[20] | s[21] | s[22] | s[23] | s[24] | s[25] | s[26] |
  375|   351k|    s[27] | s[28] | s[29] | s[30] | s[31]) - 1) >> 8) + 1;
  376|   351k|}
  377|       |
  378|       |/* From fe_mul.c */
  379|       |
  380|       |/*
  381|       |h = f * g
  382|       |Can overlap h with f or g.
  383|       |
  384|       |Preconditions:
  385|       |   |f| bounded by 1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,etc.
  386|       |   |g| bounded by 1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,etc.
  387|       |
  388|       |Postconditions:
  389|       |   |h| bounded by 1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,etc.
  390|       |*/
  391|       |
  392|       |/*
  393|       |Notes on implementation strategy:
  394|       |
  395|       |Using schoolbook multiplication.
  396|       |Karatsuba would save a little in some cost models.
  397|       |
  398|       |Most multiplications by 2 and 19 are 32-bit precomputations;
  399|       |cheaper than 64-bit postcomputations.
  400|       |
  401|       |There is one remaining multiplication by 19 in the carry chain;
  402|       |one *19 precomputation can be merged into this,
  403|       |but the resulting data flow is considerably less clean.
  404|       |
  405|       |There are 12 carries below.
  406|       |10 of them are 2-way parallelizable and vectorizable.
  407|       |Can get away with 11 carries, but then data flow is much deeper.
  408|       |
  409|       |With tighter constraints on inputs can squeeze carries into int32.
  410|       |*/
  411|       |
  412|  1.02G|void fe_mul(fe h, const fe f, const fe g) {
  413|  1.02G|  int32_t f0 = f[0];
  414|  1.02G|  int32_t f1 = f[1];
  415|  1.02G|  int32_t f2 = f[2];
  416|  1.02G|  int32_t f3 = f[3];
  417|  1.02G|  int32_t f4 = f[4];
  418|  1.02G|  int32_t f5 = f[5];
  419|  1.02G|  int32_t f6 = f[6];
  420|  1.02G|  int32_t f7 = f[7];
  421|  1.02G|  int32_t f8 = f[8];
  422|  1.02G|  int32_t f9 = f[9];
  423|  1.02G|  int32_t g0 = g[0];
  424|  1.02G|  int32_t g1 = g[1];
  425|  1.02G|  int32_t g2 = g[2];
  426|  1.02G|  int32_t g3 = g[3];
  427|  1.02G|  int32_t g4 = g[4];
  428|  1.02G|  int32_t g5 = g[5];
  429|  1.02G|  int32_t g6 = g[6];
  430|  1.02G|  int32_t g7 = g[7];
  431|  1.02G|  int32_t g8 = g[8];
  432|  1.02G|  int32_t g9 = g[9];
  433|  1.02G|  int32_t g1_19 = 19 * g1; /* 1.959375*2^29 */
  434|  1.02G|  int32_t g2_19 = 19 * g2; /* 1.959375*2^30; still ok */
  435|  1.02G|  int32_t g3_19 = 19 * g3;
  436|  1.02G|  int32_t g4_19 = 19 * g4;
  437|  1.02G|  int32_t g5_19 = 19 * g5;
  438|  1.02G|  int32_t g6_19 = 19 * g6;
  439|  1.02G|  int32_t g7_19 = 19 * g7;
  440|  1.02G|  int32_t g8_19 = 19 * g8;
  441|  1.02G|  int32_t g9_19 = 19 * g9;
  442|  1.02G|  int32_t f1_2 = 2 * f1;
  443|  1.02G|  int32_t f3_2 = 2 * f3;
  444|  1.02G|  int32_t f5_2 = 2 * f5;
  445|  1.02G|  int32_t f7_2 = 2 * f7;
  446|  1.02G|  int32_t f9_2 = 2 * f9;
  447|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g0    = f0   * (int64_t) g0;
  448|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g1    = f0   * (int64_t) g1;
  449|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g2    = f0   * (int64_t) g2;
  450|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g3    = f0   * (int64_t) g3;
  451|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g4    = f0   * (int64_t) g4;
  452|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g5    = f0   * (int64_t) g5;
  453|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g6    = f0   * (int64_t) g6;
  454|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g7    = f0   * (int64_t) g7;
  455|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g8    = f0   * (int64_t) g8;
  456|  1.02G|  int64_t f0g9    = f0   * (int64_t) g9;
  457|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g0    = f1   * (int64_t) g0;
  458|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g1_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) g1;
  459|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g2    = f1   * (int64_t) g2;
  460|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g3_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) g3;
  461|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g4    = f1   * (int64_t) g4;
  462|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g5_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) g5;

  465|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g8    = f1   * (int64_t) g8;
  466|  1.02G|  int64_t f1g9_38 = f1_2 * (int64_t) g9_19;
  467|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g0    = f2   * (int64_t) g0;
  468|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g1    = f2   * (int64_t) g1;
  469|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g2    = f2   * (int64_t) g2;

  472|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g5    = f2   * (int64_t) g5;

  474|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g7    = f2   * (int64_t) g7;
  475|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g8_19 = f2   * (int64_t) g8_19;
  476|  1.02G|  int64_t f2g9_19 = f2   * (int64_t) g9_19;
  477|  1.02G|  int64_t f3g0    = f3   * (int64_t) g0;
  478|  1.02G|  int64_t f3g1_2  = f3_2 * (int64_t) g1;
  479|  1.02G|  int64_t f3g2    = f3   * (int64_t) g2;

  497|  1.02G|  int64_t f5g0    = f5   * (int64_t) g0;

  499|  1.02G|  int64_t f5g2    = f5   * (int64_t) g2;

  501|  1.02G|  int64_t f5g4    = f5   * (int64_t) g4;
  502|  1.02G|  int64_t f5g5_38 = f5_2 * (int64_t) g5_19;

  507|  1.02G|  int64_t f6g0    = f6   * (int64_t) g0;

  509|  1.02G|  int64_t f6g2    = f6   * (int64_t) g2;
  510|  1.02G|  int64_t f6g3    = f6   * (int64_t) g3;
  511|  1.02G|  int64_t f6g4_19 = f6   * (int64_t) g4_19;

  517|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g0    = f7   * (int64_t) g0;

  519|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g2    = f7   * (int64_t) g2;
  520|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g3_38 = f7_2 * (int64_t) g3_19;
  521|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g4_19 = f7   * (int64_t) g4_19;
  522|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g5_38 = f7_2 * (int64_t) g5_19;
  523|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g6_19 = f7   * (int64_t) g6_19;
  524|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g7_38 = f7_2 * (int64_t) g7_19;
  525|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g8_19 = f7   * (int64_t) g8_19;
  526|  1.02G|  int64_t f7g9_38 = f7_2 * (int64_t) g9_19;
  527|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g0    = f8   * (int64_t) g0;
  528|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g1    = f8   * (int64_t) g1;
  529|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g2_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g2_19;
  530|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g3_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g3_19;
  531|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g4_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g4_19;
  532|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g5_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g5_19;
  533|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g6_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g6_19;
  534|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g7_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g7_19;
  535|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g8_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g8_19;
  536|  1.02G|  int64_t f8g9_19 = f8   * (int64_t) g9_19;
  537|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g0    = f9   * (int64_t) g0;
  538|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g1_38 = f9_2 * (int64_t) g1_19;
  539|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g2_19 = f9   * (int64_t) g2_19;
  540|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g3_38 = f9_2 * (int64_t) g3_19;
  541|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g4_19 = f9   * (int64_t) g4_19;
  542|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g5_38 = f9_2 * (int64_t) g5_19;
  543|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g6_19 = f9   * (int64_t) g6_19;
  544|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g7_38 = f9_2 * (int64_t) g7_19;
  545|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g8_19 = f9   * (int64_t) g8_19;
  546|  1.02G|  int64_t f9g9_38 = f9_2 * (int64_t) g9_19;
  547|  1.02G|  int64_t h0 = f0g0+f1g9_38+f2g8_19+f3g7_38+f4g6_19+f5g5_38+f6g4_19+f7g3_38+f8g2_19+f9g1_38;
  548|  1.02G|  int64_t h1 = f0g1+f1g0   +f2g9_19+f3g8_19+f4g7_19+f5g6_19+f6g5_19+f7g4_19+f8g3_19+f9g2_19;
  549|  1.02G|  int64_t h2 = f0g2+f1g1_2 +f2g0   +f3g9_38+f4g8_19+f5g7_38+f6g6_19+f7g5_38+f8g4_19+f9g3_38;
  550|  1.02G|  int64_t h3 = f0g3+f1g2   +f2g1   +f3g0   +f4g9_19+f5g8_19+f6g7_19+f7g6_19+f8g5_19+f9g4_19;
  551|  1.02G|  int64_t h4 = f0g4+f1g3_2 +f2g2   +f3g1_2 +f4g0   +f5g9_38+f6g8_19+f7g7_38+f8g6_19+f9g5_38;
  552|  1.02G|  int64_t h5 = f0g5+f1g4   +f2g3   +f3g2   +f4g1   +f5g0   +f6g9_19+f7g8_19+f8g7_19+f9g6_19;
  553|  1.02G|  int64_t h6 = f0g6+f1g5_2 +f2g4   +f3g3_2 +f4g2   +f5g1_2 +f6g0   +f7g9_38+f8g8_19+f9g7_38;
  554|  1.02G|  int64_t h7 = f0g7+f1g6   +f2g5   +f3g4   +f4g3   +f5g2   +f6g1   +f7g0   +f8g9_19+f9g8_19;
  555|  1.02G|  int64_t h8 = f0g8+f1g7_2 +f2g6   +f3g5_2 +f4g4   +f5g3_2 +f6g2   +f7g1_2 +f8g0   +f9g9_38;
  556|  1.02G|  int64_t h9 = f0g9+f1g8   +f2g7   +f3g6   +f4g5   +f5g4   +f6g3   +f7g2   +f8g1   +f9g0   ;
  557|  1.02G|  int64_t carry0;
  558|  1.02G|  int64_t carry1;
  559|  1.02G|  int64_t carry2;
  560|  1.02G|  int64_t carry3;
  561|  1.02G|  int64_t carry4;
  562|  1.02G|  int64_t carry5;
  563|  1.02G|  int64_t carry6;
  564|  1.02G|  int64_t carry7;
  565|  1.02G|  int64_t carry8;
  566|  1.02G|  int64_t carry9;
  567|       |
  568|       |  /*
  569|       |  |h0| <= (1.65*1.65*2^52*(1+19+19+19+19)+1.65*1.65*2^50*(38+38+38+38+38))
  570|       |    i.e. |h0| <= 1.4*2^60; narrower ranges for h2, h4, h6, h8
  571|       |  |h1| <= (1.65*1.65*2^51*(1+1+19+19+19+19+19+19+19+19))
  572|       |    i.e. |h1| <= 1.7*2^59; narrower ranges for h3, h5, h7, h9
  573|       |  */
  574|       |
  575|  1.02G|  carry0 = (h0 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h1 += carry0; h0 -= carry0 << 26;
  576|  1.02G|  carry4 = (h4 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h5 += carry4; h4 -= carry4 << 26;
  577|       |  /* |h0| <= 2^25 */
  578|       |  /* |h4| <= 2^25 */
  579|       |  /* |h1| <= 1.71*2^59 */
  580|       |  /* |h5| <= 1.71*2^59 */
  581|       |
  582|  1.02G|  carry1 = (h1 + (int64_t) (1<<24)) >> 25; h2 += carry1; h1 -= carry1 << 25;
  583|  1.02G|  carry5 = (h5 + (int64_t) (1<<24)) >> 25; h6 += carry5; h5 -= carry5 << 25;
  584|       |  /* |h1| <= 2^24; from now on fits into int32 */
  585|       |  /* |h5| <= 2^24; from now on fits into int32 */
  586|       |  /* |h2| <= 1.41*2^60 */
  587|       |  /* |h6| <= 1.41*2^60 */
  588|       |
  589|  1.02G|  carry2 = (h2 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h3 += carry2; h2 -= carry2 << 26;
  590|  1.02G|  carry6 = (h6 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h7 += carry6; h6 -= carry6 << 26;
  591|       |  /* |h2| <= 2^25; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  592|       |  /* |h6| <= 2^25; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  593|       |  /* |h3| <= 1.71*2^59 */
  594|       |  /* |h7| <= 1.71*2^59 */
  595|       |
  596|  1.02G|  carry3 = (h3 + (int64_t) (1<<24)) >> 25; h4 += carry3; h3 -= carry3 << 25;
  597|  1.02G|  carry7 = (h7 + (int64_t) (1<<24)) >> 25; h8 += carry7; h7 -= carry7 << 25;
  598|       |  /* |h3| <= 2^24; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  599|       |  /* |h7| <= 2^24; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  600|       |  /* |h4| <= 1.72*2^34 */
  601|       |  /* |h8| <= 1.41*2^60 */
  602|       |
  603|  1.02G|  carry4 = (h4 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h5 += carry4; h4 -= carry4 << 26;
  604|  1.02G|  carry8 = (h8 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h9 += carry8; h8 -= carry8 << 26;
  605|       |  /* |h4| <= 2^25; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  606|       |  /* |h8| <= 2^25; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  607|       |  /* |h5| <= 1.01*2^24 */
  608|       |  /* |h9| <= 1.71*2^59 */
  609|       |
  610|  1.02G|  carry9 = (h9 + (int64_t) (1<<24)) >> 25; h0 += carry9 * 19; h9 -= carry9 << 25;
  611|       |  /* |h9| <= 2^24; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  612|       |  /* |h0| <= 1.1*2^39 */
  613|       |
  614|  1.02G|  carry0 = (h0 + (int64_t) (1<<25)) >> 26; h1 += carry0; h0 -= carry0 << 26;
  615|       |  /* |h0| <= 2^25; from now on fits into int32 unchanged */
  616|       |  /* |h1| <= 1.01*2^24 */
  617|       |
  618|  1.02G|  h[0] = h0;
  619|  1.02G|  h[1] = h1;
  620|  1.02G|  h[2] = h2;
  621|  1.02G|  h[3] = h3;
  622|  1.02G|  h[4] = h4;
  623|  1.02G|  h[5] = h5;
  624|  1.02G|  h[6] = h6;
  625|  1.02G|  h[7] = h7;
  626|  1.02G|  h[8] = h8;
  627|  1.02G|  h[9] = h9;
  628|  1.02G|}
  629|       |
  630|       |/* From fe_neg.c */
  631|       |
  632|       |/*
  633|       |h = -f
  634|       |
  635|       |Preconditions:
  636|       |   |f| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
  637|       |
  638|       |Postconditions:
  639|       |   |h| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
  640|       |*/
  641|       |
  642|  26.4M|void fe_neg(fe h, const fe f) {
  643|  26.4M|  int32_t f0 = f[0];
  644|  26.4M|  int32_t f1 = f[1];
  645|  26.4M|  int32_t f2 = f[2];
  646|  26.4M|  int32_t f3 = f[3];
  647|  26.4M|  int32_t f4 = f[4];
  648|  26.4M|  int32_t f5 = f[5];
  649|  26.4M|  int32_t f6 = f[6];
  650|  26.4M|  int32_t f7 = f[7];
  651|  26.4M|  int32_t f8 = f[8];
  652|  26.4M|  int32_t f9 = f[9];
  653|  26.4M|  int32_t h0 = -f0;
  654|  26.4M|  int32_t h1 = -f1;
  655|  26.4M|  int32_t h2 = -f2;
  656|  26.4M|  int32_t h3 = -f3;
  657|  26.4M|  int32_t h4 = -f4;
  658|  26.4M|  int32_t h5 = -f5;
  659|  26.4M|  int32_t h6 = -f6;
  660|  26.4M|  int32_t h7 = -f7;
  661|  26.4M|  int32_t h8 = -f8;
  662|  26.4M|  int32_t h9 = -f9;
  663|  26.4M|  h[0] = h0;
  664|  26.4M|  h[1] = h1;
  665|  26.4M|  h[2] = h2;
  666|  26.4M|  h[3] = h3;
  667|  26.4M|  h[4] = h4;
  668|  26.4M|  h[5] = h5;
  669|  26.4M|  h[6] = h6;
  670|  26.4M|  h[7] = h7;
  671|  26.4M|  h[8] = h8;
  672|  26.4M|  h[9] = h9;
  673|  26.4M|}
  674|       |
  675|       |/* From fe_sq.c */
  676|       |
  677|       |/*
  678|       |h = f * f
  679|       |Can overlap h with f.
  680|       |
  681|       |Preconditions:
  682|       |   |f| bounded by 1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,etc.
  683|       |
  684|       |Postconditions:
  685|       |   |h| bounded by 1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,etc.
  686|       |*/
  687|       |
  688|       |/*
  689|       |See fe_mul.c for discussion of implementation strategy.
  690|       |*/
  691|       |
  692|   864M|void fe_sq(fe h, const fe f) {
  693|   864M|  int32_t f0 = f[0];
  694|   864M|  int32_t f1 = f[1];
  695|   864M|  int32_t f2 = f[2];
  696|   864M|  int32_t f3 = f[3];
  697|   864M|  int32_t f4 = f[4];
  698|   864M|  int32_t f5 = f[5];
  699|   864M|  int32_t f6 = f[6];
  700|   864M|  int32_t f7 = f[7];
  701|   864M|  int32_t f8 = f[8];
  702|   864M|  int32_t f9 = f[9];
  703|   864M|  int32_t f0_2 = 2 * f0;
  704|   864M|  int32_t f1_2 = 2 * f1;
  705|   864M|  int32_t f2_2 = 2 * f2;
  706|   864M|  int32_t f3_2 = 2 * f3;
  707|   864M|  int32_t f4_2 = 2 * f4;
  708|   864M|  int32_t f5_2 = 2 * f5;
  709|   864M|  int32_t f6_2 = 2 * f6;
  710|   864M|  int32_t f7_2 = 2 * f7;
  711|   864M|  int32_t f5_38 = 38 * f5; /* 1.959375*2^30 */
  712|   864M|  int32_t f6_19 = 19 * f6; /* 1.959375*2^30 */
  713|   864M|  int32_t f7_38 = 38 * f7; /* 1.959375*2^30 */
  714|   864M|  int32_t f8_19 = 19 * f8; /* 1.959375*2^30 */
  715|   864M|  int32_t f9_38 = 38 * f9; /* 1.959375*2^30 */
  716|   864M|  int64_t f0f0    = f0   * (int64_t) f0;
  717|   864M|  int64_t f0f1_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f1;
  718|   864M|  int64_t f0f2_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f2;
  719|   864M|  int64_t f0f3_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f3;
  720|   864M|  int64_t f0f4_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f4;
  721|   864M|  int64_t f0f5_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f5;
  722|   864M|  int64_t f0f6_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f6;
  723|   864M|  int64_t f0f7_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f7;
  724|   864M|  int64_t f0f8_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f8;
  725|   864M|  int64_t f0f9_2  = f0_2 * (int64_t) f9;
  726|   864M|  int64_t f1f1_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) f1;
  727|   864M|  int64_t f1f2_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) f2;
  728|   864M|  int64_t f1f3_4  = f1_2 * (int64_t) f3_2;
  729|   864M|  int64_t f1f4_2  = f1_2 * (int64_t) f4;
  730|   864M|  int64_t f1f5_4  = f1_2 * (int64_t) f5_2;

  825|       |/*
  826|       |h = 2 * f * f
  827|       |Can overlap h with f.
  828|       |
  829|       |Preconditions:
  830|       |   |f| bounded by 1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,1.65*2^26,1.65*2^25,etc.
  831|       |
  832|       |Postconditions:
  833|       |   |h| bounded by 1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,1.01*2^25,1.01*2^24,etc.
  834|       |*/
  835|       |

  981|       |
  982|       |/* From fe_sub.c */
  983|       |
  984|       |/*
  985|       |h = f - g
  986|       |Can overlap h with f or g.
  987|       |
  988|       |Preconditions:
  989|       |   |f| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
  990|       |   |g| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
  991|       |
  992|       |Postconditions:
  993|       |   |h| bounded by 1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,etc.
  994|       |*/
  995|       |
  996|   687M|void fe_sub(fe h, const fe f, const fe g) {
  997|   687M|  int32_t f0 = f[0];
  998|   687M|  int32_t f1 = f[1];
  999|   687M|  int32_t f2 = f[2];
 1000|   687M|  int32_t f3 = f[3];
 1001|   687M|  int32_t f4 = f[4];
 1002|   687M|  int32_t f5 = f[5];
 1003|   687M|  int32_t f6 = f[6];
 1004|   687M|  int32_t f7 = f[7];
 1005|   687M|  int32_t f8 = f[8];
 1006|   687M|  int32_t f9 = f[9];
 1007|   687M|  int32_t g0 = g[0];
 1008|   687M|  int32_t g1 = g[1];
 1009|   687M|  int32_t g2 = g[2];
 1010|   687M|  int32_t g3 = g[3];

 1038|       |
 1039|       |/* From fe_tobytes.c */
 1040|       |
 1041|       |/*
 1042|       |Preconditions:
 1043|       |  |h| bounded by 1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,etc.
 1044|       |
 1045|       |Write p=2^255-19; q=floor(h/p).
 1046|       |Basic claim: q = floor(2^(-255)(h + 19 2^(-25)h9 + 2^(-1))).
 1047|       |
 1048|       |Proof:
 1049|       |  Have |h|<=p so |q|<=1 so |19^2 2^(-255) q|<1/4.
 1050|       |  Also have |h-2^230 h9|<2^231 so |19 2^(-255)(h-2^230 h9)|<1/4.
 1051|       |
 1052|       |  Write y=2^(-1)-19^2 2^(-255)q-19 2^(-255)(h-2^230 h9).
 1053|       |  Then 0<y<1.
 1054|       |
 1055|       |  Write r=h-pq.
 1056|       |  Have 0<=r<=p-1=2^255-20.
 1057|       |  Thus 0<=r+19(2^-255)r<r+19(2^-255)2^255<=2^255-1.
 1058|       |
 1059|       |  Write x=r+19(2^-255)r+y.
 1060|       |  Then 0<x<2^255 so floor(2^(-255)x) = 0 so floor(q+2^(-255)x) = q.
 1061|       |
 1062|       |  Have q+2^(-255)x = 2^(-255)(h + 19 2^(-25) h9 + 2^(-1))
 1063|       |  so floor(2^(-255)(h + 19 2^(-25) h9 + 2^(-1))) = q.
 1064|       |*/
 1065|       |
 1066|  9.27M|void fe_tobytes(unsigned char *s, const fe h) {
 1067|  9.27M|  int32_t h0 = h[0];
 1068|  9.27M|  int32_t h1 = h[1];
 1069|  9.27M|  int32_t h2 = h[2];
 1070|  9.27M|  int32_t h3 = h[3];
 1071|  9.27M|  int32_t h4 = h[4];
 1072|  9.27M|  int32_t h5 = h[5];
 1073|  9.27M|  int32_t h6 = h[6];
 1074|  9.27M|  int32_t h7 = h[7];
 1075|  9.27M|  int32_t h8 = h[8];
 1076|  9.27M|  int32_t h9 = h[9];
 1077|  9.27M|  int32_t q;
 1078|  9.27M|  int32_t carry0;
 1079|  9.27M|  int32_t carry1;
 1080|  9.27M|  int32_t carry2;
 1081|  9.27M|  int32_t carry3;
 1082|  9.27M|  int32_t carry4;
 1083|  9.27M|  int32_t carry5;
 1084|  9.27M|  int32_t carry6;
 1085|  9.27M|  int32_t carry7;
 1086|  9.27M|  int32_t carry8;
 1087|  9.27M|  int32_t carry9;
 1088|       |
 1089|  9.27M|  q = (19 * h9 + (((int32_t) 1) << 24)) >> 25;
 1090|  9.27M|  q = (h0 + q) >> 26;
 1091|  9.27M|  q = (h1 + q) >> 25;
 1092|  9.27M|  q = (h2 + q) >> 26;
 1093|  9.27M|  q = (h3 + q) >> 25;
 1094|  9.27M|  q = (h4 + q) >> 26;
 1095|  9.27M|  q = (h5 + q) >> 25;
 1096|  9.27M|  q = (h6 + q) >> 26;
 1097|  9.27M|  q = (h7 + q) >> 25;
 1098|  9.27M|  q = (h8 + q) >> 26;
 1099|  9.27M|  q = (h9 + q) >> 25;
 1100|       |
 1101|       |  /* Goal: Output h-(2^255-19)q, which is between 0 and 2^255-20. */
 1102|  9.27M|  h0 += 19 * q;
 1103|       |  /* Goal: Output h-2^255 q, which is between 0 and 2^255-20. */
 1104|       |
 1105|  9.27M|  carry0 = h0 >> 26; h1 += carry0; h0 -= carry0 << 26;
 1106|  9.27M|  carry1 = h1 >> 25; h2 += carry1; h1 -= carry1 << 25;
 1107|  9.27M|  carry2 = h2 >> 26; h3 += carry2; h2 -= carry2 << 26;
 1108|  9.27M|  carry3 = h3 >> 25; h4 += carry3; h3 -= carry3 << 25;
 1109|  9.27M|  carry4 = h4 >> 26; h5 += carry4; h4 -= carry4 << 26;
 1110|  9.27M|  carry5 = h5 >> 25; h6 += carry5; h5 -= carry5 << 25;
 1111|  9.27M|  carry6 = h6 >> 26; h7 += carry6; h6 -= carry6 << 26;
 1112|  9.27M|  carry7 = h7 >> 25; h8 += carry7; h7 -= carry7 << 25;
 1113|  9.27M|  carry8 = h8 >> 26; h9 += carry8; h8 -= carry8 << 26;
 1114|  9.27M|  carry9 = h9 >> 25;               h9 -= carry9 << 25;
 1115|       |                  /* h10 = carry9 */
 1116|       |
 1117|       |  /*
 1118|       |  Goal: Output h0+...+2^255 h10-2^255 q, which is between 0 and 2^255-20.
 1119|       |  Have h0+...+2^230 h9 between 0 and 2^255-1;
 1120|       |  evidently 2^255 h10-2^255 q = 0.
 1121|       |  Goal: Output h0+...+2^230 h9.
 1122|       |  */
 1123|       |
 1124|  9.27M|  s[0] = h0 >> 0;
 1125|  9.27M|  s[1] = h0 >> 8;
 1126|  9.27M|  s[2] = h0 >> 16;
 1127|  9.27M|  s[3] = (h0 >> 24) | (h1 << 2);
 1128|  9.27M|  s[4] = h1 >> 6;
 1129|  9.27M|  s[5] = h1 >> 14;
 1130|  9.27M|  s[6] = (h1 >> 22) | (h2 << 3);
 1131|  9.27M|  s[7] = h2 >> 5;
 1132|  9.27M|  s[8] = h2 >> 13;
 1133|  9.27M|  s[9] = (h2 >> 21) | (h3 << 5);
 1134|  9.27M|  s[10] = h3 >> 3;
 1135|  9.27M|  s[11] = h3 >> 11;
 1136|  9.27M|  s[12] = (h3 >> 19) | (h4 << 6);
 1137|  9.27M|  s[13] = h4 >> 2;
 1138|  9.27M|  s[14] = h4 >> 10;
 1139|  9.27M|  s[15] = h4 >> 18;
 1140|  9.27M|  s[16] = h5 >> 0;
 1141|  9.27M|  s[17] = h5 >> 8;
 1142|  9.27M|  s[18] = h5 >> 16;
 1143|  9.27M|  s[19] = (h5 >> 24) | (h6 << 1);
 1144|  9.27M|  s[20] = h6 >> 7;
 1145|  9.27M|  s[21] = h6 >> 15;
 1146|  9.27M|  s[22] = (h6 >> 23) | (h7 << 3);
 1147|  9.27M|  s[23] = h7 >> 5;
 1148|  9.27M|  s[24] = h7 >> 13;
 1149|  9.27M|  s[25] = (h7 >> 21) | (h8 << 4);
 1150|  9.27M|  s[26] = h8 >> 4;
 1151|  9.27M|  s[27] = h8 >> 12;
 1152|  9.27M|  s[28] = (h8 >> 20) | (h9 << 6);
 1153|  9.27M|  s[29] = h9 >> 2;
 1154|  9.27M|  s[30] = h9 >> 10;
 1155|  9.27M|  s[31] = h9 >> 18;
 1156|  9.27M|}

 1199|  16.3M|              r[k] = 0;

 1372|  3.08M|int fe_frombytes_vartime(fe y, const unsigned char *s) {
 1373|  3.08M|  int64_t h0 = load_4(s);
 1374|  3.08M|  int64_t h1 = load_3(s + 4) << 6;
 1375|  3.08M|  int64_t h2 = load_3(s + 7) << 5;
 1376|  3.08M|  int64_t h3 = load_3(s + 10) << 3;

 1384|  3.08M|  int64_t carry1;
 1385|  3.08M|  int64_t carry2;
 1386|  3.08M|  int64_t carry3;
 1387|  3.08M|  int64_t carry4;

 1674|       |// With these select_vartime/ge_scalarmult_base_vartime I got ~25% speed up comparing to the select/ge_scalarmult_base -- sowle
 1675|  53.2M|static void select_vartime(ge_precomp *t, int pos, signed char b) {
 1676|  53.2M|  unsigned char bnegative = negative(b);
 1677|  53.2M|  unsigned char babs = b - (((-bnegative) & b) << 1);
 1678|  53.2M|  const ge_precomp* base;
 1679|       |
 1680|  53.2M|  if (babs == 0)
 1681|  3.78M|  {
 1682|  3.78M|    ge_precomp_0(t);
 1683|  3.78M|  }
 1684|  49.4M|  else if (bnegative == 0)
 1685|  23.4M|  {
 1686|  23.4M|    base = &ge_base[pos][babs - 1];
 1687|  23.4M|    fe_copy(t->yplusx,  base->yplusx);
 1688|  23.4M|    fe_copy(t->yminusx, base->yminusx);
 1689|  23.4M|    fe_copy(t->xy2d,    base->xy2d);

 1691|  26.0M|  else
 1692|  26.0M|  {
 1693|  26.0M|    base = &ge_base[pos][babs - 1];

 1699|       |
 1700|       |/*
 1701|       |h = a * B
 1702|       |where a = a[0]+256*a[1]+...+256^31 a[31]
 1703|       |B is the Ed25519 base point (x,4/5) with x positive.
 1704|       |
 1705|       |Preconditions:
 1706|       |  a[31] <= 127
 1707|       |*/
 1708|       |
 1709|      0|void ge_scalarmult_base(ge_p3 *h, const unsigned char *a) {
 1710|      0|  signed char e[64];
 1711|      0|  signed char carry;
 1712|      0|  ge_p1p1 r;
 1713|      0|  ge_p2 s;
 1714|      0|  ge_precomp t;
 1715|      0|  int i;
 1716|       |
 1717|      0|  for (i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
 1718|      0|    e[2 * i + 0] = (a[i] >> 0) & 15;
 1719|      0|    e[2 * i + 1] = (a[i] >> 4) & 15;
 1720|      0|  }
 1721|       |  /* each e[i] is between 0 and 15 */
 1722|       |  /* e[63] is between 0 and 7 */
 1723|       |
 1724|      0|  carry = 0;
 1725|      0|  for (i = 0; i < 63; ++i) {
 1726|      0|    e[i] += carry;
 1727|      0|    carry = e[i] + 8;
 1728|      0|    carry >>= 4;
 1729|      0|    e[i] -= carry << 4;
 1730|      0|  }
 1731|      0|  e[63] += carry;
 1732|       |  /* each e[i] is between -8 and 8 */
 1733|       |
 1734|      0|  ge_p3_0(h);
 1735|      0|  for (i = 1; i < 64; i += 2) {
 1736|      0|    _select(&t, i / 2, e[i]);
 1737|      0|    ge_madd(&r, h, &t); ge_p1p1_to_p3(h, &r);
 1738|      0|  }
 1739|       |
 1740|      0|  ge_p3_dbl(&r, h);  ge_p1p1_to_p2(&s, &r);
 1741|      0|  ge_p2_dbl(&r, &s); ge_p1p1_to_p2(&s, &r);
 1742|      0|  ge_p2_dbl(&r, &s); ge_p1p1_to_p2(&s, &r);
 1743|      0|  ge_p2_dbl(&r, &s); ge_p1p1_to_p3(h, &r);
 1744|       |
 1745|      0|  for (i = 0; i < 64; i += 2) {
 1746|      0|    _select(&t, i / 2, e[i]);
 1747|      0|    ge_madd(&r, h, &t); ge_p1p1_to_p3(h, &r);
 1748|      0|  }
 1749|      0|}

 1751|   837k|void ge_scalarmult_base_vartime(ge_p3 *h, const unsigned char *a) {
 1752|   837k|  signed char e[64];
 1753|   837k|  signed char carry;
 1754|   837k|  ge_p1p1 r;
 1755|   837k|  ge_p2 s;
 1756|   837k|  ge_precomp t;
 1757|   837k|  int i;
 1758|       |
 1759|  27.5M|  for (i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
 1760|  26.7M|    e[2 * i + 0] = (a[i] >> 0) & 15;
 1761|  26.7M|    e[2 * i + 1] = (a[i] >> 4) & 15;
 1762|  26.7M|  }
 1763|       |  /* each e[i] is between 0 and 15 */
 1764|       |  /* e[63] is between 0 and 7 */
 1765|       |
 1766|   837k|  carry = 0;
 1767|  53.4M|  for (i = 0; i < 63; ++i) {
 1768|  52.5M|    e[i] += carry;
 1769|  52.5M|    carry = e[i] + 8;

 1771|  52.5M|    e[i] -= carry << 4;

 1793|       |/* From ge_sub.c */

 1796|       |r = p - q
 1797|       |*/
 1798|       |
 1799|  12.8M|void ge_sub(ge_p1p1 *r, const ge_p3 *p, const ge_cached *q) {
 1800|  12.8M|  fe t0;
 1801|  12.8M|  fe_add(r->X, p->Y, p->X);

 3999|      0|static void edwardsYZ_to_x25519(unsigned char *xbytes, const fe Y, const fe Z) {
 4000|       |  // y = Y/Z
 4001|       |  // x_mont = (1 + y) / (1 - y)
 4002|       |  //        = (1 + Y/Z) / (1 - Y/Z)
 4003|       |  //        = (Z + Y) / (Z - Y)
 4004|       |
 4005|      0|  fe tmp0;
 4006|      0|  fe tmp1;

 4008|      0|  fe_sub(tmp1, Z, Y);       // Z - Y
 4009|      0|  fe_invert(tmp1, tmp1);    // 1/(Z - Y)
 4010|      0|  fe_mul(tmp0, tmp0, tmp1); // (Z + Y) / (Z - Y)
 4011|      0|  fe_tobytes(xbytes, tmp0); // tobytes((Z + Y) / (Z - Y))
 4012|      0|}

 4161|       |
 4162|       |/*
 4163|       |Since fe_add and fe_sub enforce the following conditions:
 4164|       |
 4165|       |Preconditions:
 4166|       |   |f| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
 4167|       |   |g| bounded by 1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^24,etc.
 4168|       |
 4169|       |Postconditions:
 4170|       |   |h| bounded by 1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,1.1*2^26,1.1*2^25,etc.
 4171|       |
 4172|       |We sometimes need to "reduce" field elems when they are in the poscondition's
 4173|       |larger domain to match the precondition domain. This way we can take the output
 4174|       |of fe_add or fe_sub and use it as input to another call to fe_add or fe_sub.
 4175|       |
 4176|       |We reduce by converting the field elem to its byte repr, then re-deriving the
 4177|       |field elem from the byte repr.
 4178|       |*/
 4179|       |void fe_reduce(fe reduced_f, const fe f)
 4180|  2.91M|{
 4181|  2.91M|  unsigned char f_bytes[32];
 4182|  2.91M|  fe_tobytes(f_bytes, f);
 4183|  2.91M|  fe_frombytes_vartime(reduced_f, f_bytes);
 4184|  2.91M|}
 4185|       |
 4186|       |void fe_dbl(fe h, const fe f)
 4187|  1.29M|{
 4188|       |  // Reduce the input for safety to ensure we meet the preconditions for fe_add
 4189|  1.29M|  fe f_reduced;
 4190|  1.29M|  fe_reduce(f_reduced, f);
 4191|  1.29M|  fe h_res;
 4192|  1.29M|  fe_add(h_res, f_reduced, f_reduced);
 4193|       |  // Reduce the output for safety to ensure the result can be used as input to
 4194|       |  // fe_add or fe_sub without an extra call to fe_reduce
 4195|  1.29M|  fe_reduce(h, h_res);
 4196|  1.29M|}

   51|       |static bool sqrt_ext(fe y, const fe x)
   52|   403k|{
   53|   403k|    fe y_res;
   54|       |
   55|   403k|    fe x2;

   57|       |
   58|   403k|    fe b;
   59|   403k|    fe_pow22523(b, x2);
   60|       |
   61|   403k|    fe b_sq;
   62|   403k|    fe_sq(b_sq, b);
   63|       |

   65|   403k|    fe_mul(c, x2, b_sq);
   66|       |

   68|  86.5k|    {

   70|  86.5k|        c[0] = 3;
   71|  86.5k|    }

   80|   205k|        fe_neg(y_res, y_res);
