  568|       |// c++14 doesn't have is_nothrow_swappable, and clang++ 6.0.1 doesn't like it either, so I'm making
  569|       |// my own here.
  570|       |namespace swappable {
  571|       |#if ROBIN_HOOD(CXX) < ROBIN_HOOD(CXX17)
  572|       |using std::swap;
  573|       |template <typename T>
  574|       |struct nothrow {
  575|       |    static const bool value = noexcept(swap(std::declval<T&>(), std::declval<T&>()));
  576|       |};
  577|       |#else

  588|       |
  589|       |// A custom pair implementation is used in the map because std::pair is not is_trivially_copyable,
  590|       |// which means it would  not be allowed to be used in std::memcpy. This struct is copyable, which is
  591|       |// also tested.
  592|       |template <typename T1, typename T2>
  593|       |struct pair {
  594|       |    using first_type = T1;
  595|       |    using second_type = T2;
  596|       |
  597|       |    template <typename U1 = T1, typename U2 = T2,
  598|       |              typename = typename std::enable_if<std::is_default_constructible<U1>::value &&
  599|       |                                                 std::is_default_constructible<U2>::value>::type>
  600|       |    constexpr pair() noexcept(noexcept(U1()) && noexcept(U2()))
  601|       |        : first()
  602|       |        , second() {}
  603|       |
  604|       |    // pair constructors are explicit so we don't accidentally call this ctor when we don't have to.
  605|       |    explicit constexpr pair(std::pair<T1, T2> const& o) noexcept(
  606|       |        noexcept(T1(std::declval<T1 const&>())) && noexcept(T2(std::declval<T2 const&>())))
  607|       |        : first(o.first)
  608|       |        , second(o.second) {}
  609|       |
  610|       |    // pair constructors are explicit so we don't accidentally call this ctor when we don't have to.
  611|       |    explicit constexpr pair(std::pair<T1, T2>&& o) noexcept(noexcept(
  612|       |        T1(std::move(std::declval<T1&&>()))) && noexcept(T2(std::move(std::declval<T2&&>()))))
  613|       |        : first(std::move(o.first))
  614|       |        , second(std::move(o.second)) {}
  615|       |
  616|       |    constexpr pair(T1&& a, T2&& b) noexcept(noexcept(
  617|       |        T1(std::move(std::declval<T1&&>()))) && noexcept(T2(std::move(std::declval<T2&&>()))))
  618|       |        : first(std::move(a))
  619|       |        , second(std::move(b)) {}
  620|       |
  621|       |    template <typename U1, typename U2>
  622|       |    constexpr pair(U1&& a, U2&& b) noexcept(noexcept(T1(std::forward<U1>(
  623|       |        std::declval<U1&&>()))) && noexcept(T2(std::forward<U2>(std::declval<U2&&>()))))
  624|  1.35M|        : first(std::forward<U1>(a))
  625|  1.41M|        , second(std::forward<U2>(b)) {}

  702|       |
  703|  2.26M|inline size_t hash_int(uint64_t x) noexcept {
  704|       |    // tried lots of different hashes, let's stick with murmurhash3. It's simple, fast, well tested,
  705|       |    // and doesn't need any special 128bit operations.
  706|  2.26M|    x ^= x >> 33U;
  707|  2.26M|    x *= UINT64_C(0xff51afd7ed558ccd);
  708|  2.26M|    x ^= x >> 33U;
  709|       |
  710|       |    // not doing the final step here, because this will be done by keyToIdx anyways
  711|       |    // x *= UINT64_C(0xc4ceb9fe1a85ec53);
  712|       |    // x ^= x >> 33U;
  713|  2.26M|    return static_cast<size_t>(x);
  714|  2.26M|}
  715|       |
  716|       |template<typename T> struct not_implemented { enum { value = 0 }; };
  717|       |
  718|       |// A thin wrapper around std::hash, performing an additional simple mixing step of the result.
  719|       |template <typename T, typename Enable = void>
  720|       |struct hash : public std::hash<T> {
  721|       |    size_t operator()(T const& obj) const
  722|       |        noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<std::hash<T>>().operator()(std::declval<T const&>()))) {
  723|       |        static_assert(not_implemented<T>::value, "You must specialize robin_hood::hash for this type");
  724|       |        // call base hash
  725|       |        auto result = std::hash<T>::operator()(obj);
  726|       |        // return mixed of that, to be save against identity has
  727|       |        return hash_int(static_cast<detail::SizeT>(result));
  728|       |    }
  729|       |};
  730|       |
  731|       |template <typename CharT>
  732|       |struct hash<std::basic_string<CharT>> {
  733|      0|    size_t operator()(std::basic_string<CharT> const& str) const noexcept {
  734|      0|        return hash_bytes(str.data(), sizeof(CharT) * str.size());
  735|      0|    }
  736|       |};
  737|       |
  738|       |template <typename CharT>
  739|       |struct hash<std::basic_string_view<CharT>> {
  740|      0|    size_t operator()(std::basic_string_view<CharT> const& sv) const noexcept {
  741|      0|        return hash_bytes(sv.data(), sizeof(CharT) * sv.size());
  742|      0|    }
  743|       |};
  744|       |
  745|       |template <class T>
  746|       |struct hash<T*> {
  747|       |    size_t operator()(T* ptr) const noexcept {
  748|       |        return hash_int(reinterpret_cast<detail::SizeT>(ptr));
  749|       |    }
  750|       |};
  751|       |

  753|       |struct hash<std::unique_ptr<T>> {
  754|       |    size_t operator()(std::unique_ptr<T> const& ptr) const noexcept {
  755|       |        return hash_int(reinterpret_cast<detail::SizeT>(ptr.get()));
  756|       |    }
  757|       |};

  807|       |namespace detail {
  808|       |
  809|       |template <typename T>
  810|       |struct void_type {
  811|       |    using type = void;
  812|       |};
  813|       |
  814|       |template <typename T, typename = void>
  815|       |struct has_is_transparent : public std::false_type {};
  816|       |
  817|       |template <typename T>
  818|       |struct has_is_transparent<T, typename void_type<typename T::is_transparent>::type>
  819|       |    : public std::true_type {};
  820|       |
  821|       |// using wrapper classes for hash and key_equal prevents the diamond problem when the same type
  822|       |// is used. see https://stackoverflow.com/a/28771920/48181
  823|       |template <typename T>
  824|       |struct WrapHash : public T {
  825|       |    WrapHash() = default;
  826|       |    explicit WrapHash(T const& o) noexcept(noexcept(T(std::declval<T const&>())))
  827|       |        : T(o) {}
  828|       |};
  829|       |
  830|       |template <typename T>
  831|       |struct WrapKeyEqual : public T {
  832|       |    WrapKeyEqual() = default;
  833|       |    explicit WrapKeyEqual(T const& o) noexcept(noexcept(T(std::declval<T const&>())))
  834|       |        : T(o) {}
  835|       |};
  836|       |
  837|       |// A highly optimized hashmap implementation, using the Robin Hood algorithm.
  838|       |//
  839|       |// In most cases, this map should be usable as a drop-in replacement for std::unordered_map, but
  840|       |// be about 2x faster in most cases and require much less allocations.
  841|       |//
  842|       |// This implementation uses the following memory layout:
  843|       |//
  844|       |// [Node, Node, ... Node | info, info, ... infoSentinel ]
  845|       |//
  846|       |// * Node: either a DataNode that directly has the std::pair<key, val> as member,
  847|       |//   or a DataNode with a pointer to std::pair<key,val>. Which DataNode representation to use
  848|       |//   depends on how fast the swap() operation is. Heuristically, this is automatically choosen
  849|       |//   based on sizeof(). there are always 2^n Nodes.
  850|       |//
  851|       |// * info: Each Node in the map has a corresponding info byte, so there are 2^n info bytes.
  852|       |//   Each byte is initialized to 0, meaning the corresponding Node is empty. Set to 1 means the
  853|       |//   corresponding node contains data. Set to 2 means the corresponding Node is filled, but it
  854|       |//   actually belongs to the previous position and was pushed out because that place is already
  855|       |//   taken.
  856|       |//
  857|       |// * infoSentinel: Sentinel byte set to 1, so that iterator's ++ can stop at end() without the
  858|       |//   need for a idx variable.
  859|       |//
  860|       |// According to STL, order of templates has effect on throughput. That's why I've moved the
  861|       |// boolean to the front.
  862|       |// https://www.reddit.com/r/cpp/comments/ahp6iu/compile_time_binary_size_reductions_and_cs_future/eeguck4/
  863|       |template <bool IsFlat, size_t MaxLoadFactor100, typename Key, typename T, typename Hash,
  864|       |          typename KeyEqual>
  865|       |class Table
  866|       |    : public WrapHash<Hash>,
  867|       |      public WrapKeyEqual<KeyEqual>,
  868|       |      detail::NodeAllocator<
  869|       |          typename std::conditional<
  870|       |              std::is_void<T>::value, Key,
  871|       |              robin_hood::pair<typename std::conditional<IsFlat, Key, Key const>::type, T>>::type,
  872|       |          4, 16384, IsFlat> {
  873|       |public:
  874|       |    static constexpr bool is_flat = IsFlat;
  875|       |    static constexpr bool is_map = !std::is_void<T>::value;
  876|       |    static constexpr bool is_set = !is_map;
  877|       |    static constexpr bool is_transparent =
  878|       |        has_is_transparent<Hash>::value && has_is_transparent<KeyEqual>::value;
  879|       |
  880|       |    using key_type = Key;
  881|       |    using mapped_type = T;
  882|       |    using value_type = typename std::conditional<
  883|       |        is_set, Key,
  884|       |        robin_hood::pair<typename std::conditional<is_flat, Key, Key const>::type, T>>::type;
  885|       |    using size_type = size_t;
  886|       |    using hasher = Hash;
  887|       |    using key_equal = KeyEqual;
  888|       |    using Self = Table<IsFlat, MaxLoadFactor100, key_type, mapped_type, hasher, key_equal>;
  889|       |
  890|       |private:
  891|       |    static_assert(MaxLoadFactor100 > 10 && MaxLoadFactor100 < 100,
  892|       |                  "MaxLoadFactor100 needs to be >10 && < 100");
  893|       |
  894|       |    using WHash = WrapHash<Hash>;
  895|       |    using WKeyEqual = WrapKeyEqual<KeyEqual>;
  896|       |
  897|       |    // configuration defaults
  898|       |
  899|       |    // make sure we have 8 elements, needed to quickly rehash mInfo
  900|       |    static constexpr size_t InitialNumElements = sizeof(uint64_t);
  901|       |    static constexpr uint32_t InitialInfoNumBits = 5;
  902|       |    static constexpr uint8_t InitialInfoInc = 1U << InitialInfoNumBits;
  903|       |    static constexpr size_t InfoMask = InitialInfoInc - 1U;
  904|       |    static constexpr uint8_t InitialInfoHashShift = 0;
  905|       |    using DataPool = detail::NodeAllocator<value_type, 4, 16384, IsFlat>;
  906|       |
  907|       |    // type needs to be wider than uint8_t.
  908|       |    using InfoType = uint32_t;
  909|       |
  910|       |    // DataNode ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
  911|       |
  912|       |    // Primary template for the data node. We have special implementations for small and big
  913|       |    // objects. For large objects it is assumed that swap() is fairly slow, so we allocate these
  914|       |    // on the heap so swap merely swaps a pointer.
  915|       |    template <typename M, bool>
  916|       |    class DataNode {};
  917|       |
  918|       |    // Small: just allocate on the stack.
  919|       |    template <typename M>
  920|       |    class DataNode<M, true> final {
  921|       |    public:
  922|       |        template <typename... Args>
  923|       |        explicit DataNode(M& ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(map) /*unused*/, Args&&... args) noexcept(
  924|       |            noexcept(value_type(std::forward<Args>(args)...)))
  925|       |            : mData(std::forward<Args>(args)...) {}
  926|       |
  927|       |        DataNode(M& ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(map) /*unused*/, DataNode<M, true>&& n) noexcept(
  928|       |            std::is_nothrow_move_constructible<value_type>::value)
  929|       |            : mData(std::move(n.mData)) {}
  930|       |
  931|       |        // doesn't do anything
  932|       |        void destroy(M& ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(map) /*unused*/) noexcept {}
  933|       |        void destroyDoNotDeallocate() noexcept {}
  934|       |
  935|       |        value_type const* operator->() const noexcept {
  936|       |            return &mData;
  937|       |        }
  938|       |        value_type* operator->() noexcept {
  939|       |            return &mData;
  940|       |        }
  941|       |
  942|       |        const value_type& operator*() const noexcept {
  943|       |            return mData;
  944|       |        }
  945|       |
  946|       |        value_type& operator*() noexcept {
  947|       |            return mData;
  948|       |        }
  949|       |
  950|       |        template <typename VT = value_type>
  951|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  952|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_map, typename VT::first_type&>::type getFirst() noexcept {
  953|       |            return mData.first;
  954|       |        }
  955|       |        template <typename VT = value_type>
  956|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  957|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_set, VT&>::type getFirst() noexcept {
  958|       |            return mData;
  959|       |        }
  960|       |
  961|       |        template <typename VT = value_type>
  962|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  963|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_map, typename VT::first_type const&>::type
  964|       |            getFirst() const noexcept {
  965|       |            return mData.first;
  966|       |        }
  967|       |        template <typename VT = value_type>
  968|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  969|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_set, VT const&>::type getFirst() const noexcept {
  970|       |            return mData;
  971|       |        }
  972|       |
  973|       |        template <typename MT = mapped_type>
  974|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  975|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_map, MT&>::type getSecond() noexcept {
  976|       |            return mData.second;
  977|       |        }
  978|       |
  979|       |        template <typename MT = mapped_type>
  980|       |        ROBIN_HOOD(NODISCARD)
  981|       |        typename std::enable_if<is_set, MT const&>::type getSecond() const noexcept {
  982|       |            return mData.second;
  983|       |        }
  984|       |
  985|       |        void swap(DataNode<M, true>& o) noexcept(
  986|       |            noexcept(std::declval<value_type>().swap(std::declval<value_type>()))) {
  987|       |            mData.swap(o.mData);
  988|       |        }
  989|       |
  990|       |    private:
  991|       |        value_type mData;
  992|       |    };
  993|       |
  994|       |    // big object: allocate on heap.
  995|       |    template <typename M>
  996|       |    class DataNode<M, false> {
  997|       |    public:
  998|       |        template <typename... Args>
  999|       |        explicit DataNode(M& map, Args&&... args)

 1101|       |    struct Cloner;
 1102|       |
 1103|       |    // fast path: Just copy data, without allocating anything.
 1104|       |    template <typename M>
 1105|       |    struct Cloner<M, true> {
 1106|       |        void operator()(M const& source, M& target) const {
 1107|       |            auto const* const src = reinterpret_cast<char const*>(source.mKeyVals);
 1108|       |            auto* tgt = reinterpret_cast<char*>(target.mKeyVals);
 1109|       |            auto const numElementsWithBuffer = target.calcNumElementsWithBuffer(target.mMask + 1);
 1110|       |            std::copy(src, src + target.calcNumBytesTotal(numElementsWithBuffer), tgt);
 1111|       |        }
 1112|       |    };
 1113|       |

 1173|       |
 1174|       |    // Iter ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
 1175|       |
 1176|       |    struct fast_forward_tag {};
 1177|       |
 1178|       |    // generic iterator for both const_iterator and iterator.
 1179|       |    template <bool IsConst>
 1180|       |    // NOLINTNEXTLINE(hicpp-special-member-functions,cppcoreguidelines-special-member-functions)
 1181|       |    class Iter {
 1182|       |    private:
 1183|       |        using NodePtr = typename std::conditional<IsConst, Node const*, Node*>::type;
 1184|       |
 1185|       |    public:
 1186|       |        using difference_type = std::ptrdiff_t;
 1187|       |        using value_type = typename Self::value_type;
 1188|       |        using reference = typename std::conditional<IsConst, value_type const&, value_type&>::type;
 1189|       |        using pointer = typename std::conditional<IsConst, value_type const*, value_type*>::type;
 1190|       |        using iterator_category = std::forward_iterator_tag;
 1191|       |
 1192|       |        // default constructed iterator can be compared to itself, but WON'T return true when
 1193|       |        // compared to end().
 1194|       |        Iter() = default;
 1195|       |
 1196|       |        // Rule of zero: nothing specified. The conversion constructor is only enabled for
 1197|       |        // iterator to const_iterator, so it doesn't accidentally work as a copy ctor.
 1198|       |
 1199|       |        // Conversion constructor from iterator to const_iterator.
 1200|       |        template <bool OtherIsConst,
 1201|       |                  typename = typename std::enable_if<IsConst && !OtherIsConst>::type>
 1202|       |        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(hicpp-explicit-conversions)
 1203|       |        Iter(Iter<OtherIsConst> const& other) noexcept
 1204|       |            : mKeyVals(other.mKeyVals)
 1205|       |            , mInfo(other.mInfo) {}
 1206|       |
 1207|       |        Iter(NodePtr valPtr, uint8_t const* infoPtr) noexcept

 1209|   254M|            , mInfo(infoPtr) {}

 1257|       |
 1258|       |    private:
 1259|       |        // fast forward to the next non-free info byte
 1260|       |        // I've tried a few variants that don't depend on intrinsics, but unfortunately they are
 1261|       |        // quite a bit slower than this one. So I've reverted that change again. See map_benchmark.
 1262|  28.6M|        void fastForward() noexcept {
 1263|  28.6M|            size_t n = 0;
 1264|  63.9M|            while (0U == (n = detail::unaligned_load<size_t>(mInfo))) {
 1265|  35.2M|                mInfo += sizeof(size_t);
 1266|  35.2M|                mKeyVals += sizeof(size_t);
 1267|  35.2M|            }
 1268|       |#if defined(ROBIN_HOOD_DISABLE_INTRINSICS)
 1269|       |            // we know for certain that within the next 8 bytes we'll find a non-zero one.
 1270|       |            if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(0U == detail::unaligned_load<uint32_t>(mInfo))) {
 1271|       |                mInfo += 4;
 1272|       |                mKeyVals += 4;
 1273|       |            }
 1274|       |            if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(0U == detail::unaligned_load<uint16_t>(mInfo))) {
 1275|       |                mInfo += 2;
 1276|       |                mKeyVals += 2;
 1277|       |            }
 1278|       |            if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(0U == *mInfo)) {
 1279|       |                mInfo += 1;
 1280|       |                mKeyVals += 1;
 1281|       |            }
 1282|       |#else
 1283|  28.6M|#    if ROBIN_HOOD(LITTLE_ENDIAN)
 1284|  28.6M|            auto inc = ROBIN_HOOD_COUNT_TRAILING_ZEROES(n) / 8;
 1285|       |#    else
 1286|       |            auto inc = ROBIN_HOOD_COUNT_LEADING_ZEROES(n) / 8;
 1287|       |#    endif
 1288|  28.6M|            mInfo += inc;
 1289|  28.6M|            mKeyVals += inc;
 1290|  28.6M|#endif
 1291|  28.6M|        }

 1298|       |    ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
 1299|       |
 1300|       |    // highly performance relevant code.
 1301|       |    // Lower bits are used for indexing into the array (2^n size)
 1302|       |    // The upper 1-5 bits need to be a reasonable good hash, to save comparisons.
 1303|       |    template <typename HashKey>
 1304|   133M|    void keyToIdx(HashKey&& key, size_t* idx, InfoType* info) const {
 1305|       |        // In addition to whatever hash is used, add another mul & shift so we get better hashing.
 1306|       |        // This serves as a bad hash prevention, if the given data is
 1307|       |        // badly mixed.
 1308|   133M|        auto h = static_cast<uint64_t>(WHash::operator()(key));
 1309|       |
 1310|   133M|        h *= mHashMultiplier;
 1311|   133M|        h ^= h >> 33U;
 1312|       |
 1313|       |        // the lower InitialInfoNumBits are reserved for info.
 1314|   133M|        *info = mInfoInc + static_cast<InfoType>((h & InfoMask) >> mInfoHashShift);
 1315|   133M|        *idx = (static_cast<size_t>(h) >> InitialInfoNumBits) & mMask;
 1316|   133M|    }

 1404|       |    // inserts a keyval that is guaranteed to be new, e.g. when the hashmap is resized.
 1405|       |    // @return True on success, false if something went wrong
 1406|  1.15M|    void insert_move(Node&& keyval) {
 1407|       |        // we don't retry, fail if overflowing
 1408|       |        // don't need to check max num elements
 1409|  1.15M|        if (0 == mMaxNumElementsAllowed && !try_increase_info()) {
 1410|      0|            throwOverflowError();
 1411|      0|        }
 1412|       |
 1413|  1.15M|        size_t idx{};
 1414|  1.15M|        InfoType info{};
 1415|  1.15M|        keyToIdx(keyval.getFirst(), &idx, &info);
 1416|       |
 1417|       |        // skip forward. Use <= because we are certain that the element is not there.
 1418|  1.52M|        while (info <= mInfo[idx]) {
 1419|   376k|            idx = idx + 1;
 1420|   376k|            info += mInfoInc;
 1421|   376k|        }
 1422|       |
 1423|       |        // key not found, so we are now exactly where we want to insert it.
 1424|  1.15M|        auto const insertion_idx = idx;
 1425|  1.15M|        auto const insertion_info = static_cast<uint8_t>(info);
 1426|  1.15M|        if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(insertion_info + mInfoInc > 0xFF)) {
 1427|     27|            mMaxNumElementsAllowed = 0;
 1428|     27|        }
 1429|       |
 1430|       |        // find an empty spot
 1431|  1.15M|        while (0 != mInfo[idx]) {
 1432|  2.85k|            next(&info, &idx);
 1433|  2.85k|        }
 1434|       |
 1435|  1.15M|        auto& l = mKeyVals[insertion_idx];
 1436|  1.15M|        if (idx == insertion_idx) {
 1437|  1.14M|            ::new (static_cast<void*>(&l)) Node(std::move(keyval));
 1438|  1.14M|        } else {
 1439|  2.82k|            shiftUp(idx, insertion_idx);
 1440|  2.82k|            l = std::move(keyval);
 1441|  2.82k|        }
 1442|       |
 1443|       |        // put at empty spot
 1444|  1.15M|        mInfo[insertion_idx] = insertion_info;
 1445|       |
 1446|  1.15M|        ++mNumElements;
 1447|  1.15M|    }

 1458|       |
 1459|       |    // Creates an empty hash map. Nothing is allocated yet, this happens at the first insert.
 1460|       |    // This tremendously speeds up ctor & dtor of a map that never receives an element. The
 1461|       |    // penalty is payed at the first insert, and not before. Lookup of this empty map works
 1462|       |    // because everybody points to DummyInfoByte::b. parameter bucket_count is dictated by the
 1463|       |    // standard, but we can ignore it.
 1464|       |    explicit Table(
 1465|       |        size_t ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(bucket_count) /*unused*/, const Hash& h = Hash{},
 1466|       |        const KeyEqual& equal = KeyEqual{}) noexcept(noexcept(Hash(h)) && noexcept(KeyEqual(equal)))
 1467|       |        : WHash(h)
 1468|       |        , WKeyEqual(equal) {
 1469|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1470|       |    }
 1471|       |
 1472|       |    template <typename Iter>
 1473|       |    Table(Iter first, Iter last, size_t ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(bucket_count) /*unused*/ = 0,
 1474|       |          const Hash& h = Hash{}, const KeyEqual& equal = KeyEqual{})
 1475|       |        : WHash(h)
 1476|       |        , WKeyEqual(equal) {
 1477|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1478|       |        insert(first, last);
 1479|       |    }
 1480|       |
 1481|       |    Table(std::initializer_list<value_type> initlist,
 1482|       |          size_t ROBIN_HOOD_UNUSED(bucket_count) /*unused*/ = 0, const Hash& h = Hash{},
 1483|       |          const KeyEqual& equal = KeyEqual{})
 1484|       |        : WHash(h)
 1485|       |        , WKeyEqual(equal) {
 1486|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1487|       |        insert(initlist.begin(), initlist.end());
 1488|       |    }
 1489|       |
 1490|       |    Table(Table&& o) noexcept
 1491|       |        : WHash(std::move(static_cast<WHash&>(o)))
 1492|       |        , WKeyEqual(std::move(static_cast<WKeyEqual&>(o)))
 1493|       |        , DataPool(std::move(static_cast<DataPool&>(o))) {
 1494|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1495|       |        if (o.mMask) {
 1496|       |            mHashMultiplier = std::move(o.mHashMultiplier);
 1497|       |            mKeyVals = std::move(o.mKeyVals);
 1498|       |            mInfo = std::move(o.mInfo);
 1499|       |            mNumElements = std::move(o.mNumElements);
 1500|       |            mMask = std::move(o.mMask);
 1501|       |            mMaxNumElementsAllowed = std::move(o.mMaxNumElementsAllowed);
 1502|       |            mInfoInc = std::move(o.mInfoInc);
 1503|       |            mInfoHashShift = std::move(o.mInfoHashShift);
 1504|       |            // set other's mask to 0 so its destructor won't do anything
 1505|       |            o.init();
 1506|       |        }
 1507|       |    }
 1508|       |
 1509|       |    Table& operator=(Table&& o) noexcept {
 1510|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1511|       |        if (&o != this) {
 1512|       |            if (o.mMask) {
 1513|       |                // only move stuff if the other map actually has some data
 1514|       |                destroy();
 1515|       |                mHashMultiplier = std::move(o.mHashMultiplier);
 1516|       |                mKeyVals = std::move(o.mKeyVals);
 1517|       |                mInfo = std::move(o.mInfo);
 1518|       |                mNumElements = std::move(o.mNumElements);
 1519|       |                mMask = std::move(o.mMask);
 1520|       |                mMaxNumElementsAllowed = std::move(o.mMaxNumElementsAllowed);
 1521|       |                mInfoInc = std::move(o.mInfoInc);
 1522|       |                mInfoHashShift = std::move(o.mInfoHashShift);
 1523|       |                WHash::operator=(std::move(static_cast<WHash&>(o)));
 1524|       |                WKeyEqual::operator=(std::move(static_cast<WKeyEqual&>(o)));
 1525|       |                DataPool::operator=(std::move(static_cast<DataPool&>(o)));
 1526|       |
 1527|       |                o.init();
 1528|       |
 1529|       |            } else {
 1530|       |                // nothing in the other map => just clear us.
 1531|       |                clear();
 1532|       |            }
 1533|       |        }
 1534|       |        return *this;
 1535|       |    }
 1536|       |
 1537|       |    Table(const Table& o)
 1538|       |        : WHash(static_cast<const WHash&>(o))
 1539|       |        , WKeyEqual(static_cast<const WKeyEqual&>(o))
 1540|       |        , DataPool(static_cast<const DataPool&>(o)) {
 1541|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1542|       |        if (!o.empty()) {
 1543|       |            // not empty: create an exact copy. it is also possible to just iterate through all
 1544|       |            // elements and insert them, but copying is probably faster.
 1545|       |
 1546|       |            auto const numElementsWithBuffer = calcNumElementsWithBuffer(o.mMask + 1);
 1547|       |            auto const numBytesTotal = calcNumBytesTotal(numElementsWithBuffer);
 1548|       |
 1549|       |            ROBIN_HOOD_LOG("std::malloc " << numBytesTotal << " = calcNumBytesTotal("
 1550|       |                                          << numElementsWithBuffer << ")")
 1551|       |            mHashMultiplier = o.mHashMultiplier;
 1552|       |            mKeyVals = static_cast<Node*>(
 1553|       |                detail::assertNotNull<std::bad_alloc>(ROBIN_HOOD_MALLOC(numBytesTotal)));
 1554|       |            // no need for calloc because clonData does memcpy
 1555|       |            mInfo = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(mKeyVals + numElementsWithBuffer);
 1556|       |            mNumElements = o.mNumElements;
 1557|       |            mMask = o.mMask;
 1558|       |            mMaxNumElementsAllowed = o.mMaxNumElementsAllowed;
 1559|       |            mInfoInc = o.mInfoInc;
 1560|       |            mInfoHashShift = o.mInfoHashShift;
 1561|       |            cloneData(o);
 1562|       |        }
 1563|       |    }
 1564|       |
 1565|       |    // Creates a copy of the given map. Copy constructor of each entry is used.
 1566|       |    // Not sure why clang-tidy thinks this doesn't handle self assignment, it does
 1567|       |    // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-unhandled-self-assignment,cert-oop54-cpp)
 1568|       |    Table& operator=(Table const& o) {
 1569|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1570|       |        if (&o == this) {
 1571|       |            // prevent assigning of itself
 1572|       |            return *this;
 1573|       |        }
 1574|       |
 1575|       |        // we keep using the old allocator and not assign the new one, because we want to keep
 1576|       |        // the memory available. when it is the same size.
 1577|       |        if (o.empty()) {
 1578|       |            if (0 == mMask) {
 1579|       |                // nothing to do, we are empty too
 1580|       |                return *this;
 1581|       |            }
 1582|       |
 1583|       |            // not empty: destroy what we have there
 1584|       |            // clear also resets mInfo to 0, that's sometimes not necessary.
 1585|       |            destroy();
 1586|       |            init();
 1587|       |            WHash::operator=(static_cast<const WHash&>(o));
 1588|       |            WKeyEqual::operator=(static_cast<const WKeyEqual&>(o));
 1589|       |            DataPool::operator=(static_cast<DataPool const&>(o));
 1590|       |
 1591|       |            return *this;
 1592|       |        }
 1593|       |
 1594|       |        // clean up old stuff
 1595|       |        Destroyer<Self, IsFlat && std::is_trivially_destructible<Node>::value>{}.nodes(*this);
 1596|       |
 1597|       |        if (mMask != o.mMask) {
 1598|       |            // no luck: we don't have the same array size allocated, so we need to realloc.
 1599|       |            if (0 != mMask) {
 1600|       |                // only deallocate if we actually have data!
 1601|       |                ROBIN_HOOD_LOG("std::free")
 1602|       |                ROBIN_HOOD_FREE(mKeyVals);
 1603|       |            }
 1604|       |
 1605|       |            auto const numElementsWithBuffer = calcNumElementsWithBuffer(o.mMask + 1);
 1606|       |            auto const numBytesTotal = calcNumBytesTotal(numElementsWithBuffer);
 1607|       |            ROBIN_HOOD_LOG("std::malloc " << numBytesTotal << " = calcNumBytesTotal("
 1608|       |                                          << numElementsWithBuffer << ")")
 1609|       |            mKeyVals = static_cast<Node*>(
 1610|       |                detail::assertNotNull<std::bad_alloc>(ROBIN_HOOD_MALLOC(numBytesTotal)));
 1611|       |
 1612|       |            // no need for calloc here because cloneData performs a memcpy.
 1613|       |            mInfo = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(mKeyVals + numElementsWithBuffer);
 1614|       |            // sentinel is set in cloneData
 1615|       |        }
 1616|       |        WHash::operator=(static_cast<const WHash&>(o));
 1617|       |        WKeyEqual::operator=(static_cast<const WKeyEqual&>(o));
 1618|       |        DataPool::operator=(static_cast<DataPool const&>(o));
 1619|       |        mHashMultiplier = o.mHashMultiplier;
 1620|       |        mNumElements = o.mNumElements;
 1621|       |        mMask = o.mMask;
 1622|       |        mMaxNumElementsAllowed = o.mMaxNumElementsAllowed;
 1623|       |        mInfoInc = o.mInfoInc;
 1624|       |        mInfoHashShift = o.mInfoHashShift;
 1625|       |        cloneData(o);
 1626|       |
 1627|       |        return *this;
 1628|       |    }
 1629|       |
 1630|       |    // Swaps everything between the two maps.
 1631|       |    void swap(Table& o) {
 1632|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1633|       |        using std::swap;
 1634|       |        swap(o, *this);
 1635|       |    }

 1640|  20.1k|        if (empty()) {
 1641|       |            // don't do anything! also important because we don't want to write to

 1647|       |

 1664|       |    // Checks if both tables contain the same entries. Order is irrelevant.
 1665|       |    bool operator==(const Table& other) const {
 1666|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1667|       |        if (other.size() != size()) {
 1668|       |            return false;
 1669|       |        }
 1670|       |        for (auto const& otherEntry : other) {
 1671|       |            if (!has(otherEntry)) {
 1672|       |                return false;
 1673|       |            }
 1674|       |        }
 1675|       |
 1676|       |        return true;
 1677|       |    }
 1678|       |

 1680|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1681|       |        return !operator==(other);
 1682|       |    }

 1988|       |    // Erases element at pos, returns iterator to the next element.
 1989|  5.06k|    iterator erase(iterator pos) {
 1990|  5.06k|        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 1991|       |        // we assume that pos always points to a valid entry, and not end().
 1992|  5.06k|        auto const idx = static_cast<size_t>(pos.mKeyVals - mKeyVals);
 1993|       |
 1994|  5.06k|        shiftDown(idx);
 1995|  5.06k|        --mNumElements;
 1996|       |
 1997|  5.06k|        if (*pos.mInfo) {
 1998|       |            // we've backward shifted, return this again
 1999|  1.92k|            return pos;
 2000|  1.92k|        }
 2001|       |
 2002|       |        // no backward shift, return next element
 2003|  3.13k|        return ++pos;
 2004|  5.06k|    }

 2006|      1|    size_t erase(const key_type& key) {
 2007|      1|        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 2008|      1|        size_t idx{};
 2009|      1|        InfoType info{};
 2010|      1|        keyToIdx(key, &idx, &info);
 2011|       |
 2012|       |        // check while info matches with the source idx
 2013|      1|        do {
 2014|      1|            if (info == mInfo[idx] && WKeyEqual::operator()(key, mKeyVals[idx].getFirst())) {
 2015|      1|                shiftDown(idx);
 2016|      1|                --mNumElements;
 2017|      1|                return 1;
 2018|      1|            }
 2019|      0|            next(&info, &idx);
 2020|      0|        } while (info <= mInfo[idx]);
 2021|       |
 2022|       |        // nothing found to delete
 2023|      0|        return 0;
 2024|      1|    }

 2040|       |    // If possible reallocates the map to a smaller one. This frees the underlying table.
 2041|       |    // Does not do anything if load_factor is too large for decreasing the table's size.
 2042|       |    void compact() {
 2043|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 2044|       |        auto newSize = InitialNumElements;
 2045|       |        while (calcMaxNumElementsAllowed(newSize) < mNumElements && newSize != 0) {
 2046|       |            newSize *= 2;
 2047|       |        }
 2048|       |        if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(newSize == 0)) {
 2049|       |            throwOverflowError();
 2050|       |        }
 2051|       |
 2052|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_LOG("newSize > mMask + 1: " << newSize << " > " << mMask << " + 1")
 2053|       |
 2054|       |        // only actually do anything when the new size is bigger than the old one. This prevents to
 2055|       |        // continuously allocate for each reserve() call.
 2056|       |        if (newSize < mMask + 1) {
 2057|       |            rehashPowerOfTwo(newSize, true);
 2058|       |        }
 2059|       |    }
 2060|       |
 2061|  1.28M|    size_type size() const noexcept { // NOLINT(modernize-use-nodiscard)
 2062|  1.28M|        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 2063|  1.28M|        return mNumElements;
 2064|  1.28M|    }

 2215|       |    template <typename OtherKey, typename... Args>
 2216|       |    std::pair<iterator, bool> try_emplace_impl(OtherKey&& key, Args&&... args) {
 2217|       |        ROBIN_HOOD_TRACE(this)
 2218|       |        auto idxAndState = insertKeyPrepareEmptySpot(key);
 2219|       |        switch (idxAndState.second) {
 2220|       |        case InsertionState::key_found:
 2221|       |            break;
 2222|       |
 2223|       |        case InsertionState::new_node:
 2224|       |            ::new (static_cast<void*>(&mKeyVals[idxAndState.first])) Node(
 2225|       |                *this, std::piecewise_construct, std::forward_as_tuple(std::forward<OtherKey>(key)),
 2226|       |                std::forward_as_tuple(std::forward<Args>(args)...));
 2227|       |            break;
 2228|       |
 2229|       |        case InsertionState::overwrite_node:
 2230|       |            mKeyVals[idxAndState.first] = Node(*this, std::piecewise_construct,
 2231|       |                                               std::forward_as_tuple(std::forward<OtherKey>(key)),
 2232|       |                                               std::forward_as_tuple(std::forward<Args>(args)...));
 2233|       |            break;
 2234|       |
 2235|       |        case InsertionState::overflow_error:
 2236|       |            throwOverflowError();
 2237|       |            break;
 2238|       |        }
 2239|       |
 2240|       |        return std::make_pair(iterator(mKeyVals + idxAndState.first, mInfo + idxAndState.first),
 2241|       |                              InsertionState::key_found != idxAndState.second);
 2242|       |    }

  | Unexecuted instantiation: _ZN10robin_hood6detail5TableILb0ELm80EN6p2pool6raw_ipENSt6chrono10time_pointINS4_3_V212steady_clockENS4_8durationIlSt5ratioILl1ELl1000000000EEEEEENS_4hashIS3_vEESt8equal_toIS3_EE8initDataEm

 2296|       |
 2297|       |    enum class InsertionState { overflow_error, key_found, new_node, overwrite_node };
 2298|       |
 2299|       |    // Finds key, and if not already present prepares a spot where to pot the key & value.
 2300|       |    // This potentially shifts nodes out of the way, updates mInfo and number of inserted
 2301|       |    // elements, so the only operation left to do is create/assign a new node at that spot.
 2302|       |    template <typename OtherKey>
 2303|  32.4M|    std::pair<size_t, InsertionState> insertKeyPrepareEmptySpot(OtherKey&& key) {
 2304|  32.4M|        for (int i = 0; i < 256; ++i) {
 2305|  32.4M|            size_t idx{};
 2306|  32.4M|            InfoType info{};
 2307|  32.4M|            keyToIdx(key, &idx, &info);
 2308|  32.4M|            nextWhileLess(&info, &idx);
 2309|       |
 2310|       |            // while we potentially have a match
 2311|  32.4M|            while (info == mInfo[idx]) {
 2312|  25.4M|                if (WKeyEqual::operator()(key, mKeyVals[idx].getFirst())) {
 2313|       |                    // key already exists, do NOT insert.
 2314|       |                    // see http://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/container/unordered_map/insert
 2315|  25.4M|                    return std::make_pair(idx, InsertionState::key_found);
 2316|  25.4M|                }
 2317|  76.2k|                next(&info, &idx);
 2318|  76.2k|            }
 2319|       |
 2320|       |            // unlikely that this evaluates to true
 2321|  7.01M|            if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(mNumElements >= mMaxNumElementsAllowed)) {
 2322|    793|                if (!increase_size()) {
 2323|      0|                    return std::make_pair(size_t(0), InsertionState::overflow_error);
 2324|      0|                }
 2325|    793|                continue;
 2326|    793|            }
 2327|       |
 2328|       |            // key not found, so we are now exactly where we want to insert it.
 2329|  7.01M|            auto const insertion_idx = idx;
 2330|  7.01M|            auto const insertion_info = info;
 2331|  7.01M|            if (ROBIN_HOOD_UNLIKELY(insertion_info + mInfoInc > 0xFF)) {
 2332|     19|                mMaxNumElementsAllowed = 0;
 2333|     19|            }
 2334|       |
 2335|       |            // find an empty spot
 2336|  9.49M|            while (0 != mInfo[idx]) {
 2337|  2.47M|                next(&info, &idx);
 2338|  2.47M|            }
 2339|       |
 2340|  7.01M|            if (idx != insertion_idx) {
 2341|   537k|                shiftUp(idx, insertion_idx);
 2342|   537k|            }
 2343|       |            // put at empty spot
 2344|  7.01M|            mInfo[insertion_idx] = static_cast<uint8_t>(insertion_info);
 2345|  7.01M|            ++mNumElements;
 2346|  7.01M|            return std::make_pair(insertion_idx, idx == insertion_idx
 2347|  7.01M|                                                     ? InsertionState::new_node
 2348|  7.01M|                                                     : InsertionState::overwrite_node);
 2349|  7.01M|        }
 2350|       |
 2351|       |        // enough attempts failed, so finally give up.
 2352|      0|        return std::make_pair(size_t(0), InsertionState::overflow_error);
 2353|  32.4M|    }

/home/runner/work/p2pool/p2pool/external/src/siphash/siphash.c:
    1|       |/*
    2|       |   SipHash reference C implementation
    3|       |
    4|       |   Copyright (c) 2012-2022 Jean-Philippe Aumasson
    5|       |   <jeanphilippe.aumasson@gmail.com>
    6|       |   Copyright (c) 2012-2014 Daniel J. Bernstein <djb@cr.yp.to>
    7|       |
    8|       |   To the extent possible under law, the author(s) have dedicated all copyright
    9|       |   and related and neighboring rights to this software to the public domain
   10|       |   worldwide. This software is distributed without any warranty.
   11|       |
   12|       |   You should have received a copy of the CC0 Public Domain Dedication along
   13|       |   with
   14|       |   this software. If not, see
   15|       |   <http://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/>.
   16|       | */
   17|       |
   18|       |#include "siphash.h"
   19|       |#include <assert.h>
   20|       |#include <stddef.h>
   21|       |#include <stdint.h>
   22|       |
   23|       |/* default: SipHash-2-4 */
   24|       |/* P2Pool:  SipHash-1-3 */
   25|       |#ifndef cROUNDS
   26|  1.67G|#define cROUNDS 1
   27|       |#endif
   28|       |#ifndef dROUNDS
   29|   534M|#define dROUNDS 3
   30|       |#endif
   31|       |
   32|  7.43G|#define ROTL(x, b) (uint64_t)(((x) << (b)) | ((x) >> (64 - (b))))
   33|       |
   34|       |#define U32TO8_LE(p, v)                                                        \
   35|   267M|    (p)[0] = (uint8_t)((v));                                                   \
   36|   267M|    (p)[1] = (uint8_t)((v) >> 8);                                              \
   37|   267M|    (p)[2] = (uint8_t)((v) >> 16);                                             \
   38|   267M|    (p)[3] = (uint8_t)((v) >> 24);
   39|       |
   40|       |#define U64TO8_LE(p, v)                                                        \
   41|   133M|    U32TO8_LE((p), (uint32_t)((v)));                                           \
   42|   133M|    U32TO8_LE((p) + 4, (uint32_t)((v) >> 32));
   43|       |
   44|       |#define U8TO64_LE(p)                                                           \
   45|   972M|    (((uint64_t)((p)[0])) | ((uint64_t)((p)[1]) << 8) |                        \
   46|   972M|     ((uint64_t)((p)[2]) << 16) | ((uint64_t)((p)[3]) << 24) |                 \
   47|   972M|     ((uint64_t)((p)[4]) << 32) | ((uint64_t)((p)[5]) << 40) |                 \
   48|   972M|     ((uint64_t)((p)[6]) << 48) | ((uint64_t)((p)[7]) << 56))
   49|       |
   50|       |#define SIPROUND                                                               \
   51|  1.50G|    do {                                                                       \
   52|  1.23G|        v0 += v1;                                                              \
   53|  1.23G|        v1 = ROTL(v1, 13);                                                     \
   54|  1.23G|        v1 ^= v0;                                                              \
   55|  1.23G|        v0 = ROTL(v0, 32);                                                     \
   56|  1.23G|        v2 += v3;                                                              \
   57|  1.23G|        v3 = ROTL(v3, 16);                                                     \
   58|  1.23G|        v3 ^= v2;                                                              \
   59|  1.23G|        v0 += v3;                                                              \
   60|  1.23G|        v3 = ROTL(v3, 21);                                                     \
   61|  1.23G|        v3 ^= v0;                                                              \
   62|  1.23G|        v2 += v1;                                                              \
   63|  1.23G|        v1 = ROTL(v1, 17);                                                     \
   64|  1.23G|        v1 ^= v2;                                                              \
   65|  1.23G|        v2 = ROTL(v2, 32);                                                     \
   66|  1.23G|    } while (0)
   67|       |
   68|       |#ifdef DEBUG_SIPHASH
   69|       |#include <stdio.h>
   70|       |
   71|       |#define TRACE                                                                  \
   72|       |    do {                                                                       \
   73|       |        printf("(%3zu) v0 %016" PRIx64 "\n", inlen, v0);                       \
   74|       |        printf("(%3zu) v1 %016" PRIx64 "\n", inlen, v1);                       \
   75|       |        printf("(%3zu) v2 %016" PRIx64 "\n", inlen, v2);                       \
   76|       |        printf("(%3zu) v3 %016" PRIx64 "\n", inlen, v3);                       \
   77|       |    } while (0)
   78|       |#else
   79|       |#define TRACE
   80|       |#endif
   81|       |
   82|       |/*
   83|       |    Computes a SipHash value
   84|       |    *in: pointer to input data (read-only)
   85|       |    inlen: input data length in bytes (any size_t value)
   86|       |    *k: pointer to the key data (read-only), must be 16 bytes 
   87|       |    *out: pointer to output data (write-only), outlen bytes must be allocated
   88|       |    outlen: length of the output in bytes, must be 8 or 16
   89|       |*/
   90|       |int siphash(const void *in, const size_t inlen, const void *k, uint8_t *out,
   91|   133M|            const size_t outlen) {
   92|       |
   93|   133M|    const unsigned char *ni = (const unsigned char *)in;
   94|   133M|    const unsigned char *kk = (const unsigned char *)k;
   95|       |
   96|   133M|    assert((outlen == 8) || (outlen == 16));
   97|   133M|    uint64_t v0 = UINT64_C(0x736f6d6570736575);
   98|   133M|    uint64_t v1 = UINT64_C(0x646f72616e646f6d);

  111|   133M|    v0 ^= k0;

  120|   704M|        TRACE;

  125|   704M|    }

  128|      0|    case 7:
  129|      0|        b |= ((uint64_t)ni[6]) << 48;
  130|       |        /* FALLTHRU */
  131|      0|    case 6:
  132|      0|        b |= ((uint64_t)ni[5]) << 40;
  133|       |        /* FALLTHRU */
  134|      0|    case 5:
  135|      0|        b |= ((uint64_t)ni[4]) << 32;
  136|       |        /* FALLTHRU */

  139|       |        /* FALLTHRU */

  142|       |        /* FALLTHRU */

  169|       |static FORCEINLINE uint64_t get_base_reward(uint64_t already_generated_coins)
  170|  6.73k|{
  171|  6.73k|	const uint64_t result = ~already_generated_coins >> 19;
  172|  6.73k|	return (result < BASE_BLOCK_REWARD) ? BASE_BLOCK_REWARD : result;
  173|  6.73k|}
  174|       |
  175|       |static FORCEINLINE uint64_t get_block_reward(uint64_t base_reward, uint64_t median_weight, uint64_t fees, uint64_t weight)
  176|  2.62M|{
  177|  2.62M|	if (weight <= median_weight) {
  178|  2.04k|		return base_reward + fees;
  179|  2.04k|	}
  180|       |
  181|  2.62M|	if (weight > median_weight * 2) {
  182|      0|		return 0;
  183|      0|	}
  184|       |
  185|       |	// This will overflow if median_weight >= 2^32
  186|       |	// Maybe fix it later like in Monero code, but it'll be fiiiine for now...
  187|       |	// Performance of this code is more important
  188|       |
  189|  2.62M|	uint64_t product[2];
  190|  2.62M|	product[0] = umul128(base_reward, (median_weight * 2 - weight) * weight, &product[1]);
  191|       |
  192|  2.62M|	uint64_t rem;
  193|  2.62M|	uint64_t reward = udiv128(product[1], product[0], median_weight * median_weight, &rem);
  194|       |
  195|  2.62M|	return reward + fees;
  196|  2.62M|}

  198|       |void BlockTemplate::shuffle_tx_order()
  199|  6.73k|{
  200|  6.73k|	const uint64_t n = m_mempoolTxsOrder.size();
  201|  6.73k|	if (n > 1) {
  202|  1.32k|		for (uint64_t i = 0, k; i < n - 1; ++i) {
  203|  1.31k|			umul128(m_rng(), n - i, &k);
  204|  1.31k|			std::swap(m_mempoolTxsOrder[i], m_mempoolTxsOrder[i + k]);
  205|  1.31k|		}
  206|      9|	}

  741|       |
  742|       |#if TEST_MEMPOOL_PICKING_ALGORITHM
  743|       |void BlockTemplate::fill_optimal_knapsack(const MinerData& data, uint64_t base_reward, uint64_t miner_tx_weight, uint64_t& best_reward, uint64_t& final_fees, uint64_t& final_weight)
  744|       |{
  745|       |	// Find the maximum possible fee for every weight value and remember which tx leads to this fee/weight
  746|       |	// Run time is O(N*W) where N is the number of transactions and W is the maximum block weight
  747|       |	//
  748|       |	// Actual run time is 0.02-0.05 seconds on real full blocks
  749|       |	// It's too slow and uses too much memory to be practical
  750|       |
  751|       |	constexpr uint64_t FEE_COEFF = 1000;
  752|       |
  753|       |	const uint64_t n = m_mempoolTxs.size();
  754|       |	const uint64_t max_weight = data.median_weight + (data.median_weight / 8) - miner_tx_weight;
  755|       |
  756|       |	m_knapsack.resize((n + 1) * max_weight);
  757|       |	memset(m_knapsack.data(), 0, max_weight * sizeof(uint32_t));
  758|       |
  759|       |	for (size_t i = 1; i <= n; ++i) {
  760|       |		const TxMempoolData& tx = m_mempoolTxs[i - 1];
  761|       |		const uint32_t tx_fee = static_cast<uint32_t>(tx.fee / FEE_COEFF);
  762|       |		const uint64_t tx_weight = tx.weight;
  763|       |
  764|       |		uint32_t* row = m_knapsack.data() + i * max_weight;
  765|       |		const uint32_t* prev_row = row - max_weight;
  766|       |
  767|       |		row[0] = 0;
  768|       |		memcpy(row + 1, prev_row + 1, (tx_weight - 1) * sizeof(uint32_t));
  769|       |
  770|       |#define INNER_LOOP(k) { \
  771|       |	const uint32_t fee_when_used = prev_row[w + k - tx_weight] + tx_fee; \
  772|       |	const uint32_t fee_when_not_used = prev_row[w + k]; \
  773|       |	row[w + k] = (fee_when_used > fee_when_not_used) ? fee_when_used : fee_when_not_used; \
  774|       |}
  775|       |
  776|       |		for (size_t w = tx_weight, max_w = max_weight - 3; w < max_w; w += 4) {
  777|       |			INNER_LOOP(0);
  778|       |			INNER_LOOP(1);
  779|       |			INNER_LOOP(2);
  780|       |			INNER_LOOP(3);
  781|       |		}
  782|       |
  783|       |#undef INNER_LOOP
  784|       |	}
  785|       |
  786|       |	// Now that we know which fee we can get for each weight, just find the maximum possible block reward
  787|       |	best_reward = base_reward;
  788|       |	uint64_t best_weight = 0;
  789|       |	for (uint64_t w = 0, max_w = max_weight - 3; w < max_w; ++w) {
  790|       |		const uint64_t fee = m_knapsack[n * max_weight + w] * FEE_COEFF;
  791|       |		if (fee) {
  792|       |			const uint64_t cur_reward = get_block_reward(base_reward, data.median_weight, fee, w + miner_tx_weight);
  793|       |			if (cur_reward > best_reward) {
  794|       |				best_reward = cur_reward;
  795|       |				best_weight = w;
  796|       |			}
  797|       |		}
  798|       |	}
  799|       |
  800|       |	m_numTransactionHashes = 0;
  801|       |
  802|       |	final_fees = 0;
  803|       |	final_weight = miner_tx_weight;
  804|       |
  805|       |	m_mempoolTxsOrder.clear();
  806|       |	m_transactionHashes.assign(HASH_SIZE, 0);
  807|       |	for (int i = static_cast<int>(n); (i > 0) && (best_weight > 0); --i) {
  808|       |		if (m_knapsack[i * max_weight + best_weight] > m_knapsack[(i - 1) * max_weight + best_weight]) {
  809|       |			m_mempoolTxsOrder.push_back(i - 1);
  810|       |			const TxMempoolData& tx = m_mempoolTxs[i - 1];
  811|       |			m_transactionHashes.insert(m_transactionHashes.end(), tx.id.h, tx.id.h + HASH_SIZE);
  812|       |			++m_numTransactionHashes;
  813|       |			best_weight -= tx.weight;
  814|       |			final_fees += tx.fee;
  815|       |			final_weight += tx.weight;
  816|       |		}
  817|       |	}
  818|       |
  819|       |	m_knapsack.clear();
  820|       |}
  821|       |#endif
  822|       |
  823|       |void BlockTemplate::select_mempool_transactions(const Mempool& mempool)
  824|  6.73k|{
  825|       |	// Only choose transactions that were received 5 or more seconds ago, or high fee (>= 0.006 XMR) transactions
  826|  6.73k|	m_mempoolTxs.clear();
  827|       |
  828|  6.73k|	const uint64_t cur_time = seconds_since_epoch();
  829|  6.73k|	size_t total_mempool_transactions = 0;
  830|       |
  831|  59.6k|	mempool.iterate([this, cur_time, &total_mempool_transactions](const hash&, const TxMempoolData& tx) {
  832|  59.6k|		++total_mempool_transactions;
  833|       |
  834|  59.6k|		if ((cur_time > tx.time_received + 5) || (tx.fee >= HIGH_FEE_VALUE)) {
  835|  59.6k|			m_mempoolTxs.emplace_back(tx);
  836|  59.6k|		}
  837|  59.6k|	});
  838|       |
  839|       |	// Safeguard for busy mempool moments
  840|       |	// If the block template gets too big, nodes won't be able to send and receive it because of p2p packet size limit
  841|       |	// Calculate how many transactions we can take
  842|       |
  843|  6.73k|	PoolBlock* b = m_poolBlockTemplate;
  844|  6.73k|	b->m_transactions.clear();
  845|  6.73k|	b->m_transactions.resize(1);
  846|  6.73k|	b->m_ephPublicKeys.clear();
  847|  6.73k|	b->m_outputAmounts.clear();
  848|       |
  849|       |	// Block template size without coinbase outputs and transactions (minus 2 bytes for output and tx count dummy varints)
  850|  6.73k|	size_t k = b->serialize_mainchain_data().size() + b->serialize_sidechain_data().size() - 2;
  851|       |
  852|       |	// Add output and tx count real varints
  853|  6.73k|	writeVarint(m_shares.size(), [&k](uint8_t) { ++k; });
  854|  6.73k|	writeVarint(m_mempoolTxs.size(), [&k](uint8_t) { ++k; });
  855|       |
  856|       |	// Add a rough upper bound estimation of outputs' size. All outputs have <= 5 bytes for each output's reward (< 0.034359738368 XMR per output)
  857|  6.73k|	k += m_shares.size() * (5 /* reward */ + 1 /* tx_type */ + HASH_SIZE /* stealth address */ + 1 /* viewtag */);
  858|       |
  859|       |	// >= 0.034359738368 XMR is required for a 6 byte varint, add 1 byte per each potential 6-byte varint
  860|  6.73k|	{
  861|  6.73k|		uint64_t r = BASE_BLOCK_REWARD;
  862|  59.6k|		for (const auto& tx : m_mempoolTxs) {
  863|  59.6k|			r += tx.fee;
  864|  59.6k|		}
  865|  6.73k|		k += r / 34359738368ULL;
  866|  6.73k|	}
  867|       |
  868|  6.73k|	const uint32_t max_transactions = static_cast<uint32_t>((MAX_BLOCK_SIZE > k) ? ((MAX_BLOCK_SIZE - k) / HASH_SIZE) : 0);
  869|  6.73k|	LOGINFO(6, max_transactions << " transactions can be taken with current block size limit");
  870|       |
  871|  6.73k|	if (max_transactions == 0) {
  872|      0|		m_mempoolTxs.clear();
  873|      0|	}
  874|  6.73k|	else if (m_mempoolTxs.size() > max_transactions) {
  875|      7|		std::nth_element(m_mempoolTxs.begin(), m_mempoolTxs.begin() + max_transactions, m_mempoolTxs.end());
  876|      7|		m_mempoolTxs.resize(max_transactions);
  877|      7|	}
  878|       |
  879|  6.73k|	LOGINFO(4, "mempool has " << total_mempool_transactions << " transactions, taking " << m_mempoolTxs.size() << " transactions from it");
  880|  6.73k|}
  881|       |
  882|       |int BlockTemplate::create_miner_tx(const MinerData& data, const std::vector<MinerShare>& shares, uint64_t max_reward_amounts_weight, bool dry_run)
  883|  13.4k|{
  884|       |	// Miner transaction (coinbase)
  885|  13.4k|	m_minerTx.clear();
  886|       |
  887|  13.4k|	const size_t num_outputs = shares.size();
  888|  13.4k|	m_minerTx.reserve(num_outputs * 39 + 55);
  889|       |
  890|       |	// tx version
  891|  13.4k|	m_minerTx.push_back(TX_VERSION);
  892|       |
  893|       |	// Unlock time
  894|  13.4k|	writeVarint(data.height + MINER_REWARD_UNLOCK_TIME, m_minerTx);

  951|      0|	}

  997|       |hash BlockTemplate::calc_sidechain_hash(uint32_t sidechain_extra_nonce) const
  998|  30.1k|{
  999|       |	// Calculate side-chain hash (all block template bytes + all side-chain bytes + consensus ID, replacing NONCE, EXTRA_NONCE and HASH itself with 0's)
 1000|  30.1k|	const size_t size = m_sidechainHashBlob.size();
 1001|  30.1k|	const size_t N = m_sidechainHashInputLength;
 1002|       |
 1003|  30.1k|	const size_t sidechain_extra_nonce_offset = size - HASH_SIZE - EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE;
 1004|  30.1k|	const uint8_t sidechain_extra_nonce_buf[EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE] = {
 1005|  30.1k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(sidechain_extra_nonce >> 0),
 1006|  30.1k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(sidechain_extra_nonce >> 8),
 1007|  30.1k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(sidechain_extra_nonce >> 16),
 1008|  30.1k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(sidechain_extra_nonce >> 24)
 1009|  30.1k|	};
 1010|       |
 1011|  30.1k|	hash result;
 1012|  30.1k|	uint8_t buf[288];
 1013|       |
 1014|  30.1k|	const bool b = N && (N <= sidechain_extra_nonce_offset) && (N < size) && (size - N <= sizeof(buf));
 1015|       |
 1016|       |	// Slow path: O(N)
 1017|  30.1k|	if (!b || pool_block_debug()) {
 1018|  58.3M|		keccak_custom([this, sidechain_extra_nonce_offset, &sidechain_extra_nonce_buf](int offset) -> uint8_t {
 1019|  58.3M|			const uint32_t k = static_cast<uint32_t>(offset - sidechain_extra_nonce_offset);
 1020|  58.3M|			if (k < EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE) {
 1021|       |				// cppcheck-suppress objectIndex
 1022|   118k|				return sidechain_extra_nonce_buf[k];
 1023|   118k|			}
 1024|  58.2M|			return m_sidechainHashBlob[offset];
 1025|  58.3M|		}, static_cast<int>(size), result.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1026|  30.1k|	}
 1027|       |
 1028|       |	// Fast path: O(1)
 1029|  30.1k|	if (b) {
 1030|  30.1k|		const int inlen = static_cast<int>(size - N);
 1031|       |
 1032|  30.1k|		memcpy(buf, m_sidechainHashBlob.data() + N, size - N);
 1033|  30.1k|		memcpy(buf + sidechain_extra_nonce_offset - N, sidechain_extra_nonce_buf, EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE);
 1034|       |
 1035|  30.1k|		std::array<uint64_t, 25> st = m_sidechainHashKeccakState;

 1037|       |
 1038|  30.1k|		if (pool_block_debug() && (memcmp(st.data(), result.h, HASH_SIZE) != 0)) {
 1039|      0|			LOGERR(1, "calc_sidechain_hash fast path is broken. Fix the code!");
 1040|      0|		}
 1041|       |
 1042|  30.1k|		memcpy(result.h, st.data(), HASH_SIZE);
 1043|  30.1k|	}
 1044|       |
 1045|  30.1k|	return result;

 1048|       |hash BlockTemplate::calc_miner_tx_hash(uint32_t extra_nonce) const
 1049|  16.6k|{
 1050|       |	// Calculate 3 partial hashes
 1051|  16.6k|	uint8_t hashes[HASH_SIZE * 3];
 1052|       |
 1053|  16.6k|	const uint8_t* data = m_blockTemplateBlob.data() + m_minerTxOffsetInTemplate;
 1054|       |
 1055|  16.6k|	const size_t extra_nonce_offset = m_extraNonceOffsetInTemplate - m_minerTxOffsetInTemplate;
 1056|  16.6k|	const uint8_t extra_nonce_buf[EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE] = {
 1057|  16.6k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(extra_nonce >> 0),
 1058|  16.6k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(extra_nonce >> 8),
 1059|  16.6k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(extra_nonce >> 16),
 1060|  16.6k|		static_cast<uint8_t>(extra_nonce >> 24)
 1061|  16.6k|	};
 1062|       |
 1063|       |	// Calculate sidechain id and merge mining root hash with this extra_nonce
 1064|  16.6k|	hash merge_mining_root;
 1065|  16.6k|	{
 1066|  16.6k|		const hash sidechain_id = calc_sidechain_hash(extra_nonce);
 1067|  16.6k|		const uint32_t n_aux_chains = static_cast<uint32_t>(m_poolBlockTemplate->m_auxChains.size() + 1);
 1068|  16.6k|		const uint32_t aux_slot = get_aux_slot(m_sidechain->consensus_hash(), m_poolBlockTemplate->m_auxNonce, n_aux_chains);
 1069|  16.6k|		merge_mining_root = get_root_from_proof(sidechain_id, m_poolBlockTemplate->m_merkleProof, aux_slot, n_aux_chains);
 1070|  16.6k|	}
 1071|       |
 1072|  16.6k|	const size_t merkle_root_offset = extra_nonce_offset + m_poolBlockTemplate->m_extraNonceSize + 2 + m_poolBlockTemplate->m_merkleTreeDataSize;
 1073|       |
 1074|       |	// 1. Prefix (everything except vin_rct_type byte in the end)
 1075|       |	// Apply extra_nonce in-place because we can't write to the block template here
 1076|  16.6k|	const size_t tx_size = m_minerTxSize - 1;
 1077|       |
 1078|  16.6k|	hash full_hash;
 1079|  16.6k|	uint8_t tx_buf[288];
 1080|       |
 1081|  16.6k|	const size_t N = m_minerTxKeccakStateInputLength;
 1082|  16.6k|	const bool b = N && (N <= extra_nonce_offset) && (N < tx_size) && (tx_size - N <= sizeof(tx_buf));
 1083|       |
 1084|       |	// Slow path: O(N)
 1085|  16.6k|	if (!b || pool_block_debug())
 1086|  16.7k|	{
 1087|  39.0M|		keccak_custom([data, extra_nonce_offset, &extra_nonce_buf, merkle_root_offset, &merge_mining_root](int offset) {
 1088|  39.0M|			uint32_t k = static_cast<uint32_t>(offset - static_cast<int>(extra_nonce_offset));
 1089|  39.0M|			if (k < EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE) {
 1090|       |				// cppcheck-suppress objectIndex
 1091|  65.1k|				return extra_nonce_buf[k];
 1092|  65.1k|			}
 1093|       |
 1094|  39.0M|			k = static_cast<uint32_t>(offset - static_cast<int>(merkle_root_offset));
 1095|  39.0M|			if (k < HASH_SIZE) {
 1096|   517k|				return merge_mining_root.h[k];
 1097|   517k|			}
 1098|       |
 1099|  38.5M|			return data[offset];
 1100|  39.0M|		}, static_cast<int>(tx_size), full_hash.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1101|  16.7k|		memcpy(hashes, full_hash.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1102|  16.7k|	}
 1103|       |
 1104|       |	// Fast path: O(1)
 1105|  16.6k|	if (b) {
 1106|  6.00k|		const int inlen = static_cast<int>(tx_size - N);
 1107|       |
 1108|  6.00k|		memcpy(tx_buf, data + N, inlen);
 1109|  6.00k|		memcpy(tx_buf + extra_nonce_offset - N, extra_nonce_buf, EXTRA_NONCE_SIZE);
 1110|  6.00k|		memcpy(tx_buf + merkle_root_offset - N, merge_mining_root.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1111|       |
 1112|  6.00k|		std::array<uint64_t, 25> st = m_minerTxKeccakState;
 1113|  6.00k|		keccak_finish(tx_buf, inlen, st);
 1114|       |
 1115|  6.00k|		if (pool_block_debug() && (memcmp(st.data(), full_hash.h, HASH_SIZE) != 0)) {
 1116|      0|			LOGERR(1, "calc_miner_tx_hash fast path is broken. Fix the code!");
 1117|      0|		}
 1118|       |
 1119|  6.00k|		memcpy(hashes, st.data(), HASH_SIZE);
 1120|  6.00k|	}
 1121|       |
 1122|       |	// 2. Base RCT, single 0 byte in miner tx
 1123|  16.6k|	static constexpr uint8_t known_second_hash[HASH_SIZE] = {
 1124|  16.6k|		188,54,120,158,122,30,40,20,54,70,66,41,130,143,129,125,102,18,247,180,119,214,101,145,255,150,169,224,100,188,201,138
 1125|  16.6k|	};
 1126|  16.6k|	memcpy(hashes + HASH_SIZE, known_second_hash, HASH_SIZE);
 1127|       |
 1128|       |	// 3. Prunable RCT, empty in miner tx
 1129|  16.6k|	memset(hashes + HASH_SIZE * 2, 0, HASH_SIZE);
 1130|       |
 1131|       |	// Calculate miner transaction hash
 1132|  16.6k|	hash result;
 1133|  16.6k|	keccak(hashes, sizeof(hashes), result.h);
 1134|       |
 1135|  16.6k|	return result;

 1249|       |uint32_t BlockTemplate::get_hashing_blob_nolock(uint32_t extra_nonce, uint8_t* blob) const
 1250|  9.98k|{
 1251|  9.98k|	uint8_t* p = blob;
 1252|       |
 1253|       |	// Block header
 1254|  9.98k|	memcpy(p, m_blockTemplateBlob.data(), m_blockHeaderSize);
 1255|  9.98k|	p += m_blockHeaderSize;
 1256|       |
 1257|       |	// Merkle tree hash

  379|      2|			return std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max();

  382|  1.00k|		uint64_t rem;

  519|  4.87k|		if (weight < tx.weight) return true;

   52|  4.32k|{

  139|       |	bool get_from_bytes(const hash& h, ge_p3& p, ge_cached* Ai)
