  141|  1.41M|		{
  142|  1.41M|			ReadLock lock(from_bytes_lock);
  143|       |
  144|  1.41M|			auto it = from_bytes->find(h);
  145|       |
  146|  1.41M|			if (it != from_bytes->end()) {
  147|  1.40M|				if (!it->second.m_valid) {
  148|  2.15k|					return false;
  149|  2.15k|				}
  150|       |
  151|  1.40M|				if (!Ai || it->second.m_hasAi) {
  152|  1.40M|					p = it->second.m_point;
  153|  1.40M|					if (Ai) {
  154|   616k|						memcpy(Ai, it->second.m_Ai, sizeof(ge_dsmp));
  155|   616k|					}
  156|  1.40M|					return true;
  157|  1.40M|				}
  158|  1.40M|			}
  159|  1.41M|		}
  160|       |
  161|  8.22k|		ge_p3 point = {};
  162|  8.22k|		const bool valid = (ge_frombytes_vartime(&point, h.h) == 0);
  163|       |
  164|  8.22k|		if (valid && Ai) {
  165|  5.33k|			ge_dsm_precomp(Ai, &point);
  166|  5.33k|		}
  167|       |
  168|  8.22k|		const uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(seconds_since_epoch());
  169|  8.22k|		{
  170|  8.22k|			WriteLock lock(from_bytes_lock);
  171|       |
  172|  8.22k|			auto it = from_bytes->emplace(h, FromBytesEntry(valid, point, t, Ai));
  173|       |
  174|  8.22k|			if (valid && Ai && !it.first->second.m_hasAi) {
  175|      0|				it.first->second.m_hasAi = true;
  176|      0|				memcpy(it.first->second.m_Ai, Ai, sizeof(ge_dsmp));
  177|      0|			}
  178|       |
  179|       |			// 2xPPLNS window = 4320 blocks, each block = 1 wallet (2 pubkeys), so ~8640 entries max
  180|       |			// Double it and round it up to have some leeway
  181|  8.22k|			limit_size(from_bytes, 20'000, 10'000);
  182|  8.22k|		}
  183|       |
  184|  8.22k|		if (valid) {
  185|  7.60k|			memcpy(&p, &point, sizeof(p));
  186|  7.60k|		}
  187|       |
  188|  8.22k|		return valid;
  189|  1.41M|	}
  190|       |
  191|       |	bool get_derivation(const hash& key1, const hash& key2, size_t output_index, hash& derivation, uint8_t& view_tag)
  192|  3.69M|	{
  193|  3.69M|		std::array<uint8_t, HASH_SIZE * 2> index;
  194|  3.69M|		memcpy(index.data(), key1.h, HASH_SIZE);
  195|  3.69M|		memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, key2.h, HASH_SIZE);
  196|       |
  197|  3.69M|		derivation = {};
  198|  3.69M|		{
  199|  3.69M|			ReadLock lock(derivations_lock);
  200|  3.69M|			auto it = derivations->find(index);
  201|  3.69M|			if (it != derivations->end()) {
  202|  3.29M|				const DerivationEntry& entry = it->second;
  203|  3.29M|				derivation = entry.m_derivation;
  204|  3.29M|				if (entry.find_view_tag(output_index, view_tag)) {
  205|  3.04M|					return true;
  206|  3.04M|				}
  207|  3.29M|			}
  208|  3.69M|		}
  209|       |
  210|   652k|		if (derivation.empty()) {
  211|   399k|			ge_p3 point;
  212|   399k|			ge_p2 point2;
  213|   399k|			ge_p1p1 point3;
  214|   399k|			ge_dsmp Ai;
  215|       |
  216|   399k|			if (!get_from_bytes(key1, point, Ai)) {
  217|  1.18k|				return false;
  218|  1.18k|			}
  219|       |
  220|   398k|			signed char aslide[256];
  221|   398k|			ge_scalarmult_slide(aslide, key2.h);
  222|       |
  223|   398k|			ge_scalarmult_vartime_precomp(&point2, Ai, aslide);
  224|   398k|			ge_mul8(&point3, &point2);
  225|   398k|			ge_p1p1_to_p2(&point2, &point3);
  226|   398k|			ge_tobytes(reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&derivation), &point2);
  227|   398k|		}
  228|       |
  229|   651k|		derive_view_tag(derivation, output_index, view_tag);
  230|       |
  231|   651k|		const uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(seconds_since_epoch());
  232|   651k|		{
  233|   651k|			WriteLock lock(derivations_lock);
  234|       |
  235|   651k|			auto entry = derivations->emplace(index, DerivationEntry(derivation, t)).first;
  236|   651k|			entry->second.add_view_tag(static_cast<uint32_t>(output_index << 8) | view_tag);
  237|   651k|			limit_size(derivations, 1'000'000, 500'000);
  238|   651k|		}
  239|       |
  240|   651k|		return true;
  241|   652k|	}
  242|       |
  243|       |	// "in" vector  = pairs of (view pub key, output index)
  244|       |	// "out" vector = pairs of (derivation, view tag).
  245|       |	//
  246|       |	// Negative view tag in out[i] means out[i] is invalid (get_derivation would've returned false for in[i])
  247|       |	//
  248|       |	// Returns true if all derivations were computed successfully
  249|       |	bool batch_derivations(const std::vector<std::pair<hash, size_t>>& in, const hash& txkey_sec, std::vector<std::pair<hash, int32_t>>& out)
  250|  20.7k|	{
  251|  20.7k|		std::atomic<bool> result = true;
  252|       |
  253|  20.7k|		out.clear();
  254|  20.7k|		out.reserve(in.size());
  255|       |
  256|  20.7k|		std::array<uint8_t, HASH_SIZE * 2> index;
  257|       |
  258|       |		// First read all already cached data and save the indices we will need to fill in
  259|  20.7k|		std::vector<size_t> derivation_batch;
  260|  20.7k|		std::vector<size_t> view_tag_batch;
  261|  20.7k|		{
  262|  20.7k|			ReadLock lock(derivations_lock);
  263|       |
  264|   374k|			for (const std::pair<hash, size_t>& k : in) {
  265|   374k|				memcpy(index.data(), k.first.h, HASH_SIZE);
  266|   374k|				memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, txkey_sec.h, HASH_SIZE);
  267|       |
  268|   374k|				auto it = derivations->find(index);
  269|       |
  270|   374k|				if (it == derivations->end()) {
  271|   223k|					derivation_batch.reserve(in.size());
  272|   223k|					derivation_batch.emplace_back(out.size());
  273|       |
  274|   223k|					view_tag_batch.reserve(in.size());
  275|   223k|					view_tag_batch.emplace_back(out.size());
  276|       |
  277|   223k|					out.emplace_back(hash(), 0);
  278|   223k|				}
  279|   150k|				else {
  280|   150k|					const DerivationEntry& entry = it->second;
  281|   150k|					uint8_t view_tag;
  282|       |
  283|   150k|					if (entry.find_view_tag(k.second, view_tag)) {
  284|  7.99k|						out.emplace_back(entry.m_derivation, view_tag);
  285|  7.99k|					}
  286|   142k|					else {
  287|   142k|						view_tag_batch.reserve(in.size());
  288|   142k|						view_tag_batch.emplace_back(out.size());
  289|   142k|						out.emplace_back(entry.m_derivation, 0);
  290|   142k|					}
  291|   150k|				}
  292|   374k|			}
  293|  20.7k|		}
  294|       |
  295|       |		// Then fill in the gaps in parallel
  296|  20.7k|		if (!derivation_batch.empty()) {
  297|  1.76k|			const size_t N = derivation_batch.size();
  298|       |
  299|  1.76k|			struct M {
  300|  1.76k|				ge_p2 p; // the original point p
  301|  1.76k|				fe P;    // partial products of p.Z (segmented, P_i = Z_a*Z_{a+1}*...*Z_i for a <= i < b)
  302|  1.76k|				fe Q;    // inverses of p.Z (Q_i = Z_i^-1 for 0 <= i < N). Calculated in segments.
  303|  1.76k|			};
  304|       |
  305|       |			// N*200 bytes for the scratchpad
  306|  1.76k|			std::vector<M> scratchpad(N);
  307|       |
  308|  1.76k|			std::atomic<uint32_t> counter = 0;
  309|       |
  310|  1.76k|			signed char aslide[256];
  311|  1.76k|			ge_scalarmult_slide(aslide, txkey_sec.h);
  312|       |
  313|       |			// Montgomery's trick to batch invert all Z values with a single fe_invert call (parallel version)
  314|  7.05k|			parallel_run([&](uint32_t thread_index, uint32_t total_thread_count) {
  315|       |				// Always have at least 1 element per thread
  316|  7.05k|				const uint32_t thread_count = static_cast<uint32_t>(std::min<size_t>(total_thread_count, N));
  317|       |
  318|  7.05k|				if (thread_index >= thread_count) {
  319|  1.41k|					return;
  320|  1.41k|				}
  321|       |
  322|       |				// 0 <= thread_index < thread_count <= N at this point, so
  323|       |				// 0 <= a < b <= N (non-empty segments with valid bounds) is guaranteed
  324|  5.64k|				const size_t a = (N * thread_index) / thread_count;
  325|  5.64k|				const size_t b = (N * (thread_index + 1)) / thread_count;
  326|       |
  327|  5.64k|				uint32_t next_counter = thread_count;
  328|       |
  329|   229k|				for (size_t i = a; i < b; ++i) {
  330|   223k|					ge_p3 point;
  331|   223k|					ge_p2 point2;
  332|   223k|					ge_p1p1 point3;
  333|   223k|					ge_dsmp Ai;
  334|       |
  335|   223k|					if (!get_from_bytes(in[derivation_batch[i]].first, point, Ai)) {
  336|    466|						out[derivation_batch[i]].second = -1;
  337|    466|						result = false;
  338|       |
  339|    466|						fe_1(point2.X);
  340|    466|						fe_1(point2.Y);
  341|    466|						fe_1(point2.Z);
  342|    466|					}
  343|   222k|					else {
  344|   222k|						ge_scalarmult_vartime_precomp(&point2, Ai, aslide);
  345|   222k|						ge_mul8(&point3, &point2);
  346|   222k|						ge_p1p1_to_p2(&point2, &point3);
  347|   222k|					}
  348|       |
  349|   223k|					memcpy(&scratchpad[i].p, &point2, sizeof(ge_p2));
  350|       |
  351|   223k|					if (i == a) {
  352|  5.63k|						memcpy(&scratchpad[i].P, point2.Z, sizeof(fe));
  353|  5.63k|					}
  354|   217k|					else {
  355|   217k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[i].P, scratchpad[i - 1].P, point2.Z);
  356|   217k|					}
  357|   223k|				}
  358|       |
  359|  5.64k|				const bool last = sync_point(counter, next_counter);
  360|  5.64k|				next_counter += thread_count;
  361|       |
  362|       |				// Last thread at the sync point is likely the first one to continue execution,
  363|       |				// so make it calculate each segment end's inverse using Montgomery's trick
  364|  5.64k|				if (last) {
  365|       |					// Work over the whole range 0...N-1, but inverse only each segment's end
  366|       |					// One fe_invert, thread_count*3 - 3 fe_mul calls
  367|       |
  368|       |					// Calculate partial products of segment ends
  369|  1.76k|					size_t k = N * (0 + 1) / thread_count - 1;
  370|  1.76k|					memcpy(scratchpad[k].Q, scratchpad[k].P, sizeof(fe));
  371|       |
  372|  5.65k|					for (uint32_t i = 1; i < thread_count; ++i) {
  373|  3.89k|						const size_t next_k = N * (i + 1) / thread_count - 1;
  374|  3.89k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[next_k].Q, scratchpad[k].Q, scratchpad[next_k].P);
  375|  3.89k|						k = next_k;
  376|  3.89k|					}
  377|       |
  378|       |					// Invert the product of all segment ends. k == N - 1 here (because see how the loop above exits).
  379|  1.76k|					fe t;
  380|  1.76k|					fe_invert(t, scratchpad[k].Q);
  381|       |
  382|       |					// Walk back to calculate inverses of segment ends
  383|  5.65k|					for (uint32_t i = thread_count - 1; i > 0; --i) {
  384|  3.89k|						const size_t prev_k = N * i / thread_count - 1;
  385|       |
  386|  3.89k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[k].Q, t, scratchpad[prev_k].Q);
  387|  3.89k|						fe_mul(t, t, scratchpad[k].P);
  388|       |
  389|  3.89k|						k = prev_k;
  390|  3.89k|					}
  391|       |
  392|       |					// k is now the end index of the first segment (because see how the loop above exits).
  393|  1.76k|					memcpy(scratchpad[k].Q, t, sizeof(fe));
  394|  1.76k|				}
  395|       |
  396|  5.64k|				sync_point(counter, next_counter);
  397|       |
  398|       |				// Each segment has scratchpad[b - 1].Q = (Z_a*Z_{a+1}*...*Z_{b-1})^-1 now
  399|  5.64k|				fe t;
  400|  5.64k|				memcpy(t, scratchpad[b - 1].Q, sizeof(fe));
  401|       |
  402|   217k|				for (size_t i = b - 1; i > a; --i) {
  403|   212k|					fe_mul(scratchpad[i].Q, t, scratchpad[i - 1].P);
  404|   212k|					fe_mul(t, t, scratchpad[i].p.Z);
  405|   212k|				}
  406|       |
  407|  5.64k|				memcpy(scratchpad[a].Q, t, sizeof(fe));
  408|       |
  409|       |				// Last step - replicate ge_tobytes() code for each segment
  410|   222k|				for (size_t i = a; i < b; ++i) {
  411|   217k|					const fe& r = scratchpad[i].Q;
  412|       |
  413|   217k|					fe x, y;
  414|   217k|					fe_mul(x, scratchpad[i].p.X, r);
  415|   217k|					fe_mul(y, scratchpad[i].p.Y, r);
  416|       |
  417|   217k|					unsigned char* s = out[derivation_batch[i]].first.h;
  418|   217k|					fe_tobytes(s, y);
  419|   217k|					s[31] ^= fe_isnegative(x) << 7;
  420|   217k|				}
  421|  5.64k|			}, true);
  422|  1.76k|		}
  423|       |
  424|  20.7k|		if (!view_tag_batch.empty()) {
  425|  2.25k|			const size_t N = view_tag_batch.size();
  426|       |
  427|  9.01k|			parallel_run([N, &in, &out, &view_tag_batch](uint32_t thread_index, uint32_t total_thread_count) {
  428|  9.01k|				const size_t a = (N * thread_index) / total_thread_count;
  429|  9.01k|				const size_t b = (N * (thread_index + 1)) / total_thread_count;
  430|       |
  431|   366k|				for (size_t i = a; i < b; ++i) {
  432|   357k|					std::pair<hash, int32_t>& t = out[view_tag_batch[i]];
  433|       |
  434|       |					// Skip entries with invalid derivations
  435|   357k|					if (t.second < 0) {
  436|    440|						continue;
  437|    440|					}
  438|       |
  439|   357k|					uint8_t view_tag;
  440|   357k|					derive_view_tag(t.first, in[view_tag_batch[i]].second, view_tag);
  441|   357k|					t.second = view_tag;
  442|   357k|				}
  443|  9.01k|			}, true);
  444|  2.25k|		}
  445|       |
  446|       |		// Finally fill in the cache with all new values
  447|       |
  448|       |		// When debugging, don't pollute the cache with the values calculated here.
  449|       |		// Instead, compare them with get_derivation() output.
  450|       |		// get_derivation will fill the cache the normal way.
  451|       |#ifndef P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  452|       |		const uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(seconds_since_epoch());
  453|       |
  454|       |		if (!derivation_batch.empty() || !view_tag_batch.empty()) {
  455|       |			WriteLock lock(derivations_lock);
  456|       |
  457|       |			for (size_t i = 0, n = derivation_batch.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  458|       |				const size_t j = derivation_batch[i];
  459|       |
  460|       |				// Skip entries with invalid derivations
  461|       |				if (out[j].second < 0) {
  462|       |					continue;
  463|       |				}
  464|       |
  465|       |				memcpy(index.data(), in[j].first.h, HASH_SIZE);
  466|       |				memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, txkey_sec.h, HASH_SIZE);
  467|       |
  468|       |				derivations->emplace(index, DerivationEntry(out[j].first, t));
  469|       |			}
  470|       |
  471|       |			if (!derivation_batch.empty()) {
  472|       |				limit_size(derivations, 1'000'000, 500'000);
  473|       |			}
  474|       |
  475|       |			for (size_t i = 0, n = view_tag_batch.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  476|       |				const size_t j = view_tag_batch[i];
  477|       |
  478|       |				// Skip entries with invalid derivations
  479|       |				if (out[j].second < 0) {
  480|       |					continue;
  481|       |				}
  482|       |
  483|       |				memcpy(index.data(), in[j].first.h, HASH_SIZE);
  484|       |				memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, txkey_sec.h, HASH_SIZE);
  485|       |
  486|       |				auto it = derivations->find(index);
  487|       |				if (it != derivations->end()) {
  488|       |					it->second.add_view_tag(static_cast<uint32_t>(in[j].second << 8) | static_cast<uint8_t>(out[j].second));
  489|       |				}
  490|       |			}
  491|       |		}
  492|       |#else // P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  493|  20.7k|		if (out.size() != in.size()) {
  494|      0|			LOGERR(1, "batch_derivations error: size mismatch: expected " << in.size() << ", produced " << out.size() << " elements");
  495|      0|			PANIC_STOP();
  496|      0|		}
  497|  20.7k|		else {
  498|   395k|			for (size_t i = 0, n = in.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  499|   374k|				hash derivation;
  500|   374k|				uint8_t view_tag;
  501|       |
  502|   374k|				if (get_derivation(in[i].first, txkey_sec, in[i].second, derivation, view_tag)) {
  503|   373k|					if (derivation != out[i].first) {
  504|      0|						LOGERR(1, "batch_derivations error: wrong derivation at position " << i << '/' << n);
  505|      0|						PANIC_STOP();
  506|      0|					}
  507|       |
  508|   373k|					if (view_tag != out[i].second) {
  509|      0|						LOGERR(1, "batch_derivations error: wrong view tag at position " << i << '/' << n << " (should be " << static_cast<int32_t>(view_tag) << " instead of " << out[i].second << ')');
  510|      0|						PANIC_STOP();
  511|      0|					}
  512|   373k|				}
  513|    466|				else {
  514|    466|					if (out[i].second != -1) {
  515|      0|						LOGERR(1, "batch_derivations error: wrong view tag at position " << i << '/' << n << " (should be -1 instead of " << out[i].second << ')');
  516|      0|						PANIC_STOP();
  517|      0|					}
  518|    466|				}
  519|   374k|			}
  520|  20.7k|		}
  521|  20.7k|#endif // P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  522|       |
  523|  20.7k|		return result;
  524|  20.7k|	}
  525|       |
  526|       |	bool get_public_key(const hash& derivation, size_t output_index, const hash& base, hash& derived_key)
  527|  3.48M|	{
  528|  3.48M|		std::array<uint8_t, HASH_SIZE * 2 + sizeof(size_t)> index;
  529|  3.48M|		memcpy(index.data(), derivation.h, HASH_SIZE);
  530|  3.48M|		memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, base.h, HASH_SIZE);
  531|  3.48M|		memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE * 2, &output_index, sizeof(size_t));
  532|       |
  533|  3.48M|		{
  534|  3.48M|			ReadLock lock(public_keys_lock);
  535|  3.48M|			auto it = public_keys->find(index);
  536|  3.48M|			if (it != public_keys->end()) {
  537|  2.86M|				derived_key = it->second.m_key;
  538|  2.86M|				return true;
  539|  2.86M|			}
  540|  3.48M|		}
  541|       |
  542|   621k|		uint8_t scalar[HASH_SIZE];
  543|   621k|		ge_p3 point1;
  544|   621k|		ge_p3 point2;
  545|   621k|		ge_cached point3;
  546|   621k|		ge_p1p1 point4;
  547|   621k|		ge_p2 point5;
  548|       |
  549|   621k|		if (!get_from_bytes(base, point1, nullptr)) {
  550|    996|			return false;
  551|    996|		}
  552|       |
  553|   620k|		derivation_to_scalar(derivation, output_index, scalar);
  554|   620k|		ge_scalarmult_base_vartime(&point2, reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&scalar));
  555|   620k|		ge_p3_to_cached(&point3, &point2);
  556|   620k|		ge_add(&point4, &point1, &point3);
  557|   620k|		ge_p1p1_to_p2(&point5, &point4);
  558|   620k|		ge_tobytes(derived_key.h, &point5);
  559|       |
  560|   620k|		const uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(seconds_since_epoch());
  561|   620k|		{
  562|   620k|			WriteLock lock(public_keys_lock);
  563|   620k|			public_keys->emplace(index, PublicKeyEntry{ static_cast<indexed_hash>(derived_key), t });
  564|   620k|			limit_size(public_keys, 1'000'000, 500'000);
  565|   620k|		}
  566|       |
  567|   620k|		return true;
  568|   621k|	}
  569|       |
  570|       |	// "in" vector  = tuples of (derivation, output index, public spend key)
  571|       |	// "out" vector = pairs of (derived ephemeral public key, valid)
  572|       |	//
  573|       |	// out[i].second == false means out[i] is invalid (get_public_key would've returned false for in[i])
  574|       |	//
  575|       |	// Returns true if all ephemeral public keys were computed successfully
  576|       |	bool batch_public_keys(const std::vector<batch_public_key_input>& in, std::vector<std::pair<hash, bool>>& out)
  577|  20.7k|	{
  578|  20.7k|		std::atomic<bool> result = true;
  579|       |
  580|  20.7k|		out.clear();
  581|  20.7k|		out.reserve(in.size());
  582|       |
  583|  20.7k|		std::array<uint8_t, HASH_SIZE * 2 + sizeof(size_t)> index;
  584|       |
  585|       |		// First read all already cached data and save the indices we will need to fill in
  586|  20.7k|		std::vector<size_t> batch;
  587|  20.7k|		{
  588|  20.7k|			ReadLock lock(public_keys_lock);
  589|       |
  590|   374k|			for (const batch_public_key_input& k : in) {
  591|   374k|				memcpy(index.data(), k.derivation.h, HASH_SIZE);
  592|   374k|				memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, k.base.h, HASH_SIZE);
  593|   374k|				memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE * 2, &k.output_index, sizeof(size_t));
  594|       |
  595|   374k|				auto it = public_keys->find(index);
  596|       |
  597|   374k|				if (it == public_keys->end()) {
  598|   172k|					batch.reserve(in.size());
  599|   172k|					batch.emplace_back(out.size());
  600|       |
  601|   172k|					out.emplace_back(hash(), false);
  602|   172k|				}
  603|   201k|				else {
  604|   201k|					out.emplace_back(it->second.m_key, true);
  605|   201k|				}
  606|   374k|			}
  607|  20.7k|		}
  608|       |
  609|  20.7k|		if (!batch.empty())
  610|  2.24k|		{
  611|  2.24k|			const size_t N = batch.size();
  612|       |
  613|  2.24k|			struct M {
  614|  2.24k|				ge_p2 p; // the original point p
  615|  2.24k|				fe P;    // partial products of p.Z (segmented, P_i = Z_a*Z_{a+1}*...*Z_i for a <= i < b)
  616|  2.24k|				fe Q;    // inverses of p.Z (Q_i = Z_i^-1 for 0 <= i < N). Calculated in segments.
  617|  2.24k|			};
  618|       |
  619|       |			// N*200 bytes for the scratchpad
  620|  2.24k|			std::vector<M> scratchpad(N);
  621|       |
  622|  2.24k|			std::atomic<uint32_t> counter = 0;
  623|       |
  624|       |			// Montgomery's trick to batch invert all Z values with a single fe_invert call (parallel version)
  625|  8.91k|			parallel_run([&](uint32_t thread_index, uint32_t total_thread_count) {
  626|       |				// Always have at least 1 element per thread
  627|  8.91k|				const uint32_t thread_count = static_cast<uint32_t>(std::min<size_t>(total_thread_count, N));
  628|       |
  629|  8.91k|				if (thread_index >= thread_count) {
  630|  3.00k|					return;
  631|  3.00k|				}
  632|       |
  633|       |				// 0 <= thread_index < thread_count <= N at this point, so
  634|       |				// 0 <= a < b <= N (non-empty segments with valid bounds) is guaranteed
  635|  5.91k|				const size_t a = (N * thread_index) / thread_count;
  636|  5.91k|				const size_t b = (N * (thread_index + 1)) / thread_count;
  637|       |
  638|  5.91k|				uint32_t next_counter = thread_count;
  639|       |
  640|   178k|				for (size_t i = a; i < b; ++i) {
  641|   172k|					const batch_public_key_input& t = in[batch[i]];
  642|       |
  643|   172k|					ge_p3 point1;
  644|   172k|					ge_p2 point5;
  645|       |
  646|   172k|					if (!get_from_bytes(t.base, point1, nullptr)) {
  647|    466|						result = false;
  648|    466|						out[batch[i]].second = false;
  649|       |
  650|    466|						fe_1(point5.X);
  651|    466|						fe_1(point5.Y);
  652|    466|						fe_1(point5.Z);
  653|    466|					}
  654|   172k|					else {
  655|   172k|						out[batch[i]].second = true;
  656|       |
  657|   172k|						uint8_t scalar[HASH_SIZE];
  658|   172k|						ge_p3 point2;
  659|   172k|						ge_cached point3;
  660|   172k|						ge_p1p1 point4;
  661|       |
  662|   172k|						derivation_to_scalar(t.derivation, t.output_index, scalar);
  663|   172k|						ge_scalarmult_base_vartime(&point2, reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&scalar));
  664|   172k|						ge_p3_to_cached(&point3, &point2);
  665|   172k|						ge_add(&point4, &point1, &point3);
  666|   172k|						ge_p1p1_to_p2(&point5, &point4);
  667|   172k|					}
  668|       |
  669|   172k|					memcpy(&scratchpad[i].p, &point5, sizeof(ge_p2));
  670|       |
  671|   172k|					if (i == a) {
  672|  5.91k|						memcpy(&scratchpad[i].P, point5.Z, sizeof(fe));
  673|  5.91k|					}
  674|   166k|					else {
  675|   166k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[i].P, scratchpad[i - 1].P, point5.Z);
  676|   166k|					}
  677|   172k|				}
  678|       |
  679|  5.91k|				const bool last = sync_point(counter, next_counter);
  680|  5.91k|				next_counter += thread_count;
  681|       |
  682|       |				// Last thread at the sync point is likely the first one to continue execution,
  683|       |				// so make it calculate each segment end's inverse using Montgomery's trick
  684|  5.91k|				if (last) {
  685|       |					// Work over the whole range 0...N-1, but inverse only each segment's end
  686|       |					// One fe_invert, thread_count*3 - 3 fe_mul calls
  687|       |
  688|       |					// Calculate partial products of segment ends
  689|  2.24k|					size_t k = N * (0 + 1) / thread_count - 1;
  690|  2.24k|					memcpy(scratchpad[k].Q, scratchpad[k].P, sizeof(fe));
  691|       |
  692|  5.95k|					for (uint32_t i = 1; i < thread_count; ++i) {
  693|  3.71k|						const size_t next_k = N * (i + 1) / thread_count - 1;
  694|  3.71k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[next_k].Q, scratchpad[k].Q, scratchpad[next_k].P);
  695|  3.71k|						k = next_k;
  696|  3.71k|					}
  697|       |
  698|       |					// Invert the product of all segment ends. k == N - 1 here (because see how the loop above exits).
  699|  2.24k|					fe t;
  700|  2.24k|					fe_invert(t, scratchpad[k].Q);
  701|       |
  702|       |					// Walk back to calculate inverses of segment ends
  703|  5.95k|					for (uint32_t i = thread_count - 1; i > 0; --i) {
  704|  3.71k|						const size_t prev_k = N * i / thread_count - 1;
  705|       |
  706|  3.71k|						fe_mul(scratchpad[k].Q, t, scratchpad[prev_k].Q);
  707|  3.71k|						fe_mul(t, t, scratchpad[k].P);
  708|       |
  709|  3.71k|						k = prev_k;
  710|  3.71k|					}
  711|       |
  712|       |					// k is now the end index of the first segment (because see how the loop above exits).
  713|  2.24k|					memcpy(scratchpad[k].Q, t, sizeof(fe));
  714|  2.24k|				}
  715|       |
  716|  5.91k|				sync_point(counter, next_counter);
  717|       |
  718|       |				// Each segment has scratchpad[b - 1].Q = (Z_a*Z_{a+1}*...*Z_{b-1})^-1 now
  719|  5.91k|				fe t;
  720|  5.91k|				memcpy(t, scratchpad[b - 1].Q, sizeof(fe));
  721|       |
  722|   167k|				for (size_t i = b - 1; i > a; --i) {
  723|   161k|					fe_mul(scratchpad[i].Q, t, scratchpad[i - 1].P);
  724|   161k|					fe_mul(t, t, scratchpad[i].p.Z);
  725|   161k|				}
  726|       |
  727|  5.91k|				memcpy(scratchpad[a].Q, t, sizeof(fe));
  728|       |
  729|       |				// Last step - replicate ge_tobytes() code for each segment
  730|   172k|				for (size_t i = a; i < b; ++i) {
  731|   166k|					const fe& r = scratchpad[i].Q;
  732|       |
  733|   166k|					fe x, y;
  734|   166k|					fe_mul(x, scratchpad[i].p.X, r);
  735|   166k|					fe_mul(y, scratchpad[i].p.Y, r);
  736|       |
  737|   166k|					unsigned char* s = out[batch[i]].first.h;
  738|   166k|					fe_tobytes(s, y);
  739|   166k|					s[31] ^= fe_isnegative(x) << 7;
  740|   166k|				}
  741|  5.91k|			}, true);
  742|       |
  743|       |			// Finally fill in the cache with all new values
  744|       |
  745|       |			// When debugging, don't pollute the cache with the values calculated here.
  746|       |			// Instead, compare them with get_derivation() output.
  747|       |			// get_derivation will fill the cache the normal way.
  748|       |#ifndef P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  749|       |			const uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(seconds_since_epoch());
  750|       |			{
  751|       |				WriteLock lock(public_keys_lock);
  752|       |
  753|       |				for (size_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
  754|       |					if (out[batch[i]].second) {
  755|       |						const batch_public_key_input& k = in[batch[i]];
  756|       |
  757|       |						memcpy(index.data(), k.derivation.h, HASH_SIZE);
  758|       |						memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE, k.base.h, HASH_SIZE);
  759|       |						memcpy(index.data() + HASH_SIZE * 2, &k.output_index, sizeof(size_t));
  760|       |
  761|       |						public_keys->emplace(index, PublicKeyEntry{ static_cast<indexed_hash>(out[batch[i]].first), t });
  762|       |					}
  763|       |				}
  764|       |
  765|       |				limit_size(public_keys, 1'000'000, 500'000);
  766|       |			}
  767|       |#else // P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  768|   174k|			for (size_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
  769|   172k|				const batch_public_key_input& k = in[batch[i]];
  770|       |
  771|   172k|				hash derived_key;
  772|   172k|				const bool b = get_public_key(k.derivation, k.output_index, k.base, derived_key);
  773|       |
  774|   172k|				if (b != out[batch[i]].second) {
  775|      0|					LOGERR(1, "batch_public_keys error: result mismatch: expected " << b << ", got " << out[batch[i]].second);
  776|      0|					PANIC_STOP();
  777|      0|				}
  778|       |
  779|   172k|				if (b && (derived_key != out[batch[i]].first)) {
  780|      0|					LOGERR(1, "batch_public_keys error: wrong derived key at position " << i << '/' << N);
  781|      0|					PANIC_STOP();
  782|      0|				}
  783|   172k|			}
  784|  2.24k|#endif // P2POOL_DEBUGGING
  785|  2.24k|		}
  786|       |
  787|  20.7k|		return result;
  788|  20.7k|	}

  850|       |	// If the table exceeded max_size, deletes oldest entries to shrink the table to <= max_new_size entries
  851|       |	template<typename T>
  852|       |	static void limit_size(T* table, size_t max_size, size_t max_new_size)
  853|  1.28M|	{

  855|  1.28M|			return;

  862|      0|			++ages[now - data.second.m_timestamp];
  863|      0|		}
  864|       |
  865|      0|		size_t k = 0;

  871|      0|				clean_old(table, now - it->first, k - max_new_size);

  877|       |	void clear(uint64_t timestamp)
  878|     14|	{
  879|     14|		if (timestamp) {

  884|      2|			}

  888|      2|			}

  890|      2|				WriteLock lock(tx_keys_lock);
  891|      2|				clean_old(tx_keys, t);
  892|      2|			}

  894|       |			// limit_size is what limits from_bytes instead
  895|      2|			return;
  896|      2|		}

  898|     12|		{
  899|     12|			WriteLock lock(derivations_lock);

  903|     12|		}

   49|       |std::string to_i2p_b32(const hash& dest_hash)
   50|      1|{
   51|      1|	uint8_t buf[HASH_SIZE + 1];
   52|      1|	memcpy(buf, dest_hash.h, HASH_SIZE);
   53|      1|	buf[HASH_SIZE] = 0;
   54|       |
   55|      1|	std::string result;
   56|      1|	result.reserve(60);
   57|       |
   58|      1|	uint64_t data = 0;
   59|      1|	uint64_t bit_size = 0;
   60|       |
   61|     34|	for (size_t i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE + 1; ++i) {
   62|     33|		data = (data << 8) | buf[i];
   63|     33|		bit_size += 8;
   64|       |
   65|     85|		while (bit_size >= 5) {
   66|     52|			bit_size -= 5;
   67|     52|			result += base32_alphabet[(data >> bit_size) & 31];
   68|     52|		}
   69|     33|	}
   70|       |
   71|      1|	result.append(".b32.i2p");
   72|       |
   73|      1|	return result;

   26|       |static constexpr uint32_t BUCKET_COUNT = 1U << indexed_hash::BUCKET_BITS;
   27|       |static constexpr uint32_t INDEX_MASK = (1U << indexed_hash::BUCKET_SHIFT) - 1U;
   28|       |
   29|       |// Stores hashes only
   30|       |static std::vector<hash> storage1[BUCKET_COUNT];
   31|       |
   32|       |// Stores first 4 bytes of each hash (for better cache locality during search) and reference counters
   33|       |static std::vector<std::pair<uint32_t, uint32_t>> storage2[BUCKET_COUNT];
   34|       |
   35|       |static constexpr uint32_t LOCK_COUNT = 64;
   36|       |
   37|       |static ReadWriteLock locks[LOCK_COUNT];
   38|       |
   39|       |static FORCEINLINE void decref(uint32_t index)
   40|  30.4M|{
   41|  30.4M|	if (index == std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()) {
   42|  14.3M|		return;
   43|  14.3M|	}
   44|       |
   45|  16.0M|	const uint32_t bucket = index >> indexed_hash::BUCKET_SHIFT;
   46|  16.0M|	index &= INDEX_MASK;
   47|       |
   48|  16.0M|	auto& d2 = storage2[bucket];
   49|       |
   50|  16.0M|	WriteLock lock(locks[bucket % LOCK_COUNT]);
   51|       |
   52|  16.0M|	auto& p2 = d2[index];
   53|       |
   54|  16.0M|#ifdef P2POOL_DEBUGGING
   55|  16.0M|	if (p2.second == 0) {
   56|      0|		LOGERR(0, "fatal error: reference counter is 0 when it shouldn't be 0");
   57|      0|		PANIC_STOP();
   58|      0|	}
   59|  16.0M|#endif
   60|       |
   61|  16.0M|	--p2.second;
   62|  16.0M|}

   64|       |static FORCEINLINE void incref(uint32_t index)
   65|  7.78M|{
   66|  7.78M|	if (index == std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()) {
   67|  33.5k|		return;
   68|  33.5k|	}
   69|       |
   70|  7.75M|	const uint32_t bucket = index >> indexed_hash::BUCKET_SHIFT;

   74|       |

   80|  8.37M|{
   81|  8.37M|	if (h.empty()) {
   82|  40.2k|		m_index = std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max();
   83|  40.2k|		return;
   84|  40.2k|	}

   89|  8.33M|	auto& d2 = storage2[bucket];
   90|       |

   92|  8.33M|	uint32_t first_free_slot = std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max();
   93|       |
   94|  8.33M|	WriteLock lock(locks[bucket % LOCK_COUNT]);
   95|       |
   96|  8.33M|	const uint32_t n = static_cast<uint32_t>(d1.size());

  103|  7.71M|			++p2.second;
  104|  7.71M|			return;
  105|  7.71M|		}
  106|       |

  108|   302k|			first_free_slot = i;
  109|   302k|		}
  110|   623M|	}
  111|       |
  112|   619k|	if (first_free_slot != std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()) {
  113|   173k|		m_index = (bucket << BUCKET_SHIFT) | first_free_slot;
  114|       |
  115|   173k|		d1[first_free_slot] = h;

  121|   445k|	else {

  123|       |
  124|   445k|		if ((n > INDEX_MASK) || (index == std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max())) {
  125|      0|			LOGERR(0, "fatal error: storage overflow");
  126|      0|			PANIC_STOP();
  127|      0|		}

   66|       |template<typename T, typename U>
   67|       |struct parse_wrapper<T, std::optional<U>>
   68|       |{
   69|       |	static NOINLINE bool parse(T& v, const char* name, std::optional<U>& out_value)
   70|       |	{
   71|       |		U tmp{};
   72|       |
   73|       |		if (parseValue(v, name, tmp)) {
   74|       |			out_value = std::move(tmp);
   75|       |			return true;
   76|       |		}
   77|       |
   78|       |		// Optional value is always parsed successfully, it will just be "N/A" if the actual parsing failed
   79|       |		out_value.reset();
   80|       |		return true;
   81|       |	}
   82|       |};

   22|       |namespace p2pool {
   23|       |
   24|       |#ifndef ROTL64
   25|   706M|#define ROTL64(x, y) (((x) << (y)) | ((x) >> (64 - (y))))
   26|       |#endif
   27|       |
   28|       |const uint64_t keccakf_rndc[24] = 
   29|       |{
   30|       |	0x0000000000000001, 0x0000000000008082, 0x800000000000808a,
   31|       |	0x8000000080008000, 0x000000000000808b, 0x0000000080000001,
   32|       |	0x8000000080008081, 0x8000000000008009, 0x000000000000008a,
   33|       |	0x0000000000000088, 0x0000000080008009, 0x000000008000000a,

   39|       |
   40|       |NOINLINE void keccakf_plain(std::array<uint64_t, 25>& st)
   41|  1.01M|{
   42|  25.3M|	for (int round = 0; round < KeccakParams::ROUNDS; ++round) {
   43|  24.3M|		uint64_t bc[5];
   44|       |
   45|       |		// Theta
   46|  24.3M|		bc[0] = st[0] ^ st[5] ^ st[10] ^ st[15] ^ st[20];
   47|  24.3M|		bc[1] = st[1] ^ st[6] ^ st[11] ^ st[16] ^ st[21];
   48|  24.3M|		bc[2] = st[2] ^ st[7] ^ st[12] ^ st[17] ^ st[22];
   49|  24.3M|		bc[3] = st[3] ^ st[8] ^ st[13] ^ st[18] ^ st[23];
   50|  24.3M|		bc[4] = st[4] ^ st[9] ^ st[14] ^ st[19] ^ st[24];
   51|       |
   52|   121M|#define THETA(i) { \
   53|   121M|			const uint64_t t = bc[((i) + 4) % 5] ^ ROTL64(bc[((i) + 1) % 5], 1); \
   54|   121M|			st[(i) +  0 ] ^= t; \
   55|   121M|			st[(i) +  5] ^= t; \
   56|   121M|			st[(i) + 10] ^= t; \
   57|   121M|			st[(i) + 15] ^= t; \
   58|   121M|			st[(i) + 20] ^= t; \
   59|   121M|		}
   60|       |
   61|  24.3M|		THETA(0);
   62|  24.3M|		THETA(1);
   63|  24.3M|		THETA(2);
   64|  24.3M|		THETA(3);
   65|  24.3M|		THETA(4);
   66|       |
   67|       |		// Rho Pi
   68|  24.3M|		const uint64_t t = st[1];
   69|  24.3M|		st[ 1] = ROTL64(st[ 6], 44);
   70|  24.3M|		st[ 6] = ROTL64(st[ 9], 20);
   71|  24.3M|		st[ 9] = ROTL64(st[22], 61);
   72|  24.3M|		st[22] = ROTL64(st[14], 39);
   73|  24.3M|		st[14] = ROTL64(st[20], 18);
   74|  24.3M|		st[20] = ROTL64(st[ 2], 62);
   75|  24.3M|		st[ 2] = ROTL64(st[12], 43);
   76|  24.3M|		st[12] = ROTL64(st[13], 25);
   77|  24.3M|		st[13] = ROTL64(st[19],  8);
   78|  24.3M|		st[19] = ROTL64(st[23], 56);
   79|  24.3M|		st[23] = ROTL64(st[15], 41);
   80|  24.3M|		st[15] = ROTL64(st[ 4], 27);
   81|  24.3M|		st[ 4] = ROTL64(st[24], 14);
   82|  24.3M|		st[24] = ROTL64(st[21],  2);
   83|  24.3M|		st[21] = ROTL64(st[ 8], 55);
   84|  24.3M|		st[ 8] = ROTL64(st[16], 45);
   85|  24.3M|		st[16] = ROTL64(st[ 5], 36);
   86|  24.3M|		st[ 5] = ROTL64(st[ 3], 28);
   87|  24.3M|		st[ 3] = ROTL64(st[18], 21);
   88|  24.3M|		st[18] = ROTL64(st[17], 15);
   89|  24.3M|		st[17] = ROTL64(st[11], 10);
   90|  24.3M|		st[11] = ROTL64(st[ 7],  6);
   91|  24.3M|		st[ 7] = ROTL64(st[10],  3);
   92|  24.3M|		st[10] = ROTL64(t, 1);
   93|       |
   94|       |		//  Chi
   95|   121M|#define CHI(j) { \
   96|   121M|			const uint64_t st0 = st[(j)    ]; \
   97|   121M|			const uint64_t st1 = st[(j) + 1]; \
   98|   121M|			const uint64_t st2 = st[(j) + 2]; \
   99|   121M|			const uint64_t st3 = st[(j) + 3]; \
  100|   121M|			const uint64_t st4 = st[(j) + 4]; \
  101|   121M|			st[(j)    ] ^= ~st1 & st2; \
  102|   121M|			st[(j) + 1] ^= ~st2 & st3; \
  103|   121M|			st[(j) + 2] ^= ~st3 & st4; \
  104|   121M|			st[(j) + 3] ^= ~st4 & st0; \
  105|   121M|			st[(j) + 4] ^= ~st0 & st1; \
  106|   121M|		}
  107|       |
  108|  24.3M|		CHI( 0);
  109|  24.3M|		CHI( 5);
  110|  24.3M|		CHI(10);
  111|  24.3M|		CHI(15);
  112|  24.3M|		CHI(20);
  113|       |
  114|       |		// Iota
  115|  24.3M|		st[0] ^= keccakf_rndc[round];
  116|  24.3M|	}
  117|  1.01M|}
  118|       |
  119|      1|keccakf_func keccakf = []() {
  120|      1|#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(_M_AMD64)
  121|      1|	if (randomx::Cpu().hasBmi()) {
  122|      1|		return keccakf_bmi;
  123|      1|	}
  124|      0|#endif
  125|      0|	return keccakf_plain;
  126|      1|}();
  127|       |
  128|       |NOINLINE void keccak_step(const uint8_t* &in, int &inlen, std::array<uint64_t, 25>& st)
  129|  7.88M|{
  130|  7.88M|	constexpr int rsiz = KeccakParams::HASH_DATA_AREA;
  131|  7.88M|	constexpr int rsizw = rsiz / 8;
  132|       |
  133|  7.92M|	for (; inlen >= rsiz; inlen -= rsiz, in += rsiz) {
  134|   707k|		for (int i = 0; i < rsizw; i++) {
  135|   668k|			st[i] ^= read_unaligned(reinterpret_cast<const uint64_t*>(in) + i);
  136|   668k|		}
  137|  39.3k|		keccakf(st);
  138|  39.3k|	}
  139|  7.88M|}
  140|       |
  141|       |NOINLINE void keccak_finish(const uint8_t* in, int inlen, std::array<uint64_t, 25>& st)
  142|  3.96M|{
  143|  3.96M|	constexpr int rsiz = KeccakParams::HASH_DATA_AREA;
  144|  3.96M|	constexpr int rsizw = rsiz / 8;
  145|       |
  146|  3.96M|	keccak_step(in, inlen, st);
  147|       |
  148|       |	// last block and padding
  149|  3.96M|	alignas(8) uint8_t temp[144];
  150|       |
  151|  3.96M|	memcpy(temp, in, inlen);
  152|  3.96M|	temp[inlen++] = 1;
  153|  3.96M|	memset(temp + inlen, 0, rsiz - inlen);
  154|  3.96M|	temp[rsiz - 1] |= 0x80;
  155|       |
  156|  71.2M|	for (int i = 0; i < rsizw; i++) {
  157|  67.2M|		st[i] ^= reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(temp)[i];
  158|  67.2M|	}
  159|       |
  160|  3.96M|	keccakf(st);
  161|  3.96M|}

   24|       |
   25|       |#ifndef ROTL64
   26|  4.24G|#define ROTL64(x, y) (((x) << (y)) | ((x) >> (64 - (y))))
   27|       |#endif
   28|       |
   29|       |#if defined(__GNUC__) && (__GNUC__ < 12) && !defined(_andn_u64)
   30|       |#define _andn_u64 __andn_u64

   33|       |NOINLINE void keccakf_bmi(std::array<uint64_t, 25>& st)
   34|  6.63M|{
   35|   152M|	for (int round = 0; round < KeccakParams::ROUNDS; ++round) {
   36|   146M|		uint64_t bc[5];
   37|       |
   38|       |		// Theta
   39|   146M|		bc[0] = st[0] ^ st[5] ^ st[10] ^ st[15] ^ st[20];
   40|   146M|		bc[1] = st[1] ^ st[6] ^ st[11] ^ st[16] ^ st[21];
   41|   146M|		bc[2] = st[2] ^ st[7] ^ st[12] ^ st[17] ^ st[22];
   42|   146M|		bc[3] = st[3] ^ st[8] ^ st[13] ^ st[18] ^ st[23];
   43|   146M|		bc[4] = st[4] ^ st[9] ^ st[14] ^ st[19] ^ st[24];
   44|       |
   45|   731M|#define THETA(i) { \
   46|   731M|			const uint64_t t = bc[((i) + 4) % 5] ^ ROTL64(bc[((i) + 1) % 5], 1); \
   47|   731M|			st[(i) +  0 ] ^= t; \
   48|   731M|			st[(i) +  5] ^= t; \
   49|   731M|			st[(i) + 10] ^= t; \
   50|   731M|			st[(i) + 15] ^= t; \
   51|   731M|			st[(i) + 20] ^= t; \
   52|   731M|		}
   53|       |
   54|   146M|		THETA(0);
   55|   146M|		THETA(1);
   56|   146M|		THETA(2);
   57|   146M|		THETA(3);
   58|   146M|		THETA(4);
   59|       |
   60|       |		// Rho Pi
   61|   146M|		const uint64_t t = st[1];
   62|   146M|		st[1] = ROTL64(st[6], 44);
   63|   146M|		st[6] = ROTL64(st[9], 20);
   64|   146M|		st[9] = ROTL64(st[22], 61);
   65|   146M|		st[22] = ROTL64(st[14], 39);
   66|   146M|		st[14] = ROTL64(st[20], 18);
   67|   146M|		st[20] = ROTL64(st[2], 62);
   68|   146M|		st[2] = ROTL64(st[12], 43);
   69|   146M|		st[12] = ROTL64(st[13], 25);
   70|   146M|		st[13] = ROTL64(st[19], 8);
   71|   146M|		st[19] = ROTL64(st[23], 56);
   72|   146M|		st[23] = ROTL64(st[15], 41);
   73|   146M|		st[15] = ROTL64(st[4], 27);
   74|   146M|		st[4] = ROTL64(st[24], 14);
   75|   146M|		st[24] = ROTL64(st[21], 2);
   76|   146M|		st[21] = ROTL64(st[8], 55);
   77|   146M|		st[8] = ROTL64(st[16], 45);
   78|   146M|		st[16] = ROTL64(st[5], 36);
   79|   146M|		st[5] = ROTL64(st[3], 28);
   80|   146M|		st[3] = ROTL64(st[18], 21);
   81|   146M|		st[18] = ROTL64(st[17], 15);
   82|   146M|		st[17] = ROTL64(st[11], 10);
   83|   146M|		st[11] = ROTL64(st[7], 6);
   84|   146M|		st[7] = ROTL64(st[10], 3);
   85|   146M|		st[10] = ROTL64(t, 1);
   86|       |
   87|       |		//  Chi
   88|   731M|#define CHI(j) { \
   89|   731M|			const uint64_t st0 = st[(j)    ]; \
   90|   731M|			const uint64_t st1 = st[(j) + 1]; \
   91|   731M|			const uint64_t st2 = st[(j) + 2]; \
   92|   731M|			const uint64_t st3 = st[(j) + 3]; \
   93|   731M|			const uint64_t st4 = st[(j) + 4]; \
   94|   731M|			st[(j)    ] ^= _andn_u64(st1, st2); \
   95|   731M|			st[(j) + 1] ^= _andn_u64(st2, st3); \
   96|   731M|			st[(j) + 2] ^= _andn_u64(st3, st4); \
   97|   731M|			st[(j) + 3] ^= _andn_u64(st4, st0); \
   98|   731M|			st[(j) + 4] ^= _andn_u64(st0, st1); \
   99|   731M|		}
  100|       |
  101|   146M|		CHI(0);
  102|   146M|		CHI(5);
  103|   146M|		CHI(10);
  104|   146M|		CHI(15);
  105|   146M|		CHI(20);
  106|       |
  107|       |		// Iota
  108|   146M|		st[0] ^= keccakf_rndc[round];
  109|   146M|	}

   25|       |template<int ROUNDS>
   26|       |static FORCEINLINE constexpr void keccakf(std::array<uint64_t, 25>& st)
   27|      0|{
   28|      0|	constexpr uint64_t round_constants[24] =
   29|      0|	{
   30|      0|		0x0000000000000001, 0x0000000000008082, 0x800000000000808a,
   31|      0|		0x8000000080008000, 0x000000000000808b, 0x0000000080000001,
   32|      0|		0x8000000080008081, 0x8000000000008009, 0x000000000000008a,
   33|      0|		0x0000000000000088, 0x0000000080008009, 0x000000008000000a,
   34|      0|		0x000000008000808b, 0x800000000000008b, 0x8000000000008089,
   35|      0|		0x8000000000008003, 0x8000000000008002, 0x8000000000000080,
   36|      0|		0x000000000000800a, 0x800000008000000a, 0x8000000080008081,
   37|      0|		0x8000000000008080, 0x0000000080000001, 0x8000000080008008
   38|      0|	};
   39|      0|	constexpr int order[25] = { 1, 6, 9, 22, 14, 20, 2, 12, 13, 19, 23, 15, 4, 24, 21, 8, 16, 5, 3, 18, 17, 11, 7, 10, 1 };
   40|      0|	constexpr int shift[24] = { 44, 20, 61, 39, 18, 62, 43, 25, 8, 56, 41, 27, 14, 2, 55, 45, 36, 28, 21, 15, 10, 6, 3, 1 };
   41|      0|
   42|      0|	for (int round = 0; round < ROUNDS; ++round) {
   43|      0|		uint64_t bc[5] = {};
   44|      0|
   45|      0|		// Theta
   46|      0|		for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) bc[i] = st[i] ^ st[i + 5] ^ st[i + 10] ^ st[i + 15] ^ st[i + 20];
   47|      0|
   48|      0|		for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) {
   49|      0|			const uint64_t t = bc[(i + 4) % 5] ^ ((bc[(i + 1) % 5] << 1) | (bc[(i + 1) % 5] >> 63));
   50|      0|			for (int j = 0; j < 25; j += 5) st[i + j] ^= t;
   51|      0|		}
   52|      0|
   53|      0|		// Rho Pi
   54|      0|		const auto st0 = st;
   55|      0|		for (int i = 0; i < 24; ++i) st[order[i]] = (st0[order[i + 1]] << shift[i]) | (st0[order[i + 1]] >> (64 - shift[i]));
   56|      0|
   57|      0|		//  Chi
   58|      0|		for (int i = 0; i < 25; i += 5) {
   59|      0|			const uint64_t t[5] = { st[i], st[i + 1], st[i + 2], st[i + 3], st[i + 4] };
   60|      0|			for (int j = 0; j < 5; ++j) st[i + j] ^= ~t[(j + 1) % 5] & t[(j + 2) % 5];
   61|      0|		}
   62|      0|
   63|      0|		// Iota
   64|      0|		st[0] ^= round_constants[round];

   70|       |template<int len>
   71|       |static constexpr hash keccak(const char (&input)[len])
   72|      0|{
   73|      0|	constexpr int rsiz = 136;
   74|      0|	constexpr int inlen = len - 1;
   75|      0|	static_assert(inlen < rsiz, "Too long input");
   76|      0|
   77|      0|	uint8_t temp[rsiz] = {};
   78|      0|
   79|      0|	for (int i = 0; i < inlen; ++i) {
   80|      0|		temp[i] = static_cast<uint8_t>(input[i]);
   81|      0|	}
   82|      0|
   83|      0|	temp[inlen] = 1;
   84|      0|	temp[rsiz - 1] |= 0x80;
   85|      0|
   86|      0|	std::array<uint64_t, 25> st = {};
   87|      0|
   88|      0|	for (int i = 0; i < rsiz / 8; i++) {
   89|      0|		uint64_t k = 0;
   90|      0|		for (int j = 0; j < 8; ++j) {
   91|      0|			k |= static_cast<uint64_t>(temp[i * 8 + j]) << (j * 8);
   92|      0|		}
   93|      0|		st[i] ^= k;
   94|      0|	}
   95|      0|
   96|      0|	ConstexprKeccak::keccakf<24>(st);
   97|      0|
   98|      0|	hash result{};
   99|      0|
  100|      0|	for (size_t i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE; ++i) {
  101|      0|		result.h[i] = static_cast<uint8_t>(st[i / 8] >> ((i % 8) * 8));
  102|      0|	}
  103|      0|
  104|      0|	return result;
  105|      0|}

   29|       |#define S_ISREG(M) (((M) & _S_IFMT) == _S_IFREG)

   36|       |LOG_CATEGORY(Log)

   38|       |static constexpr char log_file_name[] = "p2pool.log";
   39|       |
   40|       |namespace p2pool {
   41|       |
   42|       |namespace log {
   43|       |
   44|       |std::atomic<int> GLOBAL_LOG_LEVEL = 3;
   45|       |bool CONSOLE_COLORS = true;
   46|       |
   47|       |#ifndef P2POOL_LOG_DISABLE
   48|       |
   49|       |#ifdef _WIN32
   50|       |static const HANDLE hStdIn  = GetStdHandle(STD_INPUT_HANDLE);
   51|       |static const HANDLE hStdOut = GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE);
   52|       |
   53|       |#if defined(_MSC_VER) && !defined(NDEBUG)
   54|       |
   55|       |#include <DbgHelp.h>
   56|       |
   57|       |#pragma comment(lib, "Dbghelp.lib")
   58|       |
   59|       |LONG WINAPI UnhandledExceptionFilter(_In_ _EXCEPTION_POINTERS* exception_pointers)
   60|       |{
   61|       |	constexpr size_t MAX_FRAMES = 32;
   62|       |
   63|       |	void* stack_trace[MAX_FRAMES] = {};
   64|       |	DWORD hash;
   65|       |	CaptureStackBackTrace(1, MAX_FRAMES, stack_trace, &hash);
   66|       |
   67|       |	char buffer[sizeof(SYMBOL_INFO) + MAX_SYM_NAME * sizeof(TCHAR)] = {};
   68|       |	PSYMBOL_INFO pSymbol = reinterpret_cast<PSYMBOL_INFO>(buffer);
   69|       |
   70|       |	pSymbol->SizeOfStruct = sizeof(SYMBOL_INFO);
   71|       |	pSymbol->MaxNameLen = MAX_SYM_NAME;
   72|       |
   73|       |	IMAGEHLP_LINE64 line{};
   74|       |	line.SizeOfStruct = sizeof(IMAGEHLP_LINE64);
   75|       |
   76|       |	const HANDLE h = GetCurrentProcess();
   77|       |
   78|       |	const uint32_t code = (exception_pointers && exception_pointers->ExceptionRecord) ? exception_pointers->ExceptionRecord->ExceptionCode : 0;
   79|       |
   80|       |	fprintf(stderr, "\n\nUnhandled exception %X at:\n", code);
   81|       |	fflush(stderr);
   82|       |
   83|       |	for (size_t j = 0; j < MAX_FRAMES; ++j) {
   84|       |		const DWORD64 address = reinterpret_cast<DWORD64>(stack_trace[j]);
   85|       |		DWORD t = 0;
   86|       |		if (SymFromAddr(h, address, nullptr, pSymbol) && SymGetLineFromAddr64(h, address, &t, &line)) {
   87|       |			fprintf(stderr, "%s (%s, line %lu)\n", line.FileName, pSymbol->Name, line.LineNumber);
   88|       |			fflush(stderr);
   89|       |		}
   90|       |	}
   91|       |
   92|       |	fprintf(stderr, "\n\n");
   93|       |	fflush(stderr);
   94|       |
   95|       |	// Normal logging might be broken at this point, but try to log it anyway
   96|       |	LOGERR(0, "Unhandled exception " << log::Hex(code) << " at:");
   97|       |
   98|       |	for (size_t j = 0; j < MAX_FRAMES; ++j) {
   99|       |		const DWORD64 address = reinterpret_cast<DWORD64>(stack_trace[j]);
  100|       |		DWORD t = 0;
  101|       |		if (SymFromAddr(h, address, nullptr, pSymbol) && SymGetLineFromAddr64(h, address, &t, &line)) {
  102|       |			LOGERR(0, line.FileName << " (" << static_cast<const char*>(pSymbol->Name) << ", line " << static_cast<size_t>(line.LineNumber) << ')');
  103|       |		}
  104|       |	}
  105|       |
  106|       |	Sleep(1000);
  107|       |
  108|       |	return EXCEPTION_CONTINUE_SEARCH;
  109|       |}
  110|       |#endif // _MSC_VER && !NDEBUG
  111|       |#endif // _WIN32
  112|       |
  113|       |class Worker
  114|       |{
  115|       |public:
  116|       |	enum params : int
  117|       |	{
  118|       |		SLOT_SIZE = log::Stream::BUF_SIZE + 1,
  119|       |		BUF_SIZE = SLOT_SIZE * 8192,
  120|       |	};
  121|       |
  122|       |	FORCEINLINE explicit Worker(const Params& p)
  123|       |		: m_writePos(0)
  124|       |		, m_readPos(0)
  125|       |		, m_stopped(false)
  126|       |		, m_needReopen(false)
  127|       |		, m_consoleLogEnabled(p.m_consoleLogEnabled)
  128|       |		, m_logFileEnabled(p.m_logFileEnabled)
  129|       |		, m_logFileLastStatTime(0)
  130|       |		, m_workerWaiting(false)
  131|       |	{
  132|       |#if defined(_WIN32) && defined(_MSC_VER) && !defined(NDEBUG)
  133|       |		SetUnhandledExceptionFilter(UnhandledExceptionFilter);
  134|       |#endif
  135|       |
  136|       |		std::setlocale(LC_ALL, "C");
  137|       |
  138|       |		m_logFilePath = p.m_logFilePath.empty() ? (p.m_dataDir + log_file_name) : p.m_logFilePath;
  139|       |
  140|       |		m_buf.resize(BUF_SIZE);
  141|       |
  142|       |		int err = uv_cond_init(&m_cond);
  143|       |		if (err) {
  144|       |			fprintf(stderr, "failed to init m_cond in the logger thread (%s), aborting\n", uv_err_name(err));
  145|       |			abort();
  146|       |		}
  147|       |
  148|       |		err = uv_mutex_init(&m_mutex);
  149|       |		if (err) {
  150|       |			fprintf(stderr, "failed to init m_mutex in the logger thread (%s), aborting\n", uv_err_name(err));
  151|       |			abort();
  152|       |		}
  153|       |
  154|       |#ifdef _WIN32
  155|       |		SetConsoleMode(hStdIn, ENABLE_ECHO_INPUT | ENABLE_LINE_INPUT | ENABLE_PROCESSED_INPUT | ENABLE_EXTENDED_FLAGS);
  156|       |
  157|       |		DWORD dwConsoleMode;
  158|       |		if (GetConsoleMode(hStdOut, &dwConsoleMode)) {
  159|       |			SetConsoleMode(hStdOut, dwConsoleMode | ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING);
  160|       |		}
  161|       |#endif
  162|       |
  163|       |		const char* no_color = getenv("NO_COLOR");
  164|       |		if (no_color && *no_color) {
  165|       |			CONSOLE_COLORS = false;
  166|       |		}
  167|       |
  168|       |		err = uv_thread_create(&m_worker, run_wrapper, this);
  169|       |		if (err) {
  170|       |			fprintf(stderr, "failed to start logger thread (%s), aborting\n", uv_err_name(err));
  171|       |			abort();
  172|       |		}
  173|       |	}
  174|       |
  175|       |	~Worker()
  176|       |	{
  177|       |		try {
  178|       |			stop();
  179|       |		}
  180|       |		catch (...) {
  181|       |		}
  182|       |	}
  183|       |
  184|       |	FORCEINLINE void stop()
  185|       |	{
  186|       |		{
  187|       |			MutexLock lock(m_mutex);
  188|       |			if (m_stopped) {
  189|       |				return;
  190|       |			}
  191|       |
  192|       |			m_stopped = true;
  193|       |			LOGINFO(0, "stopped");
  194|       |		}
  195|       |
  196|       |		uv_thread_join(&m_worker);
  197|       |		uv_cond_destroy(&m_cond);
  198|       |		uv_mutex_destroy(&m_mutex);
  199|       |
  200|       |		if (m_logFileEnabled) {
  201|       |			m_logFile.close();
  202|       |		}
  203|       |	}
  204|       |
  205|       |	FORCEINLINE void write(const char* buf, uint32_t size)
  206|       |	{
  207|       |		uint32_t writePos = m_writePos.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
  208|       |
  209|       |		do {
  210|       |			if (writePos - m_readPos.load(std::memory_order_relaxed) > BUF_SIZE - SLOT_SIZE) {
  211|       |				// Buffer is full, can't log normally
  212|       |				if ((size > 3) && m_consoleLogEnabled) {
  213|       |					fwrite(buf + 3, 1, size - 3, stderr);
  214|       |				}
  215|       |				return;
  216|       |			}
  217|       |		} while (!m_writePos.compare_exchange_weak(writePos, writePos + SLOT_SIZE, std::memory_order_acq_rel, std::memory_order_relaxed));
  218|       |
  219|       |		char* p = m_buf.data() + (writePos % BUF_SIZE);
  220|       |
  221|       |		memcpy(p + 1, buf + 1, size - 1);
  222|       |
  223|       |		// Mark that everything is written into this log slot
  224|       |		std::atomic<char>* severity = reinterpret_cast<std::atomic<char>*>(p);
  225|       |		severity->store(static_cast<char>(buf[0] + 1), std::memory_order_release);
  226|       |
  227|       |		// Signal the worker thread
  228|       |		if (m_workerWaiting.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
  229|       |			uv_cond_signal(&m_cond);
  230|       |		}
  231|       |	}
  232|       |
  233|       |	const std::string& log_file_path() const { return m_logFilePath; }
  234|       |
  235|       |	FORCEINLINE void schedule_reopen()
  236|       |	{
  237|       |		m_needReopen = true;
  238|       |
  239|       |		// Signal the worker thread
  240|       |		if (m_workerWaiting.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
  241|       |			uv_cond_signal(&m_cond);
  242|       |		}
  243|       |	}
  244|       |
  245|       |private:
  246|       |	bool reopen(struct stat& st)
  247|       |	{
  248|       |		if (!m_logFileEnabled) {
  249|       |			return true;
  250|       |		}
  251|       |
  252|       |		st = {};
  253|       |
  254|       |		if (m_logFile.is_open()) {
  255|       |			m_logFile.close();
  256|       |		}
  257|       |
  258|       |		m_logFile.open(m_logFilePath, std::ios::app | std::ios::binary);
  259|       |
  260|       |		if (!m_logFile.is_open()) {
  261|       |			return false;
  262|       |		}
  263|       |
  264|       |		if (stat(m_logFilePath.c_str(), &st) != 0) {
  265|       |			st = {};
  266|       |			return false;
  267|       |		}
  268|       |
  269|       |		return true;
  270|       |	}
  271|       |
  272|       |	static void run_wrapper(void* arg) { reinterpret_cast<Worker*>(arg)->run(); }
  273|       |
  274|       |	NOINLINE void run()
  275|       |	{
  276|       |		set_thread_name("Logger");
  277|       |
  278|       |		struct stat log_file_st;
  279|       |
  280|       |		if (!reopen(log_file_st) && m_consoleLogEnabled) {
  281|       |			fprintf(stderr, "failed to open %s: error %d\n", m_logFilePath.c_str(), errno);
  282|       |		}
  283|       |
  284|       |		do {
  285|       |			{
  286|       |				MutexLock lock(m_mutex);
  287|       |
  288|       |				if (m_readPos.load(std::memory_order_relaxed) == m_writePos.load(std::memory_order_acquire)) {
  289|       |					if (m_stopped) {
  290|       |						return;
  291|       |					}
  292|       |
  293|       |					m_workerWaiting.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
  294|       |					uv_cond_wait(&m_cond, &m_mutex);
  295|       |					m_workerWaiting.store(false, std::memory_order_relaxed);
  296|       |				}
  297|       |			}
  298|       |
  299|       |			bool expected = true;
  300|       |			if (m_needReopen.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false)) {
  301|       |				// Reopen the log file on request (logrotate support)
  302|       |				if(reopen(log_file_st)) {
  303|       |					LOGINFO(0, "reopened " << m_logFilePath);
  304|       |				}
  305|       |			}
  306|       |
  307|       |			for (uint32_t writePos = m_writePos.load(std::memory_order_acquire); m_readPos.load(std::memory_order_relaxed) != writePos; writePos = m_writePos.load(std::memory_order_acquire)) {
  308|       |				// We have at least one log slot pending, possibly more than one
  309|       |				// Process everything in a loop before reading m_writePos again
  310|       |				do {
  311|       |					char* p = m_buf.data() + (m_readPos.load(std::memory_order_relaxed) % BUF_SIZE);
  312|       |
  313|       |					// Wait until everything is written into this log slot
  314|       |					std::atomic<char>* severity_ptr = reinterpret_cast<std::atomic<char>*>(p);
  315|       |
  316|       |					char severity;
  317|       |					while (!(severity = severity_ptr->load(std::memory_order_acquire))) {
  318|       |						std::this_thread::yield();
  319|       |					}
  320|       |
  321|       |					uint32_t size = static_cast<uint8_t>(p[2]);
  322|       |					size = (size << 8) + static_cast<uint8_t>(p[1]);
  323|       |
  324|       |					if (size > 3) {
  325|       |						p += 3;
  326|       |						size -= 3;
  327|       |
  328|       |						// Read CONSOLE_COLORS only once because its value can be changed in another thread
  329|       |						const bool c = CONSOLE_COLORS;
  330|       |
  331|       |						if (!c) {
  332|       |							strip_colors(p, size);
  333|       |						}
  334|       |
  335|       |						if (m_consoleLogEnabled) {
  336|       |							fwrite(p, 1, size, (severity == 1) ? stdout : stderr);
  337|       |						}
  338|       |
  339|       |						if (m_logFileEnabled && m_logFile.is_open()) {
  340|       |							if (c) {
  341|       |								strip_colors(p, size);
  342|       |							}
  343|       |
  344|       |							if (severity == 1) {
  345|       |								m_logFile.write("NOTICE  ", 8);
  346|       |							}
  347|       |							else if (severity == 2) {
  348|       |								m_logFile.write("WARNING ", 8);
  349|       |							}
  350|       |							else if (severity == 3) {
  351|       |								m_logFile.write("ERROR   ", 8);
  352|       |							}
  353|       |
  354|       |							m_logFile.write(p, size);
  355|       |						}
  356|       |					}
  357|       |
  358|       |					// Mark this log slot empty
  359|       |					severity_ptr->store('\0', std::memory_order_relaxed);
  360|       |
  361|       |				// fetch_add returns the pre-increment value; "+ SLOT_SIZE" gives us the new m_readPos
  362|       |				} while (m_readPos.fetch_add(SLOT_SIZE, std::memory_order_relaxed) + SLOT_SIZE != writePos);
  363|       |			}
  364|       |
  365|       |			// Flush the log file only after all pending log lines have been written
  366|       |			if (m_logFileEnabled && m_logFile.is_open()) {
  367|       |				m_logFile.flush();
  368|       |
  369|       |				// Automatically reopen the file if either it
  370|       |				// no longer exists or it is now a different
  371|       |				// regular file; this detects manual moving or
  372|       |				// recreating of the file
  373|       |
  374|       |				// Throttle this check to once per 500 ms
  375|       |				const uint64_t now = microseconds_since_epoch();
  376|       |
  377|       |				if (now >= m_logFileLastStatTime + 500'000) {
  378|       |					m_logFileLastStatTime = now;
  379|       |
  380|       |					struct stat buf;
  381|       |					if (stat(m_logFilePath.c_str(), &buf) == 0) {
  382|       |						if (log_file_st.st_dev && log_file_st.st_ino && S_ISREG(buf.st_mode) &&
  383|       |							(buf.st_dev != log_file_st.st_dev || buf.st_ino != log_file_st.st_ino)) {
  384|       |							reopen(log_file_st);
  385|       |						}
  386|       |					}
  387|       |					else if (errno == ENOENT) {
  388|       |						reopen(log_file_st);
  389|       |					}
  390|       |				}
  391|       |			}
  392|       |
  393|       |			fflush(stdout);
  394|       |			fflush(stderr);
  395|       |		} while (1);
  396|       |	}
  397|       |
  398|       |	static FORCEINLINE void strip_colors(char* buf, uint32_t& size)
  399|       |	{
  400|       |		const char* p_read = buf;
  401|       |		char* p_write = buf;
  402|       |		const char* buf_end = buf + size;
  403|       |
  404|       |		bool is_color = false;
  405|       |
  406|       |		while (p_read < buf_end) {
  407|       |			if (!is_color && (*p_read == '\x1b')) {
  408|       |				is_color = true;
  409|       |			}
  410|       |
  411|       |			if (!is_color) {
  412|       |				*(p_write++) = *p_read;
  413|       |			}
  414|       |
  415|       |			if (is_color && (*p_read == 'm')) {
  416|       |				is_color = false;
  417|       |			}
  418|       |
  419|       |			++p_read;
  420|       |		}
  421|       |
  422|       |		size = static_cast<uint32_t>(p_write - buf);
  423|       |	}
  424|       |
  425|       |	std::vector<char> m_buf;
  426|       |	std::atomic<uint32_t> m_writePos;
  427|       |	std::atomic<uint32_t> m_readPos;
  428|       |
  429|       |	uv_cond_t m_cond;
  430|       |	uv_mutex_t m_mutex;

  104|  11.5k|	{

  569|       |namespace {
  570|       |	template<log::Severity severity>
  571|       |	FORCEINLINE void apply_severity(log::Stream& s)
  572|       |	{
  573|       |		if constexpr (severity == log::Severity::Info) {
  574|       |			s << log::NoColor::value;
  575|       |		}
  576|       |		else if constexpr (severity == log::Severity::Warning) {
  577|       |			s << log::Yellow::value;
  578|       |		}
  579|       |		else if constexpr (severity == log::Severity::Error) {
  580|       |			s << log::Red::value;
  581|       |		}
  582|       |	}

  585|       |#define CONCAT(a, b) CONCAT2(a, b)
  586|       |#define CONCAT2(a, b) a##b
  587|       |
  588|       |// This is to check that LOG() call doesn't modify variables in scope, making program behavior dependent on the log level:
  589|       |//
  590|       |//		int some_func(int& n) { return ++n; }
  591|       |//		...
  592|       |//		LOGINFO(1, "Some important value: " << some_func(n));
  593|       |//
  594|       |// will not compile because the dummy lambda capture uses const-qualified copies of all variables.
  595|       |//
  596|       |// The check is "free": compiler will remove it entirely in release builds.
  597|       |
  598|       |struct DummyStream
  599|       |{
  600|       |	template<typename T>
  601|       |	FORCEINLINE DummyStream& operator<<(const T&)
  602|      0|	{
  603|      0|		return *this;

   20|       |// Simple memory leak detector for Windows users, works best in RelWithDebInfo configuration.
   21|       |#if defined(_WIN32) && defined(DEV_TRACK_MEMORY) && defined(_MSC_VER) && !defined(NDEBUG)
   22|       |
   23|       |#include "uv_util.h"
   24|       |#include <atomic>
   25|       |#include <type_traits>
   26|       |#include <iostream>
   27|       |#include <fstream>

   30|       |#include <DbgHelp.h>
   31|       |

   33|       |
   34|       |namespace p2pool {
   35|       |
   36|       |static bool track_memory = false;
   37|       |
   38|       |constexpr size_t N = 1 << 22;
   39|       |constexpr size_t MAX_FRAMES = 29;
   40|       |
   41|       |struct TrackedAllocation
   42|       |{
   43|       |	void* p;
   44|       |	void* stack_trace[MAX_FRAMES];
   45|       |	uint64_t allocated_size;
   46|       |	uint32_t thread_id;
   47|       |
   48|       |	FORCEINLINE bool operator<(const TrackedAllocation& rhs) { return memcmp(stack_trace, rhs.stack_trace, sizeof(stack_trace)) < 0; }
   49|       |	FORCEINLINE bool operator==(const TrackedAllocation& rhs) { return memcmp(stack_trace, rhs.stack_trace, sizeof(stack_trace)) == 0; }
   50|       |
   51|       |	void print(HANDLE h, bool& is_grpc) const
   52|       |	{
   53|       |		is_grpc = false;
   54|       |
   55|       |		char buffer[sizeof(SYMBOL_INFO) + MAX_SYM_NAME * sizeof(TCHAR)] = {};
   56|       |		PSYMBOL_INFO pSymbol = reinterpret_cast<PSYMBOL_INFO>(buffer);
   57|       |
   58|       |		pSymbol->SizeOfStruct = sizeof(SYMBOL_INFO);
   59|       |		pSymbol->MaxNameLen = MAX_SYM_NAME;
   60|       |
   61|       |		IMAGEHLP_LINE64 line{};
   62|       |		line.SizeOfStruct = sizeof(IMAGEHLP_LINE64);
   63|       |
   64|       |		for (size_t j = 0; j < MAX_FRAMES; ++j) {
   65|       |			const DWORD64 address = reinterpret_cast<DWORD64>(stack_trace[j]);
   66|       |			DWORD64 t1 = 0;
   67|       |			DWORD t2 = 0;
   68|       |			if (SymFromAddr(h, address, &t1, pSymbol) && SymGetLineFromAddr64(h, address, &t2, &line)) {
   69|       |				const char* s = line.FileName;
   70|       |				const char* file_name = nullptr;
   71|       |
   72|       |				while (*s) {
   73|       |					if ((*s == '\\') || (*s == '/')) {
   74|       |						file_name = s + 1;
   75|       |					}
   76|       |					++s;
   77|       |				}
   78|       |
   79|       |				s = pSymbol->Name;
   80|       |
   81|       |				printf("%-25s %s (line %lu)\n", file_name ? file_name : line.FileName, s, line.LineNumber);
   82|       |
   83|       |				if (!is_grpc) {
   84|       |					is_grpc = (strstr(s, "grpc::") == s) ||
   85|       |						(strstr(s, "grpc_core::") == s) ||
   86|       |						(strstr(s, "grpc_event_engine::") == s) ||
   87|       |						(strstr(s, "grpc_init") == s);
   88|       |				}
   89|       |			}
   90|       |		}
   91|       |		printf("\n");
   92|       |	}
   93|       |};
   94|       |
   95|       |static_assert(sizeof(TrackedAllocation) == 256, "");
   96|       |
   97|       |std::mutex allocation_lock;
   98|       |std::hash<void*> hasher;
   99|       |uint32_t first[N];
  100|       |uint32_t next[N];
  101|       |TrackedAllocation allocations[N];
  102|       |uint32_t num_allocations = 0;
  103|       |uint64_t total_allocated = 0;
  104|       |uint32_t cur_allocation_index = 1;
  105|       |
  106|       |void show_top_10_allocations()
  107|       |{
  108|       |	TrackedAllocation* buf = reinterpret_cast<TrackedAllocation*>(VirtualAlloc(nullptr, sizeof(TrackedAllocation) * N, MEM_COMMIT | MEM_RESERVE, PAGE_READWRITE));
  109|       |	if (!buf) {
  110|       |		return;
  111|       |	}
  112|       |
  113|       |	const HANDLE h = GetCurrentProcess();
  114|       |
  115|       |	{
  116|       |		std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(allocation_lock);
  117|       |
  118|       |		TrackedAllocation* end = buf;
  119|       |		for (size_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
  120|       |			if (allocations[i].allocated_size) {
  121|       |				*(end++) = allocations[i];
  122|       |			}
  123|       |		}
  124|       |
  125|       |		std::sort(buf, end);
  126|       |
  127|       |		TrackedAllocation* prev = buf;
  128|       |		for (TrackedAllocation* p = buf + 1; p < end; ++p) {
  129|       |			if (*p == *prev) {
  130|       |				prev->allocated_size += p->allocated_size;
  131|       |			}
  132|       |			else {
  133|       |				++prev;
  134|       |				*prev = *p;
  135|       |			}
  136|       |		}
  137|       |		end = prev + 1;
  138|       |
  139|       |		std::sort(buf, end, [](const auto& a, const auto& b) { return a.allocated_size > b.allocated_size; });
  140|       |
  141|       |		printf("%I64u total bytes allocated\n\n", total_allocated);
  142|       |		printf("Top 10 allocations:\n\n");
  143|       |
  144|       |		for (TrackedAllocation* p = buf; (p < buf + 10) && (p < end); ++p) {
  145|       |			printf("%I64u bytes allocated at:\n", p->allocated_size);
  146|       |			bool is_grpc;
  147|       |			p->print(h, is_grpc);
  148|       |		}
  149|       |	}
  150|       |
  151|       |	printf("\n");
  152|       |
  153|       |	VirtualFree(buf, 0, MEM_RELEASE);
  154|       |}
  155|       |
  156|       |static DWORD WINAPI minidump_and_crash_thread(LPVOID param)
  157|       |{
  158|       |	SetThreadPriority(GetCurrentThread(), THREAD_PRIORITY_TIME_CRITICAL);
  159|       |
  160|       |	const size_t delay = reinterpret_cast<size_t>(param);
  161|       |	Sleep(static_cast<DWORD>(delay));
  162|       |
  163|       |	HANDLE h = CreateFile(TEXT("p2pool.dmp"), GENERIC_ALL, 0, nullptr, CREATE_ALWAYS, FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL, nullptr);
  164|       |	MiniDumpWriteDump(GetCurrentProcess(), GetCurrentProcessId(), h, MiniDumpWithHandleData, nullptr, nullptr, nullptr);
  165|       |	CloseHandle(h);
  166|       |
  167|       |	TerminateProcess(GetCurrentProcess(), 123);
  168|       |
  169|       |	return 0;
  170|       |}
  171|       |
  172|       |void minidump_and_crash(size_t delay)
  173|       |{
  174|       |	HANDLE h = CreateThread(nullptr, 0, minidump_and_crash_thread, reinterpret_cast<LPVOID>(delay), 0, nullptr);
  175|       |	CloseHandle(h);
  176|       |}
  177|       |
  178|       |FORCEINLINE static void add_alocation(void* p, size_t size)
  179|       |{
  180|       |	if (!track_memory) {
  181|       |		return;
  182|       |	}
  183|       |
  184|       |	void* stack_trace[MAX_FRAMES] = {};
  185|       |	DWORD hash;
  186|       |	CaptureStackBackTrace(1, MAX_FRAMES, stack_trace, &hash);
  187|       |
  188|       |	const DWORD thread_id = GetCurrentThreadId();
  189|       |
  190|       |	const size_t index = hasher(p) & (N - 1);
  191|       |
  192|       |	std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(allocation_lock);
  193|       |
  194|       |	++num_allocations;
  195|       |	if (num_allocations >= N / 2) {
  196|       |		// Make N two times bigger if this triggers
  197|       |		__debugbreak();
  198|       |	}
  199|       |	total_allocated += size;
  200|       |
  201|       |	for (uint64_t i = cur_allocation_index;; i = (i + 1) & (N - 1)) {
  202|       |		if (i && !allocations[i].allocated_size) {
  203|       |			cur_allocation_index = static_cast<uint32_t>(i);
  204|       |			allocations[i].allocated_size = static_cast<uint32_t>(size);
  205|       |			break;
  206|       |		}
  207|       |	}
  208|       |
  209|       |	TrackedAllocation& t = allocations[cur_allocation_index];
  210|       |	t.p = p;
  211|       |	memcpy(t.stack_trace, stack_trace, sizeof(stack_trace));
  212|       |	next[cur_allocation_index] = first[index];
  213|       |	t.thread_id = thread_id;
  214|       |	first[index] = cur_allocation_index;
  215|       |}
  216|       |
  217|       |FORCEINLINE static void remove_allocation(void* p)
  218|       |{
  219|       |	if (!track_memory || !p) {
  220|       |		return;
  221|       |	}
  222|       |
  223|       |	std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(allocation_lock);
  224|       |
  225|       |	--num_allocations;
  226|       |
  227|       |	const size_t index = hasher(p) & (N - 1);
  228|       |
  229|       |	for (uint32_t prev = 0, k = first[index]; k != 0; prev = k, k = next[k]) {
  230|       |		if (allocations[k].p == p) {
  231|       |			total_allocated -= allocations[k].allocated_size;
  232|       |			allocations[k].allocated_size = 0;
  233|       |			if (prev) {
  234|       |				next[prev] = next[k];
  235|       |			}
  236|       |			else {
  237|       |				first[index] = next[k];
  238|       |			}
  239|       |			return;
  240|       |		}
  241|       |	}
  242|       |
  243|       |	// Someone tried to deallocate a pointer that wasn't allocated before
  244|       |	__debugbreak();
  245|       |}
  246|       |
  247|       |void* malloc_hook(size_t n) noexcept
  248|       |{
  249|       |	void* p = malloc(n);
  250|       |	if (p) {
  251|       |		add_alocation(p, n);
  252|       |	}
  253|       |	return p;
  254|       |}
  255|       |
  256|       |FORCEINLINE static void* allocate(size_t n)
  257|       |{
  258|       |	void* p = malloc_hook(n);
  259|       |	if (!p) {
  260|       |		throw std::bad_alloc();
  261|       |	}
  262|       |	return p;
  263|       |}
  264|       |
  265|       |void free_hook(void* p) noexcept
  266|       |{
  267|       |	remove_allocation(p);
  268|       |	free(p);
  269|       |}
  270|       |
  271|       |char* strdup_hook(const char* s) noexcept
  272|       |{
  273|       |#ifdef _MSC_VER
  274|       |	char* s1 = _strdup(s);
  275|       |#else
  276|       |	char* s1 = strdup(s);
  277|       |#endif
  278|       |	if (s1) {
  279|       |		add_alocation(s1, strlen(s) + 1);
  280|       |	}
  281|       |	return s1;
  282|       |}
  283|       |
  284|       |void* realloc_hook(void* ptr, size_t size) noexcept
  285|       |{
  286|       |	remove_allocation(ptr);
  287|       |
  288|       |	void* p = realloc(ptr, size);
  289|       |	if (p) {
  290|       |		add_alocation(p, size);
  291|       |	}
  292|       |	return p;
  293|       |}
  294|       |
  295|       |void* calloc_hook(size_t count, size_t size) noexcept
  296|       |{
  297|       |	void* p = calloc(count, size);
  298|       |	if (p) {
  299|       |		add_alocation(p, size);
  300|       |	}
  301|       |	return p;
  302|       |}
  303|       |
  304|       |} // p2pool
  305|       |
  306|       |void memory_tracking_start()
  307|       |{
  308|       |	// Trigger std::ostream initialization to avoid reporting it as leaks
  309|       |	std::cout << "Memory leak detection = " << 1 << std::endl;
  310|       |
  311|       |	// Trigger std::ofstream initialization to avoid reporting it as leaks
  312|       |	{
  313|       |		std::ofstream tmp("memory_tracking.tmp");
  314|       |	}
  315|       |
  316|       |	using namespace p2pool;
  317|       |
  318|       |	uv_replace_allocator(malloc_hook, realloc_hook, calloc_hook, free_hook);
  319|       |	track_memory = true;
  320|       |}
  321|       |
  322|       |bool memory_tracking_stop()
  323|       |{
  324|       |	using namespace p2pool;
  325|       |
  326|       |	track_memory = false;
  327|       |
  328|       |	const HANDLE h = GetCurrentProcess();
  329|       |
  330|       |	uint64_t total_leaks = 0;
  331|       |	uint64_t grpc_leaks = 0;
  332|       |
  333|       |	for (uint32_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
  334|       |		const TrackedAllocation& t = allocations[i];
  335|       |		if (t.allocated_size) {
  336|       |			printf("Memory leak detected, %I64u bytes allocated at %p by thread %u:\n", t.allocated_size, t.p, t.thread_id);
  337|       |
  338|       |			bool is_grpc;
  339|       |			t.print(h, is_grpc);
  340|       |
  341|       |			if (is_grpc) {
  342|       |				printf("^^^ grpc leak ^^^\n\n");
  343|       |				grpc_leaks += t.allocated_size;
  344|       |			}
  345|       |			else {
  346|       |				printf("^^^ non-grpc leak ^^^\n\n");
  347|       |				total_leaks += t.allocated_size;
  348|       |			}
  349|       |		}
  350|       |	}
  351|       |
  352|       |	if (total_leaks > 0) {
  353|       |		printf("%I64u bytes leaked\n\n", total_leaks);
  354|       |	}
  355|       |
  356|       |	if (grpc_leaks > 0) {
  357|       |		printf("%I64u bytes leaked by gRPC\n\n", grpc_leaks);
  358|       |	}
  359|       |
  360|       |	if ((total_leaks == 0) && (grpc_leaks == 0)) {
  361|       |		printf("No memory leaks detected\n\n");
  362|       |	}
  363|       |
  364|       |	return (total_leaks == 0);
  365|       |}
  366|       |
  367|       |NOINLINE void* operator new(size_t n) { return p2pool::allocate(n); }
  368|       |NOINLINE void* operator new[](size_t n) { return p2pool::allocate(n); }
  369|       |NOINLINE void* operator new(size_t n, const std::nothrow_t&) noexcept { return p2pool::malloc_hook(n); }
  370|       |NOINLINE void* operator new[](size_t n, const std::nothrow_t&) noexcept { return p2pool::malloc_hook(n); }
  371|       |NOINLINE void operator delete(void* p) noexcept { p2pool::free_hook(p); }
  372|       |NOINLINE void operator delete[](void* p) noexcept { p2pool::free_hook(p); }

  374|       |NOINLINE void operator delete[](void* p, size_t) noexcept { p2pool::free_hook(p); }
  375|       |

  377|       |// cppcheck-suppress functionStatic

  379|       |// cppcheck-suppress functionStatic
  380|      0|bool memory_tracking_stop() { return true; }

   27|       |void merkle_hash(const std::vector<hash>& hashes, root_hash& root)
   28|     12|{
   29|     12|	const size_t count = hashes.size();

   31|     12|	if (count == 0) {
   32|      1|		root.clear();
   33|      1|		return;
   34|      1|	}
   35|       |
   36|     11|	const uint8_t* h = hashes[0].h;
   37|       |
   38|     11|	if (count == 1) {
   39|      1|		root = root_hash(hashes[0]);
   40|      1|	}
   41|     10|	else if (count == 2) {
   42|      1|		keccak(h, HASH_SIZE * 2, root.h);
   43|      1|	}
   44|      9|	else {
   45|      9|		size_t cnt = 1;
   46|     31|		do { cnt <<= 1; } while (cnt <= count);
   47|      9|		cnt >>= 1;
   48|       |
   49|      9|		std::vector<hash> tmp_ints(cnt);
   50|       |
   51|      9|		const size_t k = cnt * 2 - count;
   52|      9|		memcpy(tmp_ints.data(), h, k * HASH_SIZE);
   53|       |

   55|     15|			keccak(h + i * HASH_SIZE, HASH_SIZE * 2, tmp_ints[j].h);
   56|     15|		}

   59|     13|			cnt >>= 1;

   62|     40|			}
   63|     13|		}
   64|       |
   65|      9|		keccak(tmp_ints[0].h, HASH_SIZE * 2, root.h);
   66|      9|	}

   69|       |void merkle_hash_full_tree(const std::vector<hash>& hashes, std::vector<std::vector<hash>>& tree)
   70|     18|{
   71|     18|	const size_t count = hashes.size();
   72|       |

   79|     17|	const uint8_t* h = hashes[0].h;
   80|       |
   81|     17|	if (count == 1) {
   82|      2|		tree.push_back(hashes);
   83|      2|	}

   85|      4|		hash tmp;

  224|       |bool get_merkle_proof(const std::vector<std::vector<hash>>& tree, const hash& h, std::vector<hash>& proof, uint32_t& path)

  343|       |void P2PServer::update_peer_connections()
  344|      0|{
  345|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
  346|       |
  347|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time = seconds_since_epoch();

  349|       |
  350|      0|	bool has_good_peers = false;
  351|      0|	m_fastestPeer = nullptr;
  352|       |
  353|      0|	uint64_t total_outgoing_p2pool = 0;
  354|      0|	uint64_t newer_version_p2pool = 0;
  355|       |
  356|      0|	unordered_set<raw_ip> connected_clients;
  357|      0|	unordered_set<std::string_view> connected_clients_domain;

  359|      0|	connected_clients.reserve(m_numConnections);
