  361|      0|	if (!m_socks5Proxy.empty()) {
  362|      0|		connected_clients_domain.reserve(m_numConnections);
  363|      0|	}
  364|       |
  365|      0|	for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
  366|      0|		const int timeout = client->m_handshakeComplete ? 300 : 10;
  367|      0|		if (client->m_lastAlive && (cur_time >= client->m_lastAlive + timeout)) {
  368|      0|			const uint64_t idle_time = static_cast<uint64_t>(cur_time - client->m_lastAlive);
  369|      0|			LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << " has been idle for " << idle_time << " seconds, disconnecting");
  370|      0|			client->close();
  371|      0|			continue;
  372|      0|		}
  373|       |
  374|      0|		if (client->m_handshakeComplete && client->m_lastBroadcastTimestamp) {
  375|       |			// - Side chain is at least 15 minutes newer (last_updated >= client->m_lastBroadcastTimestamp + 900)
  376|       |			// - It's been at least 10 seconds since side chain updated (cur_time >= last_updated + 10)
  377|       |			// - It's been at least 10 seconds since the last block request (peer is not syncing)
  378|       |			// - Peer should have sent a broadcast by now
  379|      0|			if (last_updated && (cur_time >= std::max(last_updated, client->m_lastBlockrequestTimestamp) + 10) && (last_updated >= client->m_lastBroadcastTimestamp + 900)) {
  380|      0|				const uint64_t dt = last_updated - client->m_lastBroadcastTimestamp;
  381|      0|				LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << " is not broadcasting blocks (last update " << dt << " seconds ago)");
  382|      0|				client->ban(DEFAULT_BAN_TIME);
  383|      0|				remove_peer_from_list(client);
  384|      0|				client->close();
  385|      0|				continue;
  386|      0|			}
  387|      0|		}
  388|       |
  389|      0|		if ((client->m_addressType == Client::AddressType::IPv4) || (client->m_addressType == Client::AddressType::IPv6)) {
  390|      0|			connected_clients.insert(client->m_addr);
  391|      0|		}

  393|      0|			const char* c = strchr(client->m_addrString, ':');
  394|      0|			if (c) {
  395|      0|				connected_clients_domain.emplace(client->m_addrString, c - client->m_addrString);
  396|      0|			}
  397|      0|			else {
  398|      0|				connected_clients_domain.emplace(client->m_addrString);
  399|      0|			}
  400|      0|		}
  401|       |
  402|      0|		if (client->is_good()) {
  403|      0|			has_good_peers = true;
  404|      0|			if ((client->m_pingTime >= 0) && (!m_fastestPeer || (m_fastestPeer->m_pingTime > client->m_pingTime))) {
  405|      0|				m_fastestPeer = client;
  406|      0|			}
  407|      0|			if ((client->m_SoftwareID == SoftwareID::P2Pool) && client->m_SoftwareVersion && !client->m_isIncoming) {
  408|      0|				++total_outgoing_p2pool;
  409|      0|				if (client->m_SoftwareVersion > P2POOL_VERSION) {
  410|      0|					++newer_version_p2pool;
  411|      0|				}
  412|      0|			}
  413|      0|		}
  414|      0|	}
  415|       |
  416|      0|	m_newP2PoolVersionDetected = newer_version_p2pool * 5 > total_outgoing_p2pool;
  417|       |
  418|      0|	std::vector<Peer> peer_list;

  420|      0|		MutexLock lock(m_peerListLock);

  423|       |			// Update last seen time for currently connected peers
  424|      0|			for (Peer& p : m_peerList) {
  425|      0|				if (connected_clients.find(p.m_addr) != connected_clients.end()) {
  426|      0|					p.m_lastSeen = cur_time;
  427|      0|				}
  428|      0|			}

  431|      0|			m_peerList.erase(std::remove_if(m_peerList.begin(), m_peerList.end(), [cur_time](const Peer& p) { return p.m_lastSeen + 3600 < cur_time; }), m_peerList.end());
  432|      0|		}
  433|       |
  434|      0|		peer_list = m_peerList;
  435|      0|	}
  436|       |
  437|      0|	uint32_t N = m_maxOutgoingPeers;
  438|       |
  439|       |	// Special case: when we can't find p2pool peers, scan through monerod peers (try 25 peers at a time)
  440|      0|	if (has_good_peers) {
  441|      0|		m_seenGoodPeers = true;
  442|      0|	}

  445|      0|		for (uint32_t i = 0; (i < 25) && !m_peerListMonero.empty(); ++i) {

  447|      0|			m_peerListMonero.pop_back();
  448|      0|		}
  449|      0|		N = static_cast<uint32_t>(peer_list.size());
  450|      0|	}
  451|       |
  452|      0|	uint32_t num_outgoing = m_numConnections - m_numIncomingConnections;
  453|       |
  454|       |	// Try to have at least N/2 (or N if clearnet P2P is disabled) outgoing onion connections
  455|      0|	if (!m_socks5Proxy.empty() && (m_socks5ProxyType == Params::ProxyType::TOR)) {
  456|      0|		std::vector<hash> pubkeys = m_pool->side_chain().seen_onion_pubkeys();
  457|       |
  458|       |		// Add seed nodes
  459|      0|		pubkeys.insert(pubkeys.end(), seed_onion_nodes, seed_onion_nodes + array_size(seed_onion_nodes));
  460|       |
  461|      0|		const uint32_t n = m_pool->params().m_noClearnetP2P ? N : (N / 2);
  462|       |
  463|      0|		for (uint32_t i = m_numOnionConnections; (i < n) && !pubkeys.empty();) {
  464|      0|			const uint64_t k = get_random64() % pubkeys.size();

  466|       |
  467|      0|			int port = DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_ONION;
  468|       |

  470|      0|				if (pubkeys[k] == seed_onion) {
  471|      0|					const SideChain& s = m_pool->side_chain();
  472|      0|					port = s.is_mini() ? DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_MINI : (s.is_nano() ? DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_NANO : DEFAULT_P2P_PORT);
  473|      0|					break;
  474|      0|				}
  475|      0|			}
  476|       |
  477|      0|			if ((connected_clients_domain.find(addr) == connected_clients_domain.end()) && connect_to_peer(addr, port)) {
  478|      0|				++i;
  479|      0|				++num_outgoing;
  480|      0|			}
  481|       |
  482|      0|			pubkeys[k] = pubkeys.back();
  483|      0|			pubkeys.pop_back();
  484|      0|		}
  485|      0|	}
  486|       |
  487|       |	// Try to have at least N/2 (or N if clearnet P2P is disabled) outgoing I2P connections
  488|      0|	if (!m_socks5Proxy.empty() && (m_socks5ProxyType == Params::ProxyType::I2P)) {
  489|      0|		std::vector<hash> dest_hashes = m_pool->side_chain().seen_i2p_dest_hashes();

  491|       |		// Add seed nodes

  495|       |
  496|      0|		for (uint32_t i = m_numI2PConnections; (i < n) && !dest_hashes.empty();) {
  497|      0|			const uint64_t k = get_random64() % dest_hashes.size();
  498|      0|			const std::string addr = to_i2p_b32(dest_hashes[k]);
  499|       |
  500|      0|			int port = DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_I2P;

  502|      0|			for (const hash& seed_i2p : seed_i2p_nodes) {
  503|      0|				if (dest_hashes[k] == seed_i2p) {
  504|      0|					const SideChain& s = m_pool->side_chain();
  505|      0|					port = s.is_mini() ? DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_MINI : (s.is_nano() ? DEFAULT_P2P_PORT_NANO : DEFAULT_P2P_PORT);
  506|      0|					break;
  507|      0|				}
  508|      0|			}
  509|       |
  510|      0|			if ((connected_clients_domain.find(addr) == connected_clients_domain.end()) && connect_to_peer(addr, port)) {
  511|      0|				++i;
  512|      0|				++num_outgoing;
  513|      0|			}
  514|       |
  515|      0|			dest_hashes[k] = dest_hashes.back();
  516|      0|			dest_hashes.pop_back();
  517|      0|		}
  518|      0|	}
  519|       |
  520|       |	// Try to have at least N outgoing connections (N defaults to 10, can be set via --out-peers command line parameter)
  521|      0|	if (!m_pool->params().m_noClearnetP2P) {
  522|      0|		while ((num_outgoing < N) && !peer_list.empty()) {
  523|      0|			const uint64_t k = get_random64() % peer_list.size();
  524|      0|			const Peer& peer = peer_list[k];
  525|       |
  526|      0|			if ((connected_clients.find(peer.m_addr) == connected_clients.end()) && connect_to_peer(peer.m_isV6, peer.m_addr, peer.m_port)) {
  527|      0|				++num_outgoing;
  528|      0|			}
  529|       |
  530|      0|			peer_list[k] = peer_list.back();
  531|      0|			peer_list.pop_back();
  532|      0|		}
  533|      0|	}
  534|       |
  535|      0|	if (!has_good_peers && ((m_timerCounter % 10) == 0) && (SideChain::network_type() == NetworkType::Mainnet)) {
  536|      0|		LOGERR(1, "no connections to other p2pool nodes, check your monerod/p2pool/network/firewall setup!!!");

  538|      0|		if (m_peerListMonero.empty()) {
  539|      0|			load_monerod_peer_list();
  540|      0|		}
  541|      0|	}
  542|       |

  546|      0|			stratum_server->drop_connections_async();

  548|      0|	}

  551|       |void P2PServer::update_peer_list()
  552|      0|{
  553|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
  554|       |
  555|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time = seconds_since_epoch();
  556|      0|	for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
  557|      0|		if (client->is_good() && (cur_time >= client->m_nextOutgoingPeerListRequest)) {
  558|      0|			send_peer_list_request(client, cur_time);
  559|      0|		}
  560|      0|	}
  561|      0|}
  562|       |
  563|       |void P2PServer::send_peer_list_request(P2PClient* client, uint64_t cur_time)
  564|      0|{
  565|      0|	if (client->m_peerListPendingRequests > 0) {
  566|      0|		return;
  567|      0|	}
  568|       |
  569|       |	// Send peer list requests at random intervals (60-120 seconds)
  570|      0|	client->m_nextOutgoingPeerListRequest = cur_time + (PEER_REQUEST_DELAY + (get_random64() % (PEER_REQUEST_DELAY + 1)));
  571|       |
  572|      0|	const bool result = send(client,
  573|      0|		[client](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size)
  574|      0|		{
  575|      0|			LOGINFO(6, "sending PEER_LIST_REQUEST to " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString));
  576|       |
  577|      0|			if (buf_size < 1) {
  578|      0|				return 0;
  579|      0|			}
  580|       |
  581|      0|			*buf = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::PEER_LIST_REQUEST);
  582|      0|			return 1;
  583|      0|		});
  584|       |
  585|      0|	if (result) {
  586|      0|		client->m_lastPeerListRequestTime = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
  587|      0|		++client->m_peerListPendingRequests;
  588|      0|	}
  589|      0|}
  590|       |
  591|       |void P2PServer::save_peer_list_async()
  592|      0|{
  593|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time = seconds_since_epoch();
  594|      0|	if (cur_time < m_peerListLastSaved + 300) {
  595|      0|		return;
  596|      0|	}
  597|       |
  598|      0|	struct Work
  599|      0|	{
  600|      0|		uv_work_t req;
  601|      0|		P2PServer* server;
  602|      0|	};
  603|       |
  604|      0|	Work* work = new Work{};
  605|      0|	work->req.data = work;
  606|      0|	work->server = this;
  607|       |
  608|      0|	const int err = uv_queue_work(&m_loop, &work->req,
  609|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req)
  610|      0|		{
  611|      0|			BACKGROUND_JOB_START(P2PServer::save_peer_list_async);
  612|      0|			reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data)->server->save_peer_list();
  613|      0|		},
  614|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req, int /*status*/)
  615|      0|		{
  616|      0|			delete reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data);
  617|      0|			BACKGROUND_JOB_STOP(P2PServer::save_peer_list_async);
  618|      0|		});
  619|       |
  620|      0|	if (err) {
  621|      0|		LOGERR(1, "save_peer_list_async: uv_queue_work failed, error " << uv_err_name(err));
  622|      0|		delete work;
  623|      0|	}
  624|      0|}
  625|       |
  626|       |void P2PServer::save_peer_list()
  627|      0|{
  628|       |	// This can run both from libuv worker threads and from P2PServer event loop (when shutting down), so make sure it's serialized
  629|      0|	MutexLock save_lock(m_peerListSaveLock);
  630|       |
  631|      0|	const Params& params = m_pool->params();
  632|       |
  633|      0|	const std::string path = params.m_dataDir + saved_peer_list_file_name;
  634|       |
  635|      0|	std::ofstream f(path, std::ios::binary);
  636|       |
  637|      0|	if (!f.is_open()) {
  638|      0|		LOGERR(1, "failed to save peer list " << path << ": error " << errno);
  639|      0|		return;
  640|      0|	}
  641|       |
  642|      0|	std::vector<Peer> peer_list;
  643|      0|	{
  644|      0|		MutexLock lock(m_peerListLock);
  645|      0|		peer_list = m_peerList;
  646|      0|	}
  647|       |
  648|      0|	for (const Peer& p : peer_list) {
  649|      0|		const char* addr_str;
  650|      0|		char addr_str_buf[64];
  651|       |
  652|      0|		if (p.m_isV6) {
  653|      0|			in6_addr addr{};
  654|      0|			memcpy(addr.s6_addr, p.m_addr.data, sizeof(addr.s6_addr));
  655|      0|			addr_str = inet_ntop(AF_INET6, &addr, addr_str_buf, sizeof(addr_str_buf));
  656|      0|			if (addr_str) {
  657|      0|				f << '[' << addr_str << "]:" << p.m_port << '\n';
  658|      0|			}
  659|      0|		}
  660|      0|		else {
  661|      0|			in_addr addr{};
  662|      0|			memcpy(&addr.s_addr, p.m_addr.data + sizeof(raw_ip::ipv4_prefix), sizeof(addr.s_addr));
  663|      0|			addr_str = inet_ntop(AF_INET, &addr, addr_str_buf, sizeof(addr_str_buf));
  664|      0|			if (addr_str) {
  665|      0|				f << addr_str << ':' << p.m_port << '\n';
  666|      0|			}
  667|      0|		}
  668|      0|	}
  669|       |
  670|      0|	f.flush();
  671|      0|	f.close();
  672|       |
  673|      0|	LOGINFO(5, "peer list saved (" << peer_list.size() << " peers)");
  674|       |
  675|      0|	const SideChain& s = m_pool->side_chain();
  676|       |
  677|      0|	if (s.onion_pubkeys_count() > 0) {
  678|      0|		const std::string onion_path = params.m_dataDir + saved_onion_peer_list_file_name;
  679|       |
  680|      0|		f.open(onion_path, std::ios::binary);
  681|       |
  682|      0|		if (!f.is_open()) {
  683|      0|			LOGERR(1, "failed to save onion peer list " << onion_path << ": error " << errno);
  684|      0|		}
  685|      0|		else {
  686|      0|			const std::vector<hash> pubkeys = s.seen_onion_pubkeys();
  687|       |
  688|      0|			for (const hash& h : pubkeys) {
  689|      0|				f << to_onion_v3(h) << '\n';
  690|      0|			}
  691|       |
  692|      0|			f.flush();
  693|      0|			f.close();
  694|       |
  695|      0|			LOGINFO(5, "onion peer list saved (" << pubkeys.size() << " peers)");
  696|      0|		}
  697|      0|	}
  698|       |
  699|      0|	if (s.i2p_dest_hashes_count() > 0) {
  700|      0|		const std::string i2p_path = params.m_dataDir + saved_i2p_peer_list_file_name;
  701|       |
  702|      0|		f.open(i2p_path, std::ios::binary);
  703|       |
  704|      0|		if (!f.is_open()) {
  705|      0|			LOGERR(1, "failed to save I2P peer list " << i2p_path << ": error " << errno);
  706|      0|		}
  707|      0|		else {
  708|      0|			const std::vector<hash> dest_hashes = s.seen_i2p_dest_hashes();
  709|       |

 1062|       |P2PServer::Broadcast::Broadcast(const PoolBlock& block, const PoolBlock* parent)
 1063|      6|	: id(block.m_sidechainId)

 1067|       |

 1069|      6|	const std::vector<uint8_t> mainchain_data = block.serialize_mainchain_data(nullptr, nullptr, &outputs_offset, &outputs_blob_size);
 1070|      6|	const std::vector<uint8_t> sidechain_data = block.serialize_sidechain_data();
 1071|       |
 1072|      6|	data->blob.reserve(mainchain_data.size() + sidechain_data.size());
 1073|      6|	data->blob = mainchain_data;
 1074|      6|	data->blob.insert(data->blob.end(), sidechain_data.begin(), sidechain_data.end());
 1075|       |
 1076|      6|	data->pruned_blob.reserve(mainchain_data.size() + sidechain_data.size() + 16 - outputs_blob_size);
 1077|      6|	data->pruned_blob.assign(mainchain_data.begin(), mainchain_data.begin() + outputs_offset);
 1078|       |
 1079|       |	// 0 outputs in the pruned blob
 1080|      6|	data->pruned_blob.push_back(0);
 1081|       |
 1082|      6|	const uint64_t total_reward = std::accumulate(block.m_outputAmounts.begin(), block.m_outputAmounts.end(), 0ULL,
 1083|      6|		[](uint64_t a, const PoolBlock::TxOutput& b)
 1084|  1.48k|		{
 1085|  1.48k|			return a + b.m_reward;
 1086|  1.48k|		});
 1087|       |
 1088|      6|	writeVarint(total_reward, data->pruned_blob);
 1089|      6|	writeVarint(outputs_blob_size, data->pruned_blob);
 1090|       |
 1091|      6|	data->pruned_blob.insert(data->pruned_blob.end(), block.m_sidechainId.h, block.m_sidechainId.h + HASH_SIZE);
 1092|      6|	data->pruned_blob.insert(data->pruned_blob.end(), mainchain_data.begin() + outputs_offset + outputs_blob_size, mainchain_data.end());
 1093|       |
 1094|      6|	const size_t N = block.m_transactions.size();
 1095|      6|	if ((N > 1) && parent && (parent->m_transactions.size() > 1)) {
 1096|      6|		const uint32_t tx_list_size = static_cast<uint32_t>((N - 1) * HASH_SIZE);
 1097|       |
 1098|      6|		unordered_map<hash, size_t> parent_transactions;
 1099|      6|		parent_transactions.reserve(parent->m_transactions.size());
 1100|       |
 1101|    230|		for (size_t i = 1; i < parent->m_transactions.size(); ++i) {
 1102|    224|			parent_transactions.emplace(parent->m_transactions[i], i);
 1103|    224|		}
 1104|       |
 1105|       |		// Reserve 1 additional byte per transaction to be ready for the worst case (all transactions are different in the parent block)
 1106|      6|		data->compact_blob.reserve(data->pruned_blob.capacity() + (N - 1));
 1107|      6|		data->compact_unpruned_blob.reserve(data->blob.capacity() + (N - 1));
 1108|       |
 1109|       |		// Copy pruned_blob and mainchain_data without the transaction list
 1110|      6|		data->compact_blob.assign(data->pruned_blob.begin(), data->pruned_blob.end() - tx_list_size);
 1111|      6|		data->compact_unpruned_blob.assign(mainchain_data.begin(), mainchain_data.end() - tx_list_size);
 1112|       |
 1113|      6|		std::vector<uint8_t> compact_transactions;
 1114|      6|		compact_transactions.reserve(tx_list_size + (N - 1));
 1115|       |
 1116|       |		// Process transaction hashes one by one
 1117|      6|		size_t num_found = 0;
 1118|    304|		for (size_t i = 1; i < N; ++i) {
 1119|    298|			const hash& tx = block.m_transactions[i];
 1120|    298|			auto it = parent_transactions.find(tx);
 1121|    298|			if (it != parent_transactions.end()) {
 1122|    218|				writeVarint(it->second, compact_transactions);
 1123|    218|				++num_found;
 1124|    218|			}
 1125|     80|			else {
 1126|     80|				compact_transactions.push_back(0);
 1127|     80|				compact_transactions.insert(compact_transactions.end(), tx.h, tx.h + HASH_SIZE);
 1128|     80|			}
 1129|    298|		}
 1130|      6|		LOGINFO(6, "compact blob: " << num_found << '/' << (N - 1) << " transactions were found in the parent block");
 1131|       |
 1132|      6|		data->compact_blob.insert(data->compact_blob.end(), compact_transactions.begin(), compact_transactions.end());
 1133|      6|		data->compact_blob.insert(data->compact_blob.end(), sidechain_data.begin(), sidechain_data.end());
 1134|       |
 1135|      6|		data->compact_unpruned_blob.insert(data->compact_unpruned_blob.end(), compact_transactions.begin(), compact_transactions.end());
 1136|      6|		data->compact_unpruned_blob.insert(data->compact_unpruned_blob.end(), sidechain_data.begin(), sidechain_data.end());
 1137|      6|	}
 1138|       |
 1139|      6|	data->pruned_blob.insert(data->pruned_blob.end(), sidechain_data.begin(), sidechain_data.end());

 1145|       |void P2PServer::broadcast(const PoolBlock& block, const PoolBlock* parent)
 1146|      0|{
 1147|       |	// Don't broadcast blocks when shutting down
 1148|      0|	if (m_finished.load()) {
 1149|      0|		return;
 1150|      0|	}

 1153|       |

 1156|      0|		return;
 1157|      0|	}
 1158|       |
 1159|      0|	if (block.m_txinGenHeight > miner_data.height + 2) {
 1160|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "Trying to broadcast a block " << block.m_sidechainId << " ahead on mainchain (mainchain height " << block.m_txinGenHeight << ", current height is " << miner_data.height << ')');
 1161|      0|		return;
 1162|      0|	}
 1163|       |

 1165|       |

 1167|      0|		data->compact_blob.size() << '/' <<

 1170|      0|		data->blob.size() <<

 1174|       |

 1176|      0|		delete data;

 1186|      0|		m_broadcastQueue.pop_back();

 1188|      0|	}

 1191|       |void P2PServer::on_broadcast()
 1192|      0|{
 1193|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 1194|       |
 1195|      0|	std::vector<Broadcast*> broadcast_queue;
 1196|      0|	broadcast_queue.reserve(2);
 1197|       |
 1198|      0|	{
 1199|      0|		MutexLock lock(m_broadcastLock);
 1200|      0|		broadcast_queue = m_broadcastQueue;
 1201|      0|		m_broadcastQueue.clear();
 1202|      0|	}
 1203|       |
 1204|      0|	if (broadcast_queue.empty()) {
 1205|      0|		return;
 1206|      0|	}
 1207|       |
 1208|      0|	if (pool_block_debug()) {
 1209|      0|		for (Broadcast* data : broadcast_queue) {
 1210|      0|			if (!data->compact_blob.empty()) {
 1211|      0|				PoolBlock check;
 1212|      0|				const int result = check.deserialize(data->compact_blob.data(), data->compact_blob.size(), m_pool->side_chain(), true, true);
 1213|      0|				if (result != 0) {
 1214|      0|					LOGERR(1, "compact blob broadcast is broken, error " << result);
 1215|      0|				}
 1216|      0|			}
 1217|      0|			if (!data->compact_unpruned_blob.empty()) {
 1218|      0|				PoolBlock check;
 1219|      0|				const int result = check.deserialize(data->compact_unpruned_blob.data(), data->compact_unpruned_blob.size(), m_pool->side_chain(), true, false);
 1220|      0|				if (result != 0) {
 1221|      0|					LOGERR(1, "compact unpruned blob broadcast is broken, error " << result);
 1222|      0|				}
 1223|      0|			}
 1224|      0|			{
 1225|      0|				PoolBlock check;
 1226|      0|				const int result = check.deserialize(data->pruned_blob.data(), data->pruned_blob.size(), m_pool->side_chain(), false, true);
 1227|      0|				if (result != 0) {
 1228|      0|					LOGERR(1, "pruned blob broadcast is broken, error " << result);
 1229|      0|				}
 1230|      0|			}
 1231|      0|			{
 1232|      0|				PoolBlock check;
 1233|      0|				const int result = check.deserialize(data->blob.data(), data->blob.size(), m_pool->side_chain(), false, false);
 1234|      0|				if (result != 0) {
 1235|      0|					LOGERR(1, "full blob broadcast is broken, error " << result);
 1236|      0|				}
 1237|      0|			}
 1238|      0|		}
 1239|      0|	}
 1240|       |
 1241|      0|	for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
 1242|      0|		if (!client->is_good()) {
 1243|      0|			continue;
 1244|      0|		}
 1245|       |
 1246|      0|		for (Broadcast* data : broadcast_queue) {
 1247|      0|			const bool result = send(client, [client, data](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 1248|      0|			{
 1249|      0|				uint8_t* p = buf;
 1250|       |
 1251|      0|				const hash* a = client->m_broadcastedHashes;
 1252|      0|				const hash* b = client->m_broadcastedHashes + array_size(&P2PClient::m_broadcastedHashes);
 1253|       |
 1254|       |				// If this peer already broadcasted this block to us, we don't need to broadcast it back, we just need to notify the peer
 1255|      0|				if ((client->m_protocolVersion >= PROTOCOL_VERSION_1_2) && (std::find(a, b, data->id) != b)) {
 1256|      0|					LOGINFO(6, "sending BLOCK_NOTIFY to " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString));
 1257|       |
 1258|      0|					if (buf_size < 1 + HASH_SIZE) {
 1259|      0|						return 0;
 1260|      0|					}
 1261|       |
 1262|      0|					*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::BLOCK_NOTIFY);
 1263|       |
 1264|      0|					memcpy(p, data->id.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1265|      0|					p += HASH_SIZE;
 1266|       |
 1267|      0|					return p - buf;
 1268|      0|				}
 1269|       |
 1270|      0|				bool has_parent = std::find(a, b, data->parent_hash) != b;
 1271|      0|				bool has_uncles = true;
 1272|       |
 1273|      0|				for (const hash& id : data->uncle_hashes) {
 1274|      0|					if (std::find(a, b, id) == b) {
 1275|      0|						has_uncles = false;
 1276|      0|						break;
 1277|      0|					}
 1278|      0|				}
 1279|       |
 1280|      0|				Broadcast::Type what;
 1281|       |
 1282|      0|				size_t cur_broadcast_size;
 1283|       |
 1284|      0|				if (has_parent && has_uncles) {
 1285|      0|					what = Broadcast::PRUNED;
 1286|      0|					cur_broadcast_size = data->pruned_blob.size();
 1287|      0|				}
 1288|      0|				else {
 1289|      0|					what = Broadcast::FULL;
 1290|      0|					cur_broadcast_size = data->blob.size();
 1291|      0|				}
 1292|       |
 1293|      0|				if (has_parent && (client->m_protocolVersion >= PROTOCOL_VERSION_1_1)) {
 1294|      0|					if (has_uncles && !data->compact_blob.empty() && (data->compact_blob.size() < cur_broadcast_size)) {
 1295|      0|						what = Broadcast::COMPACT;
 1296|      0|						cur_broadcast_size = data->compact_blob.size();
 1297|      0|					}
 1298|       |
 1299|      0|					if (!data->compact_unpruned_blob.empty() && (data->compact_unpruned_blob.size() < cur_broadcast_size)) {
 1300|      0|						what = Broadcast::COMPACT_UNPRUNED;
 1301|      0|						cur_broadcast_size = data->compact_unpruned_blob.size();
 1302|      0|					}
 1303|      0|				}
 1304|       |
 1305|      0|				static constexpr char broadcast_names[4][19] = {
 1306|      0|					"(full)            ",
 1307|      0|					"(pruned)          ",
 1308|      0|					"(compact)         ",
 1309|      0|					"(compact unpruned)"
 1310|      0|				};
 1311|       |
 1312|      0|				LOGINFO((what == Broadcast::FULL) ? 5 : 6, "sending BLOCK_BROADCAST " << broadcast_names[static_cast<int>(what)] << " to " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString));
 1313|       |
 1314|      0|				const std::vector<uint8_t>& blob =
 1315|      0|					(what == Broadcast::PRUNED)           ? data->pruned_blob :
 1316|      0|					(what == Broadcast::COMPACT)          ? data->compact_blob :
 1317|      0|					(what == Broadcast::COMPACT_UNPRUNED) ? data->compact_unpruned_blob :
 1318|      0|					                                     data->blob;
 1319|       |
 1320|      0|				const uint32_t len = static_cast<uint32_t>(blob.size());
 1321|      0|				if (buf_size < 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + len) {
 1322|      0|					return 0;
 1323|      0|				}
 1324|       |
 1325|      0|				*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>((what >= Broadcast::COMPACT) ? MessageId::BLOCK_BROADCAST_COMPACT : MessageId::BLOCK_BROADCAST);
 1326|       |
 1327|      0|				memcpy(p, &len, sizeof(uint32_t));
 1328|      0|				p += sizeof(uint32_t);
 1329|       |
 1330|      0|				if (len) {
 1331|      0|					memcpy(p, blob.data(), len);
 1332|      0|					p += len;
 1333|      0|				}
 1334|       |
 1335|      0|				return p - buf;
 1336|      0|			});
 1337|      0|			if (!result) {
 1338|      0|				LOGWARN(5, "failed to broadcast a block to " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << ", disconnecting");
 1339|      0|				client->close();
 1340|      0|				break;
 1341|      0|			}
 1342|      0|		}
 1343|      0|	}
 1344|       |
 1345|      0|	for (const Broadcast* data : broadcast_queue) {
 1346|      0|		const double t = static_cast<double>(microseconds_since_epoch() - data->received_timestamp) * 1e-3;
 1347|      0|		LOGINFO(5, "Block " << data->id << " took " << t << " ms to process and relay to other peers");
 1348|      0|		delete data;
 1349|      0|	}

 1428|       |int P2PServer::deserialize_block(const uint8_t* buf, uint32_t size, bool compact, bool allow_pruned, uint64_t received_timestamp)
 1429|      0|{

 1431|       |

 1434|      0|		(m_blockDeserializeBufAllowPruned == allow_pruned) &&
 1435|      0|		(memcmp(m_blockDeserializeBuf.data(), buf, size) == 0)) {
 1436|      0|		m_block->reset_offchain_data();

 1438|      0|	}
 1439|      0|	else {
 1440|      0|		result = m_block->deserialize(buf, size, m_pool->side_chain(), compact, allow_pruned);
 1441|       |
 1442|       |		// Cache only successfully deserialized blocks
 1443|      0|		if (result == 0) {
 1444|      0|			m_blockDeserializeBuf.assign(buf, buf + size);
 1445|      0|			m_blockDeserializeBufCompact = compact;
 1446|      0|			m_blockDeserializeBufAllowPruned = allow_pruned;
 1447|      0|		}
 1448|      0|		else {
 1449|      0|			m_blockDeserializeBuf.clear();

 1451|       |
 1452|      0|		m_lookForMissingBlocks = true;
 1453|      0|	}
 1454|       |

 1469|       |void P2PServer::on_timer()
 1470|      0|{
 1471|      0|	if (m_pool->stopped()) {
 1472|      0|		return;
 1473|      0|	}
 1474|       |
 1475|      0|	const uint64_t t = ++m_timerCounter;
 1476|       |
 1477|      0|	if ((t == 1) && !m_initialPeerList.empty()) {
 1478|      0|		connect_to_peers(m_initialPeerList);
 1479|      0|		m_initialPeerList.clear();
 1480|      0|		return;
 1481|      0|	}
 1482|       |
 1483|      0|	flush_cache();
 1484|      0|	download_missing_blocks();
 1485|      0|	update_peer_list();
 1486|      0|	save_peer_list_async();

 1493|      0|	clean_monero_block_broadcasts();

 1496|      0|	if ((t % 30) == 3) {

 1498|       |
 1499|      0|		s.cleanup_incoming_blocks();
 1500|       |
 1501|      0|		if (s.chainTip() && (seconds_since_epoch() >= s.last_updated() + s.block_time() * 30)) {
 1502|      0|			LOGWARN(1, "Sidechain seems to be stuck and/or out of sync");

 1506|       |
 1507|       |void P2PServer::flush_cache()
 1508|      0|{
 1509|      0|	if (!m_cache || ((m_timerCounter % 30) != 2)) {
 1510|      0|		return;
 1511|      0|	}
 1512|       |
 1513|      0|	struct Work
 1514|      0|	{
 1515|      0|		uv_work_t req;
 1516|      0|		BlockCache* cache;
 1517|      0|	};
 1518|       |
 1519|      0|	Work* work = new Work{};
 1520|      0|	work->req.data = work;
 1521|      0|	work->cache = m_cache;
 1522|       |
 1523|      0|	const int err = uv_queue_work(&m_loop, &work->req,
 1524|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req)
 1525|      0|		{
 1526|      0|			BACKGROUND_JOB_START(P2PServer::flush_cache);
 1527|      0|			reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data)->cache->flush();
 1528|      0|		},
 1529|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req, int)
 1530|      0|		{
 1531|      0|			delete reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data);
 1532|      0|			BACKGROUND_JOB_STOP(P2PServer::flush_cache);
 1533|      0|		});
 1534|       |
 1535|      0|	if (err) {
 1536|      0|		LOGERR(1, "flush_cache: uv_queue_work failed, error " << uv_err_name(err));
 1537|      0|		delete work;
 1538|      0|	}
 1539|      0|}
 1540|       |
 1541|       |void P2PServer::download_missing_blocks()
 1542|      0|{
 1543|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 1544|       |
 1545|      0|	if (!m_lookForMissingBlocks) {
 1546|      0|		return;
 1547|      0|	}
 1548|       |
 1549|      0|	unordered_set<hash> missing_blocks;
 1550|      0|	m_pool->side_chain().get_missing_blocks(missing_blocks);
 1551|       |
 1552|      0|	if (missing_blocks.empty()) {
 1553|      0|		m_lookForMissingBlocks = false;
 1554|      0|		m_missingBlockRequests.clear();
 1555|      0|		m_blockNotifyRequests.clear();
 1556|      0|		return;
 1557|      0|	}
 1558|       |
 1559|      0|	if (m_numConnections == 0) {
 1560|      0|		return;
 1561|      0|	}
 1562|       |
 1563|      0|	std::vector<P2PClient*> clients;
 1564|      0|	clients.reserve(m_numConnections);
 1565|       |
 1566|      0|	for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
 1567|      0|		if (client->is_good()) {
 1568|      0|			clients.emplace_back(client);
 1569|      0|		}
 1570|      0|	}
 1571|       |
 1572|      0|	if (clients.empty()) {
 1573|      0|		return;
 1574|      0|	}
 1575|       |
 1576|      0|	ReadLock lock2(m_cachedBlocksLock);
 1577|       |
 1578|       |	// Try to download each block from a random client
 1579|      0|	for (const hash& id : missing_blocks) {
 1580|      0|		P2PClient* client = clients[get_random64() % clients.size()];
 1581|       |
 1582|      0|		if (client->m_blockPendingRequests.size() >= MAX_PENDING_BLOCK_REQUESTS) {
 1583|       |			// Too many pending requests to this peer
 1584|      0|			continue;
 1585|      0|		}
 1586|       |
 1587|      0|		if (m_cachedBlocks) {
 1588|      0|			auto it = m_cachedBlocks->find(id);
 1589|      0|			if (it != m_cachedBlocks->end()) {
 1590|      0|				LOGINFO(5, "using cached block for id = " << id);
 1591|      0|				if (!client->handle_incoming_block_async(it->second)) {
 1592|      0|					LOGERR(5, "download_missing_blocks: handle_incoming_block_async failed");
 1593|      0|				}
 1594|      0|				continue;
 1595|      0|			}
 1596|      0|		}
 1597|       |
 1598|      0|		const auto it = m_missingBlockRequests.emplace(client->m_peerId, hash64(id));
 1599|      0|		if (!it.second) {
 1600|       |			// We already asked this peer about this block
 1601|       |			// Don't try to ask another peer, leave it for another timer tick
 1602|      0|			continue;
 1603|      0|		}
 1604|       |
 1605|      0|		const bool result = send(client,
 1606|      0|			[&id, client](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 1607|      0|			{
 1608|      0|				LOGINFO(5, "[download_missing_blocks] sending BLOCK_REQUEST for id = " << id << " to " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString));
 1609|       |
 1610|      0|				if (buf_size < 1 + HASH_SIZE) {
 1611|      0|					return 0;
 1612|      0|				}
 1613|       |
 1614|      0|				uint8_t* p = buf;
 1615|       |
 1616|      0|				*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::BLOCK_REQUEST);
 1617|       |
 1618|      0|				memcpy(p, id.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1619|      0|				p += HASH_SIZE;
 1620|       |
 1621|      0|				return p - buf;
 1622|      0|			});
 1623|       |
 1624|      0|		if (result) {
 1625|      0|			client->m_blockPendingRequests.push_back(hash64(id));
 1626|      0|		}
 1627|      0|		else {
 1628|      0|			m_missingBlockRequests.erase(it.first);
 1629|      0|		}
 1630|      0|	}

 1633|       |void P2PServer::check_host()
 1634|      0|{
 1635|      0|	if (!m_pool->startup_finished()) {
 1636|      0|		return;
 1637|      0|	}
 1638|       |
 1639|      0|	if (!m_pool->zmq_running()) {
 1640|      0|		LOGERR(1, "ZMQ is not running, restarting it");
 1641|      0|		m_pool->reconnect_to_host();
 1642|      0|		return;
 1643|      0|	}
 1644|       |
 1645|      0|	const uint64_t height = m_pool->miner_data().height;
 1646|      0|	const SideChain& side_chain = m_pool->side_chain();
 1647|       |
 1648|       |	// If the latest 5 side chain blocks are 2 or more Monero blocks ahead, then the node is probably stuck

 1654|      0|			m_pool->reconnect_to_host();
 1655|      0|			return;
 1656|      0|		}
 1657|      0|	}

 1662|       |	// If there were no ZMQ messages in the last 5 minutes, then the node is probably stuck

 1664|      0|		const uint64_t dt = static_cast<uint64_t>(cur_time - last_active);

 1858|       |void P2PServer::broadcast_aux_job_donation(const uint8_t* data, uint32_t data_size, uint64_t timestamp, const P2PClient* source, bool duplicate_check_done)
 1859|      0|{
 1860|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 1861|       |
 1862|      0|	if (!duplicate_check_done) {
 1863|      0|		hash digest;
 1864|      0|		SHA256(data, data_size, digest.h);
 1865|       |
 1866|       |		// Every message can be received from multiple peers, so broadcast it only once
 1867|      0|		if (!m_auxJobMessages.emplace(*digest.u64(), timestamp).second) {
 1868|      0|			return;
 1869|      0|		}
 1870|      0|	}
 1871|       |
 1872|      0|	m_auxJobLastMessage.assign(data, data + data_size);
 1873|      0|	m_auxJobLastMessageTimestamp = timestamp;
 1874|       |

 1877|      0|			continue;
 1878|      0|		}
 1879|      0|		send_aux_job_donation(client, data, data_size);
 1880|      0|	}

 1883|       |void P2PServer::send_aux_job_donation(P2PServer::P2PClient* client, const uint8_t* data, uint32_t data_size)
 1884|      0|{
 1885|      0|	const bool result = send(client, [client, data, data_size](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t {

 1887|       |
 1888|      0|		if (buf_size < 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + data_size) {
 1889|      0|			return 0;
 1890|      0|		}
 1891|       |
 1892|      0|		uint8_t* p = buf;
 1893|       |
 1894|      0|		*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::AUX_JOB_DONATION);
 1895|       |

 1897|      0|		p += sizeof(uint32_t);
 1898|       |
 1899|      0|		memcpy(p, data, data_size);
 1900|      0|		p += data_size;
 1901|       |
 1902|      0|		return p - buf;

 1911|       |void P2PServer::clean_aux_job_messages()
 1912|      0|{
 1913|      0|	if ((m_timerCounter & 255) != 0) {
 1914|      0|		return;
 1915|      0|	}
 1916|       |
 1917|      0|	if (m_auxJobMessages.empty()) {
 1918|      0|		return;
 1919|      0|	}
 1920|       |
 1921|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time = time(nullptr);
 1922|       |
 1923|      0|	for (auto it = m_auxJobMessages.begin(); it != m_auxJobMessages.end();) {
 1924|       |		// Delete old messages only after 3x the timeout to give some leeway for system clock adjustments
 1925|      0|		if (cur_time > it->second + static_cast<uint64_t>(AUX_JOB_TIMEOUT * 3)) {
 1926|      0|			it = m_auxJobMessages.erase(it);
 1927|      0|		}
 1928|      0|		else {
 1929|      0|			++it;
 1930|      0|		}
 1931|      0|	}

 2036|       |
 2037|       |void P2PServer::broadcast_monero_block(const uint8_t* data, uint32_t data_size, const P2PClient* source, bool duplicate_check_done)
 2038|      0|{
 2039|      0|	check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 2040|       |
 2041|      0|	if (data_size < sizeof(MoneroBlockBroadcastHeader)) {
 2042|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "broadcast_monero_block: data_size is too small: " << data_size);
 2043|      0|		return;
 2044|      0|	}
 2045|       |
 2046|      0|	if (data_size > MAX_BLOCK_SIZE) {
 2047|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "broadcast_monero_block: data_size is too big: " << data_size);
 2048|      0|		return;
 2049|      0|	}
 2050|       |
 2051|      0|	hash digest;
 2052|      0|	SHA256(data + sizeof(MoneroBlockBroadcastHeader), data_size - sizeof(MoneroBlockBroadcastHeader), digest.h);
 2053|       |
 2054|      0|	if (!duplicate_check_done && !store_monero_block_broadcast(digest)) {
 2055|      0|		LOGINFO(6, "broadcast_monero_block: skipping duplicate broadcast");
 2056|      0|		return;
 2057|      0|	}
 2058|       |
 2059|      0|	for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {

 2179|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::on_connect()
 2180|      0|{
 2181|      0|	P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 2182|       |
 2183|      0|	if (!server) {
 2184|      0|		return false;
 2185|      0|	}
 2186|       |
 2187|      0|	if (m_isIncoming && (server->m_numIncomingConnections > server->m_maxIncomingPeers)) {
 2188|      0|		LOGINFO(5, "Connection from " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << log::NoColor() << " rejected (incoming connections limit was reached)");
 2189|      0|		return false;
 2190|      0|	}
 2191|       |
 2192|       |	// Don't allow multiple connections to/from the same IP or domain (except localhost which can have up to 10 incoming connections)
 2193|      0|	if ((m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName) || !m_addr.is_localhost()) {
 2194|      0|		for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(server->m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != server->m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
 2195|      0|			if (client == this) {
 2196|      0|				continue;
 2197|      0|			}
 2198|       |
 2199|      0|			bool same = false;
 2200|       |

 2202|      0|				const char* a = strchr(m_addrString,         ':');

 2204|       |

 2206|      0|				const size_t b_len = b ? (b - client->m_addrString) : strlen(client->m_addrString);
 2207|       |
 2208|      0|				same = (a_len == b_len) && (memcmp(client->m_addrString, m_addrString, a_len) == 0);
 2209|      0|			}
 2210|      0|			else if ((m_addressType != AddressType::DomainName) && (client->m_addressType != AddressType::DomainName)) {
 2211|       |				// First catch the exact match between different combinations of IPv4 and IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses
 2212|      0|				same = client->m_addr == m_addr;
 2213|       |
 2214|       |				// For full IPv6 addresses, check the /64 prefix
 2215|      0|				if (!same && !m_addr.is_ipv4_prefix() && !client->m_addr.is_ipv4_prefix()) {

 2217|      0|				}
 2218|      0|			}
 2219|       |
 2220|      0|			if (same) {
 2221|      0|				LOGINFO(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " is already connected as " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString));

 2223|      0|			}

 2238|      0|				}
 2239|      0|			}
 2240|      0|		}

 2244|      0|		m_connectedDomain = true;
 2245|       |

 2248|      0|		} else {
 2249|      0|			++server->m_numOnionConnections;

 2306|      0|				return false;

 2323|      0|			if (m_handshakeComplete) {

 2325|      0|				ban(DEFAULT_BAN_TIME);
 2326|      0|				server->remove_peer_from_list(this);
 2327|      0|				return false;
 2328|      0|			}

 2334|      0|				if (!on_handshake_solution(buf + 1)) {
 2335|      0|					ban(DEFAULT_BAN_TIME);

 2486|      0|					return false;
 2487|      0|				}

 2489|      0|				if (bytes_left >= 2u + num_peers * 19u) {
 2490|      0|					bytes_read = 2u + num_peers * 19u;
 2491|       |

 2505|      0|				bytes_read = 1 + HASH_SIZE;
 2506|      0|				on_block_notify(buf + 1);
 2507|      0|			}
 2508|      0|			break;

 2520|      0|					return false;
 2521|      0|				}

 2529|      0|						return false;
 2530|      0|					}
 2531|      0|				}

 2533|      0|			break;

 2545|      0|					return false;
 2546|      0|				}
 2547|       |
 2548|      0|				if (bytes_left >= 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + msg_size) {
 2549|      0|					bytes_read = 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + msg_size;
 2550|       |

 2554|      0|						return false;
 2555|      0|					}
 2556|      0|				}
 2557|      0|			}
 2558|      0|			break;
 2559|      0|		}
 2560|       |

 2566|      0|		}
 2567|       |

 2569|      0|			buf += bytes_read;
 2570|      0|			bytes_left -= bytes_read;
 2571|      0|			m_lastAlive = seconds_since_epoch();
 2572|      0|		}
 2573|      0|	} while (bytes_read && bytes_left);

 2578|      0|		if (m_numRead > 0) {

 2611|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::send_handshake_challenge()
 2612|      0|{
 2613|      0|	P2PServer* owner = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 2614|      0|	m_handshakeChallenge = owner->get_random64();
 2615|       |
 2616|      0|	return owner->send(this,
 2617|      0|		[this, owner](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 2618|      0|		{
 2619|      0|			LOGINFO(5, "sending HANDSHAKE_CHALLENGE to " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString));
 2620|       |
 2621|      0|			if (buf_size < 1 + CHALLENGE_SIZE + sizeof(uint64_t)) {
 2622|      0|				return 0;
 2623|      0|			}
 2624|       |
 2625|      0|			uint8_t* p = buf;
 2626|       |
 2627|      0|			*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::HANDSHAKE_CHALLENGE);
 2628|       |
 2629|      0|			uint64_t k = m_handshakeChallenge;
 2630|      0|			for (int i = 0; i < CHALLENGE_SIZE; ++i) {
 2631|      0|				*(p++) = k & 0xFF;
 2632|      0|				k >>= 8;
 2633|      0|			}
 2634|       |
 2635|      0|			bool is_tor = false;
 2636|      0|			bool is_i2p = false;
 2637|       |
 2638|      0|			if (m_isIncoming && (m_addressType != AddressType::DomainName) && m_addr.is_localhost()) {
 2639|       |				// Incoming localhost connection. See if TOR/I2P are configured.
 2640|       |				// Mixed TOR/I2P will make it choose a special "TOR+I2P" peer ID in get_peerId.
 2641|       |				// This is a compromise we have to make - no way to distinguish TOR/I2P here.
 2642|      0|				const Params& params = owner->m_pool->params();
 2643|       |
 2644|      0|				is_tor = !params.m_onionPubkey.empty();
 2645|      0|				is_i2p = !params.m_i2pDestinationHash.empty();
 2646|      0|			}
 2647|      0|			else if ((m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName) && !m_isIncoming && (strstr(m_addrString, ".onion:"))) {
 2648|      0|				is_tor = true;
 2649|      0|				is_i2p = false;
 2650|      0|			}
 2651|      0|			else if ((m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName) && !m_isIncoming && (strstr(m_addrString, ".b32.i2p:"))) {
 2652|      0|				is_tor = false;
 2653|      0|				is_i2p = true;
 2654|      0|			}
 2655|      0|			else if ((m_addressType != AddressType::DomainName) && !(m_isIncoming && m_addr.is_localhost())) {
 2656|      0|				is_tor = false;
 2657|      0|				is_i2p = false;
 2658|      0|			}
 2659|       |
 2660|      0|			k = owner->get_peerId(is_tor, is_i2p);
 2661|      0|			memcpy(p, &k, sizeof(uint64_t));
 2662|      0|			p += sizeof(uint64_t);
 2663|       |
 2664|      0|			return p - buf;
 2665|      0|		});

 2667|       |
 2668|       |void P2PServer::P2PClient::send_handshake_solution(const uint8_t (&challenge)[CHALLENGE_SIZE])
 2669|      0|{
 2670|      0|	P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 2671|       |
 2672|      0|	struct Work
 2673|      0|	{
 2674|      0|		uv_work_t req = {};
 2675|      0|		P2PClient* client = nullptr;
 2676|      0|		P2PServer* server = nullptr;
 2677|      0|		uint32_t reset_counter = 0;
 2678|      0|		bool is_incoming = false;
 2679|       |
 2680|      0|		uint8_t challenge[CHALLENGE_SIZE] = {};
 2681|      0|		uint64_t salt = 0;
 2682|      0|		uint8_t solution_salt[CHALLENGE_SIZE] = {};
 2683|      0|		hash solution;
 2684|      0|	};
 2685|       |
 2686|      0|	Work* work = new Work{};
 2687|      0|	work->req.data = work;
 2688|      0|	work->client = this;
 2689|      0|	work->server = server;
 2690|      0|	work->reset_counter = m_resetCounter.load();
 2691|      0|	work->is_incoming = m_isIncoming;
 2692|       |
 2693|      0|	memcpy(work->challenge, challenge, CHALLENGE_SIZE);
 2694|      0|	work->salt = server->get_random64();
 2695|       |
 2696|      0|	const int err = uv_queue_work(&server->m_loop, &work->req,
 2697|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req)
 2698|      0|		{
 2699|      0|			BACKGROUND_JOB_START(P2PServer::send_handshake_solution);
 2700|       |
 2701|      0|			Work* work = reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data);
 2702|      0|			const std::vector<uint8_t>& consensus_id = work->server->m_pool->side_chain().consensus_id();
 2703|      0|			const int consensus_id_size = static_cast<int>(consensus_id.size());
 2704|       |
 2705|      0|			for (size_t iter = 1;; ++iter, ++work->salt) {
 2706|      0|				uint64_t k = work->salt;
 2707|      0|				for (size_t i = 0; i < CHALLENGE_SIZE; ++i) {
 2708|      0|					work->solution_salt[i] = k & 0xFF;
 2709|      0|					k >>= 8;
 2710|      0|				}
 2711|       |
 2712|      0|				keccak_custom(
 2713|      0|					[work, &consensus_id, consensus_id_size](int offset) -> uint8_t
 2714|      0|					{
 2715|      0|						if (offset < CHALLENGE_SIZE) {
 2716|      0|							return work->challenge[offset];
 2717|      0|						}
 2718|      0|						offset -= CHALLENGE_SIZE;
 2719|       |
 2720|      0|						if (offset < consensus_id_size) {
 2721|      0|							return consensus_id[offset];
 2722|      0|						}
 2723|       |
 2724|      0|						return work->solution_salt[offset - consensus_id_size];
 2725|      0|					}, CHALLENGE_SIZE * 2 + static_cast<int>(consensus_id.size()), work->solution.h, HASH_SIZE);
 2726|       |
 2727|       |				// We might've been disconnected while working on the challenge, do nothing in this case
 2728|      0|				if (work->client->is_gone(work->reset_counter)) {
 2729|      0|					return;
 2730|      0|				}
 2731|       |
 2732|      0|				if (work->is_incoming) {
 2733|       |					// This is an incoming connection, so it must do PoW, not us
 2734|      0|					return;
 2735|      0|				}
 2736|       |
 2737|      0|				const uint64_t* value = work->solution.u64();
 2738|       |
 2739|      0|				uint64_t high;
 2740|      0|				umul128(value[HASH_SIZE / sizeof(uint64_t) - 1], CHALLENGE_DIFFICULTY, &high);
 2741|       |
 2742|      0|				if (high == 0) {
 2743|      0|					LOGINFO(5, "found handshake challenge solution after " << iter << " iterations");
 2744|      0|					return;
 2745|      0|				}
 2746|      0|			}
 2747|      0|		},
 2748|      0|		[](uv_work_t* req, int)
 2749|      0|		{
 2750|      0|			Work* work = reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data);
 2751|       |
 2752|      0|			ON_SCOPE_LEAVE(
 2753|      0|				[work]()
 2754|      0|				{
 2755|      0|					delete work;
 2756|      0|					BACKGROUND_JOB_STOP(P2PServer::send_handshake_solution);
 2757|      0|				});
 2758|       |
 2759|       |			// We might've been disconnected while working on the challenge, do nothing in this case
 2760|      0|			if (work->client->is_gone(work->reset_counter)) {
 2761|      0|				return;
 2762|      0|			}
 2763|       |
 2764|      0|			const bool result = work->server->send(work->client,
 2765|      0|				[work](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 2766|      0|				{
 2767|      0|					LOGINFO(5, "sending HANDSHAKE_SOLUTION to " << static_cast<char*>(work->client->m_addrString));
 2768|       |
 2769|      0|					if (buf_size < 1 + HASH_SIZE + CHALLENGE_SIZE + 1 + sizeof(int32_t) + 1 + HASH_SIZE) {
 2770|      0|						return 0;
 2771|      0|					}
 2772|       |
 2773|      0|					uint8_t* p = buf;
 2774|       |
 2775|      0|					*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::HANDSHAKE_SOLUTION);
 2776|       |
 2777|      0|					memcpy(p, work->solution.h, HASH_SIZE);
 2778|      0|					p += HASH_SIZE;
 2779|       |
 2780|      0|					memcpy(p, work->solution_salt, CHALLENGE_SIZE);
 2781|      0|					p += CHALLENGE_SIZE;
 2782|       |
 2783|      0|					if (work->client->m_handshakeComplete && !work->client->m_handshakeInvalid) {
 2784|      0|						work->client->on_after_handshake(p);
 2785|      0|					}
 2786|       |
 2787|      0|					return p - buf;
 2788|      0|				});
 2789|       |
 2790|      0|			if (result) {
 2791|      0|				work->client->m_handshakeSolutionSent = true;
 2792|       |
 2793|      0|				if (work->client->m_handshakeComplete && work->client->m_handshakeInvalid) {
 2794|      0|					work->client->ban(DEFAULT_BAN_TIME);
 2795|      0|					work->server->remove_peer_from_list(work->client);
 2796|      0|					work->client->close();
 2797|      0|				}
 2798|      0|			}
 2799|      0|			else {
 2800|      0|				work->client->close();
 2801|      0|			}
 2802|      0|		});
 2803|       |
 2804|      0|	if (err) {
 2805|      0|		LOGERR(1, "send_handshake_solution: uv_queue_work failed, error " << uv_err_name(err));
 2806|      0|		delete work;
 2807|      0|	}

 2810|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::check_handshake_solution(const hash& solution, const uint8_t (&solution_salt)[CHALLENGE_SIZE]) const
 2811|      0|{
 2812|      0|	const std::vector<uint8_t>& consensus_id = static_cast<const P2PServer*>(m_owner)->m_pool->side_chain().consensus_id();
 2813|      0|	const int consensus_id_size = static_cast<int>(consensus_id.size());
 2814|       |
 2815|      0|	uint8_t challenge[CHALLENGE_SIZE];
 2816|       |
 2817|      0|	uint64_t k = m_handshakeChallenge;
 2818|      0|	for (size_t i = 0; i < CHALLENGE_SIZE; ++i) {
 2819|      0|		challenge[i] = k & 0xFF;
 2820|      0|		k >>= 8;
 2821|      0|	}
 2822|       |
 2823|      0|	hash check{};
 2824|      0|	keccak_custom(
 2825|      0|		[&challenge, &solution_salt, &consensus_id, consensus_id_size](int offset) -> uint8_t
 2826|      0|		{
 2827|      0|			if (offset < CHALLENGE_SIZE) {
 2828|      0|				return challenge[offset];
 2829|      0|			}
 2830|      0|			offset -= CHALLENGE_SIZE;
 2831|       |
 2832|      0|			if (offset < consensus_id_size) {
 2833|      0|				return consensus_id[offset];
 2834|      0|			}
 2835|       |
 2836|      0|			return solution_salt[offset - consensus_id_size];
 2837|      0|		}, CHALLENGE_SIZE * 2 + static_cast<int>(consensus_id.size()), check.h, HASH_SIZE);
 2838|       |
 2839|      0|	return solution == check;

 2842|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::on_handshake_challenge(const uint8_t* buf)
 2843|      0|{
 2844|      0|	const P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 2845|      0|	server->check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 2846|       |
 2847|      0|	uint8_t challenge[CHALLENGE_SIZE];
 2848|      0|	memcpy(challenge, buf, CHALLENGE_SIZE);
 2849|       |
 2850|      0|	uint64_t peer_id;
 2851|      0|	memcpy(&peer_id, buf + CHALLENGE_SIZE, sizeof(uint64_t));
 2852|       |
 2853|      0|	if (peer_id == 0) {
 2854|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "Peer " << static_cast<const char*>(m_addrString) << " uses invalid peer id 0");
 2855|      0|		return false;
 2856|      0|	}
 2857|       |
 2858|      0|	for (size_t i = 0, n = array_size(server->m_peerId); i < n; ++i) {
 2859|      0|		if (peer_id == server->m_peerId[i]) {

 2861|      0|			return false;
 2862|      0|		}
 2863|      0|	}
 2864|       |

 2866|       |

 2868|      0|		return (c->m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName) || (c->m_isIncoming && c->m_addr.is_localhost());
 2869|      0|	};
 2870|       |
 2871|      0|	for (const P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(server->m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != server->m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
 2872|      0|		if ((client != this) && (client->m_peerId == peer_id)) {
 2873|       |			// Peers use different IDs for different domains
 2874|      0|			if (is_domain(client) == is_domain(this)) {

 2877|      0|				return true;
 2878|      0|			}
 2879|      0|		}
 2880|      0|	}

 2882|      0|	send_handshake_solution(challenge);
 2883|      0|	return true;

 2885|       |
 2886|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::on_handshake_solution(const uint8_t* buf)
 2887|      0|{
 2888|      0|	hash solution;
 2889|      0|	uint8_t solution_salt[CHALLENGE_SIZE];
 2890|      0|	memcpy(&solution, buf, HASH_SIZE);
 2891|      0|	memcpy(solution_salt, buf + HASH_SIZE, CHALLENGE_SIZE);
 2892|       |
 2893|       |	// Check that incoming connection provided enough PoW
 2894|      0|	if (m_isIncoming) {
 2895|      0|		const uint64_t* value = solution.u64();
 2896|       |
 2897|      0|		uint64_t high;
 2898|      0|		umul128(value[HASH_SIZE / sizeof(uint64_t) - 1], CHALLENGE_DIFFICULTY, &high);
 2899|       |
 2900|      0|		if (high) {
 2901|      0|			LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " handshake doesn't have enough PoW");
 2902|      0|			m_handshakeInvalid = true;
 2903|      0|		}
 2904|      0|	}
 2905|       |
 2906|      0|	if (!check_handshake_solution(solution, solution_salt)) {
 2907|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " handshake failed");
 2908|      0|		m_handshakeInvalid = true;
 2909|      0|	}
 2910|       |
 2911|      0|	m_handshakeComplete = true;
 2912|       |
 2913|      0|	if (!m_handshakeInvalid) {
 2914|      0|		LOGINFO(5, "peer " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << log::NoColor() << " handshake completed");
 2915|      0|	}
 2916|       |
 2917|      0|	if (m_handshakeSolutionSent) {
 2918|      0|		if (m_handshakeInvalid) {
 2919|      0|			return false;
 2920|      0|		}
 2921|       |
 2922|      0|		return m_owner->send(this,

 2980|      0|{
 2981|      0|	m_lastBlockrequestTimestamp = seconds_since_epoch();

 2984|      0|	memcpy(id.h, buf, HASH_SIZE);

 2991|       |
 2992|      0|	if (!block && !id.empty()) {
 2993|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "got a request for block with id " << id << " but couldn't find it");
 2994|      0|	}
 2995|       |
 2996|       |	// Notifications about parent and uncle blocks to speed up syncing
 2997|       |	// Only notify about blocks we actually have: a peer relies on these hashes to decide whether it
 2998|       |	// can send us pruned/compact blocks, and we'd ban it if it sent one referencing a block we lack.

 3003|       |

 3008|       |

 3010|      0|			if (sidechain.find_block(uncle)) {
 3011|      0|				notify_blocks.push_back(uncle);
 3012|      0|			}
 3013|      0|		}
 3014|       |
 3015|      0|		if (parent && sidechain.find_block(parent->m_parent)) {
 3016|      0|			notify_blocks.push_back(parent->m_parent);
 3017|      0|		}
 3018|      0|	}
 3019|       |
 3020|      0|	return server->send(this,

 3024|       |
 3025|      0|			const uint32_t len = static_cast<uint32_t>(blob.size());
 3026|       |
 3027|      0|			if (buf_size < 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + len) {
 3028|      0|				return 0;
 3029|      0|			}
 3030|       |
 3031|      0|			uint8_t* p = buf;
 3032|       |
 3033|      0|			if (buf_size >= 1 + sizeof(uint32_t) + len + notify_blocks.size() * (1 + HASH_SIZE)) {
 3034|      0|				for (const hash& id : notify_blocks) {

 3036|       |
 3037|      0|					*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::BLOCK_NOTIFY);
 3038|       |
 3039|      0|					memcpy(p, id.h, HASH_SIZE);
 3040|      0|					p += HASH_SIZE;
 3041|      0|				}
 3042|      0|			}
 3043|       |
 3044|      0|			*(p++) = static_cast<uint8_t>(MessageId::BLOCK_RESPONSE);

 3047|      0|			p += sizeof(uint32_t);

 3051|      0|				p += len;
 3052|      0|			}
 3053|       |
 3054|      0|			return p - buf;
 3055|      0|		});

 3112|       |void P2PServer::clean_broadcast_throttle_data()
 3113|      0|{

 3115|      0|		return;
 3116|      0|	}
 3117|       |

 3120|       |
 3121|       |	// The longest of configured timeouts in LIMITS

 3123|       |
 3124|      0|	if (t < timeout) {
 3125|      0|		return;
 3126|      0|	}
 3127|       |

 3134|      0|		}

 3136|      0|			++it;
 3137|      0|		}

 3140|       |
 3141|       |bool P2PServer::throttle_broadcast(const P2PClient* client, uint64_t timestamp_mcs)
 3142|      0|{
 3143|      0|	static_assert(BroadcastThrottle::check_limits(), "BroadcastThrottle::LIMITS has invalid data (numbers must be non-zero and increasing)");
 3144|       |
 3145|      0|	hash address_hash;
 3146|       |
 3147|      0|	uint8_t buf[sizeof(client->m_addrString) + 1] = {};
 3148|       |
 3149|      0|	if (client->m_addressType == Client::AddressType::DomainName) {
 3150|      0|		const char* c = strchr(client->m_addrString, ':');
 3151|      0|		const size_t addr_len = c ? (c - client->m_addrString) : strlen(client->m_addrString);
 3152|       |
 3153|      0|		buf[0] = 0; // hash domain separation
 3154|      0|		memcpy(buf + 1, client->m_addrString, addr_len);
 3155|       |
 3156|      0|		SHA256(buf, addr_len + 1, address_hash.h);
 3157|      0|	}
 3158|      0|	else if (client->m_addr.is_localhost()) {
 3159|      0|		buf[0] = 1; // hash domain separation
 3160|      0|		memcpy(buf + 1, &client->m_peerId, sizeof(client->m_peerId));
 3161|       |
 3162|      0|		SHA256(buf, sizeof(client->m_peerId) + 1, address_hash.h);
 3163|      0|	}
 3164|      0|	else {
 3165|       |		// Use the /64 prefix for IPv6 addresses
 3166|      0|		const size_t addr_len = !client->m_addr.is_ipv4_prefix() ? (sizeof(client->m_addr.data) - 8) : sizeof(client->m_addr.data);
 3167|       |
 3168|      0|		buf[0] = 2; // hash domain separation
 3169|      0|		memcpy(buf + 1, client->m_addr.data, addr_len);
 3170|       |
 3171|      0|		SHA256(buf, addr_len + 1, address_hash.h);
 3172|      0|	}
 3173|       |
 3174|       |	// 1 second for default block time (P2Pool main/mini), 3 seconds for P2Pool nano
 3175|      0|	const uint64_t time_unit_mcs = (m_pool->side_chain().block_time() * 1'000'000) / 10;
 3176|       |
 3177|      0|	BroadcastThrottle& data = m_broadcastThrottle[address_hash];
 3178|       |
 3179|      0|	for (size_t i = 0; i < BroadcastThrottle::N; ++i) {
 3180|      0|		if (timestamp_mcs >= data.m_startTime[i] + BroadcastThrottle::LIMITS[i][0] * time_unit_mcs) {
 3181|      0|			data.m_startTime[i] = timestamp_mcs;
 3182|      0|			data.m_count[i] = 1;
 3183|      0|		}
 3184|      0|		else {
 3185|      0|			++data.m_count[i];
 3186|       |
 3187|      0|			if (data.m_count[i] > BroadcastThrottle::LIMITS[i][1]) {
 3188|      0|				LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<const char*>(client->m_addrString)
 3189|      0|					<< " broadcasted " << data.m_count[i] << " blocks in "
 3190|      0|					<< (static_cast<double>(timestamp_mcs - data.m_startTime[i]) * 1e-6) << " seconds");
 3191|       |
 3192|      0|				return true;
 3193|      0|			}
 3194|      0|		}
 3195|      0|	}
 3196|       |
 3197|      0|	return false;

 3265|       |
 3266|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::on_peer_list_request(const uint8_t*)
 3267|      0|{
 3268|      0|	P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 3269|      0|	server->check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
 3270|       |
 3271|      0|	Peer peers[PEER_LIST_RESPONSE_MAX_PEERS];
 3272|      0|	uint32_t num_selected_peers = 0;
 3273|       |
 3274|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time = seconds_since_epoch();
 3275|      0|	const bool first = (m_prevPeersSent == 0);
 3276|       |
 3277|       |	// Rate limit peer list requests (send an empty response if the request comes too soon)
 3278|      0|	if (first || (cur_time >= m_prevPeersSent + PEER_REQUEST_DELAY)) {
 3279|      0|		m_prevPeersSent = cur_time;
 3280|       |
 3281|       |		// Send every 4th peer on average, selected at random
 3282|      0|		const uint32_t peers_to_send_target = std::min<uint32_t>(PEER_LIST_RESPONSE_MAX_PEERS, std::max<uint32_t>(1, server->m_numConnections / 4));
 3283|      0|		uint32_t n = 0;
 3284|       |
 3285|      0|		for (P2PClient* client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(server->m_connectedClientsList->m_next); client != server->m_connectedClientsList; client = static_cast<P2PClient*>(client->m_next)) {
 3286|      0|			if (!client->is_good() || (client->m_addr == m_addr) || (client->m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName)) {
 3287|      0|				continue;
 3288|      0|			}
 3289|       |
 3290|      0|			const Peer p{ client->isV6(), client->m_addr, client->m_listenPort, 0, 0 };
 3291|      0|			++n;
 3292|       |
 3293|       |			// Use https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reservoir_sampling algorithm
 3294|      0|			if (num_selected_peers < peers_to_send_target) {
 3295|      0|				peers[num_selected_peers++] = p;
 3296|      0|				continue;
 3297|      0|			}
 3298|       |
 3299|      0|			uint64_t k;
 3300|      0|			umul128(server->get_random64(), n, &k);
 3301|       |
 3302|      0|			if (k < peers_to_send_target) {
 3303|      0|				peers[k] = p;
 3304|      0|			}
 3305|      0|		}
 3306|      0|	}
 3307|       |
 3308|       |	// Protocol version message:
 3309|       |	// - IPv4 address = 255.255.255.255
 3310|       |	// - port = 65535
 3311|       |	// - first 12 bytes of the 16-byte raw IP address are ignored by older clients if it's IPv4
 3312|       |	// - use first 8 bytes of the 16-byte raw IP address to send supported protocol version and p2pool version
 3313|      0|	if (first) {
 3314|      0|		LOGINFO(5, "sending protocol version " << (SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION >> 16) << '.' << (SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION & 0xFFFF)
 3315|      0|			<< ", P2Pool version " << P2POOL_VERSION_MAJOR << '.' << P2POOL_VERSION_MINOR << '.' << P2POOL_VERSION_PATCH
 3316|      0|			<< " to peer " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString));
 3317|       |
 3318|      0|		peers[0] = {};
 3319|       |
 3320|      0|		uint8_t* p = peers[0].m_addr.data;
 3321|       |
 3322|      0|		memcpy(p, &SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION, sizeof(uint32_t));
 3323|      0|		p += sizeof(uint32_t);
 3324|       |
 3325|      0|		memcpy(p, &P2POOL_VERSION, sizeof(uint32_t));
 3326|      0|		p += sizeof(uint32_t);
 3327|       |
 3328|      0|		uint32_t t = static_cast<uint32_t>(SoftwareID::P2Pool);
 3329|      0|		memcpy(p, &t, sizeof(uint32_t));
 3330|      0|		p += sizeof(uint32_t);
 3331|       |
 3332|      0|		t = 0xFFFFFFFFU;
 3333|      0|		memcpy(p, &t, sizeof(uint32_t));
 3334|       |
 3335|      0|		peers[0].m_port = 0xFFFF;
 3336|       |
 3337|      0|		if (num_selected_peers == 0) {
 3338|      0|			num_selected_peers = 1;
 3339|      0|		}
 3340|      0|	}
 3341|       |
 3342|      0|	return server->send(this,
 3343|      0|		[this, &peers, num_selected_peers](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 3344|      0|		{
 3345|      0|			LOGINFO(6, "sending PEER_LIST_RESPONSE to " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString));
 3346|       |
 3347|      0|			if (buf_size < 2 + static_cast<size_t>(num_selected_peers) * 19) {

 3427|      0|					);

 3454|      0|				break;
 3455|      0|			}

 3457|       |

 3530|       |bool P2PServer::P2PClient::on_aux_job_donation(const uint8_t* buf, uint32_t size)
 3531|      0|{
 3532|      0|	P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);
 3533|       |
 3534|       |	// Ignore the same message coming from other peers
 3535|      0|	if ((server->m_auxJobLastMessage.size() == size) && (memcmp(server->m_auxJobLastMessage.data(), buf, size) == 0)) {
 3536|      0|		return true;
 3537|      0|	}
 3538|       |
 3539|      0|	const time_t cur_time = time(nullptr);
 3540|      0|	const uint64_t cur_time_steady = microseconds_since_epoch();
 3541|       |
 3542|       |	// At least 0.5 seconds between messages from the same peer
 3543|      0|	if (cur_time_steady < m_lastAuxJobMessage + 500'000) {
 3544|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent the next AUX_JOB_DONATION message too fast, ignoring");
 3545|      0|		return true;
 3546|      0|	}
 3547|      0|	m_lastAuxJobMessage = cur_time_steady;
 3548|       |
 3549|       |	// Layout of the message:
 3550|       |	//
 3551|       |	// 32 bytes           | Secondary public key
 3552|       |	// 8 bytes            | Secondary public key's expiration timestamp
 3553|       |	// 64 bytes           | Master key signature signing the above 40 bytes
 3554|       |	// (size - 168) bytes | Data
 3555|       |	// 64 bytes           | Secondary key signature signing the data
 3556|       |
 3557|      0|	constexpr uint32_t DATA_OFFSET = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN + 8 + ED25519_SIGNATURE_LEN;
 3558|      0|	constexpr uint32_t OVERHEAD = DATA_OFFSET + ED25519_SIGNATURE_LEN;
 3559|      0|	constexpr uint32_t DATA_ENTRY_SIZE = HASH_SIZE * 2 + sizeof(difficulty_type);
 3560|      0|	constexpr uint32_t MIN_DATA_SIZE = sizeof(uint64_t) + DATA_ENTRY_SIZE;
 3561|       |
 3562|       |	// Ignore invalid or empty messages
 3563|      0|	if (size < OVERHEAD + MIN_DATA_SIZE) {
 3564|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an invalid AUX_JOB_DONATION message (" << size << " < " << (OVERHEAD + MIN_DATA_SIZE) << ')');
 3565|      0|		return false;
 3566|      0|	}
 3567|       |
 3568|       |	// Layout of the data:
 3569|       |	//
 3570|       |	// 8 bytes  | timestamp
 3571|       |	//
 3572|       |	// Next come one or multiple data entries:
 3573|       |	//
 3574|       |	// 32 bytes | aux_id
 3575|       |	// 32 bytes | aux_hash
 3576|       |	// 16 bytes | aux_diff
 3577|       |
 3578|      0|	const uint32_t data_size = size - OVERHEAD;
 3579|       |
 3580|      0|	const int64_t secondary_key_expiration_time = read_unaligned(reinterpret_cast<const int64_t*>(buf + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN));
 3581|       |
 3582|       |	// Ignore messages signed with an outdated secondary key
 3583|      0|	if (cur_time >= secondary_key_expiration_time) {
 3584|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an AUX_JOB_DONATION message with an expired secondary key (" << cur_time << " >= " << secondary_key_expiration_time << ")");
 3585|      0|		return true;
 3586|      0|	}
 3587|       |
 3588|       |	// Check secondary public key's signature
 3589|      0|	uint8_t signature[ED25519_SIGNATURE_LEN];
 3590|      0|	memcpy(signature, buf + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN + 8, sizeof(signature));
 3591|       |
 3592|       |	// Ignore messages with invalid signatures
 3593|      0|	if (!ED25519_verify(buf, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN + 8, signature, ED25519_MASTER_PUBLIC_KEY)) {
 3594|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an AUX_JOB_DONATION message with an invalid master key signature");
 3595|      0|		return true;
 3596|      0|	}
 3597|       |
 3598|      0|	const uint8_t* p = buf + DATA_OFFSET;
 3599|      0|	const uint8_t* data_end = p + data_size;
 3600|       |
 3601|      0|	memcpy(signature, data_end, sizeof(signature));
 3602|       |
 3603|      0|	uint8_t secondary_public_key[ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN];
 3604|      0|	memcpy(secondary_public_key, buf, sizeof(secondary_public_key));
 3605|       |
 3606|       |	// Ignore messages with invalid signatures
 3607|      0|	if (!ED25519_verify(p, data_size, signature, secondary_public_key)) {
 3608|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an AUX_JOB_DONATION message with an invalid secondary key signature");
 3609|      0|		return true;
 3610|      0|	}
 3611|       |
 3612|      0|	const int64_t data_timestamp = read_unaligned(reinterpret_cast<const int64_t*>(p));
 3613|      0|	p += sizeof(int64_t);
 3614|       |
 3615|       |	// Ignore outdated messages
 3616|      0|	if (data_timestamp > std::numeric_limits<int64_t>::max() - AUX_JOB_TIMEOUT) {
 3617|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an invalid AUX_JOB_DONATION message (timestamp = " << data_timestamp << ')');
 3618|      0|		return false;
 3619|      0|	}
 3620|       |
 3621|      0|	if (cur_time >= data_timestamp + AUX_JOB_TIMEOUT) {
 3622|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an outdated AUX_JOB_DONATION message ("  << cur_time << " >= " << data_timestamp << " + " << static_cast<int>(AUX_JOB_TIMEOUT) << ')');
 3623|      0|		return true;
 3624|      0|	}
 3625|       |
 3626|      0|	if ((data_end - p) % DATA_ENTRY_SIZE) {
 3627|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an invalid AUX_JOB_DONATION message (" << (data_end - p) << " is not a multiple of " << DATA_ENTRY_SIZE << ')');
 3628|      0|		return false;
 3629|      0|	}
 3630|       |
 3631|      0|	hash digest;
 3632|      0|	SHA256(buf, size, digest.h);
 3633|       |
 3634|       |	// Ignore repeated old messages
 3635|      0|	if (!server->m_auxJobMessages.emplace(*digest.u64(), data_timestamp).second) {
 3636|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " sent an old AUX_JOB_DONATION message (" << (cur_time - data_timestamp) << " seconds old)");
 3637|      0|		return true;
 3638|      0|	}
 3639|       |
 3640|      0|#if defined(WITH_MERGE_MINING_DONATION)
 3641|      0|	const uint32_t N = static_cast<uint32_t>(data_end - p) / DATA_ENTRY_SIZE;
 3642|       |
 3643|      0|	std::vector<IMergeMiningClient::ChainParameters> chain_params_vec(N);
 3644|       |
 3645|      0|	for (uint32_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
 3646|      0|		IMergeMiningClient::ChainParameters& chain_params = chain_params_vec[i];
 3647|       |
 3648|      0|		memcpy(chain_params.aux_id.h, p, HASH_SIZE);
 3649|      0|		p += HASH_SIZE;
 3650|       |
 3651|      0|		memcpy(chain_params.aux_hash.h, p, HASH_SIZE);
 3652|      0|		p += HASH_SIZE;
 3653|       |
 3654|      0|		chain_params.aux_diff.lo = read_unaligned(reinterpret_cast<const uint64_t*>(p));
 3655|      0|		p += sizeof(uint64_t);
 3656|       |
 3657|      0|		chain_params.aux_diff.hi = read_unaligned(reinterpret_cast<const uint64_t*>(p));
 3658|      0|		p += sizeof(uint64_t);
 3659|      0|	}
 3660|       |
 3661|      0|	server->m_pool->set_aux_job_donation(chain_params_vec);
 3662|      0|#endif
 3663|       |
 3664|      0|	server->broadcast_aux_job_donation(buf, size, data_timestamp, this, true);
 3665|      0|	return true;
 3666|      0|}

 3669|      0|{
 3670|      0|	P2PServer* server = static_cast<P2PServer*>(m_owner);

 3678|      0|	uint32_t size0 = size;
 3679|       |

 3682|       |
 3683|      0|	buf += sizeof(data);
 3684|      0|	size -= sizeof(data);

 3695|      0|	if (!tx_hashes) {

 3704|       |

 3753|       |
 3754|      0|	difficulty_type diff;

 3756|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "MONERO_BLOCK_BROADCAST: couldn't get difficulty at height " << height);
 3757|      0|		diff = miner_data.difficulty;
 3758|      0|	}
 3759|       |
 3760|       |	// Use 90% of this height's difficulty to account for possible altchain deviations
 3761|      0|	diff -= diff / 10;

 3806|       |void P2PServer::monero_block_broadcast_work_cb(uv_work_t* req)
 3807|      0|{
 3808|      0|	BACKGROUND_JOB_START(P2PServer::on_monero_block_broadcast);
 3809|       |
 3810|      0|	MoneroBlockBroadcastWork* work = reinterpret_cast<MoneroBlockBroadcastWork*>(req->data);
 3811|       |
 3812|      0|	if (work->server->is_banned(work->is_v6, work->addr) || work->client->is_gone(work->reset_counter)) {
 3813|      0|		work->server->forget_monero_block_broadcast(work->digest);
 3814|      0|		return;
 3815|      0|	}
 3816|       |
 3817|      0|	const uint8_t* buf = work->message.data();
 3818|       |
 3819|      0|	MoneroBlockBroadcastHeader data;
 3820|      0|	memcpy(&data, buf, sizeof(data));
 3821|       |
 3822|      0|	buf += sizeof(data);
 3823|       |
 3824|      0|	hash hashes[3] = { {}, keccak_0x00, {} };
 3825|       |
 3826|       |	// "miner_tx_size - 1" because the last byte is 0x00 (base rct data), it goes into the second hash
 3827|      0|	keccak(buf + data.header_size, static_cast<int>(data.miner_tx_size) - 1, hashes[0].h);
 3828|       |
 3829|      0|	std::vector<hash> transactions(work->num_transactions + 1);
 3830|      0|	keccak(reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(hashes), sizeof(hashes), transactions[0].h);
 3831|       |
 3832|      0|	if (work->num_transactions > 0) {
 3833|      0|		memcpy(transactions.data() + 1, buf + work->tx_hashes_offset, work->num_transactions * HASH_SIZE);
 3834|      0|	}
 3835|       |
 3836|      0|	root_hash root;
 3837|      0|	merkle_hash(transactions, root);
 3838|       |
 3839|      0|	std::vector<uint8_t> blob;
 3840|      0|	blob.reserve(data.header_size + HASH_SIZE + 2);
 3841|       |
 3842|      0|	blob.insert(blob.end(), buf, buf + data.header_size);
 3843|      0|	blob.insert(blob.end(), root.h, root.h + HASH_SIZE);
 3844|      0|	writeVarint(work->num_transactions + 1, blob);
 3845|       |
 3846|      0|	hash pow_hash;
 3847|      0|	if (!work->hasher->calculate(blob.data(), blob.size(), work->height, work->seed, pow_hash, false, RandomX_Hasher_Base::VM_LANE_MONERO)) {
 3848|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid MONERO_BLOCK_BROADCAST: failed to calculate PoW hash for height = " << work->height);
 3849|      0|		work->pow_check_failed = true;
 3850|      0|		return;
 3851|      0|	}
 3852|       |
 3853|      0|	if (!work->diff.check_pow(pow_hash)) {
 3854|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid MONERO_BLOCK_BROADCAST: diff check failed for height = " << work->height << ", PoW hash = " << pow_hash);
 3855|      0|		work->pow_check_failed = true;
 3856|      0|		return;
 3857|      0|	}
 3858|       |
 3859|      0|	LOGINFO(6, "on_monero_block_broadcast: PoW check passed for height = " << work->height);

 4138|      0|			}

 4180|       |template<> struct log::Stream::Entry<P2PServer::P2PClient::SoftwareDisplayName>
 4181|       |{
 4182|       |	static NOINLINE void put(P2PServer::P2PClient::SoftwareDisplayName value, Stream* wrapper)
 4183|      0|	{
 4184|      0|		switch (value.m_id) {
 4185|      0|		case SoftwareID::P2Pool:

 4189|      0|			}

 4192|      0|			}
 4193|       |
 4194|      0|			if (value.m_version <= 0x3000A) {
 4195|       |				// Encoding for versions <= 3.10
 4196|      0|				*wrapper << (value.m_version >> 16) << '.' << (value.m_version & 0xFFFF);
 4197|      0|			}
 4198|      0|			else {
 4199|       |				// Encoding for versions > 3.10
 4200|      0|				*wrapper << (value.m_version >> 16) << '.' << ((value.m_version >> 8) & 0xFF);
 4201|      0|				if (value.m_version & 0xFF) {
 4202|      0|					*wrapper << '.' << (value.m_version & 0xFF);
 4203|      0|				}
 4204|      0|			}
 4205|       |
 4206|      0|			break;
 4207|       |
 4208|      0|		default:
 4209|      0|			*wrapper << "Unknown v" << (value.m_version >> 16) << '.' << (value.m_version & 0xFFFF);
 4210|      0|			break;
 4211|      0|		}
 4212|      0|	}
 4213|       |};

   94|       |		void on_read_failed(int err) override;
   95|       |		void on_disconnected() override;
   96|       |
   97|       |		// Both peers send handshake challenge immediately after a connection is established
   98|       |		// Both peers must have the same consensus ID for handshake to succeed
   99|       |		// Consensus ID is never sent over the network
  100|       |		//
  101|       |		// Handshake sequence:
  102|       |		//
  103|       |		// - Both peers send 8-byte random challenges (and 8 bytes of peer ID) to each other
  104|       |		// - Each peer receives 8-byte challenge, chooses 8-byte random SALT and calculates H = KECCAK(CHALLENGE|CONSENSUS_ID|SALT)
  105|       |		// - Both peers send their H and SALT, calculate H of the other peer and check if it matches with what other peer calculated
  106|       |		// - Peer that initiated the connection must also provide enough PoW in H (difficulty = 10000, 5-10 ms on modern CPU)
  107|       |		// - If H doesn't match or doesn't have enough PoW, connection is closed immediately
  108|       |		enum {
  109|       |			CHALLENGE_SIZE = 8,
  110|       |			CHALLENGE_DIFFICULTY = 10000,
  111|       |		};
  112|       |
  113|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool send_handshake_challenge();
  114|       |		void send_handshake_solution(const uint8_t (&challenge)[CHALLENGE_SIZE]);
  115|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool check_handshake_solution(const hash& solution, const uint8_t (&solution_salt)[CHALLENGE_SIZE]) const;
  116|       |
  117|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_handshake_challenge(const uint8_t* buf);
  118|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_handshake_solution(const uint8_t* buf);
  119|       |		void on_after_handshake(uint8_t* &p);
  120|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_listen_port(const uint8_t* buf);
  121|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_block_request(const uint8_t* buf);
  122|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_block_response(const uint8_t* buf, uint32_t size, uint64_t expected_id);
  123|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_block_broadcast(const uint8_t* buf, uint32_t size, bool compact);
  124|       |		[[nodiscard]] bool on_peer_list_request(const uint8_t* buf);
  125|       |		void on_peer_list_response(const uint8_t* buf);
