  760|       |
  761|       |void TCPServer::on_connection_close(uv_handle_t* handle)
  762|      0|{
  763|      0|	Client* client = static_cast<Client*>(handle->data);
  764|      0|	TCPServer* owner = client->m_owner;
  765|       |
  766|      0|	LOGINFO(5, "peer " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << log::NoColor() << " disconnected");
  767|       |
  768|      0|	if (owner) {
  769|      0|		owner->check_event_loop_thread(__func__);
  770|       |
  771|      0|		Client* prev_in_list = client->m_prev;
  772|      0|		Client* next_in_list = client->m_next;
  773|       |
  774|      0|		const bool is_incoming = client->m_isIncoming;
  775|       |
  776|      0|		client->reset();
  777|       |
  778|      0|		prev_in_list->m_next = next_in_list;
  779|      0|		next_in_list->m_prev = prev_in_list;
  780|       |
  781|      0|		owner->return_client(client);
  782|       |
  783|      0|		--owner->m_numConnections;
  784|      0|		if (is_incoming) {
  785|      0|			--owner->m_numIncomingConnections;
  786|      0|		}
  787|      0|	}
  788|      0|	else {
  789|      0|		LOGERR(5, "internal error: can't find TCPServer instance for peer " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << ", this will leak memory");
  790|      0|	}
  791|      0|}
  792|       |
  793|       |void TCPServer::on_connection_error(uv_handle_t* handle)
  794|      0|{
  795|      0|	Client* client = reinterpret_cast<Client*>(handle->data);
  796|      0|	TCPServer* owner = client->m_owner;
  797|       |
  798|      0|	if (owner) {
  799|      0|		owner->return_client(client);
  800|      0|	}
  801|      0|	else {
  802|      0|		LOGERR(5, "internal error: can't find TCPServer instance for peer " << log::Gray() << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << ", this will leak memory");
  803|      0|	}

  805|       |
  806|       |void TCPServer::on_connect(uv_connect_t* req, int status)
  807|      0|{
  808|      0|	Client* client = reinterpret_cast<Client*>(req->data);
  809|       |
  810|      0|	TCPServer* server = client->m_owner;
  811|      0|	if (!server) {
  812|      0|		return;
  813|      0|	}
  814|       |
  815|      0|	if (client->m_addressType != Client::AddressType::DomainName) {
  816|      0|		server->m_pendingConnections.erase(client->m_addr);
  817|      0|	}
  818|       |
  819|      0|	if (status) {
  820|      0|		if (status == UV_ETIMEDOUT) {
  821|      0|			LOGINFO(5, "connection to " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << " timed out");
  822|      0|		}
  823|      0|		else {
  824|      0|			LOGWARN(5, "failed to connect to " << static_cast<char*>(client->m_addrString) << ", error " << uv_err_name(status));
  825|      0|		}
  826|      0|		server->on_connect_failed(client->isV6(), client->m_addr, client->m_port);
  827|      0|		client->on_connect_failed(status);
  828|      0|		uv_close(reinterpret_cast<uv_handle_t*>(&client->m_socket), on_connection_error);
  829|      0|		return;
  830|      0|	}
  831|       |
  832|      0|	server->on_new_client(nullptr, client);
  833|      0|}
  834|       |
  835|       |void TCPServer::on_new_client(uv_stream_t* server)
  836|      0|{
  837|      0|	if (m_finished.load()) {
  838|      0|		return;
  839|      0|	}
  840|       |
  841|      0|	Client* client = get_client();
  842|       |
  843|      0|	int err = uv_tcp_init(&m_loop, &client->m_socket);
  844|      0|	if (err) {
  845|      0|		LOGERR(1, "failed to create tcp client handle, error " << uv_err_name(err));
  846|      0|		return_client(client);
  847|      0|		return;
  848|      0|	}
  849|      0|	client->m_socket.data = client;
  850|      0|	client->m_owner = this;
  851|       |
  852|      0|	err = uv_tcp_nodelay(&client->m_socket, 1);
  853|      0|	if (err) {

 1200|       |void TCPServer::Client::on_read(uv_stream_t* stream, ssize_t nread, const uv_buf_t* buf)
 1201|      0|{
 1202|      0|	Client* client = static_cast<Client*>(stream->data);
 1203|      0|	client->m_readBufInUse = false;
 1204|       |
 1205|      0|	if (client->m_isClosing) {
 1206|      0|		LOGWARN(5, "client " << static_cast<const char*>(client->m_addrString) << " is being disconnected but data received from it, nread = " << nread << ". Ignoring it.");
 1207|      0|		return;
 1208|      0|	}
 1209|       |
 1210|      0|	if (nread > 0) {
 1211|      0|		if (client->m_owner && !client->m_owner->m_finished.load()) {
 1212|      0|			if (client->m_proxyProtocolState == ProxyProtocolState::ExpectingHeader) {
 1213|      0|				if (!client->on_proxy_protocol(buf->base, static_cast<uint32_t>(nread))) {
 1214|      0|					client->close();
 1215|      0|				}
 1216|      0|			}
 1217|      0|			else if (client->m_socks5ProxyState == Socks5ProxyState::Default) {
 1218|      0|				if (!client->on_read(buf->base, static_cast<uint32_t>(nread))) {
 1219|      0|					client->close();
 1220|      0|				}
 1221|      0|			}
 1222|      0|			else if (!client->on_proxy_handshake(buf->base, static_cast<uint32_t>(nread))) {
 1223|      0|				client->close();
 1224|      0|			}
 1225|      0|		}
 1226|      0|	}
 1227|      0|	else if (nread < 0) {
 1228|      0|		if (nread != UV_EOF) {
 1229|      0|			const int err = static_cast<int>(nread);
 1230|      0|			LOGWARN(5, "client " << static_cast<const char*>(client->m_addrString) << " failed to read response, err = " << uv_err_name(err));
 1231|      0|			client->on_read_failed(err);
 1232|      0|		}
 1233|      0|		else {
 1234|      0|			client->on_disconnected();
 1235|      0|		}
 1236|      0|		client->close();
 1237|      0|	}

 1239|       |
 1240|       |bool TCPServer::Client::on_proxy_handshake(const char* data, uint32_t size)
 1241|      0|{
 1242|      0|	if ((data != m_readBuf + m_numRead) || (data + size > m_readBuf + m_readBufSize)) {
 1243|      0|		LOGERR(1, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " invalid data pointer or size in on_read()");
 1244|      0|		return false;
 1245|      0|	}
 1246|      0|	m_numRead += size;
 1247|       |
 1248|      0|	uint32_t n = 0;
 1249|       |
 1250|      0|	switch (m_socks5ProxyState) {
 1251|      0|	case Socks5ProxyState::MethodSelectionSent:
 1252|      0|		if (m_numRead >= 2) {
 1253|      0|			if ((m_readBuf[0] != 5) || (m_readBuf[1] != 0)) {
 1254|      0|				LOGWARN(5, "SOCKS5 proxy returned an invalid METHOD selection message");
 1255|      0|				return false;
 1256|      0|			}
 1257|      0|			n = 2;
 1258|       |
 1259|      0|			const bool result = m_owner->send(this,
 1260|      0|				[this](uint8_t* buf, size_t buf_size) -> size_t
 1261|      0|				{
 1262|      0|					if (buf_size < ADDR_STRING_SIZE + 7) {
 1263|      0|						return 0;
 1264|      0|					}
 1265|       |
 1266|      0|					buf[0] = 5; // Protocol version (SOCKS5)
 1267|      0|					buf[1] = 1; // CONNECT
 1268|      0|					buf[2] = 0; // RESERVED
 1269|       |
 1270|      0|					if (m_addressType == AddressType::DomainName) {
 1271|      0|						buf[3] = 3; // ATYP
 1272|       |
 1273|      0|						const char* s = strchr(m_addrString, ':');
 1274|      0|						const size_t domain_len = s ? (s - m_addrString) : strlen(m_addrString);
 1275|       |
 1276|      0|						buf[4] = static_cast<uint8_t>(domain_len);
 1277|      0|						memcpy(buf + 5, m_addrString, domain_len);
 1278|       |
 1279|      0|						buf[domain_len + 5] = static_cast<uint8_t>(m_port >> 8);
 1280|      0|						buf[domain_len + 6] = static_cast<uint8_t>(m_port & 0xFF);
 1281|       |
 1282|      0|						return domain_len + 7;
 1283|      0|					}
 1284|       |
 1285|      0|					if (m_addressType == AddressType::IPv6) {
 1286|      0|						buf[3] = 4; // ATYP
 1287|      0|						memcpy(buf + 4, m_addr.data, 16);
 1288|      0|						buf[20] = static_cast<uint8_t>(m_port >> 8);
 1289|      0|						buf[21] = static_cast<uint8_t>(m_port & 0xFF);
 1290|       |
 1291|      0|						return 22;
 1292|      0|					}
 1293|       |
 1294|      0|					buf[3] = 1; // ATYP
 1295|      0|					memcpy(buf + 4, m_addr.data + sizeof(raw_ip::ipv4_prefix), 4);
 1296|      0|					buf[8] = static_cast<uint8_t>(m_port >> 8);

 1346|      0|		}
 1347|      0|		break;

 1378|       |bool TCPServer::Client::on_proxy_protocol(const char* data, uint32_t size)
 1379|      0|{
 1380|       |	// PROXY protocol v2 signature
 1381|      0|	static constexpr uint8_t ppv2_signature[12] = { 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x00, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x51, 0x55, 0x49, 0x54, 0x0A };
 1382|       |
 1383|      0|	if ((data != m_readBuf + m_numRead) || (data + size > m_readBuf + m_readBufSize)) {
 1384|      0|		LOGERR(1, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " invalid data pointer or size in on_proxy_protocol()");
 1385|      0|		return false;
 1386|      0|	}
 1387|      0|	m_numRead += size;
 1388|       |
 1389|       |	// Need at least 16 bytes for the fixed header
 1390|      0|	if (m_numRead < 16) {
 1391|      0|		return true;
 1392|      0|	}
 1393|       |
 1394|      0|	const uint8_t* p = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(m_readBuf);
 1395|       |
 1396|       |	// Validate 12-byte signature
 1397|      0|	if (memcmp(p, ppv2_signature, 12) != 0) {
 1398|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " invalid PROXY protocol v2 signature");

 1400|      0|	}
 1401|       |
 1402|       |	// Byte 12: version (high nibble) and command (low nibble)
 1403|      0|	const uint8_t ver_cmd = p[12];
 1404|      0|	const uint8_t version = (ver_cmd >> 4) & 0x0F;
 1405|      0|	const uint8_t command = ver_cmd & 0x0F;
 1406|       |
 1407|      0|	if (version != 2) {
 1408|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " unsupported PROXY protocol version " << version);
 1409|      0|		return false;
 1410|      0|	}
 1411|       |
 1412|      0|	if (command > 1) {
 1413|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " invalid PROXY protocol command " << command);
 1414|      0|		return false;
 1415|      0|	}
 1416|       |
 1417|       |	// Byte 13: address family (high nibble) and protocol (low nibble)
 1418|      0|	const uint8_t family_proto = p[13];
 1419|       |
 1420|       |	// Bytes 14-15: address data length (big-endian), includes address block + optional TLV extensions
 1421|      0|	const uint16_t addr_len = (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[14]) << 8) | static_cast<uint16_t>(p[15]);
 1422|       |
 1423|      0|	const uint32_t total_header_len = 16 + static_cast<uint32_t>(addr_len);
 1424|       |
 1425|      0|	if (total_header_len > m_readBufSize) {
 1426|      0|		LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " PROXY protocol v2 header too large (" << total_header_len << " bytes)");
 1427|      0|		return false;
 1428|      0|	}
 1429|       |
 1430|       |	// Wait for complete header
 1431|      0|	if (m_numRead < total_header_len) {
 1432|      0|		return true;
 1433|      0|	}
 1434|       |
 1435|       |	// PROXY command: extract real client address
 1436|      0|	if (command == 1) {
 1437|      0|		const uint8_t family = (family_proto >> 4) & 0x0F;
 1438|       |
 1439|      0|		if (family == 1) {
 1440|       |			// AF_INET: src(4) + dst(4) + sport(2) + dport(2) = 12 bytes minimum, may be larger with TLV extensions
 1441|      0|			if (addr_len < 12) {
 1442|      0|				LOGWARN(4, "peer " << static_cast<char*>(m_addrString) << " PROXY protocol v2 IPv4 address data too short (" << addr_len << " bytes)");
 1443|      0|				return false;
 1444|      0|			}
 1445|       |
 1446|      0|			memcpy(m_addr.data, raw_ip::ipv4_prefix, sizeof(raw_ip::ipv4_prefix));
 1447|      0|			memcpy(m_addr.data + sizeof(raw_ip::ipv4_prefix), p + 16, 4);
 1448|      0|			m_port = (static_cast<int>(p[24]) << 8) | static_cast<int>(p[25]);
 1449|      0|			m_addressType = AddressType::IPv4;
 1450|      0|		}
 1451|      0|		else if (family == 2) {
 1452|       |			// AF_INET6: src(16) + dst(16) + sport(2) + dport(2) = 36 bytes minimum, may be larger with TLV extensions
 1453|      0|			if (addr_len < 36) {

 1455|      0|				return false;
 1456|      0|			}

 1461|      0|		}
 1462|      0|		else {

 1465|      0|		}
 1466|       |
 1467|      0|		init_addr_string();

 1469|      0|	}
 1470|       |	// LOCAL command (health checks): keep original address
 1471|       |
 1472|       |	// Move remaining data to beginning of buffer
 1473|      0|	m_numRead -= total_header_len;

 1483|      0|		return false;
 1484|      0|	}
 1485|       |
 1486|      0|	if (m_owner->m_finished.load()) {
 1487|      0|		return false;
 1488|      0|	}
 1489|       |
 1490|      0|	if (!on_connect()) {
 1491|      0|		return false;
 1492|      0|	}
 1493|       |

 1495|      0|	if (m_numRead > 0) {

   33|       |

   40|       |	struct Thread
   41|       |	{
   42|       |		explicit Thread(const work_t& work)
   43|     30|			: m_stop(false)
   44|     30|			, m_thread{}
   45|     30|			, m_cond{}
   46|     30|			, m_work(work)
   47|     30|		{
   48|     30|			uv_mutex_init_checked(&m_mutex);
   49|     30|			uv_cond_init_checked(&m_cond);
   50|       |
   51|     30|			const int result = uv_thread_create(&m_thread, run, this);
   52|     30|			if (result) {
   53|      0|				LOGERR(1, "failed to create a thread, error " << uv_err_name(result));
   54|      0|				PANIC_STOP();

   56|     30|		}
   57|       |

   61|     30|			{
   62|     30|				MutexLock lock(m_mutex);
   63|     30|				uv_cond_signal(&m_cond);
   64|     30|			}

   66|       |
   67|     30|			uv_cond_destroy(&m_cond);
   68|     30|			uv_mutex_destroy(&m_mutex);

   75|   317k|			work_t w = std::move(m_work);
   76|       |

   81|   317k|		}

   93|       |

   97|     30|					uv_mutex_unlock(&m_mutex);
   98|     30|					return;
   99|     30|				}
  100|   318k|				uv_cond_wait(&m_cond, &m_mutex);
  101|   318k|			}

  116|       |	FORCEINLINE void queue_work(work_t&& callback, uint32_t N)
  117|   105k|	{
  118|   105k|		if (N == 0) {
  119|      0|			return;
  120|      0|		}
  121|       |
  122|   105k|		work_t w = std::move(callback);
  123|   105k|		{
  124|   105k|			ReadLock lock(m_threadsLock);
  125|       |
  126|   749k|			for (auto& t : m_threads) {
  127|   749k|				if (uv_mutex_trylock(&t->m_mutex) == 0) {
  128|       |					// Another concurrent queue_work might have assigned work to this thread already
  129|       |					// Or it might be the initial work that the thread has right after its creation and before the first mutex lock
  130|   385k|					if (t->m_work) {
  131|  67.2k|						uv_mutex_unlock(&t->m_mutex);
  132|  67.2k|						continue;
  133|  67.2k|					}
  134|       |
  135|       |					// This thread wasn't holding its mutex, and doesn't have any work,
  136|       |					// so we can safely pass the work to it.
  137|   318k|					t->m_work = w;
  138|   318k|					uv_cond_signal(&t->m_cond);
  139|   318k|					uv_mutex_unlock(&t->m_mutex);
  140|       |
  141|   318k|					--N;
  142|   318k|					if (N == 0) {
  143|   106k|						return;
  144|   106k|					}
  145|   318k|				}
  146|   749k|			}
  147|   105k|		}
  148|       |
  149|       |		// If there wasn't enough free threads, spawn some more
  150|  18.4E|		if (N > 0) {
  151|     12|			WriteLock lock(m_threadsLock);
  152|       |
  153|     12|			m_threads.reserve(m_threads.size() + N);
  154|       |
  155|     42|			for (uint32_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
  156|     30|				m_threads.emplace_back(std::make_unique<Thread>(w));
  157|     30|			}
  158|     12|		}
  159|  18.4E|	}
  160|       |
  161|       |	ReadWriteLock m_threadsLock;
  162|       |	std::vector<std::unique_ptr<Thread>> m_threads;

   27|       |// Calls the callback N times on N threads in parallel
   28|       |// Doesn't wait, returns immediately

   55|       |static FORCEINLINE constexpr hash from_onion_v3_const(const char* address, uint8_t* out_version = nullptr)
   56|    108|{
   57|    108|	uint8_t buf[HASH_SIZE + 4] = {};
   58|    108|	uint8_t* p = buf;
   59|       |
   60|    108|	uint64_t data = 0;
   61|    108|	uint64_t bit_size = 0;
   62|       |
   63|  6.10k|	for (size_t i = 0; i < 56; ++i) {
   64|  5.99k|		const char c = address[i];
   65|  5.99k|		uint64_t digit = 0;
   66|       |
   67|  5.99k|		if ('a' <= c && c <= 'z') {
   68|  4.96k|			digit = static_cast<uint64_t>(c - 'a');
   69|  4.96k|		}
   70|  1.03k|		else if ('A' <= c && c <= 'Z') {
   71|      4|			digit = static_cast<uint64_t>(c - 'A');
   72|      4|		}
   73|  1.03k|		else if ('2' <= c && c <= '7') {
   74|  1.03k|			digit = static_cast<uint64_t>(c - '2') + 26;
   75|  1.03k|		}
   76|      1|		else {
   77|      1|			if (out_version) {
   78|      1|				*out_version = 0;
   79|      1|			}
   80|      1|			return {};
   81|      1|		}
   82|       |
   83|  5.99k|		data = (data << 5) | digit;
   84|  5.99k|		bit_size += 5;
   85|       |
   86|  9.74k|		while (bit_size >= 8) {
   87|  3.74k|			bit_size -= 8;

   89|  3.74k|		}
   90|  5.99k|	}
   91|       |
   92|    107|	hash result;
   93|       |
   94|  3.53k|	for (size_t i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE; ++i) {
   95|  3.42k|		result.h[i] = buf[i];
   96|  3.42k|	}
   97|       |
   98|    107|	if (out_version) {
   99|    107|		*out_version = buf[HASH_SIZE + 2];
  100|    107|	}
  101|       |
  102|    107|	return result;

  224|       |NOINLINE difficulty_type& difficulty_type::operator/=(difficulty_type b)
  225|  44.0M|{
  226|  44.0M|	if (*this < b) {
  227|   287k|		lo = 0;
  228|   287k|		hi = 0;
  229|   287k|		return *this;
  230|   287k|	}
  231|       |
  232|  43.8M|	if (*this - b < b) {
  233|   812k|		lo = 1;
  234|   812k|		hi = 0;
  235|   812k|		return *this;
  236|   812k|	}
  237|       |
  238|  42.9M|	if (b.hi == 0) {
  239|  3.53M|		return operator/=(b.lo);
  240|  3.53M|	}
  241|       |
  242|  39.4M|	const uint64_t shift = bsr(b.hi) + 1;
  243|  39.4M|	const uint64_t divisor = shiftleft128(b.lo, b.hi, 64 - shift);
  244|       |
  245|  39.4M|	uint64_t t;
  246|  39.4M|	if (hi < divisor) {
  247|  29.4M|		uint64_t r;
  248|  29.4M|		t = udiv128(hi, lo, divisor, &r) >> shift;
  249|  29.4M|	}
  250|  9.97M|	else {
  251|  9.97M|		uint64_t r;
  252|  9.97M|		t = shiftright128(udiv128(hi - divisor, lo, divisor, &r), 1, shift);
  253|  9.97M|	}
  254|       |
  255|  39.4M|	difficulty_type product;
  256|  39.4M|	product.lo = umul128(b.lo, t, &product.hi);
  257|       |
  258|  39.4M|	uint64_t t1, t2;
  259|  39.4M|	t1 = umul128(b.hi, t, &t2);
  260|  39.4M|	product.hi += t1;
  261|       |
  262|  39.4M|	if (t2 || (product.hi < t1) || (*this < product)) {
  263|  19.1M|		--t;
  264|  19.1M|	}
  265|       |
  266|  39.4M|	lo = t;
  267|  39.4M|	hi = 0;
  268|       |
  269|  39.4M|	return *this;

  272|       |NOINLINE bool difficulty_type::check_pow(const hash& pow_hash) const
  273|  20.0M|{
  274|  20.0M|	const uint64_t* a = pow_hash.u64();

  276|  20.0M|	uint64_t result[6] = {};

  280|  60.0M|		for (int i = 3; i >= 0; --i) {
  281|  50.0M|			product[0] = umul128(a[i], lo, &product[1]);
  282|       |
  283|  50.0M|			uint64_t carry = 0;
  284|   210M|			for (int k = i, l = 0; k < 5; ++k, ++l) {
  285|   160M|				uint64_t t = result[k] + product[l];

  287|   160M|				t += carry;
  288|   160M|				carry = next_carry | static_cast<uint64_t>(t < result[k]);

  684|       |struct BSR8
  685|       |{
  686|       |	uint8_t data[256];
  687|       |
  688|      0|	static constexpr BSR8 init() {
  689|      0|		BSR8 result = { 55 };
  690|      0|
  691|      0|		for (int i = 1; i < 256; ++i) {
  692|      0|			int x = i;

  694|      0|			while (x < 0x80) {
  695|      0|				--result.data[i];
  696|      0|				x <<= 1;
  697|      0|			}
  698|      0|		}
  699|      0|
  700|      0|		return result;
  701|      0|	}
  702|       |};
  703|       |
  704|       |static constexpr BSR8 bsr8_table = BSR8::init();
  705|       |
  706|       |NOINLINE uint64_t bsr_reference(uint64_t x)
  707|    128|{
  708|    128|	uint32_t y = static_cast<uint32_t>(x);
  709|       |
  710|    128|	uint64_t n0 = (x == y) ? 0 : 32;
  711|    128|	y = static_cast<uint32_t>(x >> n0);
  712|    128|	n0 ^= 32;
  713|       |
  714|    128|	const uint64_t n1 = (y & 0xFFFF0000UL) ? 0 : 16;
  715|    128|	y <<= n1;
  716|       |
  717|    128|	const uint64_t n2 = (y & 0xFF000000UL) ? 0 : 8;
  718|    128|	y <<= n2;
  719|       |
  720|    128|	return bsr8_table.data[y >> 24] - n0 - n1 - n2;
  721|    128|}

  750|       |bool is_private_address(const std::string& host)
  751|      0|{
  752|      0|	if (host.empty()) {
  753|      0|		return false;
  754|      0|	}
  755|       |
  756|      0|	if (host.compare("localhost") == 0) {
  757|      0|		return true;
  758|      0|	}
  759|       |
  760|      0|	if (host.find_first_not_of("0123456789.:") != std::string::npos) {
  761|      0|		return false;
  762|      0|	}
  763|       |
  764|      0|	raw_ip addr;
  765|      0|	const bool is_v6 = host.find(':') != std::string::npos;
  766|       |
  767|      0|	if (!str_to_ip(is_v6, host.c_str(), addr)) {
  768|      0|		return false;
  769|      0|	}

  771|      0|	if (is_v6) {
  772|      0|		const uint8_t a = addr.data[0];
  773|      0|		const uint8_t b = addr.data[1];

  775|      0|		return
  776|      0|			(addr == raw_ip::localhost_ipv6) ||    // ::1 (loopback)
  777|      0|			((a & 0xfe) == 0xfc) ||                // fc00::/7 (ULA - Unique Local Address)
  778|      0|			((a == 0xfe) && ((b & 0xc0) == 0x80)); // fe80::/10 (link-local)
  779|      0|	}
  780|       |
  781|       |	// IPv4
  782|      0|	const uint8_t a = addr.data[12];
  783|      0|	const uint8_t b = addr.data[13];
  784|       |
  785|      0|	return 
  786|      0|		(a == 10) ||                              // 10.0.0.0/8 (Private/LAN)
  787|      0|		((a == 172) && (b >= 16) && (b <= 31)) || // 172.16.0.0/12 (Private/LAN)
  788|      0|		((a == 192) && (b == 168)) ||             // 192.168.0.0/16 (Private/LAN)
  789|      0|		((a == 169) && (b == 254)) ||             // 169.254.0.0/16 (link-local)

  983|       |void init_uv()
  984|      0|{
  985|       |	// Creates the default loop if it doesn't exist yet
  986|      0|	uv_default_loop();
  987|       |
  988|       |#ifdef _MSC_VER
  989|       |#define putenv _putenv
  990|       |#endif
  991|       |
  992|      0|	const uint32_t N = std::min(std::max(std::thread::hardware_concurrency(), MIN_UV_THREADPOOL_SIZE), MAX_UV_THREADPOOL_SIZE);
  993|       |
  994|      0|	static char buf[40] = {};
  995|      0|	log::Stream s(buf);
  996|      0|	s << "UV_THREADPOOL_SIZE=" << N << '\0';
  997|       |
  998|      0|	int err = putenv(buf);
  999|      0|	if (err != 0) {
 1000|      0|		err = errno;
 1001|      0|		fprintf(stderr, "init_uv_threadpool: couldn't set UV thread pool size to %u threads, putenv returned error %d\n", N, err);
 1002|      0|		abort();
 1003|      0|	}
 1004|       |
 1005|      0|	struct Work {
 1006|      0|		uv_work_t req = {};
 1007|      0|		std::shared_ptr<std::atomic<int32_t>> k = 0;
 1008|       |
 1009|      0|		FORCEINLINE void run()
 1010|      0|		{
 1011|      0|			k->fetch_sub(1, std::memory_order_acq_rel);
 1012|       |
 1013|       |			// Wait until all threads are at this point
 1014|      0|			while (k->load(std::memory_order_acquire) > 0) {
 1015|      0|				cpu_yield();
 1016|      0|			}
 1017|       |
 1018|      0|			set_uv_worker_pool_thread();
 1019|      0|		}
 1020|      0|	};
 1021|       |
 1022|      0|	std::shared_ptr<std::atomic<int32_t>> k(new std::atomic<int32_t>(static_cast<int32_t>(N)));
 1023|       |
 1024|      0|	for (uint32_t i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
 1025|      0|		Work* w = new Work{ {}, k };
 1026|      0|		w->req.data = w;
 1027|       |
 1028|      0|		err = uv_queue_work(uv_default_loop_checked(), &w->req,
 1029|      0|			[](uv_work_t* req) {
 1030|      0|				reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data)->run();
 1031|      0|			},
 1032|      0|			[](uv_work_t* req, int) {
 1033|      0|				delete reinterpret_cast<Work*>(req->data);
 1034|      0|			});
 1035|       |
 1036|      0|		if (err) {
 1037|      0|			fprintf(stderr, "init_uv_threadpool: uv_queue_work failed, error %s\n", uv_err_name(err));
 1038|      0|			abort();
 1039|      0|		}
 1040|      0|	}
 1041|       |
 1042|       |	// Bail out after 1 second
 1043|      0|	const uint64_t start_time = microseconds_since_epoch();
 1044|       |
 1045|      0|	while (k->load(std::memory_order_acquire) > 0) {
 1046|      0|		if (microseconds_since_epoch() - start_time >= 1'000'000) {
 1047|      0|			fprintf(stderr, "init_uv_threadpool: timed out waiting for %u worker threads\n", N);
 1048|      0|			abort();
 1049|      0|		}
 1050|      0|		cpu_yield();
 1051|      0|	}
 1052|      0|}

 1054|       |std::string to_onion_v3(const hash& pubkey)
 1055|    209|{
 1056|    209|	static constexpr uint8_t prefix[] = ".onion checksum";
 1057|    209|	static constexpr uint8_t version = 3;
 1058|       |
 1059|    209|	hash h;
 1060|  10.0k|	keccak_custom([&pubkey](int offset) -> uint8_t {
 1061|  10.0k|		size_t k = static_cast<size_t>(offset);
 1062|  10.0k|		if (k < sizeof(prefix) - 1) {
 1063|  3.13k|			return prefix[k];
 1064|  3.13k|		}
 1065|  6.89k|		k -= sizeof(prefix) - 1;
 1066|       |
 1067|  6.89k|		return (k < HASH_SIZE) ? pubkey.h[k] : version;
 1068|  10.0k|	}, sizeof(prefix) + HASH_SIZE, h.h, HASH_SIZE, true);
 1069|       |
 1070|       |	// pubkey (32 bytes), checksum (2 bytes), version (1 byte), and a zero byte for padding
 1071|    209|	uint8_t buf[HASH_SIZE + 4];
 1072|       |
 1073|    209|	memcpy(buf, pubkey.h, HASH_SIZE);
 1074|    209|	memcpy(buf + HASH_SIZE, h.h, 2);
 1075|    209|	buf[HASH_SIZE + 2] = version;
 1076|    209|	buf[HASH_SIZE + 3] = 0;
 1077|       |
 1078|    209|	std::string result;
 1079|    209|	result.reserve(62);
 1080|       |
 1081|    209|	uint64_t data = 0;
 1082|    209|	uint64_t bit_size = 0;
 1083|       |
 1084|  7.52k|	for (size_t i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE + 3; ++i) {
 1085|  7.31k|		data = (data << 8) | buf[i];
 1086|  7.31k|		bit_size += 8;
 1087|       |
 1088|  19.0k|		while (bit_size >= 5) {
 1089|  11.7k|			bit_size -= 5;
 1090|  11.7k|			result += base32_alphabet[(data >> bit_size) & 31];
 1091|  11.7k|		}
 1092|  7.31k|	}
 1093|       |
 1094|    209|	result.append(".onion");
 1095|       |
 1096|    209|	return result;
 1097|    209|}
 1098|       |
 1099|       |hash from_onion_v3(const std::string& address)
 1100|    110|{
 1101|    110|	if ((address.length() < 6) || (address.find(".onion") != address.length() - 6)) {
 1102|      1|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": doesn't end with \".onion\"");
 1103|      1|		return {};
 1104|      1|	}
 1105|       |
 1106|    109|	if (address.length() != 62) {
 1107|      1|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": expected length 62, got " << address.length() );
 1108|      1|		return {};
 1109|      1|	}
 1110|       |
 1111|    108|	uint8_t version = 0;
 1112|    108|	const hash result = from_onion_v3_const(address.c_str(), &version);
 1113|       |
 1114|    108|	if (result.empty()) {
 1115|      1|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": has invalid character(s)");
 1116|      1|		return {};
 1117|      1|	}
 1118|       |
 1119|    107|	if (version != 3) {
 1120|      2|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": invalid version " << version);
 1121|      2|		return {};
 1122|      2|	}
 1123|       |
 1124|       |	// Checksum validation
 1125|    105|	if (to_onion_v3(result) != tolower(address)) {
 1126|      0|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": checksum failed");
 1127|      0|		return {};
 1128|      0|	}
 1129|       |
 1130|       |	// Pubkey validation
 1131|    105|	ge_p3 point;
 1132|    105|	if (ge_frombytes_vartime(&point, result.h) != 0) {
 1133|      1|		LOGWARN(3, "Invalid onion address \"" << address << "\": invalid ed25519 pubkey");
 1134|      1|		return {};
 1135|      1|	}
 1136|       |
 1137|    104|	return result;
 1138|    105|}
 1139|       |
 1140|       |std::vector<std::vector<std::string>> parse_config(const std::string& file_name)
 1141|      2|{
 1142|      2|	std::ifstream f(file_name);
 1143|       |
 1144|      2|	if (!f.is_open() || !f.good()) {
 1145|      1|		throw std::exception();
 1146|      1|	}
 1147|       |
 1148|      1|	std::vector<std::vector<std::string>> args;
 1149|       |
 1150|    135|	auto is_alpha = [](char c) { return ('a' <= c && c <= 'z') || ('A' <= c && c <= 'Z'); };
 1151|     30|	auto is_num = [](char c) { return ('0' <= c && c <= '9'); };
 1152|       |
 1153|      1|	std::string s;
 1154|       |
 1155|     18|	while (f.good() && std::getline(f, s)) {
 1156|     17|		if (s.empty()) {
 1157|      6|			continue;
 1158|      6|		}
 1159|       |
 1160|     11|		bool quoted = false;
 1161|       |
 1162|     11|		enum {
 1163|     11|			BEFORE_NAME,
 1164|     11|			COMMENT,
 1165|     11|			NAME,
 1166|     11|			AFTER_NAME,
 1167|     11|			VALUE,
 1168|     11|			VALUE_ESCAPE_CHAR,
 1169|     11|		} state = BEFORE_NAME;
 1170|       |
 1171|    384|		for (const char c : s) {
 1172|    384|			switch (state) {
 1173|     18|				case BEFORE_NAME:
 1174|     18|					if (is_alpha(c)) {
 1175|     10|						state = NAME;
 1176|     10|						args.emplace_back(1, std::string(1, c));
 1177|     10|					}
 1178|      8|					else if (c == '#') {
 1179|      1|						state = COMMENT;
 1180|      1|					}
 1181|     18|					break;
 1182|       |
 1183|     52|				case COMMENT:
 1184|     52|					break;
 1185|       |
 1186|    117|				case NAME:
 1187|    117|					if (is_alpha(c) || is_num(c) || (c == '-')) {
 1188|    108|						args.back()[0] += c;
 1189|    108|					}
 1190|      9|					else if (c == '#') {
 1191|      1|						state = COMMENT;
 1192|      1|					}
 1193|      8|					else {
 1194|      8|						state = AFTER_NAME;
 1195|      8|					}
 1196|    117|					break;
 1197|       |
 1198|     21|				case AFTER_NAME:
 1199|     21|					if (c == '#') {
 1200|      2|						state = COMMENT;
 1201|      2|					}
 1202|     19|					else if ((c != '\t') && (c != ' ') && (c != '=')) {
 1203|     10|						state = VALUE;
 1204|     10|						quoted = (c == '"');
 1205|     10|						if (quoted) {

  331|       |struct RandomDeviceSeed
  332|       |{
  333|       |	using result_type = std::random_device::result_type;
  334|       |	static_assert(sizeof(result_type) >= 4, "result_type must have at least 32 bits");
  335|       |
  336|       |	template<typename T>
  337|       |	static void generate(T begin, T end)
  338|     11|	{

  340|  6.87k|		for (T i = begin; i != end; ++i) {
  341|  6.86k|			*i = rd();
  342|  6.86k|		}
  343|     11|	}
  344|       |

  368|       |uint64_t bsr_reference(uint64_t x);
  369|       |
  370|       |#ifdef HAVE_BUILTIN_CLZLL
  371|  39.7M|#define bsr(x) (63 - __builtin_clzll(x))
  372|       |#elif defined HAVE_BITSCANREVERSE64
  373|       |#pragma intrinsic(_BitScanReverse64)
  374|       |FORCEINLINE uint64_t bsr(uint64_t x)
  375|       |{
  376|       |	unsigned long index;
  377|       |	_BitScanReverse64(&index, x);
  378|       |	return index;
  379|       |}
  380|       |#else
  381|       |#define bsr bsr_reference
  382|       |#endif
  383|       |
  384|       |// Multiples of 64 when size <= 2048
  385|       |// Multiples of 128 when 2048 < size <= 4096
  386|       |// Multiples of 256 when 4096 < size <= 8192 and so on
  387|       |// 131072 will get index 127
  388|       |template<uint64_t MIN_GRANULARITY = 6, uint64_t BITS_TO_USE = 4>
  389|       |[[nodiscard]] FORCEINLINE uint64_t get_bucket_index(uint64_t size, uint64_t& adjusted_size)
  390|   262k|{
  391|   262k|	if (size == 0) {
  392|      1|		adjusted_size = static_cast<uint64_t>(1u) << MIN_GRANULARITY;
  393|      1|		return 0;
  394|      1|	}
  395|       |
  396|   262k|	constexpr uint64_t TOTAL_BITS = MIN_GRANULARITY + BITS_TO_USE;
  397|       |
  398|   262k|	const uint64_t bit_index = bsr(size);
  399|       |
  400|   262k|	uint64_t n = std::max<uint64_t>(bit_index, TOTAL_BITS) - BITS_TO_USE;
  401|       |
  402|   262k|	const uint64_t mask = (static_cast<uint64_t>(1u) << n) - 1u;
  403|   262k|	adjusted_size = (size + mask) & ~mask;
  404|       |
  405|   262k|	n = std::max<uint64_t>(bit_index, TOTAL_BITS) - TOTAL_BITS;
  406|   262k|	return (adjusted_size >> (n + MIN_GRANULARITY)) + (n << BITS_TO_USE) - 1;
  407|   262k|}

  263|       |
  264|       |// Thread sync point to use inside parallel_run's callback
  265|       |template<typename T>
  266|   606k|FORCEINLINE bool sync_point(std::atomic<T>& value, T k) {
  267|   606k|	if (value.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_acq_rel) + 1 >= k) {
  268|   207k|		return true;
  269|   207k|	}
  270|       |
  271|       |	// some threads are not here yet, wait for them
  272|   398k|	size_t fast_spin_count = 0;
  273|       |
  274|  1.15G|	do {
  275|  1.24G|		if (fast_spin_count < 10000) {
  276|  1.24G|			++fast_spin_count;
  277|  1.24G|			cpu_yield();
  278|  1.24G|		}
  279|  18.4E|		else {
  280|  18.4E|			std::this_thread::yield();
  281|  18.4E|		}
  282|  1.15G|	} while (value.load(std::memory_order_acquire) < k);
  283|       |
  284|   398k|	return false;
  285|   606k|}

  221|       |TEST(block_template, submit_sidechain_block)
  222|      1|{
  223|      1|	thread_pool_init();
  224|      1|	init_crypto_cache();
  225|      1|	{
  226|      1|	SideChain sidechain(nullptr, NetworkType::Mainnet, "unit_test");
  227|       |

  231|      1|	tpl.rng().seed(123);
  232|       |

  234|      1|	tpl2.rng().seed(456);
  235|       |
  236|      1|	BlockTemplate tpl3(&sidechain, nullptr);
  237|      1|	tpl3.rng().seed(789);

  241|      1|	data.height = 2762973;
  242|      1|	data.prev_id = H("81a0260b29d5224e88d04b11faff321fbdc11c4570779386b2a1817a86dc622c");
  243|      1|	data.seed_hash = H("33d0fb381466f04d6a1919ced3b698f54a28add3da5a6479b096c67df7a4974c");
  244|      1|	data.difficulty = { 300346053753ULL, 0 };
  245|      1|	data.median_weight = 300000;
  246|      1|	data.already_generated_coins = 18204981557254756780ULL;

  253|       |
  254|      1|	std::mt19937_64 rng(101112);
  255|       |

  260|    213|			tpl2.update(data, mempool, params);
  261|       |

  263|     26|				tpl3.update(data, mempool, params);

  265|     26|				ASSERT_TRUE(tpl3.submit_sidechain_block(i3, 0, 0));
  266|     26|			}
  267|       |
  268|    213|			++i2;
  269|    213|			ASSERT_TRUE(tpl2.submit_sidechain_block(i2, 0, 0));
  270|    213|		}
  271|       |
  272|  6.48k|		ASSERT_TRUE(tpl.submit_sidechain_block(i + 1, 0, 0));
  273|  6.48k|		data.median_timestamp += sidechain.block_time();
  274|  6.48k|	}
  275|       |
  276|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(sidechain.difficulty(), 219467);
  277|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(sidechain.blocksById().size(), 4493);
  278|      1|	ASSERT_TRUE(sidechain.precalcFinished());
  279|       |

  281|       |
  282|      1|	ASSERT_TRUE(tip != nullptr);
  283|      1|	ASSERT_TRUE(tip->m_verified);
  284|      1|	ASSERT_FALSE(tip->m_invalid);
  285|       |
  286|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(tip->m_txinGenHeight, data.height);
  287|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(tip->m_sidechainHeight, sidechain.chain_window_size() * 3 - 1);
  288|       |
  289|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(tip->m_sidechainId, H("12d57571a28d62d2b6dca3a647500d23ac22864138b22a133f237b459a0862da"));
  290|      1|	}

   33|       |TEST(crypto, ops)
   34|      1|{
   35|      1|	init_crypto_cache();
   36|      1|	{
   37|      1|	constexpr uint8_t entropy[] = "Test entropy";
   38|      1|	hash pub, sec;
   39|      1|	generate_keys_deterministic(pub, sec, entropy, sizeof(entropy) - 1);
   40|      1|	ASSERT_TRUE(check_keys(pub, sec));
   41|       |
   42|      1|	sec.h[HASH_SIZE - 1] = 0xff;
   43|      1|	ASSERT_FALSE(check_keys(pub, sec));
   44|       |
   45|       |	// Run the tests several times to check how crypto cache works
   46|      5|	for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
   47|      4|		if (i == 2) {
   48|      1|			clear_crypto_cache(seconds_since_epoch() - 1);
   49|      1|		}
   50|      3|		else if (i == 3) {
   51|      1|			clear_crypto_cache(seconds_since_epoch() + 1);
   52|      1|		}
   53|       |
   54|      4|		std::ifstream f("crypto_tests.txt");
   55|      4|		ASSERT_EQ(f.good() && f.is_open(), true);
   56|  4.46k|		do {
   57|  4.46k|			std::string name;
   58|  4.46k|			f >> name;
   59|  4.46k|			if (name == "generate_key_derivation") {
   60|  1.08k|				hash key1, key2, derivation, expected_derivation;
   61|  1.08k|				std::string result_str;
   62|  1.08k|				f >> key1 >> key2 >> result_str;
   63|  1.08k|				const bool result = (result_str == "true");
   64|  1.08k|				if (result) {
   65|  1.02k|					f >> expected_derivation;
   66|  1.02k|				}
   67|  1.08k|				uint8_t view_tag;
   68|  1.08k|				ASSERT_EQ(p2pool::generate_key_derivation(key1, key2, 0, derivation, view_tag), result);
   69|  1.08k|				ASSERT_EQ(p2pool::generate_key_derivation(key1, key2, 1, derivation, view_tag), result);
   70|  1.08k|				ASSERT_EQ(p2pool::generate_key_derivation(key1, key2, 2, derivation, view_tag), result);
   71|  1.08k|				ASSERT_EQ(p2pool::generate_key_derivation(key1, key2, 3, derivation, view_tag), result);
   72|  1.08k|				if (result) {
   73|  1.02k|					ASSERT_EQ(derivation, expected_derivation);
   74|  1.02k|				}
   75|  1.08k|			}
   76|  3.37k|			else if (name == "derive_public_key") {
   77|  1.08k|				hash derivation, base, derived_key, expected_derived_key;
   78|  1.08k|				std::string result_str;
   79|  1.08k|				size_t output_index;
   80|  1.08k|				f >> derivation >> output_index >> base >> result_str;
   81|  1.08k|				const bool result = (result_str == "true");
   82|  1.08k|				if (result) {
   83|  1.02k|					f >> expected_derived_key;
   84|  1.02k|				}
   85|  1.08k|				ASSERT_EQ(derive_public_key(derivation, output_index, base, derived_key), result);
   86|  1.08k|				if (result) {
   87|  1.02k|					ASSERT_EQ(derived_key, expected_derived_key);
   88|  1.02k|				}
   89|  1.08k|			}
   90|  2.28k|			else if (name == "derive_view_tag") {
   91|    280|				hash derivation;
   92|    280|				uint64_t output_index;
   93|    280|				std::string result_str;
   94|    280|				f >> derivation >> output_index >> result_str;
   95|    280|				uint8_t view_tag;
   96|    280|				p2pool::derive_view_tag(derivation, output_index, view_tag);
   97|       |
   98|    280|				char buf[log::Stream::BUF_SIZE + 1];
   99|    280|				log::Stream s(buf);
  100|    280|				s << log::hex_buf(&view_tag, 1) << '\0';
  101|       |
  102|    280|				ASSERT_EQ(buf, result_str);
  103|    280|			}
  104|  2.00k|			else if (name == "get_tx_keys") {
  105|    400|				hash wallet_spend_key, monero_block_id, pub_check, sec_check;
  106|    400|				f >> wallet_spend_key >> monero_block_id >> pub_check >> sec_check;
  107|       |
  108|    400|				hash pub, sec;
  109|    400|				p2pool::get_tx_keys(pub, sec, wallet_spend_key, monero_block_id);
  110|       |
  111|    400|				ASSERT_EQ(pub, pub_check);
  112|    400|				ASSERT_EQ(sec, sec_check);
  113|    400|			}
  114|  1.60k|			else if (name == "check_key") {
  115|  1.48k|				hash pub_key;
  116|  1.48k|				std::string result_str;
  117|       |
  118|  1.48k|				f >> pub_key >> result_str;				
  119|       |
  120|  1.48k|				ge_p3 p;
  121|  1.48k|				ASSERT_EQ(ge_frombytes_vartime(&p, pub_key.h) == 0, result_str == "true");
  122|  1.48k|			}
  123|    120|			else if (name == "check_torsion") {
  124|    116|				hash pub_key;
  125|    116|				std::string result_str;
  126|       |
  127|    116|				f >> pub_key >> result_str;				
  128|       |
  129|    116|				ge_p3 p;
  130|    116|				ASSERT_EQ(
  131|    116|					(ge_frombytes_vartime(&p, pub_key.h) == 0)
  132|    116|					&& !fcmp_pp::mul8_is_identity(p)

  134|    116|				, result_str == "true");
  135|    116|			}
  136|  4.46k|		} while (!f.eof());
  137|      4|	}
  138|      1|	}

  147|       |TEST(crypto, batch)
  148|      1|{
  149|      1|	init_crypto_cache();
  150|      1|	thread_pool_init();
  151|       |
  152|      1|	constexpr size_t N = 1000;
  153|       |
  154|      1|	std::vector<std::pair<hash, size_t>> in;
  155|      1|	std::vector<std::pair<hash, int32_t>> reference_out;
  156|       |
  157|      1|	std::vector<batch_public_key_input> in2;
  158|      1|	std::vector<std::pair<hash, bool>> reference_out2;
  159|       |
  160|      1|	size_t i = 0;
  161|       |
  162|      1|	hash txkey_pub, txkey_sec;
  163|      1|	p2pool::generate_keys_deterministic(txkey_pub, txkey_sec, reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&i), sizeof(i));
  164|       |
  165|      1|	i = N + 1;
  166|       |
  167|      1|	hash pub2, sec2;
  168|      1|	p2pool::generate_keys_deterministic(pub2, sec2, reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&i), sizeof(i));
  169|       |
  170|  1.00k|	for (i = 1; i <= N; ++i) {
  171|       |		// Generate a valid pubkey
  172|  1.00k|		hash pub, sec;
  173|  1.00k|		p2pool::generate_keys_deterministic(pub, sec, reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&i), sizeof(i));
  174|       |
  175|  1.00k|		in.emplace_back(pub, i);
  176|       |
  177|  1.00k|		hash derivation;
  178|  1.00k|		uint8_t view_tag;
  179|  1.00k|		ASSERT_TRUE(p2pool::generate_key_derivation(pub, txkey_sec, i, derivation, view_tag));
  180|       |
  181|  1.00k|		reference_out.emplace_back(derivation, view_tag);
  182|       |
  183|  1.00k|		hash derived_key;
  184|  1.00k|		bool result = p2pool::derive_public_key(derivation, i, pub2, derived_key);
  185|       |
  186|  1.00k|		in2.emplace_back(derivation, i, pub2);
  187|  1.00k|		reference_out2.emplace_back(derived_key, result);
  188|       |
  189|       |		// Now make it random (use keccak as a deterministic random number generator)
  190|  1.00k|		p2pool::keccak(pub.h, HASH_SIZE, pub.h);
  191|  1.00k|		in.emplace_back(pub, i);
  192|       |
  193|  1.00k|		result = p2pool::generate_key_derivation(pub, txkey_sec, i, derivation, view_tag);
  194|  1.00k|		reference_out.emplace_back(derivation, result ? view_tag : -1);
  195|       |
  196|  1.00k|		result = p2pool::derive_public_key(derivation, i, pub, derived_key);
  197|       |
  198|  1.00k|		in2.emplace_back(derivation, i, pub);
  199|  1.00k|		reference_out2.emplace_back(derived_key, result);
  200|  1.00k|	}
  201|       |
  202|      1|	clear_crypto_cache(0);
  203|       |
  204|      1|#ifdef WITH_INDEXED_HASHES
  205|      1|	indexed_hash::cleanup_storage();
  206|      1|#endif
  207|       |
  208|      1|	std::vector<std::pair<hash, int32_t>> out;
  209|      1|	p2pool::batch_derivations(in, txkey_sec, out);
  210|       |
  211|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(out.size(), reference_out.size());
  212|       |
  213|  2.00k|	for (size_t i = 0; i < out.size(); ++i) {
  214|  2.00k|		if (reference_out[i].second >= 0) {
  215|  1.53k|			ASSERT_EQ(out[i], reference_out[i]);
  216|  1.53k|		}
  217|    466|		else {
  218|    466|			ASSERT_EQ(out[i].second, reference_out[i].second);
  219|    466|		}
  220|  2.00k|	}
  221|       |
  222|      1|	std::vector<std::pair<hash, bool>> out2;
  223|      1|	p2pool::batch_public_keys(in2, out2);
  224|       |
  225|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(out2.size(), reference_out2.size());
  226|       |
  227|  2.00k|	for (size_t i = 0; i < out2.size(); ++i) {
  228|  2.00k|		if (reference_out2[i].second) {
  229|  1.53k|			ASSERT_EQ(out2[i].first, reference_out2[i].first);
  230|  1.53k|		}
  231|    466|		else {
  232|    466|			ASSERT_FALSE(out2[i].second);
  233|    466|		}
  234|  2.00k|	}
  235|       |
  236|      1|	thread_pool_destroy();
  237|      1|	destroy_crypto_cache();
  238|       |
  239|      1|#ifdef WITH_INDEXED_HASHES
  240|      1|	indexed_hash::cleanup_storage();
  241|      1|#endif
  242|      1|}

   79|     18|	{

  126|       |TEST(difficulty_type, mul_div)
  127|      1|{
  128|      1|	auto check = [](const difficulty_type& a, uint64_t b, const difficulty_type& product)
  129|     13|	{
  130|     13|		ASSERT_EQ(a * b, product);
  131|       |
  132|     13|		difficulty_type result = a;
  133|     13|		result *= b;
  134|     13|		ASSERT_EQ(result, product);
  135|       |
  136|     13|		if (b) {
  137|     12|			ASSERT_EQ(result / b, a);
  138|       |
  139|     12|			difficulty_type tmp = result;
  140|     12|			tmp /= difficulty_type(b, 0);
  141|     12|			ASSERT_EQ(tmp, a);
  142|       |
  143|     12|			difficulty_type tmp2 = result;
  144|     12|			tmp2 /= b;
  145|     12|			ASSERT_EQ(tmp2, a);
  146|     12|		}
  147|     13|	};
  148|       |
  149|       |	// (2^128 - 1) * 0 = 0
  150|      1|	check(max_diff, 0, { 0, 0 });
  151|       |
  152|       |	// (2^128 - 1) * 1 = 2^128 - 1
  153|      1|	check(max_diff, 1, max_diff);
  154|       |
  155|       |	// 5057672949897463733145855 * 67280421310721 = 2^128 - 1
  156|      1|	check({ 18446744073709277439ull, 274176ull }, 67280421310721ull, max_diff);
  157|       |

  159|      1|	check({ 10000000000000000000ull, 0 }, 10, { 7766279631452241920ull, 5 });

  220|       |
  221|       |static NOINLINE difficulty_type div128_ref(difficulty_type a, difficulty_type b)
  222|   703k|{
  223|   703k|	difficulty_type result{};
  224|       |
  225|  14.1M|	while (a >= b) {
  226|  13.4M|		difficulty_type t = b;
  227|  13.4M|		difficulty_type q{ 1, 0 };
  228|   526M|		while (a - t >= t) {
  229|   513M|			t += t;
  230|   513M|			q += q;
  231|   513M|		}
  232|  13.4M|		a -= t;
  233|  13.4M|		result += q;
  234|  13.4M|	}
  235|       |
  236|   703k|	return result;
  237|   703k|}
  238|       |
  239|       |TEST(difficulty_type, div128)
  240|      1|{
  241|      1|	auto check = [](difficulty_type a, difficulty_type b, difficulty_type result)
  242|  2.19k|	{
  243|  2.19k|		ASSERT_EQ(div128_ref(a, b), result);
  244|  2.19k|		ASSERT_EQ(a / b, result);
  245|  2.19k|		a /= b;
  246|  2.19k|		ASSERT_EQ(a, result);
  247|  2.19k|	};
  248|       |
  249|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / (2^128 - 1) = 1
  250|      1|	check(max_diff, max_diff, { 1, 0 });
  251|       |
  252|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / (2^64 - 1) = 2^64 + 1
  253|      1|	check(max_diff, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0 }, { 1, 1 });
  254|       |
  255|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / 2^64 = 2^64 - 1
  256|      1|	check(max_diff, { 0, 1 }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0 });
  257|       |
  258|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / (2^64 + 1) = 2^64 - 1
  259|      1|	check(max_diff, { 1, 1 }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0 });
  260|       |
  261|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / (2^64 + 2) = 2^64 - 2
  262|      1|	check(max_diff, { 2, 1 }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() - 1, 0 });
  263|       |
  264|       |	// (2^128 - 1) / 8100430714362380904069067128193 = 42007935
  265|      1|	check(max_diff, { 439125228929, 439125228929 }, { 42007935, 0 });
  266|       |
  267|       |	// (2^128 - 2^64) / (2^64 + 1) = 2^64 - 2
  268|      1|	check({ 0, std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() }, { 1, 1 }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() - 1, 0 });
  269|       |
  270|       |	// (2^128 - 2^64) / 2^64 = 2^64 - 1
  271|      1|	check({ 0, std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() }, { 0, 1 }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0 });
  272|       |
  273|       |	// (2^128 - 2^64) / (2^64 - 1) = 2^64
  274|      1|	check({ 0, std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() }, { std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0 }, { 0, 1 });
  275|       |
  276|       |	// 2^128 - 1 = 3*5*17*257*641*65537*274177*6700417*67280421310721
  277|      1|	constexpr uint64_t factors_2_128_minus_1[] = { 3, 5, 17, 257, 641, 65537, 274177, 6700417, 67280421310721 };
  278|       |
  279|    513|	for (uint32_t i = 0, n = 1U << array_size(factors_2_128_minus_1); i < n; ++i) {
  280|    512|		difficulty_type a{1, 0}, b{1, 0};
  281|       |
  282|  5.12k|		for (uint32_t j = 0; j < array_size(factors_2_128_minus_1); ++j) {
  283|  4.60k|			if (i & (1U << j)) {
  284|  2.30k|				a *= factors_2_128_minus_1[j];
  285|  2.30k|			}
  286|  2.30k|			else {
  287|  2.30k|				b *= factors_2_128_minus_1[j];
  288|  2.30k|			}
  289|  4.60k|		}
  290|       |
  291|       |		// Check that a*b = 2^128 - 1
  292|    512|		difficulty_type c;
  293|    512|		c.lo = umul128(a.lo, b.lo, &c.hi);
  294|       |
  295|    512|		uint64_t t0;
  296|    512|		const uint64_t t1 = umul128(a.lo, b.hi, &t0);
  297|    512|		ASSERT_EQ(t0, 0);
  298|       |
  299|    512|		c.hi += t1;
  300|    512|		ASSERT_GE(c.hi, t1);
  301|       |
  302|    512|		const uint64_t t2 = umul128(a.hi, b.lo, &t0);
  303|    512|		ASSERT_EQ(t0, 0);
  304|       |
  305|    512|		c.hi += t2;
  306|    512|		ASSERT_GE(c.hi, t2);
  307|       |
  308|    512|		ASSERT_EQ(c, max_diff);
  309|       |
  310|    512|		check(max_diff, a, b);
  311|    512|		check(max_diff - 1, a, b - 1);
  312|    512|		check(max_diff - a, a, b - 1);
  313|       |
  314|    512|		if (a != max_diff) {
  315|    511|			check(max_diff - a - 1, a, b - 2);
  316|    511|		}
  317|    512|	}
  318|       |
  319|      1|	{
  320|      1|		difficulty_type a = max_diff - 4;
  321|       |
  322|       |		// (2^128 - 5) / 2002733033099709041094789607565039 = 169909
  323|      1|		check(a, { 7565587230673184495, 108568375269759 }, { 169909, 0 });
  324|       |
  325|       |		// (2^128 - 5) / 2002733033099709041094789607565040 = 169908
  326|      1|		check(a, { 7565587230673184496, 108568375269759 }, { 169908, 0 });
  327|       |
  328|      1|		a -= 1;
  329|       |
  330|       |		// (2^128 - 6) / 2002733033099709041094789607565039 = 169908
  331|      1|		check(a, { 7565587230673184495, 108568375269759 }, { 169908, 0 });
  332|       |
  333|       |		// (2^128 - 6) / 2002733033099709041094789607565038 = 169909
  334|      1|		check(a, { 7565587230673184494, 108568375269759 }, { 169909, 0 });
  335|      1|	}
  336|       |
  337|       |	// Powers of 2
  338|    129|	for (difficulty_type i{ 1, 0 }, j = max_diff; !i.empty(); i += i, j /= 2) {
  339|    128|		check(max_diff, i, j);
  340|    128|	}
  341|       |
  342|       |	// Trivial tests
  343|      1|	check({ 0, 3 }, { 0, 1 }, { 3, 0 });
  344|      1|	check({ 0, 3 }, { 1, 1 }, { 2, 0 });
  345|      1|	check({ 123 * 4 - 1, 456 * 4 }, { 123, 456 }, { 3, 0 });
  346|      1|	check({ 123 * 4, 456 * 4 }, { 123, 456 }, { 4, 0 });
  347|       |
  348|       |	// Exhaustive tests (top 8 bits of each number)
  349|    256|	for (uint64_t i = 1; i < 256; ++i) {
  350|  65.2k|		for (uint64_t j = 1; j < 256; ++j) {
  351|  65.0k|			const difficulty_type a{ 0, i << 56 };
  352|  65.0k|			const difficulty_type b{ 0, j << 56 };
  353|  65.0k|			{
  354|  65.0k|				difficulty_type t = a;
  355|  65.0k|				t /= b;
  356|  65.0k|				ASSERT_EQ(t.lo, i / j);
  357|  65.0k|				ASSERT_EQ(t.hi, 0);
  358|  65.0k|			}
  359|  65.0k|		}
  360|    255|	}
  361|       |
  362|       |	// Bit patterns
  363|      1|	std::vector<difficulty_type> patterns;
  364|       |
  365|       |	// 2^N-1, 2^N, 2^N+1
  366|    129|	for (uint64_t i = 0; i < 128; ++i) {
  367|    128|		difficulty_type t;
  368|    128|		reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(&t)[i / 64] |= 1ull << (i % 64);
  369|    128|		patterns.emplace_back(t - 1);
  370|    128|		patterns.emplace_back(t);
  371|    128|		patterns.emplace_back(t + 1);
  372|    128|	}
  373|       |
  374|       |	// 2^N+2^M, 2^N-2^M
  375|      1|	bool check_bits[128] = {};
  376|      9|	for (uint64_t i = 64 - 4; i < 64 + 4; ++i) {
  377|      8|		check_bits[i] = true;
  378|      8|	}
  379|      9|	for (uint64_t i = 128 - 8; i < 128; ++i) {
  380|      8|		check_bits[i] = true;
  381|      8|	}
  382|    129|	for (uint64_t i = 0; i < 128; ++i) {
  383|    128|		if (!check_bits[i]) {
  384|    112|			continue;
  385|    112|		}
  386|     16|		difficulty_type t1;
  387|     16|		reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(&t1)[i / 64] = 1ull << (i % 64);
  388|    552|		for (uint64_t j = i + 1; j < 128; ++j) {
  389|    536|			if (!check_bits[j]) {
  390|    416|				continue;
  391|    416|			}
  392|    120|			difficulty_type t2;
  393|    120|			reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(&t2)[j / 64] = 1ull << (j % 64);
  394|    120|			patterns.emplace_back(t2 + t1);
  395|    120|			patterns.emplace_back(t2 - t1);
  396|    120|		}
  397|     16|	}
  398|       |
  399|       |	// All previous patterns, but ~X
  400|    625|	for (size_t i = 0, n = patterns.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  401|    624|		patterns.emplace_back(~patterns[i].lo, ~patterns[i].hi);
  402|    624|	}
  403|       |
  404|      1|	std::sort(patterns.begin(), patterns.end());
  405|      1|	patterns.erase(std::unique(patterns.begin(), patterns.end()), patterns.end());
  406|       |
  407|       |	// remove 0
  408|      1|	patterns.erase(patterns.begin());
  409|       |
  410|  1.18k|	for (size_t i = 0, n = patterns.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  411|  1.18k|		const difficulty_type& a = patterns[i];
  412|   702k|		for (size_t j = i + 1; j < n; ++j) {
  413|   701k|			const difficulty_type& b = patterns[j];
  414|   701k|			ASSERT_EQ(div128_ref(b, a), b / a);
  415|   701k|		}
  416|  1.18k|	}
  417|       |
  418|       |	// Random tests with fixed seed
  419|      1|	std::mt19937_64 r(0);
  420|       |
  421|  10.0M|	for (uint64_t i = 0; i < 10000000; ++i) {
  422|       |		// Random number of bits [1, 63]
  423|  10.0M|		const uint64_t N = (r() % 63) + 1;
  424|       |
  425|       |		// Random multiplier [1, 2^N - 1]
  426|  10.0M|		uint64_t k;
  427|  10.2M|		do {
  428|  10.2M|			k = r() & ((1ull << N) - 1);
  429|  10.2M|		} while (k == 0);
  430|       |
  431|  10.0M|		uint64_t t;
  432|  10.0M|		const uint64_t max_a = udiv128(1, 0, k + 1, &t);
  433|       |
  434|       |		// Random number [2^64, 2^128 / (k + 1)]
  435|  10.0M|		difficulty_type a{ r(), 0 };
  436|  10.1M|		do {
  437|  10.1M|			a.hi = r() % max_a;
  438|  10.1M|		} while (a.hi == 0);
  439|       |
  440|  10.0M|		difficulty_type b1 = a * k;
  441|  10.0M|		difficulty_type b2 = b1 - 1;
  442|  10.0M|		difficulty_type b3 = b1 + a;
  443|  10.0M|		difficulty_type b4 = b3 - 1;
  444|       |
  445|  10.0M|		b1 /= a;
  446|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b1.lo, k);
  447|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b1.hi, 0);
  448|       |
  449|  10.0M|		b2 /= a;
  450|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b2.lo, k - 1);
  451|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b2.hi, 0);
  452|       |
  453|  10.0M|		b3 /= a;
  454|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b3.lo, k + 1);
  455|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b3.hi, 0);
  456|       |
  457|  10.0M|		b4 /= a;
  458|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b4.lo, k);
  459|  10.0M|		ASSERT_EQ(b4.hi, 0);
  460|  10.0M|	}
  461|      1|}
  462|       |
  463|       |TEST(difficulty_type, compare)
  464|      1|{
  465|      1|	const difficulty_type diff[9] = {
  466|      1|		{ 0, 0 }, { 1, 0 }, { 2, 0 },
  467|      1|		{ 0, 1 }, { 1, 1 }, { 2, 1 },
  468|      1|		{ 0, 2 }, { 1, 2 }, { 2, 2 },
  469|      1|	};
  470|       |
  471|     10|	for (int i = 0; i < 9; ++i) {
  472|     90|		for (int j = 0; j < 9; ++j) {
  473|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] >  diff[j], i >  j);
  474|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] <  diff[j], i <  j);
  475|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] >= diff[j], i >= j);
  476|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] <= diff[j], i <= j);
  477|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] == diff[j], i == j);
  478|     81|			ASSERT_EQ(diff[i] != diff[j], i != j);
  479|     81|		}
  480|      9|	}
  481|      1|}
  482|       |
  483|       |TEST(difficulty_type, input_output)
  484|      1|{
  485|     11|	auto test_value = [](uint64_t lo, uint64_t hi, const char* s) {
  486|     11|		difficulty_type diff{ lo, hi };
  487|     11|		std::stringstream ss;
  488|     11|		ss << diff;
  489|     11|		ASSERT_EQ(ss.str(), s);
  490|     11|		difficulty_type diff2;
  491|     11|		ss >> diff2;
  492|     11|		ASSERT_EQ(diff2, diff);
  493|     11|	};
  494|       |
  495|      1|	test_value(0, 0, "0");
  496|      1|	test_value(1, 0, "1");
  497|      1|	test_value(340599339356ull, 0, "340599339356");
  498|      1|	test_value(std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), 0, "18446744073709551615");
  499|      1|	test_value(0, 1, "18446744073709551616");
  500|      1|	test_value(1, 1, "18446744073709551617");
  501|      1|	test_value(7766279631452241919ull, 5, "99999999999999999999");
  502|      1|	test_value(7766279631452241920ull, 5, "100000000000000000000");
  503|      1|	test_value(7766279631452241921ull, 5, "100000000000000000001");
  504|      1|	test_value(14083847773837265618ull, 6692605942ull, "123456789012345678901234567890");
  505|      1|	test_value(std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max(), "340282366920938463463374607431768211455");
  506|       |
  507|      1|	std::stringstream ss;
  508|      1|	difficulty_type diff;
  509|       |
  510|      1|	ss << "340599339356 test";
  511|      1|	ss >> diff;
  512|       |

  514|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(diff.hi, 0ull);

  518|      1|{
  519|     10|	auto test_value = [](uint64_t lo, uint64_t hi, const char* s, bool valid = true) {
  520|     10|		difficulty_type diff{ lo, hi };
  521|     10|		std::stringstream ss;
  522|     10|		ss << "{\"diff\":\"" << s << "\"}";
  523|       |

  527|       |

  529|       |

  531|     10|		ASSERT_EQ(result, valid);
  532|       |

  534|      9|			ASSERT_EQ(diff2, diff);
  535|      9|		}

  537|       |

  539|      1|	test_value(1, 0, "0x1");
  540|      1|	test_value(0x123456789abcdefull, 0, "0x123456789abcdef");
  541|      1|	test_value(0x123456789abcdefull, 0, "0x123456789ABCDEF");

  545|      1|	test_value(0x1122334455667788ull, 0x99aabbccddeeff00ull, "0x99aabbccddeeff001122334455667788");

  552|       |TEST(difficulty_type, check_pow)
  553|      1|{
  554|      1|	hash h;
  555|       |
  556|       |	// Power of 2 close to the current Monero network difficulty
  557|      1|	difficulty_type diff = { 1ull << 38, 0 };
  558|      1|	{
  559|       |		// 2^256 / 2^38 = 2^218
  560|       |		// diff.check_pow() will get 2^256 as a multiplication result = lowest possible value that fails the test
  561|      1|		uint64_t data[4] = { 0, 0, 0, 1ull << 26 };
  562|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  563|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  564|       |
  565|       |		// Now decrease the hash by 1. It should pass the test now
  566|      1|		data[0] = data[1] = data[2] = std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max();
  567|      1|		--data[3];
  568|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  569|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  570|      1|	}
  571|       |
  572|       |	/*
  573|       |	* Factors of 2^256 - 1:
  574|       |	* P1 = 3
  575|       |	* P1 = 5
  576|       |	* P2 = 17
  577|       |	* P3 = 257
  578|       |	* P3 = 641
  579|       |	* P5 = 65537
  580|       |	* P6 = 274177
  581|       |	* P7 = 6700417
  582|       |	* P14 = 67280421310721
  583|       |	* P17 = 59649589127497217
  584|       |	* P22 = 5704689200685129054721
  585|       |	*/
  586|      1|	diff = { 67280421310721ull, 0 };
  587|      1|	{
  588|       |		// (2^256 - 1) / 67280421310721 = 1721036922503113971692907638171526209875755521904893141463060735
  589|       |		// diff.check_pow() will get 2^256-1 as a multiplication result = highest possible value that still passes the test
  590|      1|		uint64_t data[4] = { 0xfffffffffffbd0ffull, 0x0000000000042f00ull, 0xfffffffffffbd0ffull, 0x42f00ull };
  591|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  592|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  593|       |
  594|       |		// Now increase the hash by 1. It should not pass the test anymore
  595|      1|		++data[0];
  596|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  597|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  598|      1|	}
  599|       |
  600|       |	// diff = 5704689200685129054721
  601|      1|	diff = { 4645281908877605377ull, 309ull };
  602|      1|	{
  603|       |		// (2^256 - 1) / 5704689200685129054721 = 20297703374166229616474325006177763232573806344580020735
  604|       |		// diff.check_pow() will get 2^256-1 as a multiplication result = highest possible value that still passes the test
  605|      1|		uint64_t data[4] = { 0xff2c1503c50eb9ffull, 0xffffffffffffffffull, 0xd3eafc3af14600ull, 0 };
  606|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  607|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  608|       |
  609|       |		// Now increase the hash by 1. It should not pass the test anymore
  610|      1|		++data[0];
  611|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  612|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  613|      1|	}
  614|       |
  615|       |	/*
  616|       |	* Factors of 2^256 + 1:
  617|       |	* P16 = 1238926361552897
  618|       |	* P62 = 93461639715357977769163558199606896584051237541638188580280321
  619|       |	*/
  620|      1|	diff = { 1238926361552897ull, 0 };
  621|      1|	{
  622|       |		// (2^256 + 1) / 1238926361552897 = 93461639715357977769163558199606896584051237541638188580280321
  623|       |		// diff.check_pow() will get 2^256+1 as a multiplication result = lowest possible non-power of 2 that fails the test
  624|      1|		uint64_t data[4] = { 0x49baa0ba2c911801ull, 0x6ee3637cab2586d0ull, 0x4c585a8f5c7073e3, 0x3a29ull };
  625|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  626|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  627|       |
  628|       |		// Now decrease the hash by 1. It should pass the test now
  629|      1|		--data[0];
  630|      1|		memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  631|      1|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  632|      1|	}
  633|       |
  634|       |	// Randomized tests with fixed seed
  635|      1|	std::mt19937_64 r(0);
  636|       |
  637|  1.00k|	for (int i = 0; i < 1000; ++i) {
  638|       |		// Random difficulty between 300G and 400G
  639|  1.00k|		diff.lo = 300000000000ull + (r() % 100000000000ull);
  640|  1.00k|		diff.hi = 0;
  641|       |
  642|       |		// All zeros
  643|  1.00k|		memset(h.h, 0, HASH_SIZE);
  644|  1.00k|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  645|       |
  646|       |		// All ones
  647|  1.00k|		memset(h.h, -1, HASH_SIZE);
  648|  1.00k|		ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  649|       |
  650|  1.00k|		{
  651|  1.00k|			uint64_t data[4];
  652|  1.00k|			uint64_t rem;
  653|  1.00k|			data[3] = udiv128(1, 0, diff.lo, &rem);
  654|  1.00k|			data[2] = udiv128(rem, 0, diff.lo, &rem);
  655|  1.00k|			data[1] = udiv128(rem, 0, diff.lo, &rem);
  656|  1.00k|			data[0] = udiv128(rem, 0, diff.lo, &rem);
  657|       |
  658|       |			// Max hash value that passes this difficulty
  659|  1.00k|			memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  660|  1.00k|			ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  661|       |
  662|       |			// Add 1 to data (256-bit number)
  663|  1.00k|			for (int j = 0; j <= 3; ++j) {
  664|  1.00k|				++data[j];
  665|  1.00k|				if (data[j]) {
  666|       |					// No carry, exit the loop
  667|  1.00k|					break;
  668|  1.00k|				}
  669|  1.00k|			}
  670|       |
  671|       |			// Min hash value that fails this difficulty
  672|  1.00k|			memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  673|  1.00k|			ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  674|  1.00k|		}
  675|       |
  676|  1.00k|		const uint64_t target = diff.target();
  677|       |
  678|       |		// Random values that pass
  679|  10.0M|		for (int j = 0; j < 10000; ++j) {
  680|  10.0M|			const uint64_t data[4] = { r(), r(), r(), r() % target };
  681|  10.0M|			memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  682|  10.0M|			ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), true);
  683|  10.0M|		}
  684|       |
  685|       |		// Random values that fail
  686|  10.0M|		for (int j = 0; j < 10000; ++j) {
  687|  10.0M|			const uint64_t data[4] = { r(), r(), r(), target + (r() % (std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max() - target + 1)) };
  688|  10.0M|			memcpy(h.h, data, HASH_SIZE);
  689|       |			ASSERT_EQ(diff.check_pow(h), false);
  690|  10.0M|		}
  691|  1.00k|	}
  692|      1|}

   49|      1|{
   50|      1|	hash hashes[HASH_SIZE + 1];
   51|       |
   52|     33|	for (size_t i = 1; i <= HASH_SIZE; ++i) {
   53|     32|		hashes[i].h[i - 1] = 1;
   54|     32|	}
   55|       |
   56|     34|	for (size_t i = 0; i <= HASH_SIZE; ++i) {
   57|  1.12k|		for (size_t j = 0; j <= HASH_SIZE; ++j) {
   58|  1.08k|			ASSERT_EQ(hashes[i] <  hashes[j], i <  j);
   59|  1.08k|			ASSERT_EQ(hashes[i] == hashes[j], i == j);

   27|       |
   28|       |// Original Monero's tree_path function to test against
   29|       |
   30|     52|size_t tree_hash_cnt(size_t count) {
   31|     52|	size_t pow = 2;
   32|    168|	while(pow < count) pow <<= 1;
   33|     52|	return pow >> 1;
   34|     52|}
   35|       |
   36|       |bool tree_path(size_t count, size_t idx, uint32_t* path)
   37|     56|{
   38|     56|	if (count == 0)
   39|      1|		return false;
   40|       |
   41|     55|	if (count == 1) {
   42|      1|		*path = 0;
   43|      1|	}
   44|     54|	else if (count == 2) {
   45|      2|		*path = idx == 0 ? 0 : 1;
   46|      2|	}
   47|     52|	else {
   48|     52|		size_t i, j;
   49|       |
   50|     52|		*path = 0;
   51|     52|		size_t cnt = tree_hash_cnt(count);
   52|       |
   53|    162|		for (i = 2 * cnt - count, j = 2 * cnt - count; j < cnt; i += 2, ++j) {
   54|    110|			if (idx == i || idx == i + 1)
   55|     32|			{
   56|     32|				*path = (*path << 1) | (idx == i ? 0 : 1);
   57|     32|				idx = j;
   58|     32|			}
   59|    110|		}
   60|     52|		assert(i == count);
   61|       |
   62|    116|		while (cnt > 2) {
   63|     64|			cnt >>= 1;
   64|    230|			for (i = 0, j = 0; j < cnt; i += 2, ++j) {
   65|    166|				if (idx == i || idx == i + 1)
   66|     64|				{
   67|     64|					*path = (*path << 1) | (idx == i ? 0 : 1);
   68|     64|					idx = j;
   69|     64|				}
   70|    166|			}
   71|     64|		}
   72|       |
   73|     52|		if (idx == 0 || idx == 1)
   74|     52|		{
   75|     52|			*path = (*path << 1) | (idx == 0 ? 0 : 1);
   76|     52|			idx = 0;
   77|     52|		}
   78|     52|	}
   79|     55|	return true;
   80|     56|}
   81|       |

  324|      1|	keccak(check[6].h, HASH_SIZE * 2, check[3].h);
  325|       |
  326|      1|	keccak(check[0].h, HASH_SIZE * 2, check[0].h);
  327|      1|	keccak(check[2].h, HASH_SIZE * 2, check[1].h);
  328|       |
  329|      1|	keccak(check[0].h, HASH_SIZE * 2, check[0].h);
  330|       |
  331|      1|	ASSERT_EQ(root, check[0]);

  336|      1|{
  337|      1|	hash input[4];
  338|      1|	uint8_t data[] = "data 0";
  339|       |
  340|      5|	for (size_t i = 0; i < 4; ++i, ++data[sizeof(data) - 2]) {
  341|      4|		keccak(data, sizeof(data) - 1, input[i].h);
  342|      4|	}
  343|       |
  344|      1|	std::vector<hash> proof;
  345|      1|	uint32_t path;
  346|       |
  347|       |	// Every level above the leaves is missing (tree.size() == 1).
  348|      1|	{
  349|      1|		const std::vector<hash> leaves = { input[0], input[1], input[2] };
  350|       |
  351|      1|		std::vector<std::vector<hash>> good;

  354|       |

  356|      1|		bad[0] = leaves;
  357|      1|		ASSERT_FALSE(get_merkle_proof(bad, input[2], proof, path));
  358|      1|	}
  359|       |
  360|       |	// The right number of levels, but level 1 is truncated.
  361|      1|	{
  362|      1|		const std::vector<hash> leaves = { input[0], input[1], input[2], input[3] };
  363|       |
  364|      1|		std::vector<std::vector<hash>> good;
  365|      1|		merkle_hash_full_tree(leaves, good);
  366|      1|		ASSERT_GE(good.size(), 2);
  367|      1|		ASSERT_TRUE(get_merkle_proof(good, input[3], proof, path));
  368|       |
  369|      1|		std::vector<std::vector<hash>> bad = good;
  370|      1|		bad[1].resize(2);
  371|      1|		ASSERT_FALSE(get_merkle_proof(bad, input[3], proof, path));
  372|      1|	}
  373|       |
  374|       |	// Proofs with extra junk in it
  375|      4|	for (int n = 1; n <= 3; ++n) {
  376|      3|		std::vector<hash> leaves;
  377|      9|		for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
  378|      6|			leaves.push_back(input[i]);
  379|      6|		}
  380|       |
  381|      3|		std::vector<std::vector<hash>> good;
  382|      3|		merkle_hash_full_tree(leaves, good);
  383|       |
  384|       |		// No junk

  142|      1|{
  143|      1|	thread_pool_init();
  144|      1|	init_crypto_cache();
  145|      1|	{
  146|      1|	struct STest
  147|      1|	{
  148|      1|		const char* m_poolName;
  149|      1|		const char* m_fileName;

  153|      1|		bool m_shuffle;
  154|      1|		hash m_templateBlobsHash;
  155|      1|	} tests[6] = {
  156|      1|		{ "default", "sidechain_dump.dat", 3456189, 11704382, 53, false, H("c84a85eebf17ab266e8a81b347dd7490043ede3a055c0dbe85e9cd378905845a") },
  157|      1|		{ "default", "sidechain_dump.dat", 3456189, 11704382, 53, true, H("c84a85eebf17ab266e8a81b347dd7490043ede3a055c0dbe85e9cd378905845a") },
  158|      1|		{ "mini", "sidechain_dump_mini.dat", 3456189, 11207082, 578, false, H("08debd1378bae899017eb58362f4c638d78e5218558025142dcbc2651c76b27e") },
  159|      1|		{ "mini", "sidechain_dump_mini.dat", 3456189, 11207082, 578, true, H("08debd1378bae899017eb58362f4c638d78e5218558025142dcbc2651c76b27e") },
  160|      1|		{ "nano", "sidechain_dump_nano.dat", 3456189, 188542, 115, false, H("dd667c41eb15ffb0eb662065545dc0dfbbcac8393348a4fc0a7367040319b0d5") },
  161|      1|		{ "nano", "sidechain_dump_nano.dat", 3456189, 188542, 115, true, H("dd667c41eb15ffb0eb662065545dc0dfbbcac8393348a4fc0a7367040319b0d5") },
  162|      1|	};
  163|       |
  164|      1|	for (const STest& t : tests)
  165|      6|	{
  166|      6|		SideChain sidechain(nullptr, NetworkType::Mainnet, t.m_poolName);
  167|       |
  168|       |		// Difficulty of block 3454976
  169|      6|		sidechain.m_testMainChainDiff = difficulty_type(625461936742ULL, 0ULL);
  170|       |
  171|      6|		std::ifstream f(t.m_fileName, std::ios::binary | std::ios::ate);
  172|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(f.good() && f.is_open(), true);
  173|       |
  174|      6|		std::vector<uint8_t> buf(f.tellg());
  175|      6|		f.seekg(0);
  176|      6|		f.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(buf.data()), buf.size());
  177|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(f.good(), true);
  178|       |
  179|      6|		std::vector<PoolBlock*> blocks;
  180|  26.8k|		for (const uint8_t *p = buf.data(), *e = buf.data() + buf.size(); p < e;) {
  181|  26.7k|			ASSERT_TRUE(p + sizeof(uint32_t) <= e);
  182|  26.7k|			const uint32_t n = *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(p);
  183|  26.7k|			p += sizeof(uint32_t);
  184|       |
  185|  26.7k|			ASSERT_TRUE(p + n <= e);
  186|       |
  187|  26.7k|			PoolBlock* b = new PoolBlock();
  188|  26.7k|			ASSERT_EQ(b->deserialize(p, n, sidechain, false, false), 0);
  189|  26.7k|			p += n;
  190|       |
  191|  26.7k|			blocks.push_back(b);
  192|  26.7k|		}
  193|       |
  194|      6|		if (t.m_shuffle) {
  195|      3|			std::mt19937_64 rng;
  196|       |
  197|  13.3k|			for (uint64_t i = 0, k, n = blocks.size(); i < n - 1; ++i) {
  198|  13.3k|				umul128(rng(), n - i, &k);
  199|  13.3k|				std::swap(blocks[i], blocks[i + k]);
  200|  13.3k|			}
  201|      3|		}
  202|       |
  203|  26.8k|		for (uint64_t i = 0, n = blocks.size(); i < n; ++i) {
  204|  26.7k|			ASSERT_TRUE(sidechain.add_block(*blocks[i]));
  205|  26.7k|			ASSERT_TRUE(sidechain.find_block(blocks[i]->m_sidechainId) != nullptr);
  206|  26.7k|			delete blocks[i];
  207|  26.7k|		}
  208|       |
  209|  26.8k|		for (auto it = sidechain.blocksById().begin(); it != sidechain.blocksById().end(); ++it) {
  210|  26.7k|			const PoolBlock* b = it->second;
  211|  26.7k|			ASSERT_TRUE(b->m_verified);
  212|  26.7k|			ASSERT_FALSE(b->m_invalid);
  213|  26.7k|		}
  214|       |
  215|      6|		const PoolBlock* tip = sidechain.chainTip();
  216|      6|		ASSERT_TRUE(tip != nullptr);
  217|      6|		ASSERT_TRUE(tip->m_verified);
  218|      6|		ASSERT_FALSE(tip->m_invalid);
  219|       |
  220|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(tip->m_txinGenHeight, t.m_txinGenHeight);
  221|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(tip->m_sidechainHeight, t.m_sidechainHeight);
  222|       |
  223|      6|		{
  224|      6|			BlockTemplate tpl(&sidechain, nullptr);
  225|      6|			auto& r = tpl.rng();
  226|      6|			r.seed(0);
  227|       |
  228|      6|			MinerData data;
  229|      6|			data.major_version = 16;
  230|      6|			data.height = t.m_txinGenHeight;
  231|      6|			data.prev_id = H("f7723462d2f4d9f605601df8de8bd483802d2275f77cbf3a6f61d8f3fc4c47bc");
  232|      6|			data.seed_hash = H("11186f5a8473d8dc7a0d3a0bf25834a07b1dffe8741d53cd543a8708c2e8b2a9");
  233|      6|			data.difficulty = { 656711234691ULL, 0 };
  234|      6|			data.median_weight = 300000;
  235|      6|			data.already_generated_coins = std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max();
  236|      6|			data.median_timestamp = (1ULL << 35) - 2;
  237|       |
  238|      6|			Mempool mempool;
  239|       |
  240|  49.1k|			for (uint64_t i = 0; i < 8192; ++i) {

  242|  49.1k|				h.u64()[0] = i;
  243|       |

  245|  49.1k|				tx.id = static_cast<indexed_hash>(h);
  246|  49.1k|				tx.fee = (r() % 1'000'000'000) + 30'000'000;
  247|  49.1k|				tx.weight = (r() % 20'000) + 1'500;
  248|  49.1k|				mempool.add(tx);
  249|  49.1k|			}

  252|       |
  253|      6|			params.m_miningWallet = Wallet("44MnN1f3Eto8DZYUWuE5XZNUtE3vcRzt2j6PzqWpPau34e6Cf4fAxt6X2MBmrm6F9YMEiMNjN6W4Shn4pLcfNAja621jwyg");
  254|      6|			params.m_subaddress = Wallet("86eQxzSW4AZfvsWRSop755WZUsog6L3x32NRZukeeShnS4mBGVpcqQhS6pCNxj44usPKNwesZ45ooHyjDku6nVZdT3Q9qrz");
  255|       |
  256|      6|			tpl.update(data, mempool, params);
  257|       |
  258|      6|			std::vector<uint8_t> blobs;
  259|      6|			uint64_t height;
  260|      6|			difficulty_type diff, aux_diff, sidechain_diff;
  261|      6|			hash seed_hash;
  262|      6|			size_t nonce_offset;
  263|      6|			uint32_t template_id;
  264|      6|			const uint32_t blob_size = tpl.get_hashing_blobs(0, 1000, blobs, height, diff, aux_diff, sidechain_diff, seed_hash, nonce_offset, template_id);
  265|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(blob_size, 77);
  266|       |
  267|      6|			hash blobs_hash;
  268|      6|			keccak(blobs.data(), static_cast<int>(blobs.size()), blobs_hash.h);
  269|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(blobs_hash, t.m_templateBlobsHash);
  270|      6|		}
  271|       |
  272|      6|		PoolBlock block;
  273|      6|		ASSERT_TRUE(block.m_minerWallet.decode("44MnN1f3Eto8DZYUWuE5XZNUtE3vcRzt2j6PzqWpPau34e6Cf4fAxt6X2MBmrm6F9YMEiMNjN6W4Shn4pLcfNAja621jwyg"));

  275|      6|		std::vector<MinerShare> shares;
  276|       |
  277|      6|		ASSERT_TRUE(sidechain.fill_sidechain_data(block, shares));
  278|       |
  279|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(block.m_sidechainHeight, t.m_sidechainHeight + 1);
  280|      6|		ASSERT_EQ(shares.size(), t.m_expectedSharesNextBlock);
  281|       |
  282|      6|		const PoolBlock* parent = sidechain.find_block(tip->m_parent);
  283|      6|		ASSERT_TRUE(parent != nullptr);
  284|       |
  285|       |		// Check pruned and compact broadcast blobs
  286|       |
  287|      6|		auto tip_full_blob = tip->serialize_mainchain_data();
  288|      6|		auto v2 = tip->serialize_sidechain_data();
  289|      6|		tip_full_blob.insert(tip_full_blob.end(), v2.begin(), v2.end());
  290|       |
  291|      6|		P2PServer::Broadcast broadcast(*tip, parent);
  292|       |
  293|      6|		{
  294|      6|			PoolBlock block2;
  295|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(block2.deserialize(broadcast.pruned_blob.data(), broadcast.pruned_blob.size(), sidechain, false, true), 0);
  296|       |
  297|      6|			auto v1 = block2.serialize_mainchain_data();
  298|      6|			v2 = block2.serialize_sidechain_data();
  299|      6|			v1.insert(v1.end(), v2.begin(), v2.end());
  300|       |
  301|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(v1, tip_full_blob);
  302|       |
  303|      6|			ASSERT_NE(block2.deserialize(broadcast.pruned_blob.data(), broadcast.pruned_blob.size(), sidechain, false, false), 0);
  304|      6|		}
  305|       |
  306|      6|		if (!broadcast.compact_blob.empty()) {
  307|      6|			PoolBlock block3;
  308|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(block3.deserialize(broadcast.compact_blob.data(), broadcast.compact_blob.size(), sidechain, true, true), 0);
  309|       |
  310|      6|			auto v1 = block3.serialize_mainchain_data();
  311|      6|			v2 = block3.serialize_sidechain_data();
  312|      6|			v1.insert(v1.end(), v2.begin(), v2.end());
  313|       |
  314|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(v1, tip_full_blob);
  315|      6|		}
  316|       |
  317|      6|		if (!broadcast.compact_unpruned_blob.empty()) {
  318|      6|			PoolBlock block4;
  319|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(block4.deserialize(broadcast.compact_unpruned_blob.data(), broadcast.compact_unpruned_blob.size(), sidechain, true, false), 0);
  320|       |
  321|      6|			auto v1 = block4.serialize_mainchain_data();
  322|      6|			v2 = block4.serialize_sidechain_data();
  323|      6|			v1.insert(v1.end(), v2.begin(), v2.end());
  324|       |
  325|      6|			ASSERT_EQ(v1, tip_full_blob);
  326|      6|		}
  327|      6|	}
  328|      1|	}

    2|       | * This file is part of the Monero P2Pool <https://github.com/SChernykh/p2pool>

   12|       | * General Public License for more details.
   13|       | *
